  /* ─── step requirement chips ───
     Helps users quickly distinguish required selections from optional
     adjustments. Three chip variants:
       .step-required  — needed to proceed (red-ish dot)
       .step-optional  — can be skipped (subtle outline)
       .step-defaults  — has sensible defaults; tweak if you want (cyan)
     Rendered in the step header just below the title. Field-level
     `.required-mark` and `.optional-mark` chips follow the same color
     vocabulary so a user scanning a long form can see at a glance which
     fields demand input vs which have a working default. */
  .step-chip {
    display: inline-block; margin: 0 0 12px;
    padding: 3px 10px;
    font-family: var(--font-display); font-size: 0.62rem;
    letter-spacing: 0.14em; text-transform: uppercase; font-weight: 600;
  }
  .step-chip.step-required {
    color: #ff7a7a; border: 1px solid rgba(255,122,122,0.35);
    background: rgba(255,122,122,0.06);
  }
  .step-chip.step-required::before { content: '● '; color: #ff7a7a; }
  .step-chip.step-optional {
    color: var(--text-faint); border: 1px solid var(--glass-border);
    background: transparent;
  }
  .step-chip.step-optional::before { content: '○ '; }
  .step-chip.step-defaults {
    color: var(--neon-cyan); border: 1px solid rgba(0,212,230,0.3);
    background: rgba(0,212,230,0.04);
  }
  .step-chip.step-defaults::before { content: '✓ '; opacity: 0.7; }
  /* Inline chip used inside .field-label to mark a single field within a
     step as required/optional/preset. Smaller and tighter than the step-
     header chip — sits beside the field title, not as its own line. */
  .field-label .required-mark,
  .field-label .optional-mark,
  .field-label .preset-mark {
    display: inline-block; margin-left: 6px;
    padding: 1px 6px;
    font-family: var(--font-body); font-size: 0.58rem;
    letter-spacing: 0.06em; text-transform: lowercase; font-weight: 500;
    vertical-align: 1px;
  }
  .field-label .required-mark { color: #ff8585; background: rgba(255,133,133,0.08); border: 1px solid rgba(255,133,133,0.3); }
  .field-label .optional-mark { color: var(--text-faint); border: 1px solid var(--glass-border); }
  .field-label .preset-mark { color: var(--neon-cyan); background: rgba(0,212,230,0.04); border: 1px solid rgba(0,212,230,0.25); }

  /* Background layer & stage are siblings at z-index 0; stage paints later in
     DOM. Original rule made stage 75% opaque black when a bg was active,
     which drowned out AI images (effective opacity dropped to ~9%). The
     visualizer canvas inside .stage retains its own appearance — we just
     make the stage's BACKGROUND transparent so the bg-img beneath can show.
     A tiny dim (15% black) keeps high-contrast visualizers readable without
     obscuring the image. */
  body.bg-active .stage { background: rgba(0,0,0,0.15); }
  body.bg-active .highway-canvas, body.bg-active .spiral-svg, body.bg-active .pulse-rings { opacity: 0.8; }

  /* ============ TOAST NOTIFICATIONS ============ */
  /* z-index: 10000 sits above every other modal layer:
     - bg-fullscreen (120), bg-lightbox preview popup (110),
     - modals (100), safety-screen (2000),
     - rule overlay (9000), stats-modal-overlay (9999).
     Toasts are ephemeral and must remain visible regardless of which
     modal happens to be open underneath them. The two z:99999 elements
     (debug banner top-right, emergency-export failsafe bottom-right)
     deliberately outrank toasts: they're both bug/recovery indicators
     that need to be visible even if a toast is showing. The failsafe
     sits at bottom:60px so it doesn't overlap the toast stack at
     bottom:14px — both bottom-right but vertically offset. */
  #toastContainer {
    position: fixed; bottom: 14px; right: 14px; z-index: 10000;
    display: flex; flex-direction: column-reverse; gap: 8px;
    max-width: 340px; pointer-events: none;
  }
  .toast {
    display: flex; align-items: flex-start; gap: 9px;
    background: #16161c;padding: 9px 12px;
    font-size: 0.78rem; line-height: 1.5; color: #e2e0d8;
    pointer-events: all; box-shadow: 0 4px 20px rgba(0,0,0,0.5);
    opacity: 0; transform: translateX(12px);
    transition: opacity 0.2s, transform 0.2s;
  }
  .toast.show { opacity: 1; transform: translateX(0); }
  .toast-info    { border-left: 3px solid #5fa0dc; }
  .toast-success { border-left: 3px solid #7dc87a; }
  .toast-warn    { border-left: 3px solid #f0a04a; }
  .toast-error   { border-left: 3px solid #e05050; }
  .toast-icon { flex-shrink: 0; margin-top: 1px; font-size: 0.82rem; }
  .toast-info .toast-icon    { color: #5fa0dc; }
  .toast-success .toast-icon { color: #7dc87a; }
  .toast-warn .toast-icon    { color: #f0a04a; }
  .toast-error .toast-icon   { color: #e05050; }
  .toast-text { flex: 1; white-space: pre-wrap; word-break: break-word; }
  .toast-close {
    background: transparent; border: none; color: rgba(255,255,255,0.3);
    font-size: 1rem; line-height: 1; cursor: pointer; padding: 0;

  /* ============ TRANSCRIPT PANEL ============ */
  .transcript-panel {
    position: absolute; inset: 0; background: rgba(0,0,0,0.92);

  .transcript-panel.visible { display: block; }
  /* When transcript is open, hide HUD elements that would otherwise float
     on top of the transcript body. text-display keeps updating with the
     current sentence even while transcript is open — letting it overlay
     scrolled transcript text creates a visually confusing double-layer
     of words. Phase-dots/indicator are above the transcript area so they
     stay; session-time and audio-indicator are inside the stage so they
     end up under the transcript naturally. */
  body.in-session:has(.transcript-panel.visible) .text-display { opacity: 0; pointer-events: none; }
  .transcript-panel .t-phase {
    color: var(--accent); font-size: 0.68rem;
    letter-spacing: 0.12em; text-transform: uppercase;
    margin: 16px 0 6px; font-weight: 500;
  }
  /* First phase header shouldn't push the transcript content down — the
     panel already has 20px of top padding. */
  .transcript-panel .t-phase:first-child { margin-top: 0; }
  /* Active-phase highlight. Lets the user open the transcript mid-session
     and immediately see where playback currently is. JS adds .t-active to
     both the header and the body div for the live phase. */
  .transcript-panel .t-phase.t-active {
    color: var(--neon-cyan);
  }
  .transcript-panel .t-phase.t-active::before {
    content: '▸ '; opacity: 0.7;
  }

  @keyframes rec-pulse { 0%,100%{opacity:0.6} 50%{opacity:1} }

  /* ─── buttons: dark with honey-amber trim (outlined). Hover lifts to neon-cyan accent. ─── */
  /* Global button styling — forces a consistent amber-trim outlined look on
     primary action buttons. Buttons that intentionally have their own
     visual language are excluded by class:
       - .icon-btn, .filter-tab, .toggle-btn — existing exclusions
       - [class*="close"]              — close-X buttons keep their compact style
       - [class*="bg-preview"]         — image preview popup buttons (action row + nav)
       - [class*="bg-fs"]              — fullscreen viewer buttons (prev/next/zoom)
       - [class*="bg-gallery-action"]  — inline gallery toolbar action chips
       - .step-chip                    — step requirement chips
       - .phase-reimagine              — Custom-block ✨ rewrite button (cyan accent)
     Without these exclusions the dark-backdrop circular arrows and floating
     overlays in the image flow render as amber-uppercase rectangles. */
  .btn, button, .preview-btn, .filter-tab {
    transition: background 0.12s, color 0.12s, border-color 0.12s, box-shadow 0.12s !important;
  }
  .btn, .preview-btn,
  button:not(.icon-btn):not(.filter-tab):not(.toggle-btn):not([class*="close"]):not([class*="bg-preview"]):not([class*="bg-fs"]):not([class*="bg-gallery-action"]):not(.step-chip):not(.phase-reimagine) {
    background: transparent !important;
    color: var(--accent-warm) !important;
    border: 1.5px solid var(--accent-warm) !important;
    box-shadow: none !important;
    text-transform: uppercase;
    letter-spacing: 0.05em;
    font-weight: 700;
  }
  .btn:hover:not(:disabled), .preview-btn:hover:not(:disabled),
  button:not(.icon-btn):not(.filter-tab):not(.toggle-btn):not([class*="close"]):not([class*="bg-preview"]):not([class*="bg-fs"]):not([class*="bg-gallery-action"]):not(.step-chip):not(.phase-reimagine):hover:not(:disabled) {
    background: rgba(0,212,230,0.06) !important;
    color: var(--neon-cyan) !important;
    border-color: var(--neon-cyan) !important;
    box-shadow: var(--glow-cyan) !important;

    .toggle-row label, .toggle-row .hint {
      font-size: 0.84rem !important;
    }
    .step-content[data-step="5"] .preview-row { grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr; }
    .script-actions, .crafted-actions { grid-template-columns: 1fr; }
  }

  /* ─── scrollbars: flat, thin, match the palette ─── */
  ::-webkit-scrollbar { width: 8px; height: 8px; }
  ::-webkit-scrollbar-track { background: var(--bg); }
  ::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb { background: var(--border); }
  ::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb:hover { background: var(--text-faint); }

  /* ─── bug fixes ─── */
  /* Hint spans inside .config-toggle-grid labels were auto-placing to grid col 1
     row 2 (the 18px checkbox column), causing one-word-per-line wrapping.
     Force them into col 2 so they flow under the title text at full width. */
  .config-toggle-grid .toggle-row label .hint { grid-column: 2; }

  /* Consent modal must render on top of the Options modal that contains its
     triggering checkbox. Both modals share z-index: 100 by default and DOM order
     would otherwise put Options on top. */
  #remoteConsentModal { z-index: 200; }

  /* ─── utility classes — extracted from repeated inline styles ─── */
  /* Compact <input type="number"> used in dense settings panels (Safety
     limits, playback dynamics, etc.). The input element gets a chunky
     1rem font-size from the global iOS-zoom guard at line 2363; this
     class wins specificity (class > element) so the inputs render at
     0.74rem to fit the row layout. */
  .compact-num-input { font-size: 0.74rem !important; padding: 2px 6px; }
  /* Suffix label after a tight input — ×, %, s, min, etc. */
  .unit-suffix { color: var(--text-faint); font-size: 0.66rem; }
  /* Tertiary hint text inside a primary .hint span (parenthetical clarifier). */
  .hint-sub { font-weight: normal; font-size: 0.66rem; }
  /* Right-aligned numeric badge that mirrors the value of a sibling slider.
     The 32px min-width covers both short ("35") and longer ("1.0×") values
     so all rows align consistently. */
  .slider-val { min-width: 32px; text-align: right; }
  /* Glass-bg panel used for Options-modal sub-sections (Safety limits,
     Playback dynamics, Session mode, etc.). */
  .panel-card {
    margin-top: 14px;
    padding: 10px 12px;
    background: var(--glass);
    border: 1px solid var(--glass-border);
  }
  /* Section header for sub-panels — sits in a flex row with action button. */
  .panel-section-title { font-size: 0.82rem; color: var(--text); }
</style>

      <div class="field-group" id="abBar" style="display:none;gap:8px;align-items:center;flex-wrap:wrap;margin-bottom:14px;padding:10px 12px;background:var(--glass);border:1px solid var(--glass-border)">

          <div class="post-checkin-val" id="depthVal">5</div>
          <input type="range" id="depthSlider" min="0" max="10" value="5">
          <div class="post-checkin-ends"><span>surface</span><span>very deep 🌊</span></div>
        </div>
      </div>

      <!-- Journal -->
      <div class="post-journal" style="position:relative">
        <textarea class="journal-area" id="journalText" placeholder="What did you notice? Any sensations, images, thoughts, or shifts?"></textarea>
        <!-- Voice journaling. Hidden until init confirms webkitSpeechRecognition
             is available — graceful degrade for Firefox / iOS Safari. -->
        <button type="button" id="journalMicBtn" title="Speak instead of typing" style="display:none;position:absolute;bottom:8px;right:8px;background:var(--glass);border:1px solid var(--glass-border);color:var(--text-dim);padding:4px 10px;font-size:0.74rem;cursor:pointer;min-height:0">🎤 speak</button>
      </div>

      <div class="script-actions" style="margin-top:8px">
        <button class="btn secondary" id="postBackToScriptBtn">← back to script</button>
        <button class="btn secondary" id="returnToSetupBtn">back to setup</button>
        <button class="btn secondary" id="exportBundleBtn" title="Download a .zip with the script, session data, and any recorded ambience">📦 export bundle</button>
        <button class="btn" id="restartBtn">start another session</button>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>

  <div class="modal" style="max-width:560px">
    <h2>auto-tempo cleanup options</h2>
    <div style="font-size:0.78rem;color:var(--text-dim);line-height:1.55;margin-bottom:12px">
      Choose which characters should be removed from the script when auto-tempo runs. Characters that aren't in this set are left alone. The PAUSE markers (in square brackets, e.g. PAUSE 3) and <code>\{\{tokens\}\}</code> are always preserved regardless.
    </div>
    <label class="toggle-row" for="autoTempoStripOrphan" style="align-items:flex-start;margin-bottom:14px">
      <input type="checkbox" id="autoTempoStripOrphan">
      <span>
        Strip orphan punctuation (recommended)
        <span class="hint" style="line-height:1.5">Removes leftover <code>" . "</code>, <code>" , "</code>, etc. patterns that the narrator otherwise reads aloud as "dot" or "comma". These are usually leftovers from stripped square-bracket annotations like a stage-direction or topic-sentence header that the AI sometimes generates.</span>
      </span>
    </label>
    <div class="pf-label" style="margin-bottom:6px">Character stripping</div>
    <div style="font-size:0.74rem;color:var(--text-faint);line-height:1.5;margin-bottom:8px">
      Click a character below to toggle whether auto-tempo removes it. Characters in the <strong>safe</strong> group can be removed without breaking script structure. Characters in <strong>caution</strong> are used by sentence flow — strip only if your scripts have noisy punctuation.
    </div>
    <div style="font-size:0.7rem;color:var(--text-faint);margin-bottom:4px">Safe to strip (formatting / decorative):</div>
    <div id="autoTempoCharGridSafe" class="auto-tempo-char-grid" style="display:flex;flex-wrap:wrap;gap:4px;margin-bottom:10px"></div>
    <div style="font-size:0.7rem;color:var(--text-faint);margin-bottom:4px">Caution (affects sentence rhythm or syntax):</div>
    <div id="autoTempoCharGridCaution" class="auto-tempo-char-grid" style="display:flex;flex-wrap:wrap;gap:4px;margin-bottom:10px"></div>
    <div class="sublabel" id="autoTempoStripPreview" style="font-size:0.72rem;margin-top:6px;color:var(--text-dim)"></div>
    <div class="modal-actions" style="margin-top:14px">
      <button class="btn secondary" id="autoTempoOptsResetBtn">reset to defaults</button>
      <button class="btn" id="autoTempoOptsCloseBtn">done</button>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>

<script>
  // ====================================================================
  // IDB STORAGE (ported from Adaptive Session Studio — MIT)
  // Replaces localStorage for reliable persistence
  // ====================================================================
  const IDB_NAME = 'stillness-db';
  const IDB_VER  = 1;
  const IDB_STORE = 'kv';
  let _idb = null;
  let _idbOpenP = null;

  function _openIdb() {
    if (_idb) return Promise.resolve(_idb);
    if (_idbOpenP) return _idbOpenP;
    _idbOpenP = new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
      const req = indexedDB.open(IDB_NAME, IDB_VER);
      req.onupgradeneeded = e => {
        const db = e.target.result;
        if (!db.objectStoreNames.contains(IDB_STORE)) db.createObjectStore(IDB_STORE);
      };
      req.onsuccess = e => {
        _idb = e.target.result;
        _idb.onclose = () => { _idb = null; _idbOpenP = null; };
        resolve(_idb);
      };
      req.onerror = e => { _idbOpenP = null; reject(e.target.error ?? new Error('IDB open failed')); };
    });
    return _idbOpenP;
  }
  async function idbGet(key) {
    try {
      const db = await _openIdb();
      return await new Promise((res, rej) => {
        const r = db.transaction(IDB_STORE, 'readonly').objectStore(IDB_STORE).get(key);
        r.onsuccess = () => res(r.result ?? null);
        r.onerror = e => rej(e.target.error);
      });
    } catch { return null; }
  }
  async function idbSet(key, value) {
    try {
      const db = await _openIdb();
      await new Promise((res, rej) => {
        const tx = db.transaction(IDB_STORE, 'readwrite');
        tx.objectStore(IDB_STORE).put(value, key);
        tx.oncomplete = res;
        tx.onerror = e => rej(e.target.error);
      });
    } catch {}
  }
  async function idbDel(key) {
    try {
      const db = await _openIdb();
      await new Promise((res, rej) => {
        const tx = db.transaction(IDB_STORE, 'readwrite');
        tx.objectStore(IDB_STORE).delete(key);
        tx.oncomplete = res;
        tx.onerror = e => rej(e.target.error);
      });
    } catch {}
  }

  // ====================================================================
  // TOAST NOTIFICATIONS (ported from Adaptive Session Studio — MIT)
  // Non-blocking replacements for alert()
  // ====================================================================
  const TOAST_ICONS    = { info: 'ℹ', success: '✓', warn: '⚠', error: '✕' };
  const TOAST_DURATION = { info: 3500, success: 3000, warn: 5000, error: 0 };

  function toast(type, message, ms) {
    const container = $('toastContainer');
    if (!container) return;
    const duration = ms !== undefined ? ms : TOAST_DURATION[type] || 3500;
    const el = document.createElement('div');
    el.className = `toast toast-${type}`;
    el.innerHTML = `<span class="toast-icon">${TOAST_ICONS[type] || 'ℹ'}</span><span class="toast-text">${escapeHtml(message)}</span><button class="toast-close">×</button>`;
    el.querySelector('.toast-close').addEventListener('click', () => dismissToast(el));
    if (duration === 0) el.addEventListener('click', () => dismissToast(el));
    container.appendChild(el);
    requestAnimationFrame(() => el.classList.add('show'));
    let timer = null;
    if (duration > 0) timer = setTimeout(() => dismissToast(el), duration);
    function dismissToast(t) { clearTimeout(timer); t.classList.remove('show'); setTimeout(() => t.remove(), 220); }
    return el;
  }
  // Toasts are suppressed during session playback for everything below the
  // critical/danger tier. Rationale: live-panel sliders frequently hit the
  // safety clamps (gateSpeechRate / gateSoundscapeVolume) and would otherwise
  // spam warnings mid-induction — distracting at best, ride-killing at worst.
  // Errors still surface (those are the genuinely actionable kind).
  // Toggle: set `state._allowToastsInSession = true` to bypass for debugging.
  function _isInSessionPlayback() {
    try { return document.body.classList.contains('in-session') && !state._allowToastsInSession; }
    catch { return false; }
  }
  const notify = {
    info:    (msg, ms) => { if (_isInSessionPlayback()) return null; return toast('info', msg, ms); },
    success: (msg, ms) => { if (_isInSessionPlayback()) return null; return toast('success', msg, ms); },
    warn:    (msg, ms) => { if (_isInSessionPlayback()) return null; return toast('warn', msg, ms); },
    // Error/critical toasts ALWAYS surface — they're the only severity worth
    // interrupting a session for (audio failure, AI fetch failure, etc.).
    error:   (msg, ms) => toast('error', msg, ms),
  };

  function confirmAsync(message, opts = {}) {
    return new Promise(resolve => {
      const backdrop = document.createElement('div');
      backdrop.className = 'modal-backdrop visible';
      backdrop.innerHTML = `<div class="modal" style="max-width:420px">
        <div style="font-size:0.88rem;line-height:1.55;color:var(--text-dim);white-space:pre-line">${escapeHtml(message)}</div>
        <div class="modal-actions" style="margin-top:16px">
          <button class="btn secondary" id="_confirmNo">${opts.cancelText || 'cancel'}</button>
          <button class="btn" id="_confirmYes">${opts.okText || 'confirm'}</button>
        </div>
      </div>`;
      document.body.appendChild(backdrop);
      const cleanup = (val) => { backdrop.remove(); resolve(val); };
      backdrop.querySelector('#_confirmYes').addEventListener('click', () => cleanup(true));
      backdrop.querySelector('#_confirmNo').addEventListener('click', () => cleanup(false));
      backdrop.addEventListener('click', e => { if (e.target === backdrop) cleanup(false); });
    });
  }

  function alertModal(title, message) {
    return new Promise(resolve => {
      const backdrop = document.createElement('div');
      backdrop.className = 'modal-backdrop visible';
      backdrop.innerHTML = `<div class="modal" style="max-width:420px">
        ${title ? `<h2 style="margin:0 0 12px">${escapeHtml(title)}</h2>` : ''}
        <div style="font-size:0.84rem;line-height:1.55;color:var(--text-dim);white-space:pre-line">${escapeHtml(message)}</div>
        <div class="modal-actions" style="margin-top:16px">
          <button class="btn" id="_alertOk">ok</button>
        </div>
      </div>`;
      document.body.appendChild(backdrop);
      const cleanup = () => { backdrop.remove(); resolve(); };
      backdrop.querySelector('#_alertOk').addEventListener('click', cleanup);
      backdrop.addEventListener('click', e => { if (e.target === backdrop) cleanup(); });
    });
  }

  /** Modal text-input prompt. Returns the entered string trimmed, or null
   *  if cancelled. Enter submits; Escape cancels. opts: title, placeholder,
   *  initial, okText, cancelText, maxLength. */
  function promptAsync(message, opts = {}) {
    return new Promise(resolve => {
      const backdrop = document.createElement('div');
      backdrop.className = 'modal-backdrop visible';
      const ml = Number.isFinite(opts.maxLength) ? opts.maxLength : 80;
      backdrop.innerHTML = `<div class="modal" style="max-width:440px">
        ${opts.title ? `<h2 style="margin:0 0 10px">${escapeHtml(opts.title)}</h2>` : ''}
        <div style="font-size:0.86rem;line-height:1.55;color:var(--text-dim);white-space:pre-line;margin-bottom:10px">${escapeHtml(message)}</div>
        <input type="text" id="_promptInput" maxlength="${ml}" placeholder="${escapeHtml(opts.placeholder || '')}" value="${escapeHtml(opts.initial || '')}" style="width:100%;padding:10px;background:var(--bg-deep);border:1px solid var(--glass-border);color:var(--text);font-size:0.95rem">
        <div class="modal-actions" style="margin-top:16px">
          <button class="btn secondary" id="_promptCancel">${escapeHtml(opts.cancelText || 'cancel')}</button>
          <button class="btn" id="_promptOk">${escapeHtml(opts.okText || 'ok')}</button>
        </div>
      </div>`;
      document.body.appendChild(backdrop);
      const input = backdrop.querySelector('#_promptInput');
      const cleanup = (val) => { backdrop.remove(); resolve(val); };
      const submit = () => {
        const v = (input.value || '').trim();
        cleanup(v || null);
      };
      backdrop.querySelector('#_promptOk').addEventListener('click', submit);
      backdrop.querySelector('#_promptCancel').addEventListener('click', () => cleanup(null));
      backdrop.addEventListener('click', e => { if (e.target === backdrop) cleanup(null); });
      input.addEventListener('keydown', e => {
        if (e.key === 'Enter')  { e.preventDefault(); submit(); }
        if (e.key === 'Escape') { e.preventDefault(); cleanup(null); }
      });
      // Focus the input on next tick (after the backdrop's transition) so
      // the user can start typing immediately.
      setTimeout(() => input.focus(), 0);
    });
  }

  // ====================================================================
  // iOS Safari viewport fix (from Perchance skill reference)
  // ====================================================================
  try {
    const isTouchScreen = window.matchMedia("(pointer: coarse)").matches;
    const isSafari = navigator.vendor?.indexOf('Apple') > -1
      && !navigator.userAgent.includes('CriOS')
      && !navigator.userAgent.includes('FxiOS');
    if (isSafari && window.innerWidth < 800 && isTouchScreen) {
      const viewportMeta = document.querySelector("[name=viewport]");
      if (viewportMeta && !viewportMeta.getAttribute("content").includes("maximum-scale")) {
        viewportMeta.setAttribute("content",
          viewportMeta.getAttribute("content") + ", maximum-scale=1");
      }
    }
  } catch(e) { console.error(e); }

  const PACING_RULES = {
    monotony: `3-6 word phrases. Repeat a small set of core phrases. No storytelling. \x5BPAUSE 4-8\x5D between phrases. Boring is the mechanism.`,
    structured: `Follow method structure exactly. \x5BPAUSE 8\x5D after each instruction. Longer phrases (8-15 words) for clarity.`,
    narrative: `Vivid sensory imagery. Phrases 8-15 words. \x5BPAUSE 3-5\x5D between sentences.`,
    focused: `All phrases reference the focal sensation. Build slowly. \x5BPAUSE 4-6\x5D. For demonstration methods: direct suggestions ("your hand IS", "you CANNOT") once state is established.`,
    confusion: `Long sentences with embedded clauses, paradoxes. Occasional brief clarity. \x5BPAUSE 3-6\x5D.`,
    fractionation: `Match cycle pattern. Mark transitions: "And opening!" / "And closing again..." Each induction deeper.`,
    'eye-roll': `Precise Spiegel sequence. \x5BPAUSE n\x5D for each step. After eye closure: breath/floating language.`,
    breath: `Voice IS metronome. Count clearly. Real timing 4 in, 7 hold, 8 out.`,
    'body-scan': `Head-to-toe order. Name, notice, release each part. Phrases 5-10 words. \x5BPAUSE 4-6\x5D.`,
    permissive: `"Perhaps", "you might notice", "in your own time". Phrases 5-12 words. \x5BPAUSE 2-6\x5D.`,
    rapid: `Short, commanding phrases. 2-5 words. No hesitation. \x5BPAUSE 1-2\x5D between commands. Speed IS the mechanism. Build speed, then sudden "SLEEP" / "DROP" / "NOW" followed by \x5BPAUSE 6-10\x5D silence.`,
    elman: `Step-by-step clinical protocol. Test each step before proceeding. "Close your eyes. Now relax them so completely that they just won't work. Test them. Good." \x5BPAUSE 3-5\x5D between steps. Confident, professional, never rushing but never lingering.`,
  };

  const INTENTION_TYPES = {
    // ── Foundation ─────────────────────────────────────────────

    trigger: { label: 'Post-hypnotic trigger', hint: '"When X, then Y". Trigger must actually occur in daily life.', guidance: `EXPLICIT trigger-response link. Trigger specific and occurrable.     behavior: { label: 'Behavior change', hint: 'Specify what to DO, not what to stop.', guidance: `Reformulate POSITIVELY. What TO do. Replacement if removing. "You'll find yourself naturally..."` },

    anchor: { label: 'Install an anchor', hint: 'Pair a physical cue (touch, gesture) with a state.', guidance: `ANCHOR: specific physical cue paired with specific state. Cue unusual enough to not trigger accidentally. "Each time you touch [anchor], you'll access [state]..."` },

    // ── Body ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
    pain: { label: 'Pain management / comfort', hint: 'Name the specific sensation and its location. Not a substitute for medical care.', guidance: `PAIN MANAGEMENT: NEVER deny the sensation exists. Instead, transform relationship to it. Dial metaphor (turn it down). Reinterpret (pressure instead of pain, warmth instead of burning). Dissociate (the sensation belongs to the hand on the table, not to you). Glove anesthesia spread. Always include: "This works alongside your medical care, not instead of it."` },
    sleep: { label: 'Sleep induction (stay asleep)', hint: 'Fall asleep during the session and don\'t wake up until morning.', guidance: `SLEEP INDUCTION: no wake or reintroduction. Final phase drifts into silence and sleep. The WAKE and REINTRODUCTION phases should be EMPTY or omitted for sleep sessions.` },
    energy: { label: 'Energy / vitality boost', hint: 'Physical energy, alertness, motivation. Where do you feel depleted?', guidance: `ENERGY: opposite of relaxation — progressive ACTIVATION.     // ── Demonstration ─────────────────────────────────────────
    convincer: { label: 'Convincer demonstration', hint: 'Prove hypnosis works to yourself with a demonstrable effect.', guidance: `CONVINCER PROTOCOL. UNDENIABLE physical/cognitive effect. Use DIRECT suggestions in present tense ("your hand IS heavier", "fingers ARE drawing together"). Build a clear challenge to test AFTER wake.` },
    novelty: { label: 'Novelty effect / party trick', hint: 'Classic stage effect — laughter trigger, name blank, etc.', guidance: `NOVELTY EFFECT. Post-hypnotic suggestion producing a fun, demonstrable effect on cue. Testable. Direct suggestions with specific triggers. Include a test.` },

    // ── Advanced ───────────────────────────────────────────────
    time: { label: 'Time distortion', hint: 'Make time stretch or compress. Specify which direction.', guidance: `TIME DISTORTION: "Each minute of clock time contains ten minutes of inner experience" OR "an hour of work will pass in what feels like five minutes." Use nested framing: "You've been here for... how long? It feels like moments. Or has it been hours? The answer no longer matters." Do NOT reference actual clock time during the script.` },
    amnesia: { label: 'Suggested amnesia', hint: 'Forget a specific thing temporarily. What should be forgotten?', guidance: `AMNESIA SUGGESTION: "The number 7 simply doesn't exist for you right now" or "you'll remember this session only when [trigger]." Frame as the information becoming temporarily unavailable — not erased. The FORGETTING is effortless. "Trying to remember will be like reaching for something that was never there." Include a clear CANCEL condition: "This will last until [time/trigger], then the memory returns completely."` },
    identity: { label: 'Persona / identity play', hint: 'Temporarily adopt a different perspective, persona, or way of being.', guidance: `IDENTITY EXPLORATION: NOT dissociation — more like method acting. "For the next 20 minutes, you are [persona]. You walk like them, think like them, feel what they feel." Build the character gradually: posture first, then breathing, then internal monologue. Always include a clean EXIT: "And stepping out of that role now, completely yourself again, but carrying with you anything useful you discovered."` },
    surrender: { label: 'Surrender / ego dissolution', hint: 'Let go of control entirely. Deep trust required.', guidance: `SURRENDER: progressive release of control, agency, decision-making. "You don't need to try. You don't need to understand. You don't even need to listen — your unconscious is doing that for you." Each layer of control released takes them deeper. The paradox: the less they do, the more happens. Frame as SAFETY, not loss: "There is nothing to hold onto, and nothing to fall from." Emergence must be thorough and grounding.` },
    conditioning: { label: 'Behavioral conditioning', hint: 'Pair a specific stimulus with a specific automatic response, over repetition.', guidance: `CONDITIONING PROTOCOL: this is classical conditioning via suggestion. Define STIMULUS clearly. Define RESPONSE clearly. Repeat the pairing MINIMUM five times in WORK phase, each time more vivid. "Every time you [stimulus], instantly and automatically you [response]." Progressive intensification. Future-pace: "Tomorrow, when [stimulus] happens, you'll notice [response] is already there, already happening." Include a test.` },
  };

  // Post-session reflection prompts — shown on the splash after completion to
  // give the user a contextual, intention-aware suggestion for what to notice
  // / do next. One short sentence; complementary to the trigger reminder, not
  // duplicative. Keys match INTENTION_TYPES; FALLBACK is used for any missing.
  const POST_SESSION_REFLECTIONS = {
    emotional:    'Notice if the state lingers. Returning to it is easier each time.',
    trigger:      'When the trigger occurs in daily life, the response should arise on its own. Notice the first time it happens.',
    behavior:     'The next opportunity to act this way may come sooner than you expect. Trust the new pattern.',
    release:      'Notice what feels lighter. Whatever you released doesn\'t need to come back.',
    perception:   'The shift may take a few days to fully settle. Catch yourself thinking differently.',
    anchor:       'Use the anchor today, even casually. Each use deepens the connection.',
    trance:       'Whatever arose — sensations, images, drift — was the practice. There was nothing to do.',
    performance:  'The state is now wired to the activity. Your first attempt with the new state will surprise you.',
    focus:        'Try the focus state on a small task today. The architecture is in place.',
    creativity:   'Let the next idea arrive without judging it. The critic stays quiet a little longer than usual.',
    pain:         'The relationship to the sensation is what shifted. Check in with the body gently throughout the day.',
    sleep:        'If you woke up here, the session worked. Otherwise you slept through — also good.',
    energy:       'The activation should carry into the next 1-2 hours. Hydrate, move, then check the energy again.',
    convincer:    'Test the effect now while it\'s fresh. The proof matters more than the method.',
    novelty:      'Let the trigger fire naturally. Don\'t force it.',
    time:         'Notice the next time hours feel like minutes — or vice versa.',
    amnesia:      'Try to recall what was forgotten. The reach for it is the demonstration.',
    identity:     'You can step back into that perspective whenever it serves. The exit is always clean.',
    surrender:    'Notice what you took back, and what you left behind. Both choices are yours.',
    conditioning: 'Repetition is the mechanism. Each pairing in daily life strengthens the bond.',
  };
  const POST_SESSION_REFLECTION_FALLBACK = 'Take what was useful. Let the rest settle on its own.';

    _webcam: {
      enabled:               false,    // user opt-in for THIS session (resets on finishSession)
      status:                'idle',   // 'idle' | 'requesting' | 'loading' | 'active' | 'error'
      stream:                null,     // MediaStream (held only while active)
      videoEl:               null,     // hidden <video> element
      faceDetected:          false,
      lastFaceAt:            0,        // wallclock ms of last face detection
      rawEAR:                null,     // 0..~1, eye openness (avg both eyes)
      motion:                0,        // 0..1, frame-to-frame change (deferred — Round 4+)
      _brightnessCanvas:     null,     // canvas reused for luminance sampling
      _brightnessCtx:        null,
      inferTimerHandle:      null,     // setInterval handle for inference loop
      brightnessTimerHandle: null,     // setInterval handle for brightness loop
      errorMessage:          null,
    },
    ambientBrightness: 0.5,            // 0..1, EMA-smoothed room luminance
    ambientDimScale:   1.0,            // multiplier for visualizer intensity (0.6..1.2)
    // Safety layer — consolidated runtime guards. User-customizable via the
    // safety panel; persisted in _buildSettingsObj. See SAFETY_DEFAULTS below.
    safetyConfig: {
      maxSpeechRate:        1.5,   // cap TTS rate; rules-engine setSpeechRate respects this
      minSpeechRate:        0.6,   // floor TTS rate; very-slow rate can cause drowsiness
      maxSoundscapeVolume:  0.85,  // cap drone vol; prevents rules from drowning narrator
      emergencyCooldownSec: 30,    // wait this long after emergency before next session
      maxSessionMinutes:    60,    // warn at session start if estimated runtime > this
    },
    extAiProvider: 'perchance',
    _extAiKey: null,
    // Per-provider key cache so users can keep both Anthropic + OpenAI keys
    // populated without re-pasting on provider switch. Session-only (underscore
    // prefix, never persisted via _buildSettingsObj). Migrated from _extAiKey
    // on first switch — see provider-change handler.
    _extAiKeysByProvider: { anthropic: null, openai: null },
    // Debug flags — session-only, NEVER persisted. See _buildSettingsObj — these are
    // deliberately excluded. Mirrored from checkboxes by _syncDebugState().
    _debug: {
      skipRescue: false,
      skipCooldown: false,
      skipSafetyGate: false,
      skipMethodGating: false,
      verbose: false,
    },
  };

  /** Sync state._debug from checkbox DOM. Called on load and on the debug toggle.
   *  Single source of truth: one `debugMode` checkbox enables every debug flag. */
  function _syncDebugState() {
    const d = state._debug;
    const on = !!document.getElementById('debugMode')?.checked;
    d.skipRescue       = on;
    d.skipCooldown     = on;
    d.skipSafetyGate   = on;
    d.skipMethodGating = on;
    d.verbose          = on;
    _renderDebugBanner();
  }

  /** Show/hide the floating "DEBUG" indicator whenever any debug flag is active. */
  function _renderDebugBanner() {
    const anyOn = Object.values(state._debug).some(Boolean);
    let el = document.getElementById('_debugBanner');
    if (anyOn && !el) {
      el = document.createElement('div');
      el.id = '_debugBanner';
      el.textContent = '\u26A0 DEBUG';
      el.style.cssText = 'position:fixed;top:8px;right:8px;z-index:99999;background:rgba(220,60,60,0.92);color:#fff;font-size:0.62rem;font-weight:600;letter-spacing:0.08em;padding:4px 8px;border-radius:4px;pointer-events:none;box-shadow:0 2px 6px rgba(0,0,0,0.3);';
      document.body.appendChild(el);
    } else if (!anyOn && el) {
      el.remove();
    }
  }

  /** Verbose Smart Director logger. No-op unless state._debug.verbose is on. */
  function sdLog(...args) {
    if (state._debug?.verbose) console.log('[SmartDirector]', ...args);
  }

  // Console-visible diagnostic shim. Read-only by design — surfaces the live
  // AI tier and a few derived signals so users can sanity-check the pipeline
  // from DevTools without ever exposing keys or mutation paths.
  // Usage: HNE.tier() / HNE.profile() / HNE.adaptive() / HNE.history(5)
  try {
    Object.defineProperty(window, 'HNE', {
      value: Object.freeze({
        tier: () => (typeof getAITier === 'function' ? getAITier() : 'perchance'),
        profile: () => (typeof getProfile === 'function' ? { ...getProfile(), changeLog: undefined } : null),
        adaptive: async () => { try { return await getAdaptive(); } catch { return null; } },
        history: async (n = 5) => { try { return ((await idbGet('stillness_history_v1')) || []).slice(0, n).map(r => ({ id: r.id, date: new Date(r.date).toISOString(), persona: r.guide, method: r.method, mood: `${r.preMood ?? '?'}→${r.postMood ?? '?'}`, completed: r.completed })); } catch { return []; } },
        smartDirector: () => !!state.smartDirectorEnabled,
        bias: () => state.directorBias ?? 0,
        // AI audit log — read-only summary of recent calls. Provider, source,
        // byte size, timestamp. Never the prompt content (privacy by design).
        audit: (n = 20) => (_aiAuditLog || []).slice(0, n).map(e => ({
          when: new Date(e.ts).toISOString(),
          provider: e.provider,
          source: e.source,
          bytes: e.bytes,
        })),
      }),
      writable: false, configurable: false,
    });
  } catch {} // already defined or non-extensible — fine

  const PHASE_DURATIONS = {
    micro:    { settling: 15,  induction: 30,  deepener: 15,  level: 10,  work: 60,   lightener: 15,  wake: 12, reintroduction: 20 },
    quick:    { settling: 30,  induction: 90,  deepener: 30,  level: 20,  work: 120,  lightener: 30,  wake: 20, reintroduction: 30 },
    short:    { settling: 60,  induction: 180, deepener: 60,  level: 40,  work: 240,  lightener: 45,  wake: 30, reintroduction: 45 },
    medium:   { settling: 90,  induction: 300, deepener: 180, level: 60,  work: 360,  lightener: 60,  wake: 40, reintroduction: 60 },
    long:     { settling: 120, induction: 420, deepener: 300, level: 90,  work: 540,  lightener: 75,  wake: 50, reintroduction: 75 },
    // Phase 6 length-picker expansion. Each step adds ~1 Work + ~1 Affirm
    // pair; marathon also doubles the deepener/lightener depth (handled in
    // getSessionPhaseBlueprint), so its `deepener` total is split across two phases.
    // The `level` value is the budget for EACH level-block when it appears
    // (only marathon auto-places one; other lengths only use it if the user
    // manually adds a second deepener via the script editor).
    extended: { settling: 150, induction: 480, deepener: 360, level: 100, work: 720,  lightener: 90,  wake: 60, reintroduction: 90 },
    deep:     { settling: 180, induction: 540, deepener: 420, level: 120, work: 900,  lightener: 105, wake: 70, reintroduction: 105 },
    marathon: { settling: 180, induction: 600, deepener: 540, level: 180, work: 1080, lightener: 120, wake: 80, reintroduction: 120 },
  };

  const _FALLBACK_PERSONA = { id: 'fallback', name: 'Guide', tagline: '', category: 'contemplative', systemPrompt: '' };
  const _FALLBACK_METHOD = METHODS[0];
  const getMethod = () => METHODS.find(m => m.id === state.selectedMethodId) || _FALLBACK_METHOD;
  const getPersona = () => (state.personas.find(p => p.id === state.selectedPersonaId) || state.personas[0]) ?? _FALLBACK_PERSONA;
  const PERSONA_KEY = 'stillness_personas_v1';

  // ── Persona schema, v2 (Perchance Character-aligned) ──────────────────────
  // The historical HNE shape was { id, name, tagline, category, systemPrompt }.
  // The Perchance Character spec (see perchance-api skill §4.1) is richer:
  // { id, uuid, name, roleInstruction, reminderMessage, imagePromptPrefix,
  //   imagePromptSuffix, metaTitle, metaDescription, metaImage, customData,
  //   creationTime, ... }. v2 is additive — back-compat preserved by writing
  // BOTH `roleInstruction` and `systemPrompt` on save and reading either on
  // load. Builtin personas don't need rewriting; normalizePersona() backfills
  // missing fields at runtime.
  const PERSONA_SCHEMA_VERSION = 2;

  /** Read a persona's role/system prompt with back-compat across schema versions.
   *  Reads `roleInstruction` first (Perchance-canonical) then falls back to the
   *  legacy `systemPrompt` field so existing data and shared personas keep working. */
  function getPersonaRole(persona) {
    if (!persona) return '';
    // Self persona gets a freshly-composed role each time, so changes to
    // profile / memory / modality reflect immediately.
    if (persona.isSelf || persona.id === SELF_PERSONA_ID) {
      return _buildSelfRoleInstruction();
    }
    return persona.roleInstruction || persona.systemPrompt || '';
  }

  /** Lazily upgrade a persona to v2 schema — backfills missing fields with
   *  sensible defaults. Called at load time (refreshPersonas) and at save
   *  time (savePersonaFromForm). Idempotent — running it twice is a no-op.
   *  Returns a NEW object; never mutates input. */
  function normalizePersona(p) {
    if (!p || typeof p !== 'object') return p;
    const role = p.roleInstruction || p.systemPrompt || '';
    return {
      // ── Core (kept) ─────────────────────────
      id:       p.id,
      name:     p.name,
      tagline:  p.tagline || '',
      category: p.category || 'contemplative',
      // ── Role text — write BOTH for back-compat when shared between versions ─
      roleInstruction: role,
      systemPrompt:    role, // alias maintained for older code paths + share recipients on older versions
      // ── New v2 fields (Perchance-aligned) ─────────────────────────
      uuid:                  p.uuid || _genPersonaUuid(),
      reminderMessage:       p.reminderMessage || '',
      imagePromptPrefix:     p.imagePromptPrefix || '',
      imagePromptSuffix:     p.imagePromptSuffix || '',
      metaTitle:             p.metaTitle || '',
      metaDescription:       p.metaDescription || '',
      metaImage:             p.metaImage || '',
      creationTime:          p.creationTime || Date.now(),
      customData:            (p.customData && typeof p.customData === 'object') ? p.customData : {},
      schemaVersion:         PERSONA_SCHEMA_VERSION,
      // ── HNE-specific recommendations (optional; null = no recommendation) ──
      recommendedSpeechRate: Number.isFinite(p.recommendedSpeechRate) ? p.recommendedSpeechRate : null,
      recommendedVisual:     p.recommendedVisual     || null,
      recommendedSoundscape: p.recommendedSoundscape || null,
      // ── Flags preserved ─────────────────────────
      ...(p.isCustom    && { isCustom:    true }),
      ...(p.isCommunity && { isCommunity: true }),
      ...(p.packName    && { packName:    p.packName }),
      // `hidden` marks a persona as unlock-gated (see HIDDEN_PERSONA_UNLOCKS).
      // Preserved here because normalizePersona drops unknown fields by design;
      // without this line the flag would be silently lost on every refresh.
      ...(p.hidden      && { hidden:      true }),
    };
  }

  /** Generate a per-persona UUID (used for share-link dedup). Cheap, no deps. */
  function _genPersonaUuid() {
    // crypto.randomUUID is widely supported but fall back to a manual generator
    // for older browsers / non-secure contexts.
    if (typeof crypto !== 'undefined' && crypto.randomUUID) {
      try { return crypto.randomUUID(); } catch {}
    }
    return 'p-' + Date.now().toString(36) + '-' + Math.random().toString(36).slice(2, 10);
  }

  // IDB-backed storage with localStorage migration
  let _personaCache = null;

  async function _initStorage() {
    // Migrate personas from localStorage → IDB
    _personaCache = await idbGet(PERSONA_KEY);
    if (!_personaCache) {
      try {
        const lsRaw = localStorage.getItem(PERSONA_KEY);
        if (lsRaw) { _personaCache = JSON.parse(lsRaw); await idbSet(PERSONA_KEY, _personaCache); localStorage.removeItem(PERSONA_KEY); }
      } catch {}
    }
    if (!Array.isArray(_personaCache)) _personaCache = [];

    // Normalize legacy history/settings/analytics stores
    try {
      const legacyHistory = (await idbGet('stillness_history_v1')) || [];
      if (Array.isArray(legacyHistory) && legacyHistory.length) {
        const normalizedHistory = legacyHistory.map(normalizeHistoryEntry).sort((a,b)=>(b.date||0)-(a.date||0));
        await idbSet('stillness_history_v1', normalizedHistory);
      }
    } catch {}
    try {
      const analytics = await idbGet(ANALYTICS_KEY) || await idbGet('stillness_analytics_v2');
      if (analytics) await idbSet(ANALYTICS_KEY, normalizeAnalyticsData(analytics));
    } catch {}
    try {
      const raw = localStorage.getItem('stillness_settings_v4') || localStorage.getItem('stillness_settings_v3') || localStorage.getItem('stillness_settings_v2');
      if (raw && !localStorage.getItem(SETTINGS_KEY)) {
        localStorage.setItem(SETTINGS_KEY, JSON.stringify(normalizeSettings(JSON.parse(raw))));
      }
    } catch {}

    // Persist browser storage so IDB isn't cleared
    if (navigator.storage?.persist) await navigator.storage.persist();
  }

  const loadCustomPersonas = () => _personaCache || [];
  const saveCustomPersonas = arr => { _personaCache = arr; idbSet(PERSONA_KEY, arr); return true; };

  // ====================================================================
  // AUDIO-REACTIVE VISUALIZER — mode/gain mapping + setter
  // ====================================================================
  // The user-facing toggle is 3-state: off / subtle / dramatic. Each maps
  // to a gain multiplier applied at visualizer read-time (not write-time)
  // so flipping modes takes effect on the next frame with no engine restart.
  // ====================================================================
  const GAIN_FOR_MODE = { off: 0, subtle: 0.3, dramatic: 1.0 };
  function setAudioReactiveMode(mode) {
    if (!(mode in GAIN_FOR_MODE)) mode = 'off';
    state.audioReactiveMode = mode;
    state.audioReactive.gain = GAIN_FOR_MODE[mode];
    // Live-panel + Step 5 segmented controls re-render
    document.querySelectorAll('[data-audio-mode]').forEach(btn => {
      btn.classList.toggle('active', btn.dataset.audioMode === mode);
    });
  }

  // ====================================================================
  // AUDIO UPDATE LOOP — single RAF producing state.audioReactive
  // ====================================================================
  // Polls the soundscape AnalyserNode + ticks the voice envelope. Started
  // by startAudioReactiveLoop() when entering a session; stopped on
  // session end. Cheap — ~0.3ms per frame.
  // ====================================================================

  // Bin ranges at fftSize 256, 48kHz sample rate (typical):
  //   bins 0..5    → ~0–1050Hz (bass)
  //   bins 6..40   → ~1050–7500Hz (mid)
  //   bins 41..100 → ~7500–18750Hz (treble)
  const BASS_BINS   = [0, 5];
  const MID_BINS    = [6, 40];
  const TREBLE_BINS = [41, 100];

  function _avgBins(buf, lo, hi) {
    let sum = 0, count = 0;
    const end = Math.min(hi, buf.length - 1);
    for (let i = lo; i <= end; i++) { sum += buf[i]; count++; }
    return count > 0 ? sum / (count * 255) : 0;  // normalize 0..1
  }
  function _rmsBins(buf) {
    let sum = 0;
    for (let i = 0; i < buf.length; i++) sum += buf[i] * buf[i];
    return Math.sqrt(sum / buf.length) / 255;
  }

  function updateAudioReactiveFrame() {
    const a = state.audioReactive;
    // `drone` is the main SoundscapeEngine instance defined later in this IIFE.
    // We reference it lazily via the enclosing scope — it's non-null once a
    // session has started and the soundscape has begun.
    const haveSoundscape = (typeof drone !== 'undefined') && drone && drone.analyser && a._fftBuffer;
    const now = performance.now();

    // FFT signals (soundscape)
    if (haveSoundscape) {
      drone.analyser.getByteFrequencyData(a._fftBuffer);
      a.bass    = _avgBins(a._fftBuffer, BASS_BINS[0],   BASS_BINS[1]);
      a.mid     = _avgBins(a._fftBuffer, MID_BINS[0],    MID_BINS[1]);
      a.treble  = _avgBins(a._fftBuffer, TREBLE_BINS[0], TREBLE_BINS[1]);
      a.overall = _rmsBins(a._fftBuffer);
    } else {
      a.bass = a.mid = a.treble = a.overall = 0;
    }

    // Voice pulse envelope (synthesized from speechSynthesis boundary events).
    //   voicePulse   — instant spike that decays linearly over 300ms
    //   voiceEnvelope — 1-pole low-pass of voicePulse (smoother, for visuals)
    const dt = (now - a._voicePulseSpawnedAt) / 300;
    a.voicePulse = Math.max(0, 1 - dt);
    a.voiceEnvelope = a.voiceEnvelope * 0.85 + a.voicePulse * 0.15;

    // active = any signal source producing meaningful data
    a.active = haveSoundscape || a.voiceEnvelope > 0.02;
  }

  function startAudioReactiveLoop() {
    const a = state.audioReactive;
    if (a._rafHandle) return; // already running
    let _lastTickMs = performance.now();
    const tick = () => {
      if (!a._rafHandle) return; // stopped
      updateAudioReactiveFrame();
      tickStateEngine(); // state-engine reads from audioReactive; needs to run after update
      // Rules engine reads engineState and fires actions when conditions hold.
      // Runs only when opt-in flag is true; default off to preserve current behavior.
      const nowMs = performance.now();
      const frameSec = Math.min(0.1, (nowMs - _lastTickMs) / 1000); // cap at 100ms (e.g. tab backgrounding)
      _lastTickMs = nowMs;
      if (state.runtimeRulesEnabled) {
        tickRulesEngine(frameSec);
        tickTriggerWindows(); // trigger windows use same opt-in flag as rules
      }
      // Playback dynamics — independent opt-in flag (state.playbackDynamics.enabled).
      // No-op when disabled, so safe to call unconditionally.
      tickPlaybackDynamics(frameSec);
      a._rafHandle = requestAnimationFrame(tick);
    };
    a._rafHandle = requestAnimationFrame(tick);
  }

  function stopAudioReactiveLoop() {
    const a = state.audioReactive;
    if (a._rafHandle) { cancelAnimationFrame(a._rafHandle); a._rafHandle = null; }
    a.bass = a.mid = a.treble = a.overall = 0;
    a.voicePulse = 0; a.voiceEnvelope = 0; a.active = false;
  }

  /** Per-frame modulation values for visualizers. Returns all-zeros when
   *  inactive or mode=off, so retrofitted visualizers are zero-regression.
   *  Callers should keep their existing autonomous motion and treat these
   *  as ADDITIVE modulators, not replacements. */
  function getAudioMod() {
    const a = state.audioReactive;
    if (!a || !a.active || a.gain === 0) {
      return { overall: 0, bass: 0, mid: 0, treble: 0, pulse: 0, envelope: 0 };
    }
    // Apply ambient-brightness dim scale — when the room is dark, viz amplitude
    // drops to 0.6×; when bright, climbs to 1.2×. Default 1.0 when webcam is off.
    const dim = state.ambientDimScale ?? 1.0;
    const g = a.gain * dim;
    return {
      overall:  a.overall       * g,
      bass:     a.bass          * g,
      mid:      a.mid           * g,
      treble:   a.treble        * g,
      pulse:    a.voicePulse    * g,
      envelope: a.voiceEnvelope * g,
    };
  }

  // ====================================================================
  // STATE ENGINE — canonical runtime metric bundle
  // ====================================================================
  // Normalizes playback signals into a single object (state.engineState)
  // that rules-engine and trigger-windows read without caring about the
  // source. EMA-smoothed `engagement` is a weighted fusion of voice +
  // audio + time signals. External callers can inject extra signals via
  // setEngineSignal().
  //
  // Published state.engineState shape:
  //   voiceEnvelope    0–1   smoothed narrator voice presence
  //   voicePulse       0–1   transient voice spike (near 1 on word onset)
  //   audioOverall     0–1   full-mix audio energy (FFT-derived)
  //   engagement       0–1   EMA-fused engagement score
  //   sessionSec       sec   wall-clock elapsed since session start (minus pauses)
  //   completedFraction 0–1  fraction of estimated total runtime elapsed
  //   sinceVoiceSec    sec   seconds since last voiceEnvelope > threshold
  //   currentPhase     str   phase id currently playing (e.g. 'settling', 'work-2')
  //   phaseKind        str   base kind ('settling'|'induction'|'deepener'|...)
  //   phaseSec         sec   seconds elapsed in current phase
  //   playing          bool  session active and not paused
  //   paused           bool  mirrors state.paused
  //
  // Adapted from /mnt/user-data/uploads/Adaptive-Session-Studio — js/state-engine.js
  // ====================================================================
  const ENGINE_DEFAULT_WEIGHTS = {
    voiceEnvelope: 0.55,  // narrator presence dominates — silent narrator = low engagement
    audioOverall:  0.30,  // soundscape + voice combined energy
    voicePresence: 0.15,  // binary "any voice heard recently" bonus
  };
  const ENGINE_EMA_ALPHA      = 0.05;   // ~3s time constant at 60fps
  const VOICE_PRESENCE_THRESHOLD = 0.12; // voiceEnvelope above this counts as "speaking"

  let _engineSmoothedEngagement = 0;
  let _engineLastVoiceAt        = 0;    // wall-clock ms of last voiceEnvelope crossing threshold
  let _enginePhaseEnteredAt     = 0;    // wall-clock ms when current phase was entered
  let _engineLastPhase          = null; // track phase transitions
  const _engineExternalSignals  = {};   // name → { value: 0–1, weight: 0–1 }

  // Seed engineState so consumers reading pre-init don't crash
  if (!state.engineState) {
    state.engineState = {
      voiceEnvelope: 0, voicePulse: 0, audioOverall: 0, engagement: 0,
      sessionSec: 0, completedFraction: 0, sinceVoiceSec: 0,
      currentPhase: null, phaseKind: null, phaseSec: 0,
      playing: false, paused: false,
    };
  }

  /** Inject an external signal into the engagement fusion. Value + weight both 0–1.
   *  Example: a physiological sensor adapter could push its own signal this way. */
  function setEngineSignal(name, value, weight = 0.3) {
    _engineExternalSignals[name] = {
      value:  Math.max(0, Math.min(1, value)),
      weight: Math.max(0, Math.min(1, weight)),
    };
  }
  function clearEngineSignals() {
    Object.keys(_engineExternalSignals).forEach(k => delete _engineExternalSignals[k]);
  }

  function _engineDeriveEngagement(voiceEnv, audioOverall, voicePresence, playing) {
    if (!playing) return _engineSmoothedEngagement;
    const weights = { ...ENGINE_DEFAULT_WEIGHTS };
    const signals = { voiceEnvelope: voiceEnv, audioOverall, voicePresence };
    for (const [name, { value, weight }] of Object.entries(_engineExternalSignals)) {
      signals[name] = value;
      weights[name] = weight;
    }
    const totalWeight = Object.values(weights).reduce((s, w) => s + w, 0) || 1;
    let raw = 0;
    for (const [name, w] of Object.entries(weights)) {
      raw += (signals[name] ?? 0) * (w / totalWeight);
    }
    raw = Math.max(0, Math.min(1, raw));
    _engineSmoothedEngagement += ENGINE_EMA_ALPHA * (raw - _engineSmoothedEngagement);
    return +_engineSmoothedEngagement.toFixed(3);
  }

  /** Main tick — call every RAF frame while a session is active.
   *  Reads audioReactive + playback state, produces engineState. */
  function tickStateEngine() {
    const now     = Date.now();
    const playing = !!state._sessionStartTime && !state.paused;
    const ar      = state.audioReactive || {};
    const voiceEnv     = ar.voiceEnvelope || 0;
    const voicePulse   = ar.voicePulse || 0;
    const audioOverall = ar.overall || 0;

    // Pause/hidden-time correction for wallclock-anchored timestamps.
    // Without this, sinceVoiceSec and phaseSec spike by the full pause-or-
    // hidden duration on the first tick after resume — the "Gentle nudge on
    // prolonged narrator silence" rule (sinceVoiceSec > 45, durationSec: 0)
    // fires immediately when the user un-pauses or returns to a hidden tab,
    // and any phaseSec-gated rule misfires the same way. Drag the anchors
    // forward to "now" while paused or hidden so the next active tick
    // starts both clocks from zero.
    if (state.paused || state._hiddenStartedAt) {
      if (_engineLastVoiceAt) _engineLastVoiceAt = now;
      if (_enginePhaseEnteredAt) _enginePhaseEnteredAt = now;
    }

    // Track last voice activity — used by triggers/rules to detect narrator silence
    if (voiceEnv >= VOICE_PRESENCE_THRESHOLD) _engineLastVoiceAt = now;
    const voicePresence = voiceEnv >= VOICE_PRESENCE_THRESHOLD ? 1 : 0;
    const sinceVoiceSec = _engineLastVoiceAt ? (now - _engineLastVoiceAt) / 1000 : Infinity;

    // Track phase transitions
    const curPhase = state._currentPhase || null;
    if (curPhase !== _engineLastPhase) {
      _enginePhaseEnteredAt = now;
      _engineLastPhase = curPhase;
    }
    const phaseSec = playing && _enginePhaseEnteredAt ? (now - _enginePhaseEnteredAt) / 1000 : 0;

    // Session time (minus paused/hidden union) — uses _inactiveTotalMs
    // (the union accumulator) so a pause+hide overlap isn't double-counted.
    let sessionSec = 0;
    if (state._sessionStartTime) {
      let inactiveMs = state._inactiveTotalMs || 0;
      if (state._inactiveStartedAt) inactiveMs += (now - state._inactiveStartedAt);
      sessionSec = Math.max(0, (now - state._sessionStartTime - inactiveMs) / 1000);
    }

    // Completion fraction against estimated total runtime (from script metrics)
    const estTotalSec = (state._estimatedTotalSec || 0);
    const completedFraction = estTotalSec > 0 ? Math.min(1, sessionSec / estTotalSec) : 0;

    state.engineState = {
      voiceEnvelope: voiceEnv,
      voicePulse,
      audioOverall,
      engagement:    _engineDeriveEngagement(voiceEnv, audioOverall, voicePresence, playing),
      sessionSec:    +sessionSec.toFixed(2),
      completedFraction: +completedFraction.toFixed(3),
      sinceVoiceSec: Number.isFinite(sinceVoiceSec) ? +sinceVoiceSec.toFixed(2) : null,
      currentPhase:  curPhase,
      phaseKind:     curPhase ? phaseBase(curPhase) : null,
      phaseSec:      +phaseSec.toFixed(2),
      playing,
      paused:        !!state.paused,
    };
  }

  /** Read a single metric by name — used by rule conditions. */
  function getEngineMetric(metric) {
    const es = state.engineState || {};
    switch (metric) {
      case 'voiceEnvelope':     return es.voiceEnvelope ?? 0;
      case 'voicePulse':        return es.voicePulse ?? 0;
      case 'audioOverall':      return es.audioOverall ?? 0;
      case 'engagement':        return es.engagement ?? 0;
      case 'sessionSec':        return es.sessionSec ?? 0;
      case 'completedFraction': return es.completedFraction ?? 0;
      case 'sinceVoiceSec':     return es.sinceVoiceSec ?? 0;
      case 'phaseSec':          return es.phaseSec ?? 0;
      // Webcam-derived. Returns 0 when webcam is off (signals not injected).
      // attention/wakefulness pull from the external-signal cache the webcam
      // adapter writes into via setEngineSignal.
      case 'attention':         return _engineExternalSignals?.attention?.value ?? 0;
      case 'wakefulness':       return _engineExternalSignals?.wakefulness?.value ?? 0;
      case 'faceDetected':      return state._webcam?.faceDetected ? 1 : 0;
      case 'ambientBrightness': return state.ambientBrightness ?? 0.5;
      default:                  return 0;
    }
  }

  /** Evaluate a [metric, op, value] condition against the current engine state.
   *  Also supports pseudo-metrics 'phaseKind' (string equality) and
   *  'currentPhase' (exact phase-id match). */
  function evalEngineCondition(condition) {
    if (!condition) return false;
    const { metric, op, value } = condition;
    // String-valued pseudo-metrics — support only '==' and '!=' ops
    if (metric === 'phaseKind' || metric === 'currentPhase') {
      const es = state.engineState || {};
      const actual = String(es[metric] || '');
      const expected = String(value || '');
      if (op === '==') return actual === expected;
      if (op === '!=') return actual !== expected;
      return false;
    }
    const actual = getEngineMetric(metric);
    switch (op) {
      case '<':  return actual <  value;
      case '>':  return actual >  value;
      case '<=': return actual <= value;
      case '>=': return actual >= value;
      case '==': return Math.abs(actual - value) < 0.001;
      case '!=': return Math.abs(actual - value) >= 0.001;
      default:   return false;
    }
  }

  /** Reset — call on session end so a new session starts with a clean slate. */
  function resetStateEngine() {
    _engineSmoothedEngagement = 0;
    _engineLastVoiceAt        = 0;
    _enginePhaseEnteredAt     = 0;
    _engineLastPhase          = null;
    clearEngineSignals();
    state.engineState = {
      voiceEnvelope: 0, voicePulse: 0, audioOverall: 0, engagement: 0,
      sessionSec: 0, completedFraction: 0, sinceVoiceSec: 0,
      currentPhase: null, phaseKind: null, phaseSec: 0,
      playing: false, paused: false,
    };
  }

  // ====================================================================
  // RULES ENGINE — behavioral scripting over runtime metrics
  // ====================================================================
  // Rules fire actions when a metric condition holds for `durationSec`,
  // with a `cooldownSec` gate between re-fires. Evaluated every RAF tick
  // during active sessions.
  //
  // Rule schema (stored in state.session.rules[] for portability — but
  // since HNE sessions are generated from config not authored, rules
  // actually live on state.runtimeRules and can be declared via
  // DEFAULT_RUNTIME_RULES or added programmatically):
  //
  // {
  //   id:           string (unique),
  //   enabled:      bool,
  //   name:         string (for notify messages),
  //   condition:    { metric, op, value } — see evalEngineCondition
  //   durationSec:  number   (cond must hold this long before firing)
  //   cooldownSec:  number   (min gap between re-fires; 0 = no gap)
  //   action:       { type, param }
  // }
  //
  // Supported action types:
  //   pause              — pauses playback (via togglePause if not already paused)
  //   resume             — resumes playback
  //   emergencyExit      — triggers full rescue sequence
  //   notifyMessage      — shows a toast (param: message string)
  //   showOnScreenText   — full-screen transient overlay (param: text)
  //   duckAudio          — temporarily reduces soundscape (param: volume 0-1 or null for default)
  //   unduckAudio        — restores soundscape to state.droneVolume
  //   setSpeechRate      — adjust TTS rate (param: 0.5..2.0)
  //   setSoundscapeVolume — set drone volume (param: 0..1)
  //
  // Adapted from /mnt/user-data/uploads/Adaptive-Session-Studio — js/rules-engine.js
  // ====================================================================
  const RULES_VALID_ACTIONS = new Set([
    'pause', 'resume', 'emergencyExit', 'notifyMessage', 'showOnScreenText',
    'duckAudio', 'unduckAudio', 'setSpeechRate', 'setSoundscapeVolume',
  ]);
  const RULES_VALID_METRICS = new Set([
    'voiceEnvelope', 'voicePulse', 'audioOverall', 'engagement',
    'sessionSec', 'completedFraction', 'sinceVoiceSec',
    'phaseSec', 'phaseKind', 'currentPhase',
    // Webcam-derived signals (only populated when state._webcam.status === 'active').
    // Rules using these are no-ops when the webcam is off because the signals
    // simply aren't injected — `getEngineMetric` returns 0, so '> 0.5' never fires.
    'attention', 'wakefulness', 'faceDetected', 'ambientBrightness',
  ]);
  const RULES_VALID_OPS = new Set(['<', '>', '<=', '>=', '==', '!=']);

  function _rulesUid() {
    return 'r_' + Date.now().toString(36) + Math.random().toString(36).slice(2, 8);
  }

  /** Normalize a partial rule spec into a canonical runtime rule. */
  function normalizeRule(r) {
    const raw = r || {};
    const cond = raw.condition || {};
    const act  = raw.action || {};
    return {
      id:          typeof raw.id === 'string' && raw.id ? raw.id : _rulesUid(),
      enabled:     raw.enabled !== false,
      name:        typeof raw.name === 'string' && raw.name ? raw.name : 'Rule',
      condition: {
        metric: RULES_VALID_METRICS.has(cond.metric) ? cond.metric : 'engagement',
        op:     RULES_VALID_OPS.has(cond.op)         ? cond.op     : '<',
        value:  Number.isFinite(cond.value) || typeof cond.value === 'string' ? cond.value : 0.3,
      },
      durationSec: Number.isFinite(raw.durationSec) ? Math.max(0, raw.durationSec) : 5,
      cooldownSec: Number.isFinite(raw.cooldownSec) ? Math.max(0, raw.cooldownSec) : 30,
      action: {
        type:  RULES_VALID_ACTIONS.has(act.type) ? act.type : 'notifyMessage',
        param: act.param ?? null,
      },
    };
  }

  // Per-rule runtime state: { conditionHeldSec, lastFiredSec }
  // `lastFiredSec` uses sessionSec so cooldowns work relative to session start.
  const _runtimeRuleState = {};

  function _getRuntimeRuleState(id) {
    if (!_runtimeRuleState[id]) _runtimeRuleState[id] = { conditionHeldSec: 0, lastFiredSec: -Infinity };
    return _runtimeRuleState[id];
  }

  function clearRuntimeRuleState() {
    Object.keys(_runtimeRuleState).forEach(k => delete _runtimeRuleState[k]);
  }

  /** Execute a rule's action. Silent on unknown types — already caught at normalize. */
  async function executeRuleAction(rule) {
    const { type, param } = rule.action;
    const n = rule.name;
    try {
      switch (type) {
        case 'pause':
          if (!state.paused) togglePause();
          notify.info(`Rule "${n}": paused`);
          break;
        case 'resume':
          if (state.paused) togglePause();
          notify.info(`Rule "${n}": resumed`);
          break;
        case 'emergencyExit':
          notify.warn(`Rule "${n}": initiating rescue`);
          emergencyEnd();
          break;
        case 'notifyMessage':
          notify.info(String(param || 'Rule fired: ' + n).slice(0, 200));
          break;
        case 'showOnScreenText': {
          if (!param) break;
          _showRuleOverlay(String(param).slice(0, 200));
          break;
        }
        case 'duckAudio': {
          // Stack a 'rules' duck request on the drone's _duckGain so it
          // composes with speakScript's per-sentence 'speech' duck instead
          // of fighting it via master.gain. The unduckAudio action removes
          // the entry. Active level = MIN of all stacked requests; empty
          // stack returns to 1.0. User's master volume (state.droneVolume,
          // owned by the live volume slider) is never touched here, so
          // sliding it mid-rule no longer gets clobbered on unduck.
          if (typeof drone !== 'undefined' && drone?.duck) {
            const level = Number.isFinite(Number(param)) ? Math.max(0, Math.min(1, Number(param))) : 0.1;
            drone.duck('rules', level);
          }
          notify.info(`Rule "${n}": ducked`);
          break;
        }
        case 'unduckAudio':
          if (typeof drone !== 'undefined' && drone?.unduck) {
            drone.unduck('rules');
          }
          break;
        case 'setSpeechRate': {
          // Apply the local 0.5..2.0 hard-floor first (sanity bound), then the
          // user-tunable safety layer cap. Two layers: never below 0.5 or above 2.0
          // regardless of safety config (those are TTS-engine bounds), then further
          // clamped by safetyConfig.minSpeechRate / maxSpeechRate (default 0.6..1.5).
          const raw = Math.max(0.5, Math.min(2.0, Number.isFinite(Number(param)) ? Number(param) : 1));
          const rate = gateSpeechRate(raw);
          state.speechRate = rate;
          // Note: in-progress utterances keep their original rate — only new ones pick up the change.
          notify.info(`Rule "${n}": speech rate → ${rate.toFixed(2)}×`);
          break;
        }
        case 'setSoundscapeVolume': {
          const raw = Number.isFinite(Number(param)) ? Number(param) : 0.35;
          const vol = gateSoundscapeVolume(raw);
          state.droneVolume = vol;
          if (typeof drone !== 'undefined' && drone?.setVolume) drone.setVolume(vol);
          const slider = $('droneVolume'); if (slider) slider.value = Math.round(vol * 100);
          const lbl = $('droneVolVal'); if (lbl) lbl.textContent = Math.round(vol * 100);
          notify.info(`Rule "${n}": soundscape volume → ${Math.round(vol * 100)}%`);
          break;
        }
      }
    } catch (e) {
      console.warn('[rules] action failed:', rule.name, type, e);
    }
  }

  /** Transient full-screen overlay text (2.5s fade). Used by showOnScreenText
   *  action. Creates style block once, then appends/removes a div. */
  function _showRuleOverlay(text) {
    if (!document.getElementById('rule-overlay-style')) {
      const s = document.createElement('style');
      s.id = 'rule-overlay-style';
      s.textContent = `@keyframes ruleOverlayFade { 0%{opacity:0;transform:translate(-50%,-45%)} 15%{opacity:1;transform:translate(-50%,-50%)} 75%{opacity:1;transform:translate(-50%,-50%)} 100%{opacity:0;transform:translate(-50%,-55%)} }`;
      document.head.appendChild(s);
    }
    const el = document.createElement('div');
    el.style.cssText = `position:fixed;top:50%;left:50%;transform:translate(-50%,-50%);
      z-index:9000;background:rgba(4,4,7,0.85);border:0.5px solid rgba(196,154,60,0.35);
      border-radius:12px;padding:20px 30px;font-size:1.1rem;color:var(--text);
      font-family:var(--font-body);text-align:center;max-width:70vw;
      backdrop-filter:blur(8px);pointer-events:none;
      animation:ruleOverlayFade 2.5s ease forwards`;
    el.textContent = text;
    document.body.appendChild(el);
    setTimeout(() => el.remove(), 2600);
  }

  /** Rules list source. state.runtimeRules is the live list; falls back to
   *  DEFAULT_RUNTIME_RULES if user hasn't customized. Writable for future UI. */
  let DEFAULT_RUNTIME_RULES = [];

  function _activeRuntimeRules() {
    const r = state.runtimeRules;
    if (Array.isArray(r)) return r;
    return DEFAULT_RUNTIME_RULES;
  }

  /** Main tick — call after tickStateEngine in the RAF loop.
   *  frameSec is the time since the previous tick (for durationSec accumulation). */
  function tickRulesEngine(frameSec) {
    const rules = _activeRuntimeRules();
    if (!rules.length) return;
    const es = state.engineState;
    if (!es || !es.playing) return;
    const now = es.sessionSec;
    for (const rule of rules) {
      if (!rule.enabled) continue;
      const rs = _getRuntimeRuleState(rule.id);
      const condMet = evalEngineCondition(rule.condition);
      if (condMet) {
        rs.conditionHeldSec += frameSec;
        if (rs.conditionHeldSec >= rule.durationSec) {
          const cooldownElapsed = now - rs.lastFiredSec;
          if (rs.lastFiredSec === -Infinity || cooldownElapsed >= rule.cooldownSec) {
            rs.lastFiredSec = now;
            rs.conditionHeldSec = 0;
            executeRuleAction(rule);
          }
        }
      } else {
        rs.conditionHeldSec = 0;
      }
    }
  }

  // ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  // Default runtime rules — shipped as sane starters. User toggles or
  // replaces via state.runtimeRules (future UI). These run only when
  // state.runtimeRulesEnabled is true (opt-in; default off).
  // ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  DEFAULT_RUNTIME_RULES = [
    normalizeRule({
      name: 'Gentle nudge on prolonged narrator silence',
      enabled: true,
      condition: { metric: 'sinceVoiceSec', op: '>', value: 45 },
      durationSec: 0,      // condition already has time built in — fire as soon as it holds
      cooldownSec: 300,    // at most every 5 minutes — not spammy
      action: { type: 'notifyMessage', param: 'Still here — take your time.' },
    }),
    normalizeRule({
      name: 'Lower soundscape during deep work phases',
      enabled: true,
      condition: { metric: 'phaseKind', op: '==', value: 'work' },
      durationSec: 8,      // sustained 8s in a work phase before ducking
      cooldownSec: 60,
      action: { type: 'duckAudio', param: 0.18 },
    }),
    normalizeRule({
      name: 'Restore soundscape when leaving work phase',
      enabled: true,
      condition: { metric: 'phaseKind', op: '!=', value: 'work' },
      durationSec: 2,
      cooldownSec: 10,
      action: { type: 'unduckAudio', param: null },
    }),
    // Webcam-aware rules. These no-op when webcam is off (the metrics read 0
    // because the adapter doesn't push signals into the fusion engine).
    normalizeRule({
      name: 'Re-alert nudge when eyes stay closed during Wake/Reintroduction',
      enabled: true,
      condition: { metric: 'wakefulness', op: '<', value: 0.35 },
      durationSec: 15,                  // give it real time — surfacing is a process
      cooldownSec: 60,
      action: { type: 'showOnScreenText', param: 'Take your time opening your eyes.' },
    }),
    normalizeRule({
      name: 'Pause when user has left the camera view',
      enabled: true,
      condition: { metric: 'faceDetected', op: '==', value: 0 },
      durationSec: 60,                  // 60s of no face = user actually walked away
      cooldownSec: 120,
      action: { type: 'pause', param: null },
    }),
    normalizeRule({
      name: 'Gentle hint that user can close their eyes anytime',
      enabled: true,
      condition: { metric: 'attention', op: '>', value: 0.7 },
      durationSec: 600,                 // 10 minutes of staring at the screen
      cooldownSec: 1800,                // at most every 30 minutes
      action: { type: 'notifyMessage', param: 'You can close your eyes anytime.' },
    }),
  ];

  // ====================================================================
  // TRIGGER WINDOWS — time-bounded conditional triggers
  // ====================================================================
  // Complements rules-engine: rules fire repeatedly when a condition holds;
  // triggers fire once per window (opens at `atSec`, waits up to
  // `windowDurSec` for the condition, then closes). Good for time-specific
  // interactions like "at the 10-minute mark, offer a mood check".
  //
  // Trigger schema (lives alongside rules on state.runtimeTriggers):
  // {
  //   id:           string,
  //   enabled:      bool,
  //   name:         string,
  //   atSec:        number    (session-seconds when window opens)
  //   windowDurSec: number    (how long the window stays open)
  //   condition:    { metric, op, value } — same evalEngineCondition
  //   successAction: { type, param } — fires if condition becomes true within window
  //   failureAction: { type, param } — fires if window closes without condition
  //   cooldownSec:  number    (min gap before same trigger can re-arm)
  // }
  //
  // Shares the action set with rules-engine (via executeRuleAction). The 'none'
  // action type means "do nothing" — useful on failureAction when you only
  // want positive reinforcement for success.
  //
  // Adapted from /mnt/user-data/uploads/Adaptive-Session-Studio — js/trigger-windows.js
  // ====================================================================

  function _trigUid() {
    return 't_' + Date.now().toString(36) + Math.random().toString(36).slice(2, 8);
  }

  function normalizeTrigger(t) {
    const raw = t || {};
    const cond = raw.condition || {};
    const normalizeAct = (a) => ({
      type:  a && (RULES_VALID_ACTIONS.has(a.type) || a.type === 'none') ? a.type : 'none',
      param: a?.param ?? null,
    });
    return {
      id:           typeof raw.id === 'string' && raw.id ? raw.id : _trigUid(),
      enabled:      raw.enabled !== false,
      name:         typeof raw.name === 'string' && raw.name ? raw.name : 'Trigger',
      atSec:        Number.isFinite(raw.atSec)        ? Math.max(0, raw.atSec)        : 60,
      windowDurSec: Number.isFinite(raw.windowDurSec) ? Math.max(1, raw.windowDurSec) : 10,
      condition: {
        metric: RULES_VALID_METRICS.has(cond.metric) ? cond.metric : 'engagement',
        op:     RULES_VALID_OPS.has(cond.op)         ? cond.op     : '>=',
        value:  Number.isFinite(cond.value) || typeof cond.value === 'string' ? cond.value : 0.6,
      },
      successAction: normalizeAct(raw.successAction),
      failureAction: normalizeAct(raw.failureAction),
      cooldownSec:   Number.isFinite(raw.cooldownSec) ? Math.max(0, raw.cooldownSec) : 120,
    };
  }

  // Runtime window state per trigger id:
  // { status: 'idle'|'open'|'success'|'failed', openedAt: sessionSec, lastFiredAt: sessionSec }
  const _triggerWindowState = {};

  function _getTriggerWindowState(id) {
    if (!_triggerWindowState[id]) _triggerWindowState[id] = { status: 'idle', openedAt: 0, lastFiredAt: -Infinity };
    return _triggerWindowState[id];
  }

  function clearTriggerWindowState() {
    Object.keys(_triggerWindowState).forEach(k => delete _triggerWindowState[k]);
  }

  /** Execute a trigger action. Shares the rule action set, plus 'none' as a
   *  no-op (useful for failureAction when you want no penalty). */
  function executeTriggerAction(action, triggerName, outcome) {
    if (!action || action.type === 'none') return;
    // Adapt to executeRuleAction's shape — it expects { action: {...}, name: '...' }
