      customPersona: (state.personas.find(p => p.id === state.selectedPersonaId)?.isCustom)
        ? state.personas.find(p => p.id === state.selectedPersonaId) : null,
    };
    setLoading('shareConfigBtn', true);
    try {
      const url = await createShareUpload(payload, { fileName: 'session-config' });
      if (url) {
        await showShareLinkModal(url, 'Share session config');
        await markFeatureUsed(FEAT.SHARE);
      } else {
        // Fallback: use the legacy hash-URL format (smaller, no upload)
        const params = new URLSearchParams();
        const c = payload.config;
        if (c.selectedPersonaId) params.set('p', c.selectedPersonaId);
        if (c.selectedMethodId) params.set('m', c.selectedMethodId);
        if (c.intentionType) params.set('i', c.intentionType);
        if (c.length) params.set('l', c.length);
        if (c.soundscape) params.set('s', c.soundscape);
        if (c.vizOverride) params.set('v', c.vizOverride);
        if (c.intentionInput) params.set('t', encodeURIComponent(c.intentionInput));
        if (c.userAffirmations) params.set('a', encodeURIComponent(c.userAffirmations));
        if (c.affirmationMode) params.set('am', c.affirmationMode);
        const fallback = window.location.origin + window.location.pathname + '#cfg=' + params.toString();
        await showShareLinkModal(fallback, 'Share session config (basic)');
      }
    } finally { setLoading('shareConfigBtn', false, '🔗 share link'); }
  }

  /** Share the persona currently open in the editor. */
  async function shareCurrentPersona() {
    const id = state.editingPersonaId;
    const name = $('personaFormName')?.value?.trim();
    const tagline = $('personaFormTagline')?.value?.trim();
    const category = $('personaFormCategory')?.value || 'contemplative';
    const systemPrompt = $('personaFormPrompt')?.value?.trim();
    if (!name || !systemPrompt) { notify.warn('Fill in name and voice instruction first.'); return; }
    const payload = {
      version: 1, kind: 'custom-persona', createdAt: Date.now(),
      persona: { id: id || ('shared-' + Date.now().toString(36)), name, tagline, category, systemPrompt, isCustom: true },
    };
    setLoading('personaShareBtn', true);
    try {
      const url = await createShareUpload(payload, { fileName: 'persona' });
      if (url) { await showShareLinkModal(url, 'Share persona'); await markFeatureUsed(FEAT.SHARE); }
      else notify.warn('Share link unavailable — uploadPlugin may not be loaded yet. Try again in a moment.');
    } finally { setLoading('personaShareBtn', false, '🔗 share'); }
  }

  // ====================================================================
  // PHASE REGEN DIFF — when a user retries a phase, show the
  // old and new text side-by-side so they can pick the better one rather
  // than blindly overwriting.
  // ====================================================================
  /**
   * @param [string] oldText  Original phase text
   * @param [string] newText  Newly generated phase text
   * @param [string] phaseLabel  Human-friendly label for modal header
   * @returns {Promise<'new'|'old'>} Which text to keep
   */
  function showPhaseDiffModal(oldText, newText, phaseLabel) {
    return new Promise(resolve => {
      const backdrop = document.createElement('div');
      backdrop.className = 'modal-backdrop visible';
      const wc = (t) => (t || '').trim().split(/\s+/).filter(Boolean).length;
      const oldW = wc(oldText), newW = wc(newText);
      backdrop.innerHTML = `<div class="modal diff-modal">
        <h2>Compare: ${escapeHtml(phaseLabel)}</h2>
        <div style="font-size:0.8rem; color:var(--text-dim); line-height:1.55; margin-bottom:10px">
          Pick which version to keep. You can always regenerate again.
        </div>
        <div class="diff-compare">
          <div class="diff-col diff-old">
            <div class="diff-col-label">← original</div>
            <div class="diff-col-text">${escapeHtml(oldText || '(empty)')}</div>
            <div class="diff-col-meta">${oldW} words</div>
          </div>
          <div class="diff-col diff-new">
            <div class="diff-col-label">new →</div>
            <div class="diff-col-text">${escapeHtml(newText || '(empty)')}</div>
            <div class="diff-col-meta">${newW} words</div>
          </div>
        </div>
        <div class="modal-actions">
          <button class="btn secondary" id="_diffKeepOld">keep original</button>
          <button class="btn" id="_diffKeepNew">use new version</button>
        </div>
      </div>`;
      document.body.appendChild(backdrop);
      const cleanup = (val) => { backdrop.remove(); resolve(val); };
      backdrop.querySelector('#_diffKeepOld').addEventListener('click', () => cleanup('old'));
      backdrop.querySelector('#_diffKeepNew').addEventListener('click', () => cleanup('new'));
      backdrop.addEventListener('click', e => { if (e.target === backdrop) cleanup('new'); });
    });
  }

  // ====================================================================
  // SETTINGS PERSISTENCE (auto-save/load to localStorage)
  // ====================================================================
  const SETTINGS_KEY = 'stillness_settings_v5';
  const _kv = (typeof root !== 'undefined' && root.kv) ? root.kv : null;
  let _saveTimeout = null;

  function _buildSettingsObj() {
    return {
      selectedPersonaId: state.selectedPersonaId,
      selectedMethodId: state.selectedMethodId,
      intentionType: state.intentionType,
      length: state.length,
      soundscape: state.soundscape,
      soundscapeOverridden: !!state._soundscapeOverridden,
      droneEnabled: state.droneEnabled,
      duckingEnabled: state.duckingEnabled,
      speechRate: state.speechRate,
      voicePitch: state.voicePitch,
      voiceVolume: state.voiceVolume,
      tempoMultiplier: state.tempoMultiplier,
      bgSource: state.bgSource,
      bgPrompt: state.bgPrompt,
      bgNegativePrompt: state.bgNegativePrompt,
      bgOrientation: state.bgOrientation,
      bgGuidance: state.bgGuidance,
      bgSteps: state.bgSteps,
      // Style preset chips selected on the bg prompt — additive modifiers
      // composed inside bgEngine._currentParams() at generation time.
      bgActiveStylePresetIds: Array.isArray(state.bgActiveStylePresetIds) ? state.bgActiveStylePresetIds : [],
      // Gallery view preferences — persist sort + favorites-only filter so
      // reopening the modal feels stateful.
      bgGallerySort: state.bgGallerySort || 'newest',
      bgGalleryFavOnly: state.bgGalleryFavOnly === true,
      bgRotationSec: state.bgRotationSec,
      bgTransitionDuration: state.bgTransitionDuration,
      bgPlaybackOrder: state.bgPlaybackOrder,
      bgEnabledTransitions: state.bgEnabledTransitions,
      bgYoutubeUrl: state.bgYoutubeUrl,
      bgStaticUrl: state.bgStaticUrl,
      bgAutoGen: state.bgAutoGen,
      bgImageOpacity: state.bgImageOpacity,
      droneVolume: state.droneVolume,
      vizOverride: state.vizOverride,
      audioReactiveMode: state.audioReactiveMode,
      vizSettings: state.vizSettings,
      // Color override for canvas visualizers — null means "use --accent".
      // Stored as a 6-digit hex string when set; tolerates legacy missing values.
      vizColor: state.vizColor || null,
      hud: state.hud,
      personaFilter: state.personaFilter,
      methodFilter: state.methodFilter,
      intentionInput: $('intentionInput')?.value || '',
      userAffirmations: $('userAffirmations')?.value || '',
      affirmationMode: $('affirmationMode')?.value || 'woven',
      voiceName: $('voiceSelect')?.value || '',
      // Local AI voice (Phase 6) — opt-in offline TTS via Transformers.js
      useLocalTTS: !!state.useLocalTTS,
      localVoiceSpeaker: state.localVoiceSpeaker || 'clb',
      // Reimagine mode for Custom blocks (Phase 7) — 'guide' or 'smart'
      reimagineMode: state.reimagineMode || 'smart',
      remoteProcessingOptIn: !!$('remoteProcessingOptIn')?.checked,
      smartDirectorEnabled: !!$('smartDirectorEnabled')?.checked,
      directorBias: Number($('directorBiasSlider')?.value ?? state.directorBias ?? 0),
      directorPace: Number($('directorPaceSlider')?.value ?? state.directorPace ?? 0),
      directorBiasByDomain: { ...(state.directorBiasByDomain || {}) },
      runtimeRulesEnabled: !!$('runtimeRulesEnabled')?.checked,
      // A/B compare mode visibility — once revealed, persists across sessions
      abModeRevealed: !!state.abModeRevealed,
      // Session mode preset id — bundles rules+triggers+safety overrides
      activeSessionMode: state.activeSessionMode || 'standard',
      // Playback dynamics — adaptive speech-rate modulation config
      playbackDynamics: { ...PLAYBACK_DYNAMICS_DEFAULTS, ...(state.playbackDynamics || {}) },
      // Safety layer — persist the whole config object so users get their tuned
      // caps back across sessions. Only persist the fields we actually expose.
      safetyConfig: { ...SAFETY_DEFAULTS, ...(state.safetyConfig || {}) },
      // Auto-tempo cleanup options — character stripping list + orphan-punctuation toggle
      autoTempoOptions: {
        stripChars: state.autoTempoOptions?.stripChars || '',
        stripOrphanPunctuation: state.autoTempoOptions?.stripOrphanPunctuation !== false,
      },
      settingsVersion: 5,
    };
  }

  function saveUserSettings() {
    clearTimeout(_saveTimeout);
    _saveTimeout = setTimeout(async () => {
      try {
        const settings = _buildSettingsObj();
        // Primary: Perchance KV storage (cloud-backed, cross-device)
        if (_kv) {
          await _kv.settings.set(SETTINGS_KEY, settings);
        }
        // Fallback: localStorage (local-only, survives page refresh)
        localStorage.setItem(SETTINGS_KEY, JSON.stringify(settings));
      } catch {}
    }, 1500);
  }

  function normalizeSettings(raw = {}) {
    const s = { ...(raw || {}) };
    if (s.voiceName == null) s.voiceName = s.narratorVoice || s.selectedVoice || s.voice || '';
    if (s.userAffirmations == null) s.userAffirmations = s.affirmations || s.customAffirmations || '';
    if (s.affirmationMode == null) s.affirmationMode = s.affirmMode || 'woven';
    if (s.vizSettings == null && s.visualSettings) s.vizSettings = s.visualSettings;
    if (s.vizOverride == null && s.visualMode) s.vizOverride = s.visualMode;
    if (s.tempoMultiplier == null && s.pauseTempo != null) s.tempoMultiplier = s.pauseTempo;
    if (s.length == null && s.sessionLength) s.length = s.sessionLength;
    if (s.intentionInput == null && s.intentionText) s.intentionInput = s.intentionText;
    if (s.remoteProcessingOptIn == null) s.remoteProcessingOptIn = !!(s.remoteBackupOptIn || s.cloudSyncOptIn || s.serverConsent);
    if (s.smartDirectorEnabled == null) s.smartDirectorEnabled = s.profileInjectionEnabled == null ? true : !!s.profileInjectionEnabled;
    if (s.directorBias == null) s.directorBias = 0;
    // Clamp to -3..+3 (slider range). Tolerates legacy/bad values without breaking the app.
    s.directorBias = Math.max(-3, Math.min(3, Math.round(Number(s.directorBias) || 0)));
    // Pace axis — same -3..+3 clamp.
    if (s.directorPace == null) s.directorPace = 0;
    s.directorPace = Math.max(-3, Math.min(3, Math.round(Number(s.directorPace) || 0)));
    // Per-domain offsets — older saved settings don't have this field.
    // Coerce values to clamped integers, drop zeros to keep storage clean.
    if (!s.directorBiasByDomain || typeof s.directorBiasByDomain !== 'object') {
      s.directorBiasByDomain = {};
    } else {
      const cleaned = {};
      for (const [k, v] of Object.entries(s.directorBiasByDomain)) {
        const n = Math.max(-3, Math.min(3, Math.round(Number(v) || 0)));
        if (n !== 0) cleaned[k] = n;
      }
      s.directorBiasByDomain = cleaned;
    }
    if (s.settingsVersion == null) s.settingsVersion = 1;
    return s;
  }

  function normalizeHistoryEntry(raw = {}) {
    const entry = { ...(raw || {}) };
    if (!entry.id) entry.id = entry.date || `legacy-${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2)}`;
    if (!entry.date) entry.date = Date.now();
    if (entry.intentionText == null) entry.intentionText = entry.intentionInput || entry.prompt || '';
    if (entry.userAffirmations == null) entry.userAffirmations = entry.affirmations || entry.customAffirmations || '';
    if (entry.affirmationMode == null) entry.affirmationMode = entry.affirmMode || 'woven';
    if (entry.voiceName == null) entry.voiceName = entry.narratorVoice || entry.selectedVoice || '';
    if (entry.workBlockCount == null && entry.metrics?.workBlocks != null) entry.workBlockCount = entry.metrics.workBlocks;
    if (!Array.isArray(entry.phaseOrder) || !entry.phaseOrder.length) {
      try {
        if (entry.script) {
          const parsed = parseScript(entry.script);
          entry.phaseOrder = parsed?.ordered?.length ? parsed.ordered : (entry.phaseOrder || []);
        }
      } catch {}
    }
    if (entry.workflowVersion == null) entry.workflowVersion = entry.stepsTotal === 6 ? 10 : 9;
    if (entry.stepsTotal == null) entry.stepsTotal = entry.workflowVersion >= 10 ? 6 : 5;
    return entry;
  }

  function normalizeAnalyticsData(data) {
    const base = {
      v: 3,
      p: {}, m: {}, i: {}, s: {}, z: {}, ln: {}, am: {}, vc: {},
      hr: new Array(24).fill(0), dw: new Array(7).fill(0),
      mood: { preSum: 0, postSum: 0, deltaSum: 0, n: 0, postN: 0 },
      comp: { total: 0, finished: 0, emergency: 0 },
      dur: { sum: 0, n: 0 },
      shape: { phaseSum: 0, workSum: 0, affirmSum: 0, n: 0 },
      cfg: { rateSum: 0, pitchSum: 0, volSum: 0, droneSum: 0, n: 0 },
      feat: 0,
      ts: Date.now(),
    };
    const a = { ...base, ...(data || {}) };
    a.v = 3;
    a.p = a.p || {}; a.m = a.m || {}; a.i = a.i || {}; a.s = a.s || {}; a.z = a.z || {}; a.ln = a.ln || {};
    a.am = a.am || {}; a.vc = a.vc || {};
    a.hr = Array.isArray(a.hr) ? Array.from({length:24}, (_,i)=>Number(a.hr[i]||0)) : base.hr.slice();
    a.dw = Array.isArray(a.dw) ? Array.from({length:7}, (_,i)=>Number(a.dw[i]||0)) : base.dw.slice();
    a.mood = { ...base.mood, ...(a.mood || {}) };
    a.comp = { ...base.comp, ...(a.comp || {}) };
    a.dur = { ...base.dur, ...(a.dur || {}) };
    a.shape = { ...base.shape, ...(a.shape || {}) };
    a.cfg = { ...base.cfg, ...(a.cfg || {}) };
    return a;
  }

  function mergeAnalyticsData(existing, incoming) {
    const a = normalizeAnalyticsData(existing);
    const b = normalizeAnalyticsData(incoming);
    const sumMaps = ['p','m','i','s','z','ln','am','vc'];
    for (const key of sumMaps) {
      for (const [k,v] of Object.entries(b[key] || {})) a[key][k] = (a[key][k] || 0) + (Number(v) || 0);
    }
    a.hr = a.hr.map((v,i) => v + (Number(b.hr[i]) || 0));
    a.dw = a.dw.map((v,i) => v + (Number(b.dw[i]) || 0));
    for (const k of ['preSum','postSum','deltaSum','n','postN']) a.mood[k] = (a.mood[k] || 0) + (b.mood[k] || 0);
    for (const k of ['total','finished','emergency']) a.comp[k] = (a.comp[k] || 0) + (b.comp[k] || 0);
    for (const k of ['sum','n']) a.dur[k] = (a.dur[k] || 0) + (b.dur[k] || 0);
    for (const k of ['phaseSum','workSum','affirmSum','n']) a.shape[k] = (a.shape[k] || 0) + (b.shape[k] || 0);
    for (const k of ['rateSum','pitchSum','volSum','droneSum','n']) a.cfg[k] = (a.cfg[k] || 0) + (b.cfg[k] || 0);
    a.feat = (a.feat || 0) | (b.feat || 0);
    a.ts = Math.max(a.ts || 0, b.ts || 0, Date.now());
    return a;
  }

  async function loadUserSettings() {
    try {
      let s = null;
      // Try Perchance KV first (cloud-backed)
      if (_kv) {
        try { s = await _kv.settings.get(SETTINGS_KEY); } catch {}
      }
      // Fallback to localStorage
      if (!s) {
        const raw = localStorage.getItem(SETTINGS_KEY) || localStorage.getItem('stillness_settings_v4') || localStorage.getItem('stillness_settings_v3') || localStorage.getItem('stillness_settings_v2');
        if (raw) s = JSON.parse(raw);
      }
      if (!s) return false;
      s = normalizeSettings(s);

      if (s.selectedPersonaId) state.selectedPersonaId = s.selectedPersonaId;
      if (s.selectedMethodId) state.selectedMethodId = s.selectedMethodId;
      if (s.intentionType) { state.intentionType = s.intentionType; const el = $('intentionType'); if (el) el.value = s.intentionType; }
      if (s.length) { state.length = s.length; const el = $('lengthSelect'); if (el) el.value = s.length; }
      if (s.soundscape != null) { state.soundscape = s.soundscape; state.droneEnabled = s.soundscape !== 'none'; const el = $('soundscapeSelect'); if (el) el.value = s.soundscape; }
      if (s.soundscapeOverridden) state._soundscapeOverridden = true;
      if (s.speechRate != null) { state.speechRate = s.speechRate; const el = $('speechRate'); if (el) el.value = Math.round(s.speechRate * 100); const v = $('speechRateVal'); if (v) v.textContent = s.speechRate.toFixed(2) + '×'; }
      if (s.voicePitch != null) { state.voicePitch = s.voicePitch; const el = $('voicePitch'); if (el) el.value = Math.round(s.voicePitch * 100); const v = $('voicePitchVal'); if (v) v.textContent = s.voicePitch.toFixed(2); }
      if (s.voiceVolume != null) { state.voiceVolume = s.voiceVolume; const el = $('voiceVolume'); if (el) el.value = Math.round(s.voiceVolume * 100); const v = $('voiceVolVal'); if (v) v.textContent = Math.round(s.voiceVolume * 100); }
      if (s.tempoMultiplier != null) { state.tempoMultiplier = s.tempoMultiplier; const el = $('tempoSlider'); if (el) el.value = Math.round(s.tempoMultiplier * 100); const v = $('tempoVal'); if (v) v.textContent = s.tempoMultiplier.toFixed(1) + '×'; }
      if (s.bgSource) {
        // File objects don't survive reload; if a local file source was saved
        // but state._bgFile is missing, fall back to 'none' to avoid a broken bg at session start.
        let restoredSource = s.bgSource;
        if ((restoredSource === 'local-image' || restoredSource === 'local-video') && !state._bgFile) {
          restoredSource = 'none';
        }
        state.bgSource = restoredSource;
        const el = $('bgSourceSelect');
        if (el) { el.value = restoredSource; el.dispatchEvent(new Event('change')); }
      }
      if (s.bgPrompt) { state.bgPrompt = s.bgPrompt; const el = $('bgPromptInput'); if (el) el.value = s.bgPrompt; }
      if (s.bgNegativePrompt != null) {
        state.bgNegativePrompt = s.bgNegativePrompt;
        const el = $('bgNegativePromptInput'); if (el) el.value = s.bgNegativePrompt;
      }
      if (s.bgOrientation) {
        state.bgOrientation = s.bgOrientation;
        const el = $('bgOrientationSelect'); if (el) el.value = s.bgOrientation;
      }
      if (s.bgGuidance != null) {
        state.bgGuidance = Number(s.bgGuidance);
        const el = $('bgGuidanceSlider'); if (el) el.value = state.bgGuidance;
        const lbl = $('bgGuidanceVal'); if (lbl) lbl.textContent = state.bgGuidance;
      }
      if (s.bgSteps != null) {
        state.bgSteps = Number(s.bgSteps);
        const el = $('bgStepsSlider'); if (el) el.value = state.bgSteps;
        const lbl = $('bgStepsVal'); if (lbl) lbl.textContent = state.bgSteps;
      }
      // Style preset chip selections — filter unknown ids out (would happen if
      // a preset was renamed or removed between sessions).
      if (Array.isArray(s.bgActiveStylePresetIds)) {
        state.bgActiveStylePresetIds = s.bgActiveStylePresetIds.filter(id => !!BG_STYLE_PRESET_MAP[id]);
        // Rebuild chip UI with restored selections (no-op if not yet built).
        try { buildBgStyleChips(); } catch (e) { /* not fatal — page may still be initialising */ }
      }
      // Gallery view preferences
      if (typeof s.bgGallerySort === 'string' && ['newest','oldest','favorites','seed'].includes(s.bgGallerySort)) {
        state.bgGallerySort = s.bgGallerySort;
      }
      if (typeof s.bgGalleryFavOnly === 'boolean') state.bgGalleryFavOnly = s.bgGalleryFavOnly;
      if (s.bgRotationSec != null) {
        state.bgRotationSec = Number(s.bgRotationSec);
        const el = $('bgRotationSlider'); if (el) el.value = state.bgRotationSec;
        const lbl = $('bgRotationVal'); if (lbl) lbl.textContent = state.bgRotationSec;
        const hint = $('bgRotationHint'); if (hint) hint.textContent = state.bgRotationSec;
      }
      if (s.bgTransitionDuration != null) {
        state.bgTransitionDuration = Number(s.bgTransitionDuration);
        const el = $('bgTransitionDurSlider'); if (el) el.value = state.bgTransitionDuration;
        const lbl = $('bgTransitionDurVal'); if (lbl) lbl.textContent = state.bgTransitionDuration.toFixed(1);
      }
      if (s.bgPlaybackOrder) {
        state.bgPlaybackOrder = s.bgPlaybackOrder;
        const seg = $('bgOrderSeg');
        if (seg) seg.querySelectorAll('button').forEach(b => b.classList.toggle('active', b.dataset.order === s.bgPlaybackOrder));
      }
      if (Array.isArray(s.bgEnabledTransitions)) {
        // Filter against the current registry — keys may have been renamed/removed
        state.bgEnabledTransitions = s.bgEnabledTransitions.filter(k => BG_TRANSITIONS[k]);
        if (typeof buildBgTransitionGrid === 'function') buildBgTransitionGrid();
      }
      if (s.bgYoutubeUrl) { state.bgYoutubeUrl = s.bgYoutubeUrl; const el = $('bgYoutubeUrl'); if (el) el.value = s.bgYoutubeUrl; }
      if (s.bgStaticUrl) { state.bgStaticUrl = s.bgStaticUrl; const el = $('bgUrlInput'); if (el) el.value = s.bgStaticUrl; }
      if (s.bgAutoGen != null) { state.bgAutoGen = s.bgAutoGen; const el = $('bgAutoGenToggle'); if (el) el.checked = s.bgAutoGen; }
      if (s.bgImageOpacity != null) {
        state.bgImageOpacity = Math.max(0.05, Math.min(1, Number(s.bgImageOpacity) || 0.35));
        document.documentElement.style.setProperty('--bg-img-opacity', state.bgImageOpacity);
        const setupSlider = $('bgImgOpacitySlider'); const setupVal = $('bgImgOpacityVal');
        if (setupSlider) setupSlider.value = Math.round(state.bgImageOpacity * 100);
        if (setupVal) setupVal.textContent = Math.round(state.bgImageOpacity * 100);
        const liveSlider = $('liveBgOpacity'); const liveVal = $('liveBgOpacityVal');
        if (liveSlider) liveSlider.value = Math.round(state.bgImageOpacity * 100);
        if (liveVal) liveVal.textContent = Math.round(state.bgImageOpacity * 100) + '%';
      }
      if (s.droneVolume != null) { state.droneVolume = s.droneVolume; const el = $('droneVolume'); if (el) el.value = Math.round(s.droneVolume * 100); const v = $('droneVolVal'); if (v) v.textContent = Math.round(s.droneVolume * 100); }
      if (s.vizOverride != null) { state.vizOverride = s.vizOverride; const el = $('vizSelect'); if (el) el.value = s.vizOverride; }
      // Audio reactive mode — with prefers-reduced-motion OS honor on first load
      if (s.audioReactiveMode == null) {
        const prm = window.matchMedia?.('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)').matches;
        if (prm && typeof setAudioReactiveMode === 'function') setAudioReactiveMode('off');
      } else if (typeof setAudioReactiveMode === 'function') {
        setAudioReactiveMode(s.audioReactiveMode);
      }
      if (s.hud) state.hud = { ...state.hud, ...s.hud };
      if (s.duckingEnabled != null) { state.duckingEnabled = s.duckingEnabled; const el = $('duckingToggle'); if (el) el.checked = s.duckingEnabled; }
      if (s.vizSettings) {
        state.vizSettings = { ...state.vizSettings, ...s.vizSettings };
        const vs = state.vizSettings;
        const speedEl = $('vizSpeed'); if (speedEl) { speedEl.value = Math.round(vs.speed * 100); const v = $('vizSpeedVal'); if (v) v.textContent = vs.speed.toFixed(1) + '×'; }
        const intEl = $('vizIntensity'); if (intEl) { intEl.value = Math.round(vs.intensity * 100); const v = $('vizIntensityVal'); if (v) v.textContent = vs.intensity.toFixed(1) + '×'; }
        const cmpEl = $('vizComplexity'); if (cmpEl) { cmpEl.value = Math.round(vs.complexity * 100); const v = $('vizComplexityVal'); if (v) v.textContent = vs.complexity.toFixed(1) + '×'; }
        const trEl = $('vizTrail'); if (trEl) { trEl.value = Math.round(vs.trail * 100); const v = $('vizTrailVal'); if (v) v.textContent = vs.trail.toFixed(2); }
      }
      // Restore color override. Validate as a 6-digit hex; anything else is
      // dropped on the floor (user gets accent fallback rather than a broken
      // renderer trying to parse "red" or "#bad" or whatever was persisted).
      if (s.vizColor && typeof s.vizColor === 'string' && /^#[0-9a-fA-F]{6}$/.test(s.vizColor)) {
        state.vizColor = s.vizColor.toLowerCase();
        const picker = $('vizColorPicker'); if (picker) picker.value = state.vizColor;
        const v = $('vizColorVal'); if (v) v.textContent = state.vizColor;
      } else {
        state.vizColor = null;
        // Picker defaults to the accent so the swatch starts visually correct.
        try {
          const accent = (getComputedStyle(document.documentElement).getPropertyValue('--accent').trim() || '#6b94b8').toLowerCase();
          const picker = $('vizColorPicker'); if (picker) picker.value = accent;
        } catch {}
        const v = $('vizColorVal'); if (v) v.textContent = 'accent';
      }
      // Restore HUD toggle checkboxes
      const hudMap = { fixationDot:'cfgFixationDot', textDisplay:'cfgTextDisplay', phaseDots:'cfgPhaseDots', phaseLabel:'cfgPhaseLabel', progressBar:'cfgProgressBar', sessionTime:'cfgSessionTime', labels:'cfgLabels' };
      for (const [key, elId] of Object.entries(hudMap)) { const el = $(elId); if (el) el.checked = state.hud[key] !== false; }
      // Restore filter tabs
      if (s.personaFilter) state.personaFilter = s.personaFilter;
      if (s.methodFilter) state.methodFilter = s.methodFilter;
      if (s.intentionInput) { const el = $('intentionInput'); if (el) el.value = s.intentionInput; }
      if (s.userAffirmations != null) { const el = $('userAffirmations'); if (el) el.value = s.userAffirmations; }
      if (s.affirmationMode) { const el = $('affirmationMode'); if (el) el.value = s.affirmationMode; state.affirmationMode = s.affirmationMode; }
      // Auto-tempo cleanup options — load with defensive validation. stripChars
      // must be a string (not an array — an old version may have persisted as such).
      // stripOrphanPunctuation defaults to true (the bug-fix behavior).
      if (s.autoTempoOptions && typeof s.autoTempoOptions === 'object') {
        const ato = s.autoTempoOptions;
        state.autoTempoOptions = {
          stripChars: typeof ato.stripChars === 'string' ? ato.stripChars.slice(0, 50) : '',
          stripOrphanPunctuation: ato.stripOrphanPunctuation !== false,
        };
      }
      if (s.voiceName) state._savedVoiceName = s.voiceName;
      // Local AI voice (Phase 6) — restore toggle + speaker; don't auto-load the
      // model on startup (heavy), wait for the user to actually start a session.
      state.useLocalTTS = !!s.useLocalTTS;
      state.localVoiceSpeaker = s.localVoiceSpeaker || 'clb';
      const ltcb = $('localVoiceToggle'); if (ltcb) ltcb.checked = state.useLocalTTS;
      const ltsel = $('localVoiceSpeaker'); if (ltsel) {
        ltsel.value = state.localVoiceSpeaker;
        ltsel.style.display = state.useLocalTTS ? '' : 'none';
      }
      if (state.useLocalTTS) {
        const lts = $('localVoiceStatus');
        if (lts) lts.textContent = 'Local voice was enabled — re-loading the model (~80 MB cached after first download)…';
        // Fire-and-forget: model prep happens in the background while the user
        // configures the rest of the session.
        _localTTS.init(state.localVoiceSpeaker).then((ok) => {
          if (lts) lts.textContent = ok
            ? '✓ Ready. Local AI voice will be used for the next session.'
            : 'Could not load the local voice model — will fall back to the system voice.';
        });
      }
      // Reimagine mode (Phase 7) — defaults to 'smart' if Smart Director is on, else 'guide'
      state.reimagineMode = s.reimagineMode || (s.smartDirectorEnabled === false ? 'guide' : 'smart');
      state.remoteProcessingOptIn = !!s.remoteProcessingOptIn;
      const rpcb = $('remoteProcessingOptIn'); if (rpcb) rpcb.checked = !!state.remoteProcessingOptIn;
      state.smartDirectorEnabled = s.smartDirectorEnabled !== false;
      const sdcb = $('smartDirectorEnabled'); if (sdcb) sdcb.checked = state.smartDirectorEnabled;
      state.directorBias = s.directorBias ?? 0;
      const dbSlider = $('directorBiasSlider'); if (dbSlider) dbSlider.value = state.directorBias;
      // Pace axis
      state.directorPace = s.directorPace ?? 0;
      const dpSlider = $('directorPaceSlider'); if (dpSlider) dpSlider.value = state.directorPace;
      _updateDirectorBiasUI(); // update label + enabled state
      // Per-domain offsets
      state.directorBiasByDomain = (s.directorBiasByDomain && typeof s.directorBiasByDomain === 'object') ? { ...s.directorBiasByDomain } : {};
      if (typeof renderDirectorDomainOffsets === 'function') renderDirectorDomainOffsets();
      state.runtimeRulesEnabled = !!s.runtimeRulesEnabled;
      const rrcb = $('runtimeRulesEnabled'); if (rrcb) rrcb.checked = state.runtimeRulesEnabled;
      // A/B compare mode visibility — restore from saved
      state.abModeRevealed = !!s.abModeRevealed;
      // Safety config — merge defaults with persisted overrides. Sanitizes via
      // explicit field-by-field clamping so a corrupted persisted value can't
      // override a hard limit (e.g. negative cooldown, NaN cap).
      if (s.safetyConfig && typeof s.safetyConfig === 'object') {
        const sc = s.safetyConfig;
        state.safetyConfig = {
          maxSpeechRate:        Number.isFinite(sc.maxSpeechRate)        ? Math.max(0.5, Math.min(2.0, sc.maxSpeechRate))      : SAFETY_DEFAULTS.maxSpeechRate,
          minSpeechRate:        Number.isFinite(sc.minSpeechRate)        ? Math.max(0.5, Math.min(2.0, sc.minSpeechRate))      : SAFETY_DEFAULTS.minSpeechRate,
          maxSoundscapeVolume:  Number.isFinite(sc.maxSoundscapeVolume)  ? Math.max(0.0, Math.min(1.0, sc.maxSoundscapeVolume)) : SAFETY_DEFAULTS.maxSoundscapeVolume,
          emergencyCooldownSec: Number.isFinite(sc.emergencyCooldownSec) ? Math.max(0, Math.min(300, sc.emergencyCooldownSec)) : SAFETY_DEFAULTS.emergencyCooldownSec,
          maxSessionMinutes:    Number.isFinite(sc.maxSessionMinutes)    ? Math.max(5, Math.min(180, sc.maxSessionMinutes))    : SAFETY_DEFAULTS.maxSessionMinutes,
        };
        // If min > max (corrupt or hand-edited), reset to defaults — a swapped
        // pair would lock speech rate at the boundary instead of producing a range.
        if (state.safetyConfig.minSpeechRate >= state.safetyConfig.maxSpeechRate) {
          state.safetyConfig.minSpeechRate = SAFETY_DEFAULTS.minSpeechRate;
          state.safetyConfig.maxSpeechRate = SAFETY_DEFAULTS.maxSpeechRate;
        }
      }
      _renderSafetyPanel?.(); // refresh inputs if panel already exists in DOM
      // Session mode — restore the user's last selection. Apply on load so rules + triggers
      // + safety overrides all snap to the saved mode at startup. If the saved mode is unknown
      // (e.g. mode list changed in a release), silently fall back to standard.
      const savedMode = typeof s.activeSessionMode === 'string' ? s.activeSessionMode : 'standard';
      const modeExists = SESSION_MODES.some(m => m.id === savedMode);
      const finalMode = modeExists ? savedMode : 'standard';
      const modeSel = $('sessionModeSelect'); if (modeSel) modeSel.value = finalMode;
      // Apply silently — but don't notify on initial load (would be noise on every page load).
      // Replicate applySessionMode's effects without the notify call.
      const mode = SESSION_MODES.find(m => m.id === finalMode);
      if (mode) {
        state.activeSessionMode = mode.id;
        if (mode.rules === null)    state.runtimeRules = null;
        else                         state.runtimeRules = mode.rules.map(r => normalizeRule(r));
        if (mode.triggers === null) state.runtimeTriggers = null;
        else                         state.runtimeTriggers = mode.triggers.map(t => normalizeTrigger(t));
        // Safety overrides applied AFTER the safetyConfig load above — mode wins on conflict.
        if (mode.safetyOverrides) {
          const cur = state.safetyConfig || { ...SAFETY_DEFAULTS };
          const ov = mode.safetyOverrides;
          state.safetyConfig = {
            ...cur,
            ...(Number.isFinite(ov.maxSpeechRate)        && { maxSpeechRate:        Math.max(0.5, Math.min(2.0, ov.maxSpeechRate)) }),
            ...(Number.isFinite(ov.minSpeechRate)        && { minSpeechRate:        Math.max(0.5, Math.min(2.0, ov.minSpeechRate)) }),
            ...(Number.isFinite(ov.maxSoundscapeVolume)  && { maxSoundscapeVolume:  Math.max(0.0, Math.min(1.0, ov.maxSoundscapeVolume)) }),
            ...(Number.isFinite(ov.emergencyCooldownSec) && { emergencyCooldownSec: Math.max(0, Math.min(300, ov.emergencyCooldownSec)) }),
            ...(Number.isFinite(ov.maxSessionMinutes)    && { maxSessionMinutes:    Math.max(5, Math.min(180, ov.maxSessionMinutes)) }),
          };
          _renderSafetyPanel?.();
        }
        // Description + icon refresh
        const ic = $('sessionModeIcon');        if (ic) ic.textContent = mode.icon;
        const dc = $('sessionModeDescription'); if (dc) dc.textContent = mode.description;
      }
      // Playback dynamics — restore saved config with field-by-field clamping
      // so a corrupted persisted value can't bypass the intended ranges.
      if (s.playbackDynamics && typeof s.playbackDynamics === 'object') {
        const pd = s.playbackDynamics;
        const validModes  = new Set(['time', 'engagement', 'adaptive']);
        const validCurves = new Set(['linear', 'sine', 'exponential']);
        state.playbackDynamics = {
          enabled:        pd.enabled === true,
          mode:           validModes.has(pd.mode) ? pd.mode : PLAYBACK_DYNAMICS_DEFAULTS.mode,
          curve:          validCurves.has(pd.curve) ? pd.curve : PLAYBACK_DYNAMICS_DEFAULTS.curve,
          startScale:     Number.isFinite(pd.startScale)    ? Math.max(0.5, Math.min(1.5, pd.startScale))    : PLAYBACK_DYNAMICS_DEFAULTS.startScale,
          endScale:       Number.isFinite(pd.endScale)      ? Math.max(0.5, Math.min(1.5, pd.endScale))      : PLAYBACK_DYNAMICS_DEFAULTS.endScale,
          engagementMin:  Number.isFinite(pd.engagementMin) ? Math.max(0.5, Math.min(1.5, pd.engagementMin)) : PLAYBACK_DYNAMICS_DEFAULTS.engagementMin,
          engagementMax:  Number.isFinite(pd.engagementMax) ? Math.max(0.5, Math.min(1.5, pd.engagementMax)) : PLAYBACK_DYNAMICS_DEFAULTS.engagementMax,
          smoothingSec:   Number.isFinite(pd.smoothingSec)  ? Math.max(1, Math.min(30, pd.smoothingSec))     : PLAYBACK_DYNAMICS_DEFAULTS.smoothingSec,
        };
        // Repair start/end inversion (would map intensity backward) and engagement floor/ceiling swap
        if (state.playbackDynamics.startScale > state.playbackDynamics.endScale) {
          state.playbackDynamics.startScale = PLAYBACK_DYNAMICS_DEFAULTS.startScale;
          state.playbackDynamics.endScale   = PLAYBACK_DYNAMICS_DEFAULTS.endScale;
        }
        if (state.playbackDynamics.engagementMin > state.playbackDynamics.engagementMax) {
          state.playbackDynamics.engagementMin = PLAYBACK_DYNAMICS_DEFAULTS.engagementMin;
          state.playbackDynamics.engagementMax = PLAYBACK_DYNAMICS_DEFAULTS.engagementMax;
        }
        _renderPlaybackDynamicsPanel?.();
      }
      applySavedVoiceSelection();
      updateVoiceInfo();
      return true;
    } catch { return false; }
  }

  // ====================================================================
  // DIFF-BOUNCE — change tracking for user inputs
  // ====================================================================
  // Each tracked input has a DiffBouncer instance. On any edit, .ping()
  // resets an idle timer. When the idle period elapses, a magnitude check
  // decides whether the accumulated change is worth logging. Raises the
  // signal-to-noise ratio of profile.changeLog.
  // ====================================================================

  // Tunable thresholds — SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH. Change values here to
  // adjust sensitivity. Read at check-time, so live tuning works:
  //   DIFF_BOUNCE_THRESHOLDS.idleMs = 3000   // takes effect on next ping
  const DIFF_BOUNCE_THRESHOLDS = {
    idleMs:               8000,   // 8s idle before magnitude check
    textChangePct:        0.10,   // 10% of chars changed (long-text)
    csvChangeCount:       2,      // ≥2 entries shifted (CSV / newline-list)
    imageSimilarityMax:   0.70,   // Jaccard ≥ this = same exploration, skip
    commitOnSessionStart: true,   // hard-commit anchor at session start
    logCap:               200,    // max entries in profile.changeLog
  };

  class DiffBouncer {
    /**
     * @param [object] cfg
     * @param [string] cfg.source    Identifier (e.g. 'pfGoals', 'bgImage')
     * @param {'text'|'csv'|'image'} cfg.type
     * @param [Function] cfg.getState () => current snapshot for this source
     * @param [Function] cfg.onCommit (diffDetail, currentState, meta) => void
     */
    constructor(cfg) {
      this.source = cfg.source;
      this.type = cfg.type;
      this.getState = cfg.getState;
      this.onCommit = cfg.onCommit;
      this.lastSnapshot = null;
      this._idleTimer = null;
      this._latestState = null; // convenience slot for push-based sources (image)
    }

    ping() {
      const current = this.getState();
      if (current === undefined || current === null) return; // defensive
      if (this.lastSnapshot === null) {
        // First observation seeds the baseline — don't commit yet.
        // Exception: image type always seeds via the first-snapshot branch
        // inside _diffImage, which is handled in _check below.
        if (this.type === 'image') {
          // Run the check immediately so the first-snapshot commit lands
          if (this._idleTimer) clearTimeout(this._idleTimer);
          this._idleTimer = setTimeout(() => { this._idleTimer = null; this._check('idle'); }, DIFF_BOUNCE_THRESHOLDS.idleMs);
          return;
        }
        this.lastSnapshot = current;
        return;
      }
      if (this._idleTimer) clearTimeout(this._idleTimer);
      this._idleTimer = setTimeout(() => {
        this._idleTimer = null;
        this._check('idle');
      }, DIFF_BOUNCE_THRESHOLDS.idleMs);
    }

    /** Bypass magnitude gate — used at session start to seed baselines */
    hardCommit(reason) {
      if (this._idleTimer) { clearTimeout(this._idleTimer); this._idleTimer = null; }
      const current = this.getState();
      if (current === undefined || current === null) return;
      if (this.lastSnapshot === null) {
        this.lastSnapshot = current;
        return;
      }
      const diff = this._computeDiff(this.lastSnapshot, current);
      if (diff && diff.hasChange) {
        this.onCommit(diff.detail, current, { trigger: reason, force: true });
        this.lastSnapshot = current;
      }
    }

    _check(trigger) {
      const current = this.getState();
      if (current === undefined || current === null) return;
      if (this.lastSnapshot === null) {
        // Image type: first-snapshot seed path
        if (this.type === 'image') {
          const seed = _diffImage(null, current);
          if (seed && seed.meetsThreshold) {
            this.onCommit(seed.detail, current, { trigger });
          }
          this.lastSnapshot = current;
          return;
        }
        this.lastSnapshot = current;
        return;
      }
      const result = this._computeDiff(this.lastSnapshot, current);
      if (!result) return;
      if (result.meetsThreshold) {
        this.onCommit(result.detail, current, { trigger });
        this.lastSnapshot = current;
      }
      // Sub-threshold: keep lastSnapshot unchanged so small changes
      // accumulate until one crosses the threshold.
    }

    _computeDiff(before, after) {
      if (this.type === 'text')  return _diffText(before, after);
      if (this.type === 'csv')   return _diffCSV(before, after);
      if (this.type === 'image') return _diffImage(before, after);
      return null;
    }
  }

  // Capped Levenshtein — inputs truncated to 1500 chars each to bound cost
  function _lev(a, b) {
    if (a === b) return 0;
    if (!a.length) return b.length;
    if (!b.length) return a.length;
    const A = a.length > 1500 ? a.slice(0, 1500) : a;
    const B = b.length > 1500 ? b.slice(0, 1500) : b;
    const m = A.length, n = B.length;
    let prev = new Array(n + 1); for (let j = 0; j <= n; j++) prev[j] = j;
    let curr = new Array(n + 1);
    for (let i = 1; i <= m; i++) {
      curr[0] = i;
      for (let j = 1; j <= n; j++) {
        const cost = A.charCodeAt(i - 1) === B.charCodeAt(j - 1) ? 0 : 1;
        curr[j] = Math.min(curr[j - 1] + 1, prev[j] + 1, prev[j - 1] + cost);
      }
      [prev, curr] = [curr, prev];
    }
    return prev[n];
  }

  function _diffText(before, after) {
    before = before || ''; after = after || '';
    if (before === after) return { hasChange: false, meetsThreshold: false };
    const denom = Math.max(before.length, after.length, 1);
    const dist = _lev(before, after);
    const pct = dist / denom;
    return {
      hasChange: true,
      meetsThreshold: pct >= DIFF_BOUNCE_THRESHOLDS.textChangePct,
      detail: {
        beforeLen: before.length,
        afterLen: after.length,
        changePct: +pct.toFixed(3),
        preview: after.slice(0, 80),
      },
    };
  }

  function _parseCSVEntries(raw) {
    if (!raw) return [];
    // Splits on newline AND comma — handles userAffirmations (newline-delimited)
    // and bgPromptInput / pfGoals (comma-separated) uniformly.
    return String(raw)
      .split(/[\n,]+/)
      .map(s => s.trim().toLowerCase())
      .filter(Boolean);
  }

  function _diffCSV(before, after) {
    const a = new Set(_parseCSVEntries(before));
    const b = new Set(_parseCSVEntries(after));
    const added = [...b].filter(x => !a.has(x));
    const removed = [...a].filter(x => !b.has(x));
    const kept = [...a].filter(x => b.has(x));
    const changeCount = added.length + removed.length;
    if (changeCount === 0) return { hasChange: false, meetsThreshold: false };
    return {
      hasChange: true,
      meetsThreshold: changeCount >= DIFF_BOUNCE_THRESHOLDS.csvChangeCount,
      detail: { added, removed, kept },
    };
  }

  function _diffImage(before, after) {
    // State shape: { tags: [...], summary: '...', prompt: '...', ts: 0 }
    const lastTags = (before && before.tags) || [];
    const newTags  = (after  && after.tags)  || [];
    const a = new Set(lastTags);
    const b = new Set(newTags);
    if (!a.size && !b.size) return { hasChange: false, meetsThreshold: false };
    if (!a.size) {
      // First-snapshot seed — always commit so the user has an initial anchor.
      return {
        hasChange: true,
        meetsThreshold: true,
        detail: {
          summary: after.summary || '',
          tags: newTags,
          prompt: (after.prompt || '').slice(0, 200),
          reason: 'first-snapshot',
        },
      };
    }
    const inter = [...a].filter(x => b.has(x));
    const union = new Set([...a, ...b]);
    const jaccard = inter.length / union.size;
    if (jaccard >= DIFF_BOUNCE_THRESHOLDS.imageSimilarityMax) {
      return { hasChange: false, meetsThreshold: false };
    }
    return {
      hasChange: true,
      meetsThreshold: true,
      detail: {
        summary: after.summary || '',
        tags: newTags,
        prompt: (after.prompt || '').slice(0, 200),
        similarity: +jaccard.toFixed(3),
      },
    };
  }

  // Map holding active DiffBouncer instances — one per source id
  const diffBouncers = {};

  /** Derive a 6-10 word aesthetic summary + 3-6 tags from an image prompt.
   *  Consent-aware. Uses LLM when remote processing is enabled; otherwise
   *  falls back to rule-based tokenization. Never throws. */
  async function summarizeImagePrompt({ prompt, negativePrompt }) {
    // Stop-list for rule-based fallback — common filler words
    const STOP = new Set(['the','and','with','for','that','this','from','into','over','very','more','less','really','some','just','only','also','than','then','when','make','made','very','highly']);

    const fallback = () => {
      const words = String(prompt || '').toLowerCase().match(/[a-z][a-z-]{2,}/g) || [];
      const tags = [...new Set(words.filter(w => !STOP.has(w)))].slice(0, 6);
      return {
        summary: tags.slice(0, 6).join(', ') || 'untagged image',
        tags,
        prompt: String(prompt || '').slice(0, 200),
        source: 'fallback',
      };
    };

    // No consent → skip LLM, use local tokenization only
    if (!state.remoteProcessingOptIn) return fallback();
    if (typeof root === 'undefined' || !root.aiTextPlugin) return fallback();

    const instruction = [
      'Summarize this image generation prompt in exactly 6-10 words, focusing on visual aesthetic.',
      'Output STRICT JSON only: {"summary":"…","tags":["…","…"]}',
      'Tags: 3-6 short aesthetic descriptors (adjectives + subject nouns). Lowercase, no prose.',
      `Prompt: ${String(prompt || '').slice(0, 400)}`,
      negativePrompt ? `Negative: ${String(negativePrompt).slice(0, 200)}` : '',
    ].filter(Boolean).join('\n');

    try {
      try { logAICall('perchance', 'image-tag', instruction); } catch {}
      const data = await root.aiTextPlugin({
        instruction,
        startWith: '{',
        stopSequences: ['\n\n\n', '\n\n[[', '\n[['],
        hideStartWith: false,
      });
      const raw = String(data?.text || data || '').trim();
      // Salvage parse — find outermost { } in the response
      const s = raw.indexOf('{'), e = raw.lastIndexOf('}');
      if (s < 0 || e <= s) return fallback();
      const parsed = JSON.parse(raw.slice(s, e + 1));
      if (!parsed.summary || !Array.isArray(parsed.tags)) return fallback();
      return {
        summary: String(parsed.summary).slice(0, 120),
        tags: parsed.tags.map(t => String(t).toLowerCase().trim()).filter(Boolean).slice(0, 6),
        prompt: String(prompt || '').slice(0, 200),
        source: 'llm',
      };
    } catch (e) {
      console.warn('[DB] image summary LLM failed, using fallback:', e);
      return fallback();
    }
  }

  // ====================================================================
  // USER PROFILE (bio, appearance, goals — for AI personalization)
  // ====================================================================
  const PROFILE_KEY = 'stillness_profile_v1';
  let _profileSaveTimeout = null;
  let _profileChangeLog = []; // in-memory mirror of profile.changeLog

  /** Commit a diff entry to the change log (ring buffer, newest-first). */
  function commitChangeLogEntry(source, type, diff, meta = {}) {
    const entry = {
      ts: Date.now(),
      source,
      type,
      diff,
      meta: { trigger: meta.trigger || 'idle' },
    };
    _profileChangeLog.unshift(entry);
    if (_profileChangeLog.length > DIFF_BOUNCE_THRESHOLDS.logCap) {
      _profileChangeLog.length = DIFF_BOUNCE_THRESHOLDS.logCap;
    }
    saveProfile();
    renderProfileDiffs(); // refresh the visible "what AI sees" panel
  }

  /** Render the recent profile-diffs panel. Mirrors what getProfileContext
   *  serves to the AI so users see exactly what the Director has been told.
   *  Decay weight visualized as opacity. Empty state shown when nothing
   *  has been committed yet. */
  function renderProfileDiffs() {
    const list = $('profileDiffsList');
    if (!list) return;
    const recent = (_profileChangeLog || []).slice(0, 12);
    if (!recent.length) {
      list.innerHTML = '<div style="opacity:0.55;font-style:italic">No recent edits logged.</div>';
      return;
    }
    const srcLabel = id => ({ pfGoals:'goals', pfAbout:'about', pfAppearance:'appearance', pfName:'name', intentionInput:'intention', userAffirmations:'affirmations', bgPromptInput:'bg-prompt', bgImage:'image-gen' }[id] || id);
    const now = Date.now();
    const dayMs = 86400000;
    list.innerHTML = recent.map(e => {
      const ageDays = Math.max(0, (now - e.ts) / dayMs);
      const w = Math.exp(-ageDays / 14);
      const opacity = Math.max(0.35, w).toFixed(2);
      const rel = (typeof _relTimeDB === 'function') ? _relTimeDB(e.ts) : 'recently';
      let summary = '';
      if (e.type === 'csv' && e.diff) {
        const add = (e.diff.added || []).slice(0, 3).join(', ');
        const rem = (e.diff.removed || []).slice(0, 3).join(', ');
        const bits = [];
        if (add) bits.push(`<span style="color:var(--accent-green)">+${escapeHtml(add)}</span>`);
        if (rem) bits.push(`<span style="color:#d46a6a">-${escapeHtml(rem)}</span>`);
        summary = bits.join(' ');
      } else if (e.type === 'text' && e.diff) {
        summary = `${Math.round((e.diff.changePct || 0) * 100)}% text change`;
      } else if (e.type === 'image' && e.diff) {
        const tags = (e.diff.tags || []).slice(0, 4).join(', ');
        summary = e.diff.summary || (tags ? `[${escapeHtml(tags)}]` : '');
      }
      return `<div style="padding:3px 0;border-bottom:1px solid var(--glass-border);opacity:${opacity}"><span style="color:var(--text-faint);text-transform:uppercase;font-size:0.58rem;letter-spacing:0.06em">${escapeHtml(rel)}</span> · <strong>${escapeHtml(srcLabel(e.source))}</strong>${summary ? ' · ' + summary : ''}</div>`;
    }).join('');
  }

  function getProfile() {
    return {
      name: $('pfName')?.value?.trim() || '',
      useFirstName: !!$('pfUseFirstName')?.checked,
      pronouns: $('pfPronouns')?.value || '',
      age: $('pfAge')?.value || '',
      experience: $('pfExperience')?.value || 'beginner',
      about: $('pfAbout')?.value?.trim() || '',
      appearance: $('pfAppearance')?.value?.trim() || '',
      goals: $('pfGoals')?.value?.trim() || '',
      // Per-field "share with AI" toggles. Default true so older profiles
      // (saved before these existed) keep their previous behavior of
      // sharing everything. The local Director never reads these — they
      // only gate AI prompt assembly in getProfileContext.
      shareAge:        $('pfShareAge')        ? !!$('pfShareAge').checked        : true,
      shareAppearance: $('pfShareAppearance') ? !!$('pfShareAppearance').checked : true,
      shareGoals:      $('pfShareGoals')      ? !!$('pfShareGoals').checked      : true,
    };
  }

  /** Audio/viz safety relaxation gate. Returns true when the user has
   *  set their profile age — a low-friction signal that they're an
   *  engaged adult who's made their own decisions about the app. When
   *  this is true the various audio/viz auto-degrades and confirmation
   *  modals are skipped: intense visualizers stay where the user puts
   *  them, gamma-flicker doesn't gate behind a popup, prefers-reduced-
   *  motion is honored as a setting but not as a forced override, and
   *  warning labels soften.
   *
   *  When age is unset, all the original safeties stay in effect — that
   *  catches casual / drive-by users who haven't given the app any
   *  signal of who they are. Falls open (returns false / safeties on)
   *  if the form element isn't yet rendered. */
  function _audioVizSafetiesRelaxed() {
    try {
      const ageEl = $('pfAge');
      if (!ageEl) return false; // pre-render; safeties default on
      return !!ageEl.value;
    } catch { return false; }
  }

  function applyProfileToForm(profile, opts = {}) {
    const p = profile || {};
    const setVal = (id, value, fallback = '') => {
      const el = $(id);
      if (el) el.value = value ?? fallback;
    };
    setVal('pfName', p.name, '');
    // Backward-compat: older profiles without useFirstName default to false (privacy-safe).
    const useName = $('pfUseFirstName');
    if (useName) useName.checked = !!p.useFirstName;
    setVal('pfPronouns', p.pronouns, '');
    setVal('pfAge', p.age, '');
    setVal('pfExperience', p.experience, 'beginner');
    setVal('pfAbout', p.about, '');
    setVal('pfAppearance', p.appearance, '');
    setVal('pfGoals', p.goals, '');
    // Share toggles — default true when missing so old profiles keep parity.
    const shareAge = $('pfShareAge'); if (shareAge) shareAge.checked = (p.shareAge !== false);
    const shareApp = $('pfShareAppearance'); if (shareApp) shareApp.checked = (p.shareAppearance !== false);
    const shareGoals = $('pfShareGoals'); if (shareGoals) shareGoals.checked = (p.shareGoals !== false);
    // Sync universal toggle to reflect the mirrors. ON only when every
    // mirror is true; older profiles that explicitly set any field to
    // false roll into "off" so the user has to opt back in deliberately.
    const shareAll = $('pfShareAll');
    if (shareAll) shareAll.checked = (p.shareAge !== false) && (p.shareAppearance !== false) && (p.shareGoals !== false);
    const nameEl = $('sidebarDisplayName');
    if (nameEl) nameEl.textContent = p.name || 'Practitioner';
    if (opts.save !== false) saveProfile();
  }

  function saveProfile() {
    clearTimeout(_profileSaveTimeout);
    _profileSaveTimeout = setTimeout(async () => {
      try {
        const profile = getProfile();
        // Attach the in-memory change log (ring-buffered, newest-first)
        profile.changeLog = _profileChangeLog.slice(0, DIFF_BOUNCE_THRESHOLDS.logCap);
        if (_kv) await _kv.settings.set(PROFILE_KEY, profile);
        localStorage.setItem(PROFILE_KEY, JSON.stringify(profile));
        // Update sidebar display name
        const nameEl = $('sidebarDisplayName');
        if (nameEl) nameEl.textContent = profile.name || 'Practitioner';
      } catch {}
    }, 1000);
  }

  async function loadProfile() {
    try {
      let p = null;
      if (_kv) { try { p = await _kv.settings.get(PROFILE_KEY); } catch {} }
      if (!p) { const raw = localStorage.getItem(PROFILE_KEY); if (raw) p = JSON.parse(raw); }
      if (!p) return;
      // Additive migration — seed changeLog for old profiles missing the field
      _profileChangeLog = Array.isArray(p.changeLog) ? p.changeLog : [];
      applyProfileToForm(p, { save: false });
      // Populate the visible diff panel with whatever's in the log so the
      // user sees the same time-aware context the AI does.
      if (typeof renderProfileDiffs === 'function') renderProfileDiffs();
    } catch {}
  }

  /** Tracked fields for DiffBouncer coverage.
   *  type: 'text' for prose-ish fields (pct-of-chars threshold)
   *        'csv'  for list-ish fields (entry-count threshold) */
  const TRACKED_FIELDS = [
    { id: 'pfGoals',          type: 'csv'  }, // "Goals" textarea
    { id: 'pfAbout',          type: 'text' }, // "About You" freeform
    { id: 'intentionInput',   type: 'text' }, // per-session intention
    { id: 'userAffirmations', type: 'csv'  }, // newline-delimited affirmations
    { id: 'bgPromptInput',    type: 'csv'  }, // image-gen prompt tags
  ];

  function initDiffBouncers() {
    // Text/CSV field bouncers — one per TRACKED_FIELD
    TRACKED_FIELDS.forEach(({ id, type }) => {
      const el = document.getElementById(id);
      if (!el) return; // defensive — missing field means no coverage, no crash
      const bouncer = new DiffBouncer({
        source: id,
        type,
        getState: () => el.value || '',
        onCommit: (diff, _current, meta) => commitChangeLogEntry(id, type, diff, meta),
      });
      // Seed baseline from current value — hardCommit is a no-op here
      // because lastSnapshot is null and the impl returns without committing.
      bouncer.lastSnapshot = el.value || '';
      diffBouncers[id] = bouncer;
      el.addEventListener('input', () => bouncer.ping());
    });

    // Image generation — single bouncer for all image-gen events.
    // State shape is set by the bgEngine hook (Task 6).
    diffBouncers.bgImage = new DiffBouncer({
      source: 'bgImage',
      type: 'image',
      getState: () => diffBouncers.bgImage._latestState || null,
      onCommit: (diff, _current, meta) => commitChangeLogEntry('bgImage', 'image', diff, meta),
    });

    // Timeline UI — render when the Session History section opens.
    // Changelog content lives inside Session History (moved 2026-04-25);
    // listening on the parent details ensures lazy render still happens.
    document.getElementById('sessionHistoryDetails')?.addEventListener('toggle', e => {
      if (e.target.open) renderChangeLog();
    });

    console.log('[DB] diff bouncers initialized:', Object.keys(diffBouncers).length);
  }

  // HTML escape for user-generated content shown in the changelog view
  function _escDB(s) { return String(s).replace(/[<>&"']/g, c => ({ '<':'&lt;', '>':'&gt;', '&':'&amp;', '"':'&quot;', "'":'&#39;' }[c])); }

  function _relTimeDB(ts) {
    const now = Date.now();
    const d = Math.floor((now - ts) / 1000);
    if (d < 60) return 'just now';
    if (d < 3600) return Math.floor(d / 60) + 'm ago';
    if (d < 86400) return Math.floor(d / 3600) + 'h ago';
    if (d < 86400 * 2) return 'yesterday';
    if (d < 86400 * 7) return Math.floor(d / 86400) + 'd ago';
    return new Date(ts).toISOString().slice(0, 10);
  }

  function renderChangeLog() {
    const list = document.getElementById('pfChangeLogList');
    const count = document.getElementById('pfChangeLogCount');
    if (!list) return;
    const entries = _profileChangeLog || [];
    if (count) count.textContent = entries.length ? `(${entries.length})` : '';
    if (!entries.length) {
      list.innerHTML = '<div class="sublabel" style="padding:8px 4px">no changes logged yet — edit a tracked field or generate an image.</div>';
      return;
    }
    const srcLabel = id => ({
      pfGoals: 'goals',
      pfAbout: 'about',
      intentionInput: 'intention',
      userAffirmations: 'affirmations',
      bgPromptInput: 'bg prompt',
      bgImage: 'image',
    }[id] || id);
    const html = entries.slice(0, DIFF_BOUNCE_THRESHOLDS.logCap).map(e => {
      const ts = _relTimeDB(e.ts);
      const src = srcLabel(e.source);
      let body = '';
      if (e.type === 'csv' && e.diff) {
        const addHtml = (e.diff.added || []).map(a => `<span class="cl-chip cl-add">+${_escDB(a)}</span>`).join('');
        const remHtml = (e.diff.removed || []).map(r => `<span class="cl-chip cl-rem">−${_escDB(r)}</span>`).join('');
        body = addHtml + remHtml || '<em>no entries shifted</em>';
      } else if (e.type === 'text' && e.diff) {
        const pct = Math.round((e.diff.changePct || 0) * 100);
        body = `<span class="sublabel">${pct}% changed</span> — <em>${_escDB(e.diff.preview || '')}…</em>`;
      } else if (e.type === 'image' && e.diff) {
        const tags = (e.diff.tags || []).map(t => `<span class="cl-chip">${_escDB(t)}</span>`).join('');
        body = `<div>${_escDB(e.diff.summary || '')}</div><div style="margin-top:3px">${tags}</div>`;
      } else {
        body = '<em>(unknown entry type)</em>';
      }
      return `<div class="pf-changelog-entry"><div class="cl-head"><span class="cl-ts">${_escDB(ts)}</span> · <span class="cl-src">${_escDB(src)}</span></div><div class="cl-body">${body}</div></div>`;
    }).join('');
    list.innerHTML = html;
  }

  /** Build an AI context string from the user's profile for prompt injection */
  // ====================================================================
  // PHASE 4 SIGNALS — time-of-day picks + modality inference
  // ====================================================================
  // Two lightweight derived signals from the existing analytics store. Both
  // are read-only — they don't add new persistence, just compute over `hr`
  // (hourly distribution) + frequency maps + current settings.

  /** What persona/method does this user most often pick at the given hour?
   *  Returns null if there's not enough signal (fewer than 3 sessions in this
   *  hour bucket OR no clear winner). Cheap — runs in-memory after analytics
   *  load. Used to surface a "matches your usual hour" hint on Step 1.
   *
   *  Note: The current analytics store keeps a single hourly counter (`hr`),
   *  not a per-(hour × persona) cross-tab. So this function approximates by
   *  combining global persona popularity with the user's typical hour. If
   *  the user has substantial activity at the requested hour AND has a clear
   *  top persona, return that persona — it's the best available signal
   *  without expanding the schema.
   *
   *  @returns {Promise<{personaId?:string, methodId?:string} | null>} */
  async function getTimeOfDayPick(hour) {
    try {
      const a = await getAnalytics();
      if (!a) return null;
      const hourCount = (a.hr && a.hr[hour]) || 0;
      // Need a meaningful baseline — ignore single-session noise.
      if (hourCount < 3) return null;
      // Pick the user's globally most-used persona/method as the recommendation
      // for this hour. (A future schema upgrade could store hourly cross-tabs;
      // for now this is the highest-signal field.)
      const topKey = (map) => {
        let best = null, bestN = 0;
        for (const k of Object.keys(map || {})) {
          if (map[k] > bestN) { best = k; bestN = map[k]; }
        }
        return bestN >= 3 ? best : null;
      };
      const personaId = topKey(a.p);
      const methodId  = topKey(a.m);
      if (!personaId && !methodId) return null;
      return { personaId, methodId, hourCount };
    } catch (e) { return null; }
  }

  /** Conservative inference of the user's preferred sensory modality from
   *  current settings + recent usage. Returns 'visual'|'auditory'|'kinesthetic'
   *  ONLY when the signal is strong. Otherwise returns null and we say nothing.
   *
   *  Used to lightly steer AI generation: a kinesthetic user gets more body
   *  references; an auditory user gets sound-rich descriptions. Wrong
   *  inference is worse than no inference, so the bar is high.
   *
   *  Signals (each 0 or 1):
   *    visual:       text-display ON, viz-override is intense (not none/candle/colorwash),
   *                  recent methods include visual fixation patterns
   *    auditory:     text-display ON, soundscape on, audio-reactive on, voice volume high
   *    kinesthetic:  somatic-category methods dominate, breath/body-scan in usage,
   *                  webcam/biofeedback features used
   *
   *  Requires score >= 2 in a single modality AND that modality must be the
   *  clear leader (margin >= 1) to return non-null. */
  async function inferModality() {
    try {
      const scores = { visual: 0, auditory: 0, kinesthetic: 0 };
      // Visual signals
      if (state.vizOverride && !['none', 'candle', 'colorwash'].includes(state.vizOverride)) scores.visual++;
      if (state.hud?.fixationDot !== false) scores.visual++;
      // Auditory signals
      if (state.cfgTextDisplay !== false) scores.auditory++; // narration-text on suggests they want spoken reinforcement
      if (state.soundscape && state.soundscape !== 'none') scores.auditory++;
      if ((state.audioReactiveMode || 'subtle') !== 'off') scores.auditory++;
      // Kinesthetic signals — read from analytics method usage
      const a = await getAnalytics();
      if (a && a.m) {
        const SOMATIC_METHODS = new Set(['hand-heaviness', 'arm-catalepsy', 'progressive', 'body-awareness',
          'floating-body', 'glove-anesthesia', 'heartbeat-sync', 'breath-478', 'pain-dial', 'arm-drop']);
        let somaticPlays = 0, totalPlays = 0;
        for (const k of Object.keys(a.m)) {
          totalPlays += a.m[k];
          if (SOMATIC_METHODS.has(k)) somaticPlays += a.m[k];
        }
        if (totalPlays >= 5 && (somaticPlays / totalPlays) > 0.4) scores.kinesthetic += 2;
      }
      // Pick the leader if it's clear
      const entries = Object.entries(scores).sort((a, b) => b[1] - a[1]);
      const [winner, wScore] = entries[0];
      const [, runner] = entries[1];
      if (wScore < 2 || (wScore - runner) < 1) return null;
      return winner;
    } catch (e) { return null; }
  }

  /** Cached inference — refreshed asynchronously after each session and at
   *  init. `getProfileContext` is sync (called during prompt assembly), so
   *  it reads from this cache rather than awaiting. Stale by at most one
   *  session, which is fine. */
  state._inferredModality = state._inferredModality || null;
  async function refreshInferredModality() {
    try { state._inferredModality = await inferModality(); }
    catch { state._inferredModality = null; }
  }
  // Refresh once at module load so first session has signal if usage is heavy
  setTimeout(() => { refreshInferredModality().catch(() => {}); }, 1500);

  function getProfileContext() {
    if (state.smartDirectorEnabled === false) {
      sdLog('getProfileContext returning empty — Smart Director off');
      return '';
    }
    const p = getProfile();
    const parts = [];
    // Only pass the subject's name to the AI when the user has opted in.
    if (p.name && p.useFirstName) parts.push(`The subject's name is ${p.name}. You may address them by name occasionally; do not overuse it.`);
    if (p.pronouns) parts.push(`Pronouns: ${p.pronouns}.`);
    // Per-field share toggles — see profile form. Default true so older
    // profiles keep their previous behavior of sharing everything.
    if (p.age && p.shareAge !== false)               parts.push(`Age range: ${p.age}.`);
    if (p.experience)                                parts.push(`Experience: ${p.experience}.`);
    if (p.about)                                     parts.push(`About the subject: ${p.about}.`);
    if (p.appearance && p.shareAppearance !== false) parts.push(`Appearance: ${p.appearance}.`);
    if (p.goals && p.shareGoals !== false)           parts.push(`Goals: ${p.goals}.`);

    // Phase 4: inferred sensory modality. Only surfaced when inference is
    // confident (see inferModality). Wrong guidance is worse than silence.
    if (state._inferredModality) {
      const guidance = {
        visual:       'Lean into visual descriptions (light, color, distance, scenes the user can see in their mind).',
        auditory:     'Lean into auditory descriptions (sounds, rhythms, the texture of the voice itself, named sounds).',
        kinesthetic:  'Lean into bodily sensation (weight, warmth, breath, where in the body the experience lands).',
      }[state._inferredModality];
      if (guidance) parts.push(`Inferred preferred modality: ${state._inferredModality}. ${guidance}`);
    }

    // Recent change signals from the diff-bounce log — gives the director
    // a time-aware view of what the user's been exploring lately. Decay-
    // weighted by age (exp(-days/14)); entries below 0.1 weight drop out.
    // Recent (≤7 days) changes also get a "first session since" callout so
    // the AI explicitly knows this generation reflects a new direction.
    const srcLabel = id => ({ pfGoals:'goals', pfAbout:'about', intentionInput:'intention', userAffirmations:'affirmations', bgPromptInput:'bg-prompt', bgImage:'image-gen' }[id] || id);
    try {
      const recent = (_profileChangeLog || []).slice(0, 10);
      if (recent.length) {
        const now = Date.now();
        const dayMs = 86400000;
        const weighted = recent.map(e => {
          const ageDays = Math.max(0, (now - e.ts) / dayMs);
          const w = Math.exp(-ageDays / 14);
          return { e, ageDays, w };
        }).filter(x => x.w >= 0.1);
        const lines = weighted.map(({ e }) => {
          const rel = _relTimeDB(e.ts);
          if (e.type === 'csv' && e.diff) {
            const add = (e.diff.added || []).slice(0, 3).join(',');
            const rem = (e.diff.removed || []).slice(0, 3).join(',');
            const bits = [];
            if (add) bits.push('+' + add);
            if (rem) bits.push('-' + rem);
            return `${rel} · ${srcLabel(e.source)}: ${bits.join(' ')}`.trim();
          } else if (e.type === 'text' && e.diff) {
            return `${rel} · ${srcLabel(e.source)}: ${Math.round((e.diff.changePct || 0) * 100)}% text change`;
          } else if (e.type === 'image' && e.diff) {
            const tags = (e.diff.tags || []).slice(0, 4).join(',');
            const sum = e.diff.summary || (tags ? `[${tags}]` : '');
            return `${rel} · image: ${sum}`;
          }
          return `${rel} · ${srcLabel(e.source)}`;
        }).filter(Boolean);
        if (lines.length) parts.push('Recent changes: ' + lines.join(' | '));
        // First-session-since callout for very recent meaningful edits.
        // Helps the AI understand this generation reflects a NEW direction.
        const veryRecent = weighted.filter(x => x.ageDays <= 7 && (x.e.type === 'text' || x.e.type === 'csv'));
        if (veryRecent.length) {
          const fields = [...new Set(veryRecent.map(x => srcLabel(x.e.source)))];
          parts.push(`This is the first session since the user updated their ${fields.join(' and ')}; lean into the change rather than echoing prior sessions.`);
        }
      }
    } catch (e) { /* never break context assembly */ }

    const result = parts.length > 0 ? '\nSUBJECT PROFILE: ' + parts.join(' ') : '';
    sdLog('getProfileContext built', { fieldCount: parts.length, charCount: result.length });
    return result;
  }

  // ====================================================================
  // FRESHNESS INJECTION — combinatorial anchor phrases via Perchance DSL
  // ====================================================================
  // Breaks AI mode-collapse by injecting a small set of profile-filtered
  // sensory anchors into sharedContext on each script generation. The DSL
  // picker is stateless; rotation memory (last-used phrases) lives here.
  // ====================================================================
  const FRESHNESS_COUNT = 4;              // phrases per script generation
  const FRESHNESS_MIN_TAGS = 0;           // min profile tags required (0 = always)
  const FRESHNESS_ROTATION_CAP = 30;      // max "recent" entries remembered
  let _recentAnchors = [];                // FIFO buffer of recently-picked phrases

  function deriveProfileTags() {
    const tags = new Set();
    const p = getProfile();
    // Experience → pacing tag
    if (p.experience === 'beginner') tags.add('slow');
    if (p.experience === 'advanced') tags.add('dense');
    // Goals text → simple keyword map (deliberately broad)
    const goals = (p.goals || '').toLowerCase();
    if (/(sleep|insomnia|bedtime)/.test(goals)) tags.add('sleep');
    if (/(calm|relax|anxiety|stress)/.test(goals)) tags.add('calm');
    if (/(focus|concentration|productivity)/.test(goals)) tags.add('focus');
    if (/(creative|art|write)/.test(goals)) tags.add('creative');
    if (/(confidence|self)/.test(goals)) tags.add('confidence');
    // Intention → imagery tag (intention text comes from the intention input)
    const intention = ($('intentionInput')?.value || '').toLowerCase();
    if (/(water|ocean|river|rain)/.test(intention)) tags.add('water');
    if (/(warm|sun|fire|glow)/.test(intention)) tags.add('warm');
    if (/(cool|cold|ice|winter)/.test(intention)) tags.add('cool');
    // Aesthetic preferences from the diff-bounce image log, if populated
    try {
      const imgEntries = (_profileChangeLog || []).filter(e => e.source === 'bgImage').slice(0, 5);
      imgEntries.forEach(e => (e.diff?.tags || []).forEach(t => tags.add(t)));
    } catch {}
    return [...tags];
  }

  function buildFreshnessBlock() {
    if (state.smartDirectorEnabled === false) return '';
    if (typeof root === 'undefined' || !root.freshSeeds || typeof root.freshSeeds.pick !== 'function') return '';
    try {
      const tags = deriveProfileTags();
      if (tags.length < FRESHNESS_MIN_TAGS) return '';
      const phrases = root.freshSeeds.pick({
        profileTags: tags,
        n: FRESHNESS_COUNT,
        excludeSet: _recentAnchors,
      });
      if (!phrases || !phrases.length) return '';
      // Update rotation state (FIFO cap)
      _recentAnchors.push(...phrases);
      if (_recentAnchors.length > FRESHNESS_ROTATION_CAP) {
        _recentAnchors = _recentAnchors.slice(-FRESHNESS_ROTATION_CAP);
      }
      sdLog('freshness block built', { count: phrases.length, tags });
      return `\nOPTIONAL ANCHORS (use only if they fit the moment — ignore otherwise):\n${phrases.map(p => '  • ' + p).join('\n')}`;
    } catch (e) {
      console.warn('[freshSeeds] pick failed:', e);
      return '';
    }
  }

  // ====================================================================
  // LIVING PROFILE + DIRECTOR BRIEF
  // ====================================================================
  // Merges profile + memory + adaptive + history into one on-demand view, then
  // runs rule-based logic over it to produce a session brief. No new persistence
  // layer — source stores own their data.
  //
  // IMPORTANT: director logic stays RULE-BASED. AI-inferred clinical judgment is
  // a slippery slope this app should not take.

  /**
   * Build a unified "living profile" by merging user-edited profile + AI-observed
   * memory summaries + adaptive signals + recent session snapshot.
   *
   * Pure read — does not write anywhere. Returns `null` when Smart Director is
   * off OR when any underlying read fails.
   *
   * @returns {Promise<object|null>}
   */
  async function buildLivingProfile() {
    if (state.smartDirectorEnabled === false) {
      sdLog('buildLivingProfile skipped — Smart Director off');
      return null;
    }
    try {
      const stated = getProfile();
      const memory = await getMemory();
      const adaptive = await getAdaptive();
      const history = ((await idbGet('stillness_history_v1')) || []).map(normalizeHistoryEntry);
      const analytics = await getAnalytics();

      // Recent mood trajectory — last 5 completed sessions with both pre+post scores.
      // Index 0 = most recent. A negative value = user felt worse after the session.
      const moodSessions = history.filter(s => s.completed && s.preMood != null && s.postMood != null).slice(0, 5);
      const recentMoodDeltas = moodSessions.map(s => s.postMood - s.preMood);
      const avgRecentMoodDelta = recentMoodDeltas.length > 0
        ? recentMoodDeltas.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / recentMoodDeltas.length
        : null;

      // Completion rate over last 10 — captures "bailing" patterns earlier than
      // the lifetime rate from analytics.comp which dilutes with history.
      const recent10 = history.slice(0, 10);
      const completionRate = recent10.length > 0
        ? recent10.filter(s => s.completed).length / recent10.length
        : null;

      // Average self-rated depth in recent sessions — helps calibrate next session.
      const recentDepths = history.slice(0, 5).map(s => s.depth).filter(d => d != null);
      const avgRecentDepth = recentDepths.length > 0
        ? recentDepths.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / recentDepths.length
        : null;

      // Top preference signals from analytics (what the user reaches for most).
      const topKey = (map) => {
        const entries = Object.entries(map || {});
        if (!entries.length) return null;
        return entries.sort((a, b) => b[1] - a[1])[0][0];
      };

      const living = {
        version: 1,
        updatedAt: Date.now(),
        stated: {
          name: stated.name,
          useFirstName: stated.useFirstName,
          pronouns: stated.pronouns,
          age: stated.age,
          experience: stated.experience,
          about: stated.about,
          appearance: stated.appearance,
          goals: stated.goals,
        },
        observed: {
          longTermProfile: memory.longTermProfile || '',
          rollingPatterns: memory.rollingWindow || '',
          digestCount: memory.sessionDigests?.length || 0,
        },
        adaptive: {
          traits: adaptive.traits || {},
          difficulty: adaptive.difficulty || {},
          memorySummary: adaptive.memorySummary || '',
          topPersona: topKey(analytics.p),
          topMethod: topKey(analytics.m),
          topIntention: topKey(analytics.i),
        },
        snapshot: {
          sessionCount: history.length,
          completedCount: history.filter(s => s.completed).length,
          emergencyExitCount: analytics.comp?.emergency || 0,
          completionRate,
          recentMoodDeltas,
          avgRecentMoodDelta,
          avgRecentDepth,
          lastSessionAt: history[0]?.date || null,
          daysSinceLast: history[0]?.date
            ? Math.floor((Date.now() - history[0].date) / 86400000)
            : null,
        },
      };

      sdLog('buildLivingProfile', living);
      return living;
    } catch (e) {
      console.warn('buildLivingProfile failed:', e);
      return null;
    }
  }

  /**
   * Compute a structured Director Brief from the living profile. Rule-based —
   * every recommendation includes a plain-English reason that can be surfaced
   * to the user (Pass 3).
   *
   * Cold-start (fewer than 3 completed sessions): returns a brief with
   * `coldStart: true` and no active recommendations, so the AI falls back to
   * defaults rather than pretending expertise the app hasn't earned.
   *
   * @param [object|null] living     output of buildLivingProfile()
   * @param [object]       session   { persona, method, intention, length }
   * @returns [object|null]
   */
  function computeDirectorBrief(living, session = {}) {
    if (!living) return null;
    const snap = living.snapshot;
    // User-adjustable bias, -3..+3. Negative = lean cautious (easier triggers,
    // stricter caps); positive = lean forward (harder triggers, looser caps).
    // Per-domain offsets allow per-intentionType tuning (e.g. cautious for
    // sleep, forward for skill-building) layered on top of the global bias.
    // Clamped defensively in case the setting is stale or out of range.
    const globalBias = Math.max(-3, Math.min(3, Math.round(Number(state.directorBias) || 0)));
    const domain = (session.intention && state.intentionType) || state.intentionType || null;
    const domainOffset = domain && state.directorBiasByDomain
      ? Math.round(Number(state.directorBiasByDomain[domain]) || 0)
      : 0;
    const bias = Math.max(-3, Math.min(3, globalBias + domainOffset));
    const brief = {
      generatedAt: Date.now(),
      bias,
      forSession: {
        persona: session.persona || null,
        method: session.method || null,
        intention: session.intention || null,
        length: session.length || null,
      },
      coldStart: false,
      recoveryMode: false,
      recommendations: {}, // { suggestedDepth, suggestedLength, skipIntensification, maxDepth, preferBreath }
      warnings: [],        // user-facing flags
      reasoning: [],       // plain-English rationale (one entry per rule that fired)
    };

    // Bias-adjusted thresholds. The math is deliberately simple — each step
    // moves the threshold by a human-meaningful unit, so moving one notch
    // produces an observable shift without flipping the app's personality.
    const coldStartLimit     = Math.max(0, 3 - bias);           // B=-3: 6, 0: 3, +3: 0 (no cold start)
    const recoveryMoodLimit  = -2 - bias * 0.5;                  // B=-3: -0.5 (easy), 0: -2, +3: -3.5 (hard)
    const completionFloor    = 0.5 - bias * 0.05;                // B=-3: 0.65, 0: 0.5, +3: 0.35
    const daysSinceRentry    = 14 + bias * 3;                    // B=-3: 5, 0: 14, +3: 23
    const positivePacingFloor = 1.5 - bias * 0.3;                // B=-3: 2.4 (harder to praise), +3: 0.6 (easier)
