        const url = await generatePersonaPortrait(p);
        if (btn) { btn.disabled = false; btn.textContent = url ? '🔄 regenerate portrait' : '🎨 generate portrait'; }
        if (url) _setPortraitImage(url, p.id);
      });
    }
    // Wire the chat-with-guide affordances (Phase 3)
    _wirePersonaChat(p);
  }

  /** Look up cached portrait for a persona and inject it into the detail panel
   *  if found. Does nothing if no portrait is cached or if the panel isn't
   *  showing this persona anymore (e.g. user clicked another persona during load). */
  async function _hydratePersonaPortrait(personaId) {
    const url = await personaPortraitGet(personaId);
    if (!url) return;
    _setPortraitImage(url, personaId);
    // Update the button label to reflect that a portrait already exists
    const btn = $('generatePortraitBtn');
    if (btn) btn.textContent = '🔄 regenerate portrait';
  }

  /** Replace the placeholder in #personaPortraitBox with an <img> for the URL.
   *  Skips the swap if the box now belongs to a different persona (race guard). */
  function _setPortraitImage(url, expectedPersonaId) {
    const box = $('personaPortraitBox');
    if (!box) return;
    if (expectedPersonaId && box.dataset.personaId !== expectedPersonaId) {
      // Persona changed during async generation — revoke and bail
      URL.revokeObjectURL(url);
      return;
    }
    box.innerHTML = `<img src="${url}" alt="portrait" style="width:100%;height:100%;object-fit:cover">`;
  }

  // ====================================================================
  // CHAT-WITH-GUIDE (Phase 3) — try a persona before committing
  // ====================================================================
  // Lets users have a short conversation with each persona to get a feel
  // for voice and style before locking in a Step-2 commitment. Per persona
  // history is ephemeral — kept only in memory, cleared on persona switch
  // or step navigation. Hard cap on turns to bound token spend.
  const PERSONA_CHAT_MAX_TURNS = 6; // user/persona exchanges before input disables
  const _personaChats = {}; // personaId → [{role: 'user'|'persona', text}]

  /** Suggested starter prompts shown as chips when chat is empty. The chip
   *  text becomes the user's message verbatim. Self persona gets reflective
   *  prompts that lean into the mirror dynamic. */
  function _personaChatStarters(persona) {
    if (persona && persona.isSelf) {
      return [
        "what's actually been on my mind?",
        'what am I avoiding right now?',
        'what would I tell myself if I really listened?',
        "what's the truest thing I haven't said yet?",
      ];
    }
    return [
      'how would you start a session with me?',
      "what's your style?",
      "what makes you different from other guides?",
      'tell me what to expect.',
    ];
  }

  /** Build the AI instruction for a chat turn. Uses Perchance's canonical
   *  chat-completion format from §2.1 of the perchance-api skill.
   *  If `directive` is non-empty it's a transient steer for THIS reply only —
   *  appended to the persona role, then dropped. */
  function _personaChatInstruction(persona, history, userMsg, directive = '') {
    const role = getPersonaRole(persona) || `You are ${persona.name}, a hypnosis guide.`;
    const isSelf = !!persona.isSelf;
    // Chat-format messages block — model continues from the trailing [[Persona]]:
    const messages = history.concat([{ role: 'user', text: userMsg }])
      .map(m => m.role === 'user'
        ? `[[User]]: ${m.text}`
        : `[[${persona.name}]]: ${m.text}`)
      .join('\n');
    // Different instruction for the Self mirror: not a "preview chat with a
    // guide" — it's a conversation with one's own inner voice. The AI is
    // the user's reflection, speaking honestly back at them.
    const instructionTask = isSelf
      ? `You are speaking as the user themself — their inner voice, reflected back. The "user" in the [[User]] turns IS you; the [[${persona.name}]] turns are your own deeper / quieter / honest voice replying. First-person throughout. Don't perform wisdom — be honest, be uncertain where uncertainty lives. 1-3 sentences per reply.`
      : `You are ${persona.name}. The user is considering choosing you as their hypnosis guide for a session, and is asking you questions to get a feel for your voice and style. This is a SHORT preview chat, NOT a hypnosis session — do not induce trance, do not give a script. Reply in your own voice as defined above. Keep replies brief (1-3 sentences). Stay fully in character — your voice, vocabulary, and cadence are the answer.`;
    // Optional one-shot steering directive (from [System: …] or a Pass-B
    // slash command). Inserted near the very end with high salience so the
    // model honors it; dropped after this single reply.
    const directiveBlock = directive
      ? `\n\n>>> ONE-OFF STEERING (apply to this reply only, then return to normal voice): ${directive}`
      : '';
    return `<ROLE>
${role}
</ROLE>

${instructionTask}${directiveBlock}

<MESSAGES>
${messages}
</MESSAGES>

>>> TASK: Write the next message from ${persona.name}, in voice. Keep it short.`;
  }

  /** Set up event listeners for the chat panel. Called from renderPersonaDetail
   *  every time the panel is re-rendered (which is every persona switch). */
  function _wirePersonaChat(persona) {
    if (!persona) return;
    const toggleBtn = $('personaChatToggleBtn');
    const panel = $('personaChatPanel');
    const closeBtn = $('personaChatCloseBtn');
    const transcript = $('personaChatTranscript');
    const suggestions = $('personaChatSuggestions');
    const input = $('personaChatInput');
    const sendBtn = $('personaChatSendBtn');
    const commitBtn = $('personaChatCommitBtn');
    const status = $('personaChatStatus');
    if (!panel || !transcript) return;

    const renderTranscript = () => {
      const history = _personaChats[persona.id] || [];
      if (!history.length) {
        transcript.innerHTML = `<div class="pc-empty">Say hello, or pick a starter below.</div>`;
        suggestions.style.display = '';
      } else {
        transcript.innerHTML = history.map(m =>
          m.role === 'user'
            ? `<div class="pc-msg user">${escapeHtml(m.text)}</div>`
            : `<div class="pc-msg persona"><span class="pc-msg-name">${escapeHtml(persona.name)}</span>${escapeHtml(m.text)}</div>`
        ).join('');
        // Hide starters once the conversation has begun
        suggestions.style.display = history.length === 0 ? '' : 'none';
        transcript.scrollTop = transcript.scrollHeight;
      }
      // Update turn-cap status
      const turns = history.filter(m => m.role === 'user').length;
      const remaining = PERSONA_CHAT_MAX_TURNS - turns;
      if (remaining <= 0) {
        if (status) status.textContent = `Preview cap reached — pick this guide if it's a fit.`;
        if (input) { input.disabled = true; input.placeholder = 'preview cap reached'; }
        if (sendBtn) sendBtn.disabled = true;
      } else if (turns > 0) {
        if (status) status.textContent = `${remaining} more question${remaining === 1 ? '' : 's'} in this preview.`;
      } else {
        if (status) status.textContent = `Ephemeral — clears when you switch guides or change steps.`;
      }
    };

    const renderSuggestions = () => {
      const starters = _personaChatStarters(persona);
      suggestions.innerHTML = starters.map(s =>
        `<button type="button" class="pc-suggest">${escapeHtml(s)}</button>`
      ).join('');
      suggestions.querySelectorAll('.pc-suggest').forEach(b => {
        b.addEventListener('click', () => {
          input.value = b.textContent;
          sendChat();
        });
      });
    };

    /** Parse the chat input and decide what KIND of turn it is.
     *    [System: be more direct]    → { kind: 'system', directive }
     *    /regen, /clear, /help, …    → { kind: 'command', cmd, args }
     *    anything else               → { kind: 'message', text }
     *  Recognized self-only commands: /honestly, /question, /show */
    const parseChatInput = (raw) => {
      const t = String(raw || '').trim();
      if (!t) return { kind: 'empty' };
      const sys = t.match(/^\[\s*(?:system|sys)\s*:\s*([\s\S]+?)\s*\]\s*$/i);
      if (sys) return { kind: 'system', directive: sys[1].trim() };
      const slash = t.match(/^\/([a-z]+)(?:\s+([\s\S]+))?$/i);
      if (slash) return { kind: 'command', cmd: slash[1].toLowerCase(), args: (slash[2] || '').trim() };
      return { kind: 'message', text: t };
    };

    /** Render a one-off system bubble inline in the transcript. Optionally
     *  styled as an error. Used for slash-command results and steering acks. */
    const renderSystemMessage = (text, opts = {}) => {
      const empty = transcript.querySelector('.pc-empty');
      if (empty) empty.remove();
      const el = document.createElement('div');
      el.className = 'pc-msg system' + (opts.error ? ' error' : '');
      el.innerHTML = `<span class="pc-msg-tag">${escapeHtml(opts.tag || 'system')}</span>${escapeHtml(text)}`;
      transcript.appendChild(el);
      transcript.scrollTop = transcript.scrollHeight;
    };

    /** Build the help card content based on whether this is the Self persona. */
    const renderHelpCard = () => {
      const empty = transcript.querySelector('.pc-empty');
      if (empty) empty.remove();
      const isSelf = !!persona.isSelf;
      const card = document.createElement('div');
      card.className = 'pc-help-card';
      const baseRows = [
        ['<code>[System: …]</code>', 'one-off steering for the next reply only — try <code>[System: be more direct]</code> or <code>[System: shorter, 1 sentence]</code>'],
        ['<code>/regen</code>', 'redo the last reply'],
        ['<code>/clear</code>', "clear this guide's chat history"],
        ['<code>/help</code>', 'show this card'],
      ];
      const selfRows = isSelf ? [
        ['<code>/honestly</code>', 'next reply — strip the wisdom-performing layer; raw is fine'],
        ['<code>/question</code>', 'instead of reflecting, have the mirror ask YOU a question'],
        ['<code>/show</code>', 'dump what the AI is being told about you (debug)'],
      ] : [];
      card.innerHTML = `<button class="pc-help-close" aria-label="close help">✕</button>
        <h4>commands</h4>
        ${baseRows.concat(selfRows).map(([k, v]) => `<div class="pc-help-row"><span>${k}</span><span>${v}</span></div>`).join('')}`;
      transcript.appendChild(card);
      transcript.scrollTop = transcript.scrollHeight;
      card.querySelector('.pc-help-close')?.addEventListener('click', () => card.remove());
    };

    let _streaming = false;
    let _pendingDirective = ''; // consumed by the next AI call, then cleared
    /** Core AI-reply path — used by normal messages, /regen, and Pass-B
     *  command-driven sends. Pulls _pendingDirective into the instruction
     *  builder for one-shot steering. */
    const sendToAI = async (userText) => {
      if (_streaming) return;
      _streaming = true;
      sendBtn.disabled = true;
      const placeholder = document.createElement('div');
      placeholder.className = 'pc-msg persona streaming';
      placeholder.innerHTML = `<span class="pc-msg-name">${escapeHtml(persona.name)}</span><span class="pc-msg-body"></span>`;
      transcript.appendChild(placeholder);
      transcript.scrollTop = transcript.scrollHeight;
      const bodyEl = placeholder.querySelector('.pc-msg-body');
      const directive = _pendingDirective; // capture, then clear (one-shot)
      _pendingDirective = '';
      try {
        // History excludes the just-pushed user message AND any system bubbles
        // (those are display-only; they don't go into the chat-format payload).
        const aiHistory = (_personaChats[persona.id] || []).filter(m => m.role === 'user' || m.role === 'persona');
        const instruction = _personaChatInstruction(persona, aiHistory.slice(0, -1), userText, directive);
        let acc = '';
        if (typeof root !== 'undefined' && root.aiTextPlugin) {
          const startWith = `[[${persona.name}]]:`;
          const streamObj = root.aiTextPlugin({
            instruction,
            startWith,
            hideStartWith: true,
            stopSequences: ['\n[[User]]:', '\n\n[[', '\n[[', '\n\n\n'],
            onChunk: (data) => {
              if (data.isFromStartWith) return;
              const chunk = data.textChunk || '';
              if (!chunk) return;
              acc += chunk;
              const cleaned = acc.replace(/^\s*\[\[[^\]]+\]\]:\s*/, '').trim();
              bodyEl.textContent = cleaned;
              transcript.scrollTop = transcript.scrollHeight;
            },
          });
          const data = await streamObj;
          if (data?.stopReason === 'error' && !acc) throw new Error('plugin error');
        } else {
          const data = await aiGenerate(instruction, { stopSequences: ['\n[[User]]:'] });
          acc = (data?.generatedText || '').trim();
          bodyEl.textContent = acc.replace(/^\s*\[\[[^\]]+\]\]:\s*/, '').trim();
        }
        let finalText = (acc || '').replace(/^\s*\[\[[^\]]+\]\]:\s*/, '').trim();
        if (!finalText) throw new Error('empty reply');
        placeholder.classList.remove('streaming');
        bodyEl.textContent = finalText;
        _personaChats[persona.id].push({ role: 'persona', text: finalText });
        renderTranscript();
      } catch (e) {
        placeholder.classList.remove('streaming');
        bodyEl.textContent = `(${persona.name} couldn't respond — try again in a moment)`;
        bodyEl.style.color = 'var(--text-faint)';
        bodyEl.style.fontStyle = 'italic';
      } finally {
        _streaming = false;
        sendBtn.disabled = false;
      }
    };

    const sendChat = async () => {
      if (_streaming) return;
      const raw = input.value || '';
      input.value = '';
      // Auto-shrink textarea after clearing
      input.style.height = 'auto';
      const parsed = parseChatInput(raw);
      if (parsed.kind === 'empty') return;
      const history = _personaChats[persona.id] = _personaChats[persona.id] || [];

      // Slash command → handled locally, does NOT count against the turn cap
      if (parsed.kind === 'command') {
        const handled = await handleSlashCommand(parsed.cmd, parsed.args);
        if (!handled) renderSystemMessage(`unknown command: /${parsed.cmd}. type /help for a list.`, { error: true });
        return;
      }

      // System directive → queued for the next AI call, shown faded
      if (parsed.kind === 'system') {
        _pendingDirective = parsed.directive;
        renderSystemMessage(parsed.directive, { tag: 'steering' });
        return;
      }

      // Regular message → respect the turn cap and send to AI
      if (history.filter(m => m.role === 'user').length >= PERSONA_CHAT_MAX_TURNS) return;
      history.push({ role: 'user', text: parsed.text });
      renderTranscript();
      await sendToAI(parsed.text);
    };

    /** Slash command dispatch. Returns true if the command was recognized
     *  (even if it errored), false if unknown. Self-persona-only commands
     *  are gated by persona.isSelf. */
    const handleSlashCommand = async (cmd, args) => {
      const isSelf = !!persona.isSelf;
      const history = _personaChats[persona.id] = _personaChats[persona.id] || [];
      switch (cmd) {
        case 'help':
        case '?':
          renderHelpCard();
          return true;

        case 'clear': {
          _personaChats[persona.id] = [];
          renderTranscript();
          renderSystemMessage('chat cleared.');
          return true;
        }

        case 'regen': {
          // Find the last user message; drop any persona reply that came after it,
          // then re-send. If there's no prior user message, complain.
          let lastUserIdx = -1;
          for (let i = history.length - 1; i >= 0; i--) {
            if (history[i].role === 'user') { lastUserIdx = i; break; }
          }
          if (lastUserIdx < 0) {
            renderSystemMessage('nothing to regenerate yet.', { error: true });
            return true;
          }
          const lastUserText = history[lastUserIdx].text;
          // Trim history to just before the persona reply we want to redo
          _personaChats[persona.id] = history.slice(0, lastUserIdx + 1);
          renderTranscript();
          renderSystemMessage('regenerating…');
          await sendToAI(lastUserText);
          return true;
        }

        // ── Pass B: Self-persona-only commands ─────────────────────────────
        case 'honestly': {
          if (!isSelf) {
            renderSystemMessage('/honestly only works with the Self persona (debug mode).', { error: true });
            return true;
          }
          // Same as a steering directive, but command-formatted.
          _pendingDirective = 'Strip away any wisdom-performing layer. Be raw, even if rough. Speak the way someone actually talks to themselves at 2am, not the way a guide speaks to a client. Imperfect honesty over polished insight.';
          renderSystemMessage('next reply will be unvarnished. send a message to continue.', { tag: 'steering' });
          return true;
        }

        case 'question': {
          if (!isSelf) {
            renderSystemMessage('/question only works with the Self persona (debug mode).', { error: true });
            return true;
          }
          _pendingDirective = 'Instead of reflecting back, ask the user (yourself) ONE question that cuts to what is actually going on. Just the question — no preamble, no setup, no answer. The kind of question that you would only ask yourself if you were really paying attention.';
          renderSystemMessage('next reply will be a question instead of a reflection.', { tag: 'steering' });
          return true;
        }

        case 'show': {
          if (!isSelf) {
            renderSystemMessage('/show only works with the Self persona (debug mode).', { error: true });
            return true;
          }
          // Compose the same instruction the AI would receive (with no user
          // message — they haven't sent one yet) and dump it as a system msg.
          const aiHistory = history.filter(m => m.role === 'user' || m.role === 'persona');
          const sample = _personaChatInstruction(persona, aiHistory, '<your next message would go here>', _pendingDirective);
          // Render as a <pre>-style system bubble so whitespace + line breaks stay
          const empty = transcript.querySelector('.pc-empty');
          if (empty) empty.remove();
          const el = document.createElement('div');
          el.className = 'pc-msg system';
          el.innerHTML = `<span class="pc-msg-tag">debug · payload</span><pre style="white-space:pre-wrap;font-family:var(--font-body);font-size:0.74rem;line-height:1.45;margin:6px 0 0;color:var(--text);max-height:280px;overflow:auto">${escapeHtml(sample)}</pre>`;
          transcript.appendChild(el);
          transcript.scrollTop = transcript.scrollHeight;
          return true;
        }
      }
      return false; // unknown command
    };

    if (toggleBtn) {
      toggleBtn.addEventListener('click', () => {
        const isOpen = panel.classList.contains('visible');
        if (isOpen) {
          panel.classList.remove('visible');
        } else {
          panel.classList.add('visible');
          renderSuggestions();
          renderTranscript();
          setTimeout(() => input?.focus(), 50);
        }
      });
    }
    if (closeBtn) closeBtn.addEventListener('click', () => panel.classList.remove('visible'));
    const helpBtn = $('personaChatHelpBtn');
    if (helpBtn) helpBtn.addEventListener('click', renderHelpCard);
    if (sendBtn) sendBtn.addEventListener('click', sendChat);
    if (input) {
      input.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => {
        // Enter sends; Shift+Enter inserts newline
        if (e.key === 'Enter' && !e.shiftKey) {
          e.preventDefault();
          sendChat();
        }
      });
      // Auto-grow textarea up to max-height
      input.addEventListener('input', () => {
        input.style.height = 'auto';
        input.style.height = Math.min(input.scrollHeight, 120) + 'px';
      });
    }
    if (commitBtn) {
      commitBtn.addEventListener('click', () => {
        // Persona is already selected (user is viewing its detail); commit
        // means advance to step 2 with the chat history cleared.
        clearAllPersonaChats();
        notify.success(`${persona.name} is your guide. Choose a method →`);
        goToStep(2);
      });
    }
  }

  /** Clear ephemeral chat history for ALL personas. Called when leaving
   *  Step 1 (via goToStep), since the chats are explicitly preview-only. */
  function clearAllPersonaChats() {
    for (const k of Object.keys(_personaChats)) delete _personaChats[k];
  }

  function renderMethodGrid() {
    const grid = $('methodGrid');
    grid.innerHTML = '';
    const filtered = state.methodFilter === 'all' ? METHODS : METHODS.filter(m => m.category === state.methodFilter);
    const lockState = state._methodLockState; // populated by applyMethodGating
    filtered.forEach(m => {
      const card = document.createElement('div');
      const lockReason = lockState?.reasonFor?.(m.category) || '';
      const isLocked = !!lockReason;
      card.className = `card category-${m.category}` + (m.id === state.selectedMethodId ? ' selected' : '') + (isLocked ? ' locked' : '');
      card.dataset.methodId = m.id;
      if (isLocked) card.title = lockReason;
      const lockBadge = isLocked ? `<span class="card-badge" style="background:transparent;border:1px solid var(--text-faint);color:var(--text-faint)">🔒 locked</span>` : '';
      // Usage badge — same pattern as persona cards. Skipped if locked (lock
      // badge already occupies the inline slot).
      const ms = state._pickerStats?.method?.[m.id];
      let usageBadge = '';
      if (!isLocked && ms && ms.total >= 2) {
        const compPct = Math.round((ms.completed / ms.total) * 100);
        const moodPart = ms.deltaN >= 2
          ? ` · Δ${ms.deltaSum / ms.deltaN >= 0 ? '+' : ''}${(ms.deltaSum / ms.deltaN).toFixed(1)}`
          : '';
        usageBadge = `<div class="card-usage-badge" style="position:absolute;bottom:6px;right:8px;font-size:0.58rem;letter-spacing:0.04em;color:var(--text-faint);font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums" title="Your history with ${escapeHtml(m.name)}: ${ms.total} session${ms.total === 1 ? '' : 's'}, ${compPct}% completion${ms.deltaN >= 2 ? `, average mood Δ ${ms.deltaSum / ms.deltaN >= 0 ? '+' : ''}${(ms.deltaSum / ms.deltaN).toFixed(1)}` : ''}">${ms.total}× · ${compPct}%${moodPart}</div>`;
      }
      card.style.position = 'relative';
      card.innerHTML = `${usageBadge}<div class="card-category-label">${escapeHtml(m.category)}</div><div class="card-name">${escapeHtml(m.name)}${m.recommended ? `<span class="card-badge">recommended</span>` : ''}${lockBadge}</div><div class="card-tagline">${escapeHtml(m.tagline)}</div><div class="card-tags">${m.tags.map(t => `<span class="card-tag">${escapeHtml(t)}</span>`).join('')}</div>`;
      card.addEventListener('click', () => {
        if (isLocked) {
          notify.info(lockReason);
          return;
        }
        state.selectedMethodId = m.id;
        saveUserSettings();
        // Update soundscape default when method changes — but only if the user
        // hasn't explicitly customized it. Otherwise switching methods would silently
        // overwrite their chosen soundscape.
        if (!state._soundscapeOverridden) {
          state.soundscape = m.drone ? 'drone' : 'none';
          state.droneEnabled = m.drone;
          const ssSelect = $('soundscapeSelect');
          if (ssSelect) ssSelect.value = state.soundscape;
        }
        renderMethodGrid(); renderMethodDetail(); hideScript(); updateStepUI();
      });
      grid.appendChild(card);
    });
    // Phase 4: time-of-day badge on the method most-used at this hour
    _applyTimeOfDayBadge('method');
  }
  function renderMethodDetail() {
    const m = getMethod();
    const el = $('methodDetail');
    if (!m) { el.innerHTML = ''; return; }
    el.innerHTML = `<div class="detail-title">${escapeHtml(m.name)}</div><div>${escapeHtml(m.detail)}</div>`;
  }
  function updateIntentionHint() {
    const type = $('intentionType').value;
    state.intentionType = type;
    const typeData = INTENTION_TYPES[type];
    $('intentionTypeHint').textContent = typeData ? typeData.hint : '';
    hideCrafted();

    // Types that skip crafting — hide the craft button and intention input
    const skipCraft = ['trance', 'sleep'].includes(type);
    const craftBtn = $('craftBtn');
    const intentionInput = $('intentionInput');
    const intentionLabel = intentionInput?.previousElementSibling;

    if (skipCraft) {
      if (craftBtn) craftBtn.style.display = 'none';
      if (craftBtn?.nextElementSibling) craftBtn.nextElementSibling.style.display = 'none'; // sublabel
      if (intentionInput) intentionInput.style.display = 'none';
      if (intentionLabel) intentionLabel.style.display = 'none';
      showEl('affirmationGroup', false);
    } else {
      if (craftBtn) craftBtn.style.display = '';
      if (craftBtn?.nextElementSibling) craftBtn.nextElementSibling.style.display = '';
      if (intentionInput) intentionInput.style.display = '';
      if (intentionLabel) intentionLabel.style.display = '';
      showEl('affirmationGroup', true);
    }

    updateStepUI();
  }
  function updateBriefPreview() {
    const p = getPersona(); const m = getMethod();
    const rawIntent = $('intentionInput').value.trim();
    const intentFallback = state.intentionType === 'sleep' ? '[sleep induction]'
      : state.intentionType === 'trance' ? '[pure trance]'
      : '[no theme]';
    const intention = rawIntent || intentFallback;
    const el = $('briefPreview');
    if (!p) { el.textContent = ''; return; }
    const action = m.category === 'demonstration' ? `demonstrate the <strong>${escapeHtml(m.name)}</strong> effect` : `use the <strong>${escapeHtml(m.name)}</strong> method`;
    el.innerHTML = `<strong>Hey ${escapeHtml(p.name)}</strong> — ${action} for a <strong>${escapeHtml(state.length)}</strong> session on the theme: <strong>"${escapeHtml(intention)}"</strong>${state.craftedSuggestion ? ' (with the crafted suggestion embedded)' : ''}. Write the full script.`;
  }

  // ====================================================================
  // CONFIGURE PANEL (Step 5) — dynamic context rendering
  // ====================================================================
  function renderConfigSummary() {
    const p = getPersona(), m = getMethod();
    const el = $('configSummary');
    if (!el || !p || !m) return;

    // Category badges
    const catColors = {
      contemplative: 'var(--accent)', showman: 'var(--accent-warm)',
      somatic: 'var(--accent-green)', authoritarian: '#d46a6a',
      therapeutic: '#8a9aaa', demonstration: 'var(--accent-purple)',
      rapid: '#d4a74a', advanced: '#e06888',
    };
    const pColor = catColors[p.category] || 'var(--text-dim)';
    const mColor = catColors[m.category] || 'var(--text-dim)';

    el.innerHTML = `
      <div style="display:flex;gap:12px;align-items:flex-start;flex-wrap:wrap">
        <div style="flex:1;min-width:140px">
          <div style="font-size:0.62rem;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:0.1em;color:var(--text-faint);margin-bottom:4px">guide</div>
          <div style="font-size:0.92rem;color:var(--text);font-weight:500">${escapeHtml(p.name)}</div>
          <div style="font-size:0.68rem;color:${pColor};margin-top:2px">${escapeHtml(p.category)}</div>
        </div>
        <div style="flex:1;min-width:140px">
          <div style="font-size:0.62rem;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:0.1em;color:var(--text-faint);margin-bottom:4px">method</div>
          <div style="font-size:0.92rem;color:var(--text);font-weight:500">${escapeHtml(m.name)}</div>
          <div style="font-size:0.68rem;color:${mColor};margin-top:2px">${escapeHtml(m.category)}</div>
        </div>
      </div>`;

    // Auto-select recommended soundscape based on method
    const ssSelect = $('soundscapeSelect');
    const ssHint = $('soundscapeHint');
    if (ssSelect && ssHint) {
      // Recommend a soundscape based on method
      const recommendations = {
        highway: 'drone', spiral: 'drone', confusion: 'drone',
        'breath-478': 'none', progressive: 'ocean', ericksonian: 'rain',
        'heartbeat-sync': 'heartbeat', 'floating-body': 'binaural',
        'time-distortion': 'binaural', somnambulism: 'binaural',
        'double-induction': 'brown',
      };
      const rec = recommendations[m.id];
      // Only apply the recommendation if the user hasn't customized the soundscape.
      // Otherwise coming back to Configure would silently overwrite their choice.
      if (rec && ssSelect.value === state.soundscape && !state._soundscapeOverridden) {
        ssSelect.value = rec; state.soundscape = rec; state.droneEnabled = rec !== 'none';
      }
      if (m.drone) ssHint.textContent = `Recommended for ${escapeHtml(m.name)}. Procedurally generated — never loops.`;
      else ssHint.textContent = 'Optional. Procedurally generated — never loops. Use headphones for binaural.';
    }

    // Speed hint based on method category
    const speedHint = $('speechRateHint');
    if (speedHint) {
      if (m.category === 'rapid') speedHint.textContent = 'Rapid methods work well at normal or slightly faster speed.';
      else if (m.category === 'advanced') speedHint.textContent = 'Advanced methods benefit from a slow, deliberate pace.';
      else if (p.category === 'authoritarian') speedHint.textContent = 'Authoritarian guides often work well at a slightly faster pace.';
      else speedHint.textContent = 'Slower is more hypnotic.';
    }

    // Length recommendation hint
    const lengthHint = $('lengthHint');
    if (lengthHint) {
      if (m.category === 'rapid') lengthHint.textContent = 'Rapid methods are designed to be short. "Short" is usually plenty.';
      else if (m.category === 'advanced') lengthHint.textContent = 'Advanced methods benefit from longer sessions to build depth.';
      else if (state.intentionType === 'sleep') lengthHint.textContent = 'Sleep sessions work best at medium or long — give yourself time to drift off.';
      else lengthHint.textContent = '';
    }

    // Warnings for specific combinations
    const warnEl = $('configWarning');
    if (warnEl) {
      const warnings = [];
      if (m.category === 'advanced' && state.length === 'short') {
        warnings.push('Advanced methods may not reach full depth in a short session. Consider medium or long.');
      }
      if (p.category === 'authoritarian' && m.category === 'therapeutic' && m.pacing === 'permissive') {
        warnings.push('An authoritarian guide with a permissive method is an unusual combination. It can work, but the tones may clash.');
      }
      if (state.intentionType === 'sleep') {
        warnings.push('Sleep sessions skip wake and reintroduction. You will not be re-alerted — the session ends in silence.');
      }
      if (warnings.length) {
        warnEl.style.display = 'block';
        warnEl.innerHTML = '<strong>Note:</strong> ' + warnings.join(' · ');
      } else {
        warnEl.style.display = 'none';
      }
    }
  }

  // ====================================================================
  // CONFIG PRESETS — save/load named bundles of Configure-step settings
  // ====================================================================
  const PRESETS_KEY = 'stillness_config_presets_v1';

  /** Capture the current Configure-step settings as a serializable snapshot.
   *  Persona/method live on Steps 1-2 and intentionally not part of presets;
   *  presets are about HOW you experience a session, not what it's about. */
  function getCurrentConfigSnapshot() {
    return {
      length:            state.length,
      voiceName:         $('voiceSelect')?.value || state._savedVoiceName || '',
      speechRate:        state.speechRate,
      voicePitch:        state.voicePitch,
      voiceVolume:       state.voiceVolume,
      useLocalTTS:       !!state.useLocalTTS,
      soundscape:        state.soundscape,
      droneVolume:       state.droneVolume,
      droneEnabled:      state.droneEnabled,
      vizOverride:       state.vizOverride,
      vizSettings:       { ...(state.vizSettings || {}) },
      vizColor:          state.vizColor || null,
      audioReactiveMode: state.audioReactiveMode,
      duckingEnabled:    state.duckingEnabled,
      hud:               { ...(state.hud || {}) },
    };
  }

  /** Apply a saved snapshot back to state + DOM. Mirrors the per-field
   *  restore logic from loadUserSettings — kept in sync with that function;
   *  if you add a setting there, mirror it here. */
  function applyConfigSnapshot(snap) {
    if (!snap || typeof snap !== 'object') return;
    if (snap.length) {
      state.length = snap.length;
      const el = $('lengthSelect'); if (el) el.value = snap.length;
    }
    if (snap.voiceName) {
      const sel = $('voiceSelect');
      if (sel) {
        // Only set if the voice actually exists in the list (cross-device
        // loads may have mismatched voice catalogs; falling back keeps the
        // current selection rather than blanking it).
        if ([...sel.options].some(o => o.value === snap.voiceName)) {
          sel.value = snap.voiceName;
          state._savedVoiceName = snap.voiceName;
        }
      }
    }
    const setNum = (key, stateKey, sliderId, valId, mul, fmt) => {
      if (snap[key] == null) return;
      state[stateKey] = snap[key];
      const sl = $(sliderId); if (sl) sl.value = mul ? Math.round(snap[key] * mul) : snap[key];
      const v  = $(valId); if (v) v.textContent = fmt ? fmt(snap[key]) : String(snap[key]);
    };
    setNum('speechRate', 'speechRate', 'speechRate', 'speechRateVal', 100, n => n.toFixed(2) + '×');
    setNum('voicePitch', 'voicePitch', 'voicePitch', 'voicePitchVal', 100, n => n.toFixed(2));
    if (snap.voiceVolume != null) {
      state.voiceVolume = snap.voiceVolume;
      const sl = $('voiceVolume'); if (sl) sl.value = Math.round(snap.voiceVolume * 100);
      const v  = $('voiceVolumeVal'); if (v) v.textContent = Math.round(snap.voiceVolume * 100);
    }
    if (snap.useLocalTTS != null) {
      state.useLocalTTS = !!snap.useLocalTTS;
      const cb = $('useLocalTTS'); if (cb) cb.checked = state.useLocalTTS;
    }
    if (snap.soundscape) {
      state.soundscape = snap.soundscape;
      const el = $('soundscapeSelect'); if (el) el.value = snap.soundscape;
    }
    if (snap.droneVolume != null) {
      state.droneVolume = snap.droneVolume;
      const el = $('droneVolume'); if (el) el.value = Math.round(snap.droneVolume * 100);
      const v  = $('droneVolVal'); if (v) v.textContent = Math.round(snap.droneVolume * 100);
    }
    if (snap.droneEnabled != null) state.droneEnabled = !!snap.droneEnabled;
    if (snap.vizOverride) {
      state.vizOverride = snap.vizOverride;
      const el = $('vizSelect'); if (el) el.value = snap.vizOverride;
    }
    if (snap.vizSettings) {
      state.vizSettings = { ...state.vizSettings, ...snap.vizSettings };
      const vs = state.vizSettings;
      const speedEl = $('vizSpeed'); if (speedEl) { speedEl.value = Math.round(vs.speed * 100); const v = $('vizSpeedVal'); if (v) v.textContent = vs.speed.toFixed(1) + '×'; }
      const intEl   = $('vizIntensity'); if (intEl)   { intEl.value = Math.round(vs.intensity * 100); const v = $('vizIntensityVal'); if (v) v.textContent = vs.intensity.toFixed(1) + '×'; }
      const cmpEl   = $('vizComplexity'); if (cmpEl)   { cmpEl.value = Math.round(vs.complexity * 100); const v = $('vizComplexityVal'); if (v) v.textContent = vs.complexity.toFixed(1) + '×'; }
      const trEl    = $('vizTrail'); if (trEl)    { trEl.value = Math.round(vs.trail * 100); const v = $('vizTrailVal'); if (v) v.textContent = vs.trail.toFixed(2); }
    }
    if (snap.vizColor && /^#[0-9a-fA-F]{6}$/.test(snap.vizColor)) {
      state.vizColor = snap.vizColor.toLowerCase();
      const picker = $('vizColorPicker'); if (picker) picker.value = state.vizColor;
      const v = $('vizColorVal'); if (v) v.textContent = state.vizColor;
    }
    if (snap.audioReactiveMode && typeof setAudioReactiveMode === 'function') {
      setAudioReactiveMode(snap.audioReactiveMode);
    }
    if (snap.duckingEnabled != null) {
      state.duckingEnabled = !!snap.duckingEnabled;
      const el = $('duckingToggle'); if (el) el.checked = state.duckingEnabled;
    }
    if (snap.hud) state.hud = { ...state.hud, ...snap.hud };
    // Persist to settings + refresh derived UI.
    saveUserSettings();
    if (typeof renderConfigSummary === 'function') renderConfigSummary();
  }

  async function listConfigPresets() {
    try { return (await idbGet(PRESETS_KEY)) || []; } catch { return []; }
  }
  async function saveConfigPreset(name, snapshot) {
    const list = await listConfigPresets();
    // Replace by-name (idempotent overwrite). Newest first.
    const filtered = list.filter(p => p.name !== name);
    filtered.unshift({ name, createdAt: Date.now(), snapshot });
    if (filtered.length > 50) filtered.length = 50;
    await idbSet(PRESETS_KEY, filtered);
    return filtered;
  }
  async function deleteConfigPreset(name) {
    const list = await listConfigPresets();
    const filtered = list.filter(p => p.name !== name);
    await idbSet(PRESETS_KEY, filtered);
    return filtered;
  }

  /** Refresh the dropdown to reflect current saved presets. Idempotent;
   *  preserves the previously-selected value if it still exists. */
  async function renderPresetDropdown() {
    const sel = $('presetSelect');
    if (!sel) return;
    const prev = sel.value;
    const list = await listConfigPresets();
    sel.innerHTML = '<option value="">— choose a preset —</option>'
      + list.map(p => `<option value="${escapeHtml(p.name)}">${escapeHtml(p.name)}</option>`).join('');
    if (prev && list.some(p => p.name === prev)) sel.value = prev;
  }

  // ====================================================================
  // VOICES, AUDIO, VISUAL
  // ====================================================================
  function loadVoices() {
    const voices = speechSynthesis.getVoices();
    const select = $('voiceSelect');
    select.innerHTML = '';
    const scored = voices.filter(v => v.lang.startsWith('en')).map(v => {
      let s = 0;
      const n = v.name + ' ' + v.lang;
      if (/neural|natural|online/i.test(n)) s += 100;
      if (/enhanced|premium/i.test(n)) s += 90;
      if (/siri/i.test(n)) s += 85;
      if (/google/i.test(n) && !v.localService) s += 50;
      if (/male/i.test(n) && !/female/i.test(n)) s += 10;
      if (/uk|british|gb/i.test(n)) s += 5;
      if (/daniel|oliver|arthur|james|aaron/i.test(n)) s += 4;
      if (/espeak|compact/i.test(n)) s -= 50;
      if (v.localService) s += 2;
      const tier = s >= 85 ? 'neural' : s >= 50 ? 'cloud' : s > 0 ? 'local' : 'basic';
      return { voice: v, score: s, tier };
    }).sort((a, b) => b.score - a.score);

    // Group by tier
    const tiers = { neural: '★ Neural / Premium', cloud: '● Cloud', local: 'Local', basic: 'Basic' };
    const groups = {};
    scored.forEach(item => {
      if (!groups[item.tier]) groups[item.tier] = [];
      groups[item.tier].push(item);
    });

    let first = true;
    for (const tier of ['neural', 'cloud', 'local', 'basic']) {
      if (!groups[tier] || groups[tier].length === 0) continue;
      const og = document.createElement('optgroup');
      og.label = tiers[tier];
      groups[tier].forEach(item => {
        const opt = document.createElement('option');
        opt.value = item.voice.name;
        opt.textContent = item.voice.name + ' (' + item.voice.lang + ')';
        opt.dataset.tier = tier;
        opt.dataset.local = item.voice.localService ? '1' : '0';
        if (first) { opt.selected = true; first = false; }
        og.appendChild(opt);
      });
      select.appendChild(og);
    }
    applySavedVoiceSelection();
    updateVoiceInfo();
  }
  function applySavedVoiceSelection() {
    const select = $('voiceSelect');
    if (!select) return;
    if (state._savedVoiceName) {
      const opts = [...select.querySelectorAll('option')];
      const exact = opts.find(opt => opt.value === state._savedVoiceName);
      if (exact) select.value = state._savedVoiceName;
    }
  }

  // ── Voice Pack System — persona-matched voice recommendations ──
  const VOICE_PACKS = {
    contemplative: { names: ['Samantha', 'Karen', 'Moira', 'Fiona', 'Google UK English Female', 'Microsoft Hazel'], hints: ['Female', 'en-GB'], rate: 0.58, pitch: 0.8 },
    authoritarian: { names: ['Alex', 'Daniel', 'James', 'Google UK English Male', 'Microsoft George'], hints: ['Male', 'en-GB', 'en-US'], rate: 0.63, pitch: 0.75 },
    showman: { names: ['Tom', 'Rishi', 'Google US English', 'Microsoft Mark'], hints: ['Male', 'en-US'], rate: 0.68, pitch: 0.9 },
    somatic: { names: ['Samantha', 'Moira', 'Fiona', 'Google UK English Female'], hints: ['Female', 'en-GB'], rate: 0.55, pitch: 0.82 },
  };
  function getVoicePackFor(personaCategory) {
    return VOICE_PACKS[personaCategory] || VOICE_PACKS.contemplative;
  }
  function autoSelectVoiceForPersona() {
    if (state._savedVoiceName) return; // user manually chose — don't override
    const persona = getPersona();
    const pack = getVoicePackFor(persona.category);
    const voices = speechSynthesis.getVoices().filter(v => v.lang.startsWith('en'));
    for (const name of pack.names) {
      const match = voices.find(v => v.name.includes(name));
      if (match) { $('voiceSelect').value = match.name; state.speechRate = pack.rate; state.voicePitch = pack.pitch; break; }
    }
  }

  function updateVoiceInfo() {
    const select = $('voiceSelect');
    const info = $('voiceInfo');
    if (!info || !select.selectedOptions[0]) return;
    const opt = select.selectedOptions[0];
    const tier = opt.dataset.tier || 'local';
    const local = opt.dataset.local === '1';
    const labels = { neural: '★ Neural voice — highest quality', cloud: '● Cloud voice — good quality, slight latency', local: 'Local voice — instant, lower quality', basic: 'Basic voice — functional' };
    info.textContent = (labels[tier] || '') + (local ? ' · runs offline' : ' · requires internet');
  }
  if (typeof speechSynthesis !== 'undefined') { loadVoices(); speechSynthesis.onvoiceschanged = loadVoices; }
  const getSelectedVoice = () => { const name = $('voiceSelect').value; return speechSynthesis.getVoices().find(v => v.name === name) || null; };

  // ====================================================================
  // SOUNDSCAPE ENGINE — procedural ambient audio (replaces AudioDrone)
  // Recipes from ROADMAP: rain, ocean, fire, bowls, heartbeat, binaural,
  // white/pink/brown noise, plus the original engine drone.
  // ====================================================================
  class SoundscapeEngine {
    constructor() { this.ctx = null; this.master = null; this.compressor = null; this.nodes = []; this.running = false; this.type = 'drone'; this._duckGain = null; this._duckRequests = new Map(); }

    async start(volume = 0.35, type = 'drone') {
      if (this.running) await this.stop();
      this.type = type;
      this.ctx = new (window.AudioContext || window.webkitAudioContext)();
      if (this.ctx.state === 'suspended') await this.ctx.resume();

      // Master chain: sceneGain → compressor → destination
      this.compressor = this.ctx.createDynamicsCompressor();
      this.compressor.threshold.value = -18; this.compressor.knee.value = 24;
      this.compressor.ratio.value = 3; this.compressor.attack.value = 0.01; this.compressor.release.value = 0.25;
      this.compressor.connect(this.ctx.destination);
      this._duckGain = this.ctx.createGain(); this._duckGain.gain.value = 1.0;
      this._duckGain.connect(this.compressor);

      // Audio-reactive tap — AnalyserNode between master and _duckGain.
      // Tap reads the mixed signal before ducking, so soundscape reactivity
      // isn't muted during narrator speech. Doesn't disturb the signal path.
      this.analyser = this.ctx.createAnalyser();
      this.analyser.fftSize = 256;             // 128 frequency bins
      this.analyser.smoothingTimeConstant = 0.7;
      state.audioReactive._fftBuffer = new Uint8Array(this.analyser.frequencyBinCount);

      this.master = this.ctx.createGain(); this.master.gain.value = 0;
      this.master.connect(this.analyser);
      this.analyser.connect(this._duckGain);

      const c = this.ctx, m = this.master, ns = this.nodes;
      const mkNoise = (filterType, freq, q) => {
        const buf = c.createBuffer(1, 2 * c.sampleRate, c.sampleRate);
        const d = buf.getChannelData(0); let lo = 0;
        for (let i = 0; i < d.length; i++) {
          const w = Math.random() * 2 - 1;
          if (filterType === 'brown') { d[i] = (lo + 0.02 * w) / 1.02; lo = d[i]; d[i] *= 3.5; }
          else d[i] = w;
        }
        const src = c.createBufferSource(); src.buffer = buf; src.loop = true;
        if (filterType !== 'white' && filterType !== 'brown') {
          const f = c.createBiquadFilter(); f.type = filterType; f.frequency.value = freq || 1000; if (q) f.Q.value = q;
          src.connect(f); ns.push(src, f); return f;
        }
        ns.push(src); return src;
      };
      const mkOsc = (freq, type = 'sine') => { const o = c.createOscillator(); o.type = type; o.frequency.value = freq; ns.push(o); return o; };
      const mkGain = (v) => { const g = c.createGain(); g.gain.value = v; ns.push(g); return g; };
      const connect = (...chain) => { for (let i = 0; i < chain.length - 1; i++) chain[i].connect(chain[i + 1]); };
      const startAll = () => ns.forEach(n => { try { n.start && n.start(); } catch(e) {} });

      if (type === 'drone') {
        const bass = mkOsc(70); const bg = mkGain(0.4); connect(bass, bg, m);
        const harm = mkOsc(141); const hg = mkGain(0.18); connect(harm, hg, m);
        const noise = mkNoise('brown'); const ng = mkGain(0.25); const nf = c.createBiquadFilter(); nf.type = 'lowpass'; nf.frequency.value = 280; ns.push(nf);
        connect(noise, nf, ng, m);
        const lfo = mkOsc(0.07); const lg = mkGain(0.08); connect(lfo, lg); lg.connect(bg.gain);
      } else if (type === 'rain') {
        const bed = mkNoise('bandpass', 1200, 0.8); const bg = mkGain(0.5); connect(bed, bg, m);
        const rumble = mkNoise('brown'); const rf = c.createBiquadFilter(); rf.type = 'lowpass'; rf.frequency.value = 200; ns.push(rf);
        const rg = mkGain(0.08); connect(rumble, rf, rg, m);
        // Droplet scheduler
        const droplet = () => { if (!this.running) return;
          const osc = c.createOscillator(); osc.frequency.value = 2000 + Math.random() * 4000;
          const g = c.createGain(); g.gain.value = 0; osc.connect(g).connect(m);
          const t = c.currentTime; g.gain.setValueAtTime(0, t); g.gain.linearRampToValueAtTime(0.15 + Math.random() * 0.1, t + 0.005);
          g.gain.exponentialRampToValueAtTime(0.0001, t + 0.02 + Math.random() * 0.015);
          osc.start(t); osc.stop(t + 0.04);
          setTimeout(droplet, 20 + Math.random() * 60);
        };
        setTimeout(droplet, 500);
      } else if (type === 'ocean') {
        const noise = mkNoise('bandpass', 650, 0.5); const ng = mkGain(0.45);
        const lfo = mkOsc(1 / 11); const lg = mkGain(0.35); connect(lfo, lg); lg.connect(ng.gain);
        connect(noise, ng, m);
        const foam = mkNoise('bandpass', 3000, 1); const fg = mkGain(0.06); connect(foam, fg, m);
      } else if (type === 'fire') {
        const bed = mkNoise('brown'); const bg = mkGain(0.12);
        const bf = c.createBiquadFilter(); bf.type = 'lowpass'; bf.frequency.value = 400; ns.push(bf);
        connect(bed, bf, bg, m);
        const crackle = () => { if (!this.running) return;
          const b = c.createBuffer(1, Math.floor(c.sampleRate * 0.008), c.sampleRate); const d = b.getChannelData(0);
          for (let i = 0; i < d.length; i++) d[i] = (Math.random() * 2 - 1) * (1 - i / d.length);
          const s = c.createBufferSource(); s.buffer = b;
          const f = c.createBiquadFilter(); f.type = 'bandpass'; f.frequency.value = 1500 + Math.random() * 3000; f.Q.value = 2;
          const g = c.createGain(); g.gain.value = 0.15 + Math.random() * 0.2;
          s.connect(f).connect(g).connect(m); s.start();
          setTimeout(crackle, 80 + Math.random() * 300);
        };
        setTimeout(crackle, 200);
      } else if (type === 'bowls') {
        const ratios = [1, 2.02, 3.07, 4.16, 5.38]; const decays = [10, 7, 5, 4, 3];
        const strike = () => { if (!this.running) return;
          const root = 180 + Math.random() * 60;
          ratios.forEach((r, i) => {
            const o = c.createOscillator(); o.frequency.value = root * r;
            const g = c.createGain(); g.gain.value = 0.12 / (i + 1);
            o.connect(g).connect(m); const t = c.currentTime;
            g.gain.setValueAtTime(g.gain.value, t); g.gain.exponentialRampToValueAtTime(0.0001, t + decays[i]);
            o.start(t); o.stop(t + decays[i] + 0.1);
          });
          setTimeout(strike, 8000 + Math.random() * 12000);
        };
        strike();
      } else if (type === 'heartbeat') {
        const beat = () => { if (!this.running) return;
          [0, 0.14].forEach(delay => {
            const o = c.createOscillator(); o.frequency.value = 55;
            const g = c.createGain(); g.gain.value = 0;
            const f = c.createBiquadFilter(); f.type = 'lowpass'; f.frequency.value = 200;
            o.connect(f).connect(g).connect(m); const t = c.currentTime + delay;
            g.gain.setValueAtTime(0, t); g.gain.linearRampToValueAtTime(0.5, t + 0.03);
            g.gain.exponentialRampToValueAtTime(0.0001, t + 0.25);
            o.start(t); o.stop(t + 0.3);
          });
          setTimeout(beat, 1050 + Math.random() * 120);
        };
        beat();
      } else if (type === 'binaural') {
        // Phase-locked binaural beats. Carrier 200Hz is comfortable below
        // most ambient noise floors. Beat frequency starts at theta (6Hz)
        // but setPhaseFreq() now ramps it per-phase: alpha for settling/
        // wake, theta for induction/work, slower theta-delta for deepener.
        // Refs stashed on `this` so setPhaseFreq can find them.
        const carrier = 200, beatHz = 6;
        const oL = mkOsc(carrier - beatHz / 2); const oR = mkOsc(carrier + beatHz / 2);
        const gL = mkGain(0.3); const gR = mkGain(0.3);
        const merger = c.createChannelMerger(2); ns.push(merger);
        oL.connect(gL).connect(merger, 0, 0); oR.connect(gR).connect(merger, 0, 1);
        merger.connect(m);
        this._binauralRefs = { oL, oR, gL, gR, carrier };
      } else if (type === 'isochronic') {
        // Isochronic tones — single carrier with amplitude modulation at
        // the entrainment frequency. Works on any speaker (no stereo
        // separation needed), unlike binaural. Same per-phase frequency
        // map. The LFO is shaped through a bandpass to soften the AM
        // envelope away from harsh on/off clicking.
        const carrier = 220;
        const beatHz = 6; // initial; setPhaseFreq ramps from here
        const carrierOsc = mkOsc(carrier);
        const carrierGain = mkGain(0); // baseline silenced; LFO drives it
        carrierOsc.connect(carrierGain).connect(m);
        // LFO: a sine that the carrier gain follows, biased to 0..0.4 swing.
        const lfo = mkOsc(beatHz);
        const lfoDepth = mkGain(0.2);     // half-amplitude of swing
        const lfoBias  = c.createConstantSource(); ns.push(lfoBias);
        lfoBias.offset.value = 0.2;        // baseline so swing is 0..0.4
        lfoBias.start();
        lfo.connect(lfoDepth).connect(carrierGain.gain);
        lfoBias.connect(carrierGain.gain);
        this._isochronicRefs = { lfo, carrierOsc, carrierGain };
      } else if (type === 'pink' || type === 'brown' || type === 'white') {
        const noise = mkNoise(type === 'pink' ? 'lowpass' : type, type === 'pink' ? 800 : undefined);
        const ng = mkGain(type === 'white' ? 0.15 : 0.35); connect(noise, ng, m);
      } else if (type === 'forest') {
        // Layered: breeze (filtered brown), bird chirps (scheduled sine pips)
        const breeze = mkNoise('lowpass', 500); const bg = mkGain(0.2); connect(breeze, bg, m);
        const chirp = () => {
          if (!this.running) return;
          const o = c.createOscillator(); o.frequency.value = 2200 + Math.random() * 2000;
          const g = c.createGain(); o.connect(g).connect(m);
          const t = c.currentTime + Math.random() * 0.5;
          g.gain.setValueAtTime(0, t); g.gain.linearRampToValueAtTime(0.08, t + 0.02);
          g.gain.exponentialRampToValueAtTime(0.0001, t + 0.08 + Math.random() * 0.1);
          o.start(t); o.stop(t + 0.15);
          setTimeout(chirp, 1500 + Math.random() * 4000);
        };
        chirp(); setTimeout(chirp, 800);
      } else if (type === 'stream') {
        // Gentle water: filtered white noise + subtle babble modulation
        const water = mkNoise('bandpass', 1200, 0.5); const wg = mkGain(0.3); connect(water, wg, m);
        const lfo = mkOsc(0.3); const lg = mkGain(100); lfo.connect(lg);
        const wf = c.createBiquadFilter(); wf.type = 'bandpass'; wf.frequency.value = 1200; ns.push(wf);
        lg.connect(wf.frequency); water.connect(wf).connect(wg);
      } else if (type === 'wind') {
        // Gusting wind: brown noise with slow LFO on gain
        const wind = mkNoise('lowpass', 600); const wg = mkGain(0.35); connect(wind, wg, m);
        const lfo = mkOsc(0.08 + Math.random() * 0.04); const lg = mkGain(0.2);
        lfo.connect(lg); lg.connect(wg.gain);
      } else if (type === 'thunder') {
        // Deep rumble base + periodic thunder rolls
        const rumble = mkNoise('lowpass', 120); const rg = mkGain(0.3); connect(rumble, rg, m);
        const rain = mkNoise('highpass', 3000); const raing = mkGain(0.08); connect(rain, raing, m);
        const roll = () => {
          if (!this.running) return;
          // Inline a self-contained noise burst so we can explicitly start/stop it.
          // (mkNoise pushes its source into ns expecting startAll(), which has already run.)
          const buf = c.createBuffer(1, Math.max(1, Math.floor(c.sampleRate * 0.2)), c.sampleRate);
          const d = buf.getChannelData(0);
          for (let i = 0; i < d.length; i++) d[i] = Math.random() * 2 - 1;
          const src = c.createBufferSource(); src.buffer = buf; src.loop = true;
          const f = c.createBiquadFilter(); f.type = 'lowpass'; f.frequency.value = 80;
          const g = c.createGain();
          src.connect(f).connect(g).connect(m);
          const t = c.currentTime;
          const dur = 3 + Math.random() * 2;
          g.gain.setValueAtTime(0, t);
          g.gain.linearRampToValueAtTime(0.5, t + 0.3);
          g.gain.exponentialRampToValueAtTime(0.0001, t + dur);
          try { src.start(t); src.stop(t + dur + 0.1); } catch {}
          setTimeout(roll, 8000 + Math.random() * 15000);
        };
        setTimeout(roll, 3000 + Math.random() * 5000);
      } else if (type === 'chimes') {
        // Random tuned sine pips with long decay
        const base = mkNoise('bandpass', 400, 0.3); const bg = mkGain(0.05); connect(base, bg, m);
        const chime = () => {
          if (!this.running) return;
          const freqs = [523, 587, 659, 784, 880, 1047, 1175];
          const f = freqs[Math.floor(Math.random() * freqs.length)];
          const o = c.createOscillator(); o.frequency.value = f; o.type = 'sine';
          const g = c.createGain(); o.connect(g).connect(m);
          const t = c.currentTime;
          g.gain.setValueAtTime(0.15, t); g.gain.exponentialRampToValueAtTime(0.0001, t + 2 + Math.random() * 2);
          o.start(t); o.stop(t + 4);
          setTimeout(chime, 2000 + Math.random() * 5000);
        };
        chime(); setTimeout(chime, 1200);
      } else if (type === 'cave') {
        // Sparse drips with long reverb/echo
        const bg = mkNoise('bandpass', 120, 0.3); const bgg = mkGain(0.03); connect(bg, bgg, m);
        const drip = () => {
          if (!this.running) return;
          const o = c.createOscillator(); o.frequency.value = 2000 + Math.random() * 2000; o.type = 'sine';
          const g = c.createGain(); o.connect(g).connect(m);
          const t = c.currentTime;
          g.gain.setValueAtTime(0.08, t); g.gain.exponentialRampToValueAtTime(0.0001, t + 0.8 + Math.random());
          o.start(t); o.stop(t + 2);
          setTimeout(drip, 3000 + Math.random() * 8000);
        };
        drip(); setTimeout(drip, 1500); setTimeout(drip, 4500);
      } else if (type === 'crickets') {
        // Chirping insects — filtered noise bursts at cricket-like rates
        const wind = mkNoise('lowpass', 300); const wg = mkGain(0.04); connect(wind, wg, m);
        const chirp = () => {
          if (!this.running) return;
          const o = c.createOscillator(); o.frequency.value = 4000 + Math.random() * 2000; o.type = 'sine';
          const g = c.createGain(); o.connect(g).connect(m);
          const t = c.currentTime, dur = 0.04 + Math.random() * 0.02;
          const reps = 3 + Math.floor(Math.random() * 4);
          for (let i = 0; i < reps; i++) {
            g.gain.setValueAtTime(0.06, t + i * dur * 2); g.gain.setValueAtTime(0, t + i * dur * 2 + dur);
          }
          o.start(t); o.stop(t + reps * dur * 2 + 0.1);
          setTimeout(chirp, 1500 + Math.random() * 4000);
        };
        chirp(); setTimeout(chirp, 800); setTimeout(chirp, 2200);
      } else if (type === 'temple') {
        // Deep bell strikes + sustained tonal drone
        const drone1 = c.createOscillator(); drone1.frequency.value = 110; drone1.type = 'sine';
        const drone2 = c.createOscillator(); drone2.frequency.value = 165; drone2.type = 'triangle';
        const dg1 = mkGain(0.12); const dg2 = mkGain(0.06);
        connect(drone1, dg1, m); connect(drone2, dg2, m);
        this.nodes.push(drone1, drone2);
        const bell = () => {
          if (!this.running) return;
          const f = [220, 277, 330, 440][Math.floor(Math.random() * 4)];
          const o = c.createOscillator(); o.frequency.value = f; o.type = 'sine';
          const g = c.createGain(); o.connect(g).connect(m);
          const t = c.currentTime;
          g.gain.setValueAtTime(0.2, t); g.gain.exponentialRampToValueAtTime(0.0001, t + 5 + Math.random() * 3);
          o.start(t); o.stop(t + 8);
          setTimeout(bell, 6000 + Math.random() * 10000);
        };
        bell(); setTimeout(bell, 3000);
      } else if (type === 'spaceship') {
        // Low engine hum + subtle sci-fi bleeps
        const eng1 = c.createOscillator(); eng1.frequency.value = 42; eng1.type = 'sawtooth';
        const eng2 = c.createOscillator(); eng2.frequency.value = 43.5; eng2.type = 'sawtooth';
        const lp = c.createBiquadFilter(); lp.type = 'lowpass'; lp.frequency.value = 120;
        const eg1 = mkGain(0.15); const eg2 = mkGain(0.12);
        eng1.connect(eg1).connect(lp).connect(m); eng2.connect(eg2).connect(lp);
        this.nodes.push(eng1, eng2);
        const bleep = () => {
          if (!this.running) return;
          const f = [880, 1320, 1760, 2200][Math.floor(Math.random() * 4)];
          const o = c.createOscillator(); o.frequency.value = f; o.type = 'sine';
          const g = c.createGain(); o.connect(g).connect(m);
          const t = c.currentTime;
          g.gain.setValueAtTime(0.02, t); g.gain.exponentialRampToValueAtTime(0.0001, t + 0.15);
          o.start(t); o.stop(t + 0.2);
          setTimeout(bleep, 4000 + Math.random() * 12000);
        };
        bleep(); setTimeout(bleep, 6000);
      } else if (type === 'underwater') {
        // Deep submerged ambience with bubble chirps
        const noise = mkNoise('brown'); const ng = mkGain(0.35);
        const lp = c.createBiquadFilter(); lp.type = 'lowpass'; lp.frequency.value = 400; ns.push(lp);
        connect(noise, ng, lp, m);
        const drone = mkOsc(55, 'sine'); const dg = mkGain(0.08); connect(drone, dg, m);
        const bubble = () => {
          if (!this.running) return;
          const f = 1200 + Math.random() * 2000;
          const o = c.createOscillator(); o.frequency.value = f; o.type = 'sine';
          const g = c.createGain(); o.connect(g).connect(m);
          const t = c.currentTime;
          g.gain.setValueAtTime(0.015, t); g.gain.exponentialRampToValueAtTime(0.0001, t + 0.08);
          o.start(t); o.stop(t + 0.1);
          setTimeout(bubble, 800 + Math.random() * 4000);
        };
        bubble(); setTimeout(bubble, 2000); setTimeout(bubble, 4500);
      } else if (type === 'clock') {
        // Grandfather clock — tick-tock + periodic chime
        const tick = () => {
          if (!this.running) return;
          const o = c.createOscillator(); o.frequency.value = 800; o.type = 'sine';
          const g = c.createGain(); o.connect(g).connect(m);
          const t = c.currentTime;
          g.gain.setValueAtTime(0.06, t); g.gain.exponentialRampToValueAtTime(0.0001, t + 0.03);
          o.start(t); o.stop(t + 0.05);
          setTimeout(tick, 1000);
        };
        tick();
        const roomNoise = mkNoise('brown'); const rg = mkGain(0.04);
        const rlp = c.createBiquadFilter(); rlp.type = 'lowpass'; rlp.frequency.value = 200; ns.push(rlp);
        connect(roomNoise, rg, rlp, m);
        const chime = () => {
          if (!this.running) return;
          [220, 330, 440].forEach((f, i) => {
            const o = c.createOscillator(); o.frequency.value = f; o.type = 'sine';
            const g = c.createGain(); o.connect(g).connect(m);
            const t = c.currentTime + i * 0.3;
            g.gain.setValueAtTime(0.04, t); g.gain.exponentialRampToValueAtTime(0.0001, t + 2);
            o.start(t); o.stop(t + 2.5);
          });
          setTimeout(chime, 90000 + Math.random() * 60000);
        };
        setTimeout(chime, 30000);
      } else if (type === 'train') {
        // Railway car — rhythmic clack + rumble
        const rumble = mkNoise('brown'); const rg = mkGain(0.3);
        const rlp = c.createBiquadFilter(); rlp.type = 'lowpass'; rlp.frequency.value = 180; ns.push(rlp);
        connect(rumble, rg, rlp, m);
        const clack = () => {
          if (!this.running) return;
          const o = mkNoise('white'); const g = mkGain(0.08);
          const bp = c.createBiquadFilter(); bp.type = 'bandpass'; bp.frequency.value = 2000; bp.Q.value = 3; ns.push(bp);
          o.connect(g).connect(bp).connect(m);
          const t = c.currentTime;
          g.gain.setValueAtTime(0.08, t); g.gain.exponentialRampToValueAtTime(0.001, t + 0.06);
          try { o.start(t); o.stop(t + 0.08); } catch {}
          setTimeout(clack, 450 + Math.random() * 100);
        };
        clack(); setTimeout(clack, 225);
      } else if (type === 'city') {
        // City night — distant traffic + ambient
        const traffic = mkNoise('brown'); const tg = mkGain(0.15);
        const tlp = c.createBiquadFilter(); tlp.type = 'lowpass'; tlp.frequency.value = 150; ns.push(tlp);
        connect(traffic, tg, tlp, m);
        const hum = mkOsc(60, 'sine'); const hg = mkGain(0.03); connect(hum, hg, m);
        const siren = () => {
          if (!this.running) return;
          const o = c.createOscillator(); o.type = 'sine';
          const g = c.createGain(); o.connect(g).connect(m);
          const t = c.currentTime;
          o.frequency.setValueAtTime(600, t); o.frequency.linearRampToValueAtTime(800, t + 1);
          o.frequency.linearRampToValueAtTime(600, t + 2);
          g.gain.setValueAtTime(0.005, t); g.gain.linearRampToValueAtTime(0.012, t + 0.5);
          g.gain.exponentialRampToValueAtTime(0.0001, t + 3);
          o.start(t); o.stop(t + 3.5);
          setTimeout(siren, 30000 + Math.random() * 60000);
        };
        setTimeout(siren, 15000 + Math.random() * 20000);
      } else if (type === 'whales') {
        // Deep ocean abyss — sub bass bed + long whale-call glissandos. Calls
        // are scheduled on detached oscillators so they fade naturally and
        // don't bloat `nodes` (which is for sustained sources).
        const abyss = mkNoise('lowpass', 200); const ag = mkGain(0.18); connect(abyss, ag, m);
        const bed = mkOsc(40, 'sine'); const bg = mkGain(0.1); connect(bed, bg, m);
        const call = () => {
          if (!this.running) return;
          // Whale calls are very long (4–9s) low glissandos in 80–280Hz range
          const startF = 80 + Math.random() * 100;
          const endF   = 140 + Math.random() * 140;
          const dur    = 4 + Math.random() * 5;
          const o = c.createOscillator(); o.type = 'sine';
          const lp = c.createBiquadFilter(); lp.type = 'lowpass'; lp.frequency.value = 400;
          const g = c.createGain();
          o.connect(lp).connect(g).connect(m);
          const t = c.currentTime;
          o.frequency.setValueAtTime(startF, t);
          o.frequency.exponentialRampToValueAtTime(endF, t + dur * 0.6);
          o.frequency.exponentialRampToValueAtTime(startF * 0.85, t + dur);
          g.gain.setValueAtTime(0, t);
          g.gain.linearRampToValueAtTime(0.18, t + 0.8);
          g.gain.linearRampToValueAtTime(0.18, t + dur - 1.5);
          g.gain.exponentialRampToValueAtTime(0.0001, t + dur);
          try { o.start(t); o.stop(t + dur + 0.1); } catch {}
          setTimeout(call, 8000 + Math.random() * 18000);
        };
        setTimeout(call, 2000 + Math.random() * 3000);
      } else if (type === 'storm') {
        // Heavy rain bed + frequent thunder + gusting wind. Combines layers
        // we already know how to do — this is the "weather event" preset.
        const rainBed = mkNoise('bandpass', 1400, 0.6); const rbg = mkGain(0.55); connect(rainBed, rbg, m);
        const heavyRumble = mkNoise('lowpass', 150); const hrg = mkGain(0.25); connect(heavyRumble, hrg, m);
        const wind = mkNoise('lowpass', 500); const wg = mkGain(0.2); connect(wind, wg, m);
        // Slow gust LFO modulates wind volume so the wind "breathes"
        const wlfo = mkOsc(0.06, 'sine'); const wlg = mkGain(0.12); connect(wlfo, wlg); wlg.connect(wg.gain);
        // Droplet scheduler — denser than plain rain
        const droplet = () => {
          if (!this.running) return;
          const o = c.createOscillator(); o.frequency.value = 1500 + Math.random() * 5000;
          const g = c.createGain(); g.gain.value = 0; o.connect(g).connect(m);
          const t = c.currentTime;
          g.gain.setValueAtTime(0, t); g.gain.linearRampToValueAtTime(0.18 + Math.random() * 0.12, t + 0.005);
          g.gain.exponentialRampToValueAtTime(0.0001, t + 0.02 + Math.random() * 0.015);
          try { o.start(t); o.stop(t + 0.04); } catch {}
          setTimeout(droplet, 12 + Math.random() * 35);
        };
        setTimeout(droplet, 200);
        // Thunder rolls — same pattern as `thunder` but more frequent
        const roll = () => {
          if (!this.running) return;
          const buf = c.createBuffer(1, Math.max(1, Math.floor(c.sampleRate * 0.2)), c.sampleRate);
          const d = buf.getChannelData(0);
          for (let i = 0; i < d.length; i++) d[i] = Math.random() * 2 - 1;
          const src = c.createBufferSource(); src.buffer = buf; src.loop = true;
          const f = c.createBiquadFilter(); f.type = 'lowpass'; f.frequency.value = 90;
          const g = c.createGain();
          src.connect(f).connect(g).connect(m);
          const t = c.currentTime;
          const dur = 4 + Math.random() * 3;
          g.gain.setValueAtTime(0, t);
          g.gain.linearRampToValueAtTime(0.6, t + 0.4);
          g.gain.exponentialRampToValueAtTime(0.0001, t + dur);
          try { src.start(t); src.stop(t + dur + 0.1); } catch {}
          setTimeout(roll, 5000 + Math.random() * 10000);
        };
        setTimeout(roll, 2500);
      } else if (type === 'fan') {
        // White-noise box fan — even broadband hiss + faint motor hum at 60Hz.
        // Useful for users who actually sleep with one (familiar comfort).
        const noise = mkNoise('lowpass', 1800); const ng = mkGain(0.42); connect(noise, ng, m);
        const motor = mkOsc(60, 'sine'); const mg = mkGain(0.04); connect(motor, mg, m);
        // Tiny tonal wobble — fan blades aren't perfectly balanced
        const wob = mkOsc(0.7, 'sine'); const wg = mkGain(0.012); connect(wob, wg); wg.connect(ng.gain);
      } else if (type === 'owls') {
        // Forest at night — wind through trees + sparse owl hoots + rare hoot
        // pair. Calls are rare (every 12–35s) so the silence between is the
        // texture, not the calls themselves.
        const breeze = mkNoise('lowpass', 400); const bg = mkGain(0.18); connect(breeze, bg, m);
        const hoot = (twoCall) => {
          if (!this.running) return;
          // Owl hoot — soft sine pip with quick attack, long decay; two
          // hoots at slightly different pitches, then quiet.
          const callOnce = (offset, pitch) => {
            const o = c.createOscillator(); o.type = 'sine'; o.frequency.value = pitch;
            const lp = c.createBiquadFilter(); lp.type = 'lowpass'; lp.frequency.value = 1200;
            const g = c.createGain();
            o.connect(lp).connect(g).connect(m);
            const t = c.currentTime + offset;
            g.gain.setValueAtTime(0, t);
            g.gain.linearRampToValueAtTime(0.18, t + 0.08);
            g.gain.linearRampToValueAtTime(0.14, t + 0.35);
            g.gain.exponentialRampToValueAtTime(0.0001, t + 0.9);
            try { o.start(t); o.stop(t + 1.0); } catch {}
          };
          callOnce(0, 380 + Math.random() * 80);
          if (twoCall) callOnce(0.55, 360 + Math.random() * 80);
          setTimeout(() => hoot(Math.random() < 0.4), 12000 + Math.random() * 23000);
        };
        setTimeout(() => hoot(Math.random() < 0.5), 4000 + Math.random() * 6000);
      }

      startAll();
      this.master.gain.linearRampToValueAtTime(volume, this.ctx.currentTime + 4);
      this.running = true;
    }

    setVolume(v) { if (this.master && this.ctx) this.master.gain.linearRampToValueAtTime(Math.max(0, v), this.ctx.currentTime + 0.5); }

    // Ducking with multiple owners. speakScript ducks per-sentence with
    // source 'speech' (level 0.15); the rules engine's duckAudio action
    // ducks with source 'rules' (level from rule param). They used to
    // fight because 'rules' mutated master.gain while 'speech' mutated
    // _duckGain, multiplying their effects (a 0.10 rule + 0.15 speech duck
    // produced effective 0.015× — silent ambience). Now both stack into
    // _duckRequests and the active level is the strictest (lowest) request.
    // Empty stack → 1.0 (unducked). Default args keep the legacy zero-arg
    // call sites working.
    _recomputeDuckLevel() {
      if (!this._duckGain || !this.ctx) return;
      let target = 1.0;
      for (const lvl of this._duckRequests.values()) {
        if (lvl < target) target = lvl;
      }
      this._duckGain.gain.cancelScheduledValues(this.ctx.currentTime);
      // Asymmetric ramp matches the original feel: fast duck-down (0.25s),
      // slower release (1.2s) so ambience swells gently back into voice gaps.
      const ramp = target < 1.0 ? 0.25 : 1.2;
      this._duckGain.gain.linearRampToValueAtTime(target, this.ctx.currentTime + ramp);
    }
    duck(source = 'speech', level = 0.15) {
      if (!this._duckGain || !this.ctx) return;
      this._duckRequests.set(source, Math.max(0, Math.min(1, level)));
      this._recomputeDuckLevel();
    }
    unduck(source = 'speech') {
      if (!this._duckGain || !this.ctx) return;
      this._duckRequests.delete(source);
      this._recomputeDuckLevel();
    }

    setPhaseFreq(phase) {
      const baseKey = String(phase || '').replace(/-\d+$/, '');
      // Drone carrier ramp — original behavior, only on type 'drone'.
      if (this.type === 'drone' && this.ctx) {
        const freqMap = { settling: 70, induction: 67, deepener: 63, level: 62, work: 60, affirm: 64, lightener: 66, wake: 72, reintroduction: 74, emergence: 72 };
        const target = freqMap[baseKey] || 70;
        if (this.nodes[0]?.frequency) this.nodes[0].frequency.linearRampToValueAtTime(target, this.ctx.currentTime + 4);
        if (this.nodes[2]?.frequency) this.nodes[2].frequency.linearRampToValueAtTime(target * 2.01, this.ctx.currentTime + 4);
      }
      // Phase-locked binaural beat — ramps L/R oscillators around the
      // carrier so the difference frequency tracks the user's intended
      // brain-wave state. Long ramp (6s) is below auditory threshold for
      // pitch change so the user perceives a smooth deepening, not a
      // jump. Stays in healthy frequency bands per literature: alpha
      // 8-12Hz for awake/relaxed; theta 4-7Hz for hypnotic/meditative;
      // theta-delta 3.5Hz for deepest trance (kept above pure delta to
      // avoid putting the user fully asleep mid-session).
      const beatMap = {
        settling:       8,    // alpha — calm awareness
        induction:      6,    // theta — light trance entry
        deepener:       3.5,  // theta-delta — deeper
        level:          4,    // theta hold
        work:           5,    // theta — receptive
        affirm:         5,    // theta
        lightener:      7,    // theta — emerging
        wake:          12,    // alpha — re-alertness
        reintroduction: 14,   // beta — fully alert
        emergence:     12,
      };
      if (this.type === 'binaural' && this.ctx && this._binauralRefs) {
        const beatHz = beatMap[baseKey] ?? 6;
        const { oL, oR, carrier } = this._binauralRefs;
        if (oL?.frequency && oR?.frequency) {
          const t = this.ctx.currentTime + 6;
          oL.frequency.linearRampToValueAtTime(carrier - beatHz / 2, t);
          oR.frequency.linearRampToValueAtTime(carrier + beatHz / 2, t);
        }
      }
      // Isochronic tones — same beat map applied to the AM LFO. The
      // amplitude-modulating sine ramps from its old rate to the new
      // phase rate over 6s, audibly cross-fading the entrainment band.
      if (this.type === 'isochronic' && this.ctx && this._isochronicRefs) {
        const beatHz = beatMap[baseKey] ?? 6;
        const { lfo } = this._isochronicRefs;
        if (lfo?.frequency) {
          lfo.frequency.linearRampToValueAtTime(beatHz, this.ctx.currentTime + 6);
        }
      }
    }

    async stop() {
      if (!this.running || !this.ctx) return;
      this.master.gain.linearRampToValueAtTime(0, this.ctx.currentTime + 2);
      await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 2100));
      this.nodes.forEach(n => { try { n.stop?.(); n.disconnect?.(); } catch(e) {} });
      if (this.analyser) { try { this.analyser.disconnect(); } catch {} this.analyser = null; }
      state.audioReactive._fftBuffer = null;
      try { this.ctx.suspend(); } catch(e) {}
      this.ctx = null; this.master = null; this._duckGain = null; this.compressor = null; this.nodes = []; this.running = false;
      this._binauralRefs = null; // drop binaural osc refs so fresh sessions get fresh nodes
      this._isochronicRefs = null; // same for isochronic LFO refs
      // Drop any pending duck requests — fresh start next session, otherwise
      // a rule that ducked but didn't unduck before backToEdit would leak
      // its 'rules' entry into the next session's first _recomputeDuckLevel.
      this._duckRequests.clear();
    }
  }
  const drone = new SoundscapeEngine();

  // ====================================================================
  // BACKGROUND IMAGE ENGINE
  // ====================================================================
  // Max concurrent image generations. Perchance text-to-image-plugin handles up
  // to 4 parallel requests well; beyond that the provider starts queuing and it
  // just adds latency rather than throughput.
  const BG_MAX_PARALLEL = 4;

  // Orientation → resolution map. "default" delegates to the plugin default.
  const BG_RESOLUTIONS = {
    default: null,            // omit resolution → plugin picks
    square: '512x512',
    portrait: '512x768',
    landscape: '768x512',
  };

  // ====================================================================
  // BG STYLE PRESETS — additive prompt modifiers (chip UI)
  // ====================================================================
  // Curated set of style fragments tuned for hypnosis aesthetics: soft,
  // non-figurative, dim, dreamlike. Each chip the user toggles contributes
  // its `prompt` and `negative` fragments to the final call WITHOUT
  // mutating the textarea — so users can freely edit their own prompt
  // and the chips compose on top.
  //
  // Active selections live in state.bgActiveStylePresetIds (string[]).
  // Composed inside bgEngine._currentParams() and rendered as chips by
  // buildBgStyleChips(). Categories are purely visual — they group the
  // chips into rows but don't restrict selection (you can stack as many
  // as you like across categories).
  //
  // To add a preset: append to the array. To rename a category: change
  // both the `cat` value here AND the matching label in BG_STYLE_CATEGORIES.
  // To migrate ids: filter unknown ids out gracefully on load (the loader
  // already does this).
  // ====================================================================
  const BG_STYLE_CATEGORIES = [
    { id: 'atmosphere', label: 'Atmosphere' },
    { id: 'nature',     label: 'Nature'     },
    { id: 'abstract',   label: 'Abstract'   },
    { id: 'style',      label: 'Style'      },
  ];
  const BG_STYLE_PRESETS = [
    // Atmosphere — environmental qualities (lighting, weather, time of day)
    { id: 'misty',      cat: 'atmosphere', label: 'Misty',        prompt: 'misty fog, drifting low clouds, ethereal haze, soft visibility',                       negative: 'sharp clarity, hard edges' },
    { id: 'twilight',   cat: 'atmosphere', label: 'Twilight',     prompt: 'twilight blue hour, indigo and violet sky, soft fading light',                         negative: 'high noon, harsh sunlight' },
    { id: 'underwater', cat: 'atmosphere', label: 'Underwater',   prompt: 'underwater scene, soft refracted light beams, slow drifting particles, deep blue-green', negative: 'bright sun, dry land' },
    { id: 'aurora',     cat: 'atmosphere', label: 'Aurora',       prompt: 'aurora borealis ribbons, soft green and violet light bands, starry night',             negative: 'daytime, blue sky' },
    { id: 'cosmic',     cat: 'atmosphere', label: 'Cosmic',       prompt: 'deep space nebula, soft star clusters, swirling cosmic dust, indigo and magenta',       negative: 'earth, ground, mundane' },
    // Nature — recognisable landscapes
    { id: 'forest',     cat: 'nature',     label: 'Forest',       prompt: 'ancient forest at dusk, soft mossy ground, dappled fading light, deep shadows',         negative: 'open field, bright midday' },
    { id: 'ocean',      cat: 'nature',     label: 'Ocean',        prompt: 'calm ocean horizon, slow gentle waves, soft pastel sky, infinite distance',             negative: 'storm, choppy waves' },
    { id: 'mountains',  cat: 'nature',     label: 'Mountains',    prompt: 'distant snow-capped mountains, soft layered haze, muted blue-gray palette',             negative: 'close-up, sharp detail' },
