    { id: 'meadow',     cat: 'nature',     label: 'Meadow',       prompt: 'soft grass meadow at dawn, golden mist, gentle suggestion of breeze, warm muted tones', negative: 'concrete, urban' },
    { id: 'cave',       cat: 'nature',     label: 'Cave',         prompt: 'dim luminous cave interior, soft bioluminescent glow, smooth wet stone, deep shadows',  negative: 'bright, exposed, daylight' },
    // Abstract — non-figurative
    { id: 'flowing',    cat: 'abstract',   label: 'Flowing',      prompt: 'flowing abstract liquid forms, slow swirling color gradients, soft ink-in-water',       negative: 'rigid geometry, sharp angles' },
    { id: 'fractal',    cat: 'abstract',   label: 'Fractal',      prompt: 'soft fractal mandala, gentle radial symmetry, muted dreamlike palette',                 negative: 'literal scene, recognizable objects' },
    { id: 'gradient',   cat: 'abstract',   label: 'Gradient',     prompt: 'pure soft color gradient, smooth transitions of indigo violet and amber, no objects',   negative: 'figures, shapes, faces' },
    { id: 'particles',  cat: 'abstract',   label: 'Particles',    prompt: 'floating dust motes, soft light particles drifting, dark moody backdrop',               negative: 'crisp focus, sharp' },
    // Style — rendering medium
    { id: 'watercolor', cat: 'style',      label: 'Watercolor',   prompt: 'soft watercolor wash, gentle color bleeds, subtle paper texture, hand-painted',         negative: 'photographic, digital, sharp' },
    { id: 'oil',        cat: 'style',      label: 'Oil paint',    prompt: 'soft oil painting, romantic style, visible brush strokes, muted impressionist palette', negative: 'photograph, sharp digital' },
    { id: 'charcoal',   cat: 'style',      label: 'Charcoal',     prompt: 'soft charcoal sketch, monochromatic gray tones, atmospheric haze, hand-drawn lines',    negative: 'colorful, photo' },
    { id: 'longexp',    cat: 'style',      label: 'Long exposure',prompt: 'long exposure photograph, soft motion blur trails, smooth water and clouds, dreamlike', negative: 'frozen action, sharp' },
    { id: 'film',       cat: 'style',      label: 'Film',         prompt: 'analog film photograph, soft grain, faded vintage palette, warm muted highlights',     negative: 'crisp digital, oversaturated' },
  ];
  // Quick lookup by id (used by _currentParams + chip toggle logic)
  const BG_STYLE_PRESET_MAP = Object.fromEntries(BG_STYLE_PRESETS.map(p => [p.id, p]));

  // ====================================================================
  // BG TRANSITION REGISTRY — 12 effects, backported from Slideshow spinoff.
  // Adapted for <img> elements (not background-image divs) and for the
  // session-bg dim cap of 0.35 (so text stays readable over the images).
  //
  // Each entry: { label, run(oldEl, newEl, url, durSec) }
  //   • Sets newEl.src, arranges z-index, and starts the visual transition.
  //   • Safe to call concurrently — scheduleCleanup guards against clobber.
  //
  // Fade-through-black/white use the #bgFlash overlay inside #bgLayer,
  // which dims/lightens everything below it (images) without touching UI.
  // ====================================================================
  const BG_ACTIVE_OPACITY = 0.35;

  function _bgPrep(oldEl, newEl) {
    // Reset newEl state so the next transition starts clean
    newEl.style.transition = 'none';
    newEl.style.transform = '';
    newEl.style.filter = '';
    newEl.style.clipPath = '';
    newEl.style.opacity = '0';
    newEl.style.zIndex = '2';
    oldEl.style.zIndex = '1';
    void newEl.offsetWidth; // force reflow so subsequent transitions animate
  }
  function _bgScheduleCleanup(el, delayMs) {
    setTimeout(() => {
      if (el.style.zIndex !== '2') { // don't clobber a slide the next transition just promoted
        el.style.transition = 'none';
        el.style.transform = '';
        el.style.filter = '';
        el.style.clipPath = '';
        el.style.opacity = '0';
        el.classList.remove('active');
      }
    }, delayMs);
  }

  const BG_TRANSITIONS = {
    cut: {
      label: 'Cut',
      run(oldEl, newEl, url, dur) {
        _bgPrep(oldEl, newEl);
        newEl.src = url;
        newEl.style.opacity = String(BG_ACTIVE_OPACITY);
        newEl.classList.add('active');
        oldEl.classList.remove('active');
        oldEl.style.opacity = '0';
      },
    },
    crossfade: {
      label: 'Crossfade',
      run(oldEl, newEl, url, dur) {
        _bgPrep(oldEl, newEl);
        newEl.src = url;
        void newEl.offsetWidth;
        newEl.style.transition = `opacity ${dur}s ease`;
        oldEl.style.transition = `opacity ${dur}s ease`;
        newEl.style.opacity = String(BG_ACTIVE_OPACITY);
        newEl.classList.add('active');
        oldEl.style.opacity = '0';
        oldEl.classList.remove('active');
      },
    },
    fadeBlack: {
      label: 'Fade → black',
      async run(oldEl, newEl, url, dur) {
        const flash = $('bgFlash');
        if (flash) {
          flash.style.background = '#000';
          flash.style.transition = `opacity ${dur / 2}s ease`;
          flash.style.opacity = '1';
        }
        await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, dur * 500));
        _bgPrep(oldEl, newEl);
        newEl.src = url;
        newEl.style.opacity = String(BG_ACTIVE_OPACITY);
        newEl.classList.add('active');
        oldEl.classList.remove('active');
        oldEl.style.opacity = '0';
        await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 50));
        if (flash) flash.style.opacity = '0';
      },
    },
    fadeWhite: {
      label: 'Fade → white',
      async run(oldEl, newEl, url, dur) {
        const flash = $('bgFlash');
        if (flash) {
          flash.style.background = '#fff';
          flash.style.transition = `opacity ${dur / 2}s ease`;
          flash.style.opacity = '1';
        }
        await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, dur * 500));
        _bgPrep(oldEl, newEl);
        newEl.src = url;
        newEl.style.opacity = String(BG_ACTIVE_OPACITY);
        newEl.classList.add('active');
        oldEl.classList.remove('active');
        oldEl.style.opacity = '0';
        await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 50));
        if (flash) {
          flash.style.opacity = '0';
          setTimeout(() => { flash.style.background = '#000'; }, dur * 1000 + 300);
        }
      },
    },
    slideLeft: {
      label: 'Slide left',
      run(oldEl, newEl, url, dur) {
        _bgPrep(oldEl, newEl);
        newEl.src = url;
        newEl.style.opacity = String(BG_ACTIVE_OPACITY);
        newEl.style.transform = 'translateX(100%)';
        void newEl.offsetWidth;
        newEl.style.transition = `transform ${dur}s ease`;
        oldEl.style.transition = `transform ${dur}s ease`;
        newEl.style.transform = 'translateX(0)';
        oldEl.style.transform = 'translateX(-100%)';
        newEl.classList.add('active');
        _bgScheduleCleanup(oldEl, dur * 1000 + 100);
      },
    },
    slideUp: {
      label: 'Slide up',
      run(oldEl, newEl, url, dur) {
        _bgPrep(oldEl, newEl);
        newEl.src = url;
        newEl.style.opacity = String(BG_ACTIVE_OPACITY);
        newEl.style.transform = 'translateY(100%)';
        void newEl.offsetWidth;
        newEl.style.transition = `transform ${dur}s ease`;
        oldEl.style.transition = `transform ${dur}s ease`;
        newEl.style.transform = 'translateY(0)';
        oldEl.style.transform = 'translateY(-100%)';
        newEl.classList.add('active');
        _bgScheduleCleanup(oldEl, dur * 1000 + 100);
      },
    },
    zoomIn: {
      label: 'Zoom in',
      run(oldEl, newEl, url, dur) {
        _bgPrep(oldEl, newEl);
        newEl.src = url;
        newEl.style.transform = 'scale(0.3)';
        void newEl.offsetWidth;
        newEl.style.transition = `opacity ${dur}s ease, transform ${dur}s ease`;
        newEl.style.opacity = String(BG_ACTIVE_OPACITY);
        newEl.style.transform = 'scale(1)';
        newEl.classList.add('active');
        oldEl.style.transition = `opacity ${dur}s ease`;
        oldEl.style.opacity = '0';
        oldEl.classList.remove('active');
      },
    },
    zoomOut: {
      label: 'Zoom out',
      run(oldEl, newEl, url, dur) {
        _bgPrep(oldEl, newEl);
        newEl.src = url;
        newEl.style.transform = 'scale(1.8)';
        void newEl.offsetWidth;
        newEl.style.transition = `opacity ${dur}s ease, transform ${dur}s ease`;
        newEl.style.opacity = String(BG_ACTIVE_OPACITY);
        newEl.style.transform = 'scale(1)';
        newEl.classList.add('active');
        oldEl.style.transition = `opacity ${dur}s ease`;
        oldEl.style.opacity = '0';
        oldEl.classList.remove('active');
      },
    },
    kenburns: {
      label: 'Ken Burns',
      run(oldEl, newEl, url, dur) {
        // Crossfade in, then slow zoom over the full rotation interval
        const intervalSec = Number(state.bgRotationSec) || 45;
        const zooms = [
          { from: 'scale(1.0) translate(0, 0)',      to: 'scale(1.18) translate(-3%, -2%)' },
          { from: 'scale(1.18) translate(-3%, -2%)', to: 'scale(1.0) translate(0, 0)'      },
          { from: 'scale(1.0) translate(0, 0)',      to: 'scale(1.15) translate(3%, -2%)'  },
          { from: 'scale(1.12) translate(2%, 2%)',   to: 'scale(1.0) translate(0, 0)'      },
        ];
        const z = zooms[Math.floor(Math.random() * zooms.length)];
        _bgPrep(oldEl, newEl);
        newEl.src = url;
        newEl.style.transform = z.from;
        void newEl.offsetWidth;
        newEl.style.transition = `opacity ${dur}s ease, transform ${intervalSec}s ease-in-out`;
        newEl.style.opacity = String(BG_ACTIVE_OPACITY);
        newEl.style.transform = z.to;
        newEl.classList.add('active');
        oldEl.style.transition = `opacity ${dur}s ease`;
        oldEl.style.opacity = '0';
        oldEl.classList.remove('active');
      },
    },
    blur: {
      label: 'Blur',
      run(oldEl, newEl, url, dur) {
        _bgPrep(oldEl, newEl);
        newEl.src = url;
        newEl.style.filter = 'blur(30px)';
        void newEl.offsetWidth;
        newEl.style.transition = `opacity ${dur}s ease, filter ${dur}s ease`;
        newEl.style.opacity = String(BG_ACTIVE_OPACITY);
        newEl.style.filter = 'blur(0px)';
        newEl.classList.add('active');
        oldEl.style.transition = `opacity ${dur}s ease, filter ${dur}s ease`;
        oldEl.style.filter = 'blur(30px)';
        oldEl.style.opacity = '0';
        oldEl.classList.remove('active');
        setTimeout(() => { oldEl.style.filter = ''; }, dur * 1000 + 150);
      },
    },
    iris: {
      label: 'Iris',
      run(oldEl, newEl, url, dur) {
        _bgPrep(oldEl, newEl);
        newEl.src = url;
        newEl.style.opacity = String(BG_ACTIVE_OPACITY);
        newEl.style.clipPath = 'circle(0% at 50% 50%)';
        void newEl.offsetWidth;
        newEl.style.transition = `clip-path ${dur}s ease`;
        newEl.style.clipPath = 'circle(150% at 50% 50%)';
        newEl.classList.add('active');
        setTimeout(() => {
          oldEl.classList.remove('active');
          oldEl.style.opacity = '0';
        }, dur * 1000 + 50);
      },
    },
    wipe: {
      label: 'Wipe',
      run(oldEl, newEl, url, dur) {
        _bgPrep(oldEl, newEl);
        newEl.src = url;
        newEl.style.opacity = String(BG_ACTIVE_OPACITY);
        newEl.style.clipPath = 'inset(0 100% 0 0)';
        void newEl.offsetWidth;
        newEl.style.transition = `clip-path ${dur}s ease`;
        newEl.style.clipPath = 'inset(0 0 0 0)';
        newEl.classList.add('active');
        setTimeout(() => {
          oldEl.classList.remove('active');
          oldEl.style.opacity = '0';
        }, dur * 1000 + 50);
      },
    },
  };

  // Default-enabled transition keys — calm ones that suit a hypnosis session.
  // User can toggle any subset via the checkbox grid in Step 5.
  const BG_DEFAULT_ENABLED = ['crossfade', 'fadeBlack', 'kenburns', 'blur'];

  /** True when the user has configured their own AI provider (Anthropic or
   *  OpenAI) with a populated key. The default state is `extAiProvider:'perchance'`
   *  with `_extAiKey:null`. We use this flag to decide whether to send the
   *  advanced bg-image parameters to root.textToImagePlugin — the documented
   *  Perchance plugin contract is `{prompt, negative, style}` only, and any
   *  extras get DSL-evaluated which breaks brace-containing user input. Users
   *  with their own keys have opted into the advanced/risky path. */
  function _isUsingUserApiKey() {
    return state.extAiProvider !== 'perchance' && !!state._extAiKey;
  }

  /** Sync the disabled state of the Step-5 advanced bg controls (orientation /
   *  prompt strength / quality steps) to the current API-key situation. Called
   *  on init, on AI-provider change, and on key-input change. The accompanying
   *  `#advancedBgControlsNote` element shows/hides in lockstep. */
  function _applyAdvancedBgControlGating() {
    const enabled = _isUsingUserApiKey();
    const ids = ['bgOrientationSelect', 'bgGuidanceSlider', 'bgStepsSlider'];
    ids.forEach(id => {
      const el = $(id);
      if (!el) return;
      el.disabled = !enabled;
      el.style.opacity = enabled ? '' : '0.5';
      el.style.cursor  = enabled ? '' : 'not-allowed';
      el.title = enabled
        ? ''
        : 'Inactive on the free Perchance image plugin (which only accepts prompt + negative). Set up your own API key in Step-5 AI provider settings to enable.';
    });
    const note = $('advancedBgControlsNote');
    if (note) note.style.display = enabled ? 'none' : '';
  }

  const bgEngine = {
    source: 'none',
    prompt: '',
    queue: [],
    currentSlot: 0,
    interval: null,
    activeGenerations: 0,   // replaces boolean `generating` — counter for parallel tracking
    rotationSec: 45,
    _localObjectUrl: null,

    /** True if any generation is in flight. Preserved for UI handlers. */
    get generating() { return this.activeGenerations > 0; },

    /** Read current generation parameters from state. Pure — no DOM access.
     *  Centralized so generateOne() + anyone else stays in sync.
     *
     *  Composes three sources into the final prompt and negative prompt:
     *    1. The user's typed prompt (state.bgPrompt)            — highest priority
     *    2. Active style preset chips (state.bgActiveStylePresetIds) — additive
     *    3. Sensible defaults                                    — fallback only
     *  Selected presets contribute both `prompt` and `negative` fragments, so
     *  e.g. picking "Misty" automatically negates "sharp clarity, hard edges".
     *
     *  Generates a fresh client-side seed each call (not -1) so the seed can
     *  be captured into the cache manifest for sort-by-seed and future
     *  "regenerate with same seed" workflows. The plugin still randomizes if
     *  seed is omitted, but explicit seeds make the output reproducible. */
    _currentParams() {
      const resolution = BG_RESOLUTIONS[state.bgOrientation || 'landscape'] || null;

      const userPrompt = (this.prompt || state.bgPrompt || '').trim();
      const userNegative = (state.bgNegativePrompt || '').trim();
      const activeIds = Array.isArray(state.bgActiveStylePresetIds) ? state.bgActiveStylePresetIds : [];
      const activePresets = activeIds.map(id => BG_STYLE_PRESET_MAP[id]).filter(Boolean);

      // Compose: user fragment first (it's primary intent), preset fragments
      // appended in selection order. Empty pieces filtered out so we never
      // emit a stray leading/trailing comma.
      const promptParts = [userPrompt, ...activePresets.map(p => p.prompt)].filter(Boolean);
      const finalPrompt = promptParts.length
        ? promptParts.join(', ')
        : 'abstract dark ethereal misty landscape, dreamlike, hypnotic, soft focus';

      const negativeParts = [userNegative, ...activePresets.map(p => p.negative).filter(Boolean)].filter(Boolean);
      const finalNegative = negativeParts.length
        ? negativeParts.join(', ')
        : 'text, words, letters, bright, harsh, sharp, neon, ugly, deformed';

      // Fresh seed per call. Range matches what most SD-style plugins accept
      // (signed 32-bit positive). Mirrors the persona portrait code's pattern.
      const seed = Math.floor(Math.random() * 2147483647);

      return {
        prompt: finalPrompt,
        negativePrompt: finalNegative,
        resolution,
        guidanceScale: Number(state.bgGuidance) || 7,
        numInferenceSteps: Number(state.bgSteps) || 30,
        seed,
      };
    },

    /** Generate exactly one image. Safe to call concurrently up to BG_MAX_PARALLEL;
     *  additional callers wait briefly for a free slot. Returns objectUrl or null. */
    async generateOne() {
      if (typeof root === 'undefined' || !root.textToImagePlugin) { console.warn('bgEngine: textToImagePlugin not available'); return null; }
      // Fairness spinlock — yield to the event loop until a slot frees up.
      while (this.activeGenerations >= BG_MAX_PARALLEL) {
        await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 150));
      }
      this.activeGenerations++;
      try {
        const params = this._currentParams();
        // The text-to-image-plugin's documented contract is `{prompt, negative, style}`
        // (perchance-api skill §3). The default path for free Perchance users
        // sticks to that contract — sending undocumented extras like
        // `negativePrompt`, `numInferenceSteps`, `steps`, `seed`, `guidanceScale`,
        // `resolution` routes their string values through Perchance's DSL
        // evaluator, which parses `{listName}` as a list reference and throws
        // on brace-containing user input.
        //
        // When the user has supplied their own API key (Anthropic/OpenAI), they
        // get the advanced controls — orientation / prompt-strength / quality
        // steps. The Step-5 sliders are visually disabled for free Perchance
        // users with a note explaining why; see _applyAdvancedBgControlGating().
        const call = {
          prompt:   params.prompt,
          negative: params.negativePrompt,
        };
        if (_isUsingUserApiKey()) {
          call.guidanceScale     = params.guidanceScale;
          call.numInferenceSteps = params.numInferenceSteps;
          call.seed              = params.seed;
          if (params.resolution) call.resolution = params.resolution;
        }
        const result = await root.textToImagePlugin(call);
        const dataUrl = result.dataUrl || String(result);
        if (dataUrl && dataUrl.startsWith('data:')) {
          // Store the actual seed used so the gallery can sort by it and a
          // future "regenerate with same seed" feature has the value to hand.
          await bgCacheStore(dataUrl, params.prompt, params.seed);
          const blob = await fetch(dataUrl).then(r => r.blob());
          const objectUrl = URL.createObjectURL(blob);
          this.queue.push(objectUrl);
          // Diff-bounce ingestion — summarize + ping the image bouncer.
          // Fire-and-forget; never block the image return on this.
          (async () => {
            try {
              const summary = await summarizeImagePrompt({
                prompt: params.prompt,
                negativePrompt: params.negativePrompt,
              });
              if (diffBouncers.bgImage) {
                diffBouncers.bgImage._latestState = {
                  tags: summary.tags,
                  summary: summary.summary,
                  prompt: summary.prompt,
                  ts: Date.now(),
                };
                diffBouncers.bgImage.ping();
              }
            } catch (e) { console.warn('[DB] bgImage ingestion failed:', e); }
          })();
          return objectUrl;
        }
        return null;
      } catch(e) { console.error('bgEngine generate:', e); return null; }
      finally { this.activeGenerations--; }
    },

    /** Fire N image generations in parallel, respecting BG_MAX_PARALLEL.
     *  Returns array of objectUrls (nulls removed) in completion order. */
    async generateBatch(count = BG_MAX_PARALLEL) {
      const n = Math.min(Math.max(1, count), BG_MAX_PARALLEL);
      const results = await Promise.all(
        Array.from({ length: n }, () => this.generateOne())
      );
      return results.filter(Boolean);
    },

    async pregenerate(count = 5) {
      // Load from Blob cache first
      const urls = await bgCacheGetAllUrls();
      if (urls.length > 0) this.queue = urls;
      // Kick off rotation now if we have cached images and source is 'ai'
      if (this.source === 'ai' && this.queue.length > 0) this.startRotation();
      // Generate new ones in parallel batches (up to BG_MAX_PARALLEL at a time)
      const needed = Math.max(0, count - this.queue.length);
      if (needed === 0) return;
      let produced = 0;
      while (produced < needed) {
        if (state.abortFlag) break;
        const batchSize = Math.min(BG_MAX_PARALLEL, needed - produced);
        await this.generateBatch(batchSize);
        produced += batchSize;
        // Kick off rotation as soon as the first batch lands
        if (this.source === 'ai' && this.queue.length > 0 && !this.interval) this.startRotation();
      }
    },

    startRotation() {
      if (this.source !== 'ai' || this.queue.length === 0) return;
      if (this.interval) return; // already rotating — don't stack intervals
      this._playOrder = this._buildPlayOrder();
      this._playCursor = 0;
      this.swap(this.queue[this._playOrder[0]]);
      const secs = Number(state.bgRotationSec) || this.rotationSec;
      // Tick at 1s and accumulate active time (excluding paused / tab-hidden
      // windows) so a long pause doesn't leave a stale image visible for
      // up to 2× rotationSec after resume. The original setInterval
      // approach skipped the swap when paused but didn't reset the timer,
      // so a 60s pause on a 45s rotation could bleed into a 150s wait.
      let lastTickAt = Date.now();
      let activeSinceSwap = 0;
      this.interval = setInterval(() => {
        const now = Date.now();
        // Cap the per-tick delta so a tab-backgrounding wallclock jump
        // doesn't dump a huge chunk into the accumulator on visible.
        const dt = Math.min(1500, now - lastTickAt);
        lastTickAt = now;
        if (state.paused || document.hidden) return;
        activeSinceSwap += dt;
        if (activeSinceSwap < secs * 1000) return;
        activeSinceSwap = 0;
        this._playCursor = (this._playCursor + 1) % this._playOrder.length;
        // Rebuild order at each full wrap — picks up new queue items + re-shuffles
        if (this._playCursor === 0) this._playOrder = this._buildPlayOrder();
        const idx = this._playOrder[this._playCursor];
        if (idx != null && this.queue[idx]) this.swap(this.queue[idx]);
        // Silently generate more in background while under the cap (if enabled)
        if (state.bgAutoGen && !this.generating && this.queue.length < 20) this.generateOne();
      }, 1000);
    },

    /** Rebuild the play-order array — identity for sequential, shuffled for random */
    _buildPlayOrder() {
      const order = this.queue.map((_, i) => i);
      if (state.bgPlaybackOrder === 'random') {
        for (let i = order.length - 1; i > 0; i--) {
          const j = Math.floor(Math.random() * (i + 1));
          [order[i], order[j]] = [order[j], order[i]];
        }
      }
      return order;
    },

    stopRotation() {
      if (this.interval) { clearInterval(this.interval); this.interval = null; }
    },

    /** Swap to a new image using a random enabled transition.
     *  Replaces the old single-style crossfade(). Falls back to crossfade if
     *  the randomly-picked one throws or no transitions are enabled. */
    swap(src) {
      const imgs = [$('bgImg0'), $('bgImg1')];
      if (!imgs[0] || !imgs[1] || !src) return;
      const oldEl = imgs[this.currentSlot];
      const newEl = imgs[1 - this.currentSlot];
      const enabled = Array.isArray(state.bgEnabledTransitions) && state.bgEnabledTransitions.length
        ? state.bgEnabledTransitions
        : BG_DEFAULT_ENABLED;
      const validEnabled = enabled.filter(k => BG_TRANSITIONS[k]);
      const pick = validEnabled.length
        ? validEnabled[Math.floor(Math.random() * validEnabled.length)]
        : 'crossfade';
      const tx = BG_TRANSITIONS[pick] || BG_TRANSITIONS.crossfade;
      const dur = Number(state.bgTransitionDuration) || 3;
      try {
        Promise.resolve(tx.run(oldEl, newEl, src, dur)).catch(e => {
          console.error('bg transition async fail:', pick, e);
        });
      } catch (e) {
        console.error('bg transition sync fail:', pick, e);
        try { BG_TRANSITIONS.crossfade.run(oldEl, newEl, src, dur); } catch {}
      }
      this.currentSlot = 1 - this.currentSlot;
    },

    /** Legacy alias — some callers still reference crossfade() directly. */
    crossfade(src) { this.swap(src); },

    setStaticImage(src) {
      const img = $('bgImg0');
      if (img) { img.src = src; img.classList.add('active'); }
      if ($('bgImg1')) $('bgImg1').classList.remove('active');
    },

    setLocalFile(file) {
      if (this._localObjectUrl) URL.revokeObjectURL(this._localObjectUrl);
      this._localObjectUrl = URL.createObjectURL(file);
      if (file.type.startsWith('video/')) {
        this.source = 'local-video';
        const vid = $('bgLocalVideo');
        if (vid) { vid.src = this._localObjectUrl; vid.style.display = 'block'; vid.play().catch(() => {}); }
      } else {
        this.source = 'local-image';
        this.setStaticImage(this._localObjectUrl);
      }
    },

    setYouTube(url) {
      const match = url.match(/(?:v=|youtu\.be\/|\/embed\/)([a-zA-Z0-9_-]{11})/);
      if (!match) return;
      const vid = match[1];
      const wrap = $('bgYoutubeWrap');
      const iframe = $('bgYoutube');
      if (wrap && iframe) {
        const cc = $('bgSubtitleToggle')?.checked ? 1 : 0;
        iframe.src = `https://www.youtube.com/embed/${vid}?autoplay=1&mute=1&loop=1&playlist=${vid}&controls=0&showinfo=0&cc_load_policy=${cc}`;
        wrap.style.display = 'block';
      }
    },

    activate() {
      const layer = $('bgLayer');
      if (layer) layer.style.display = '';
      document.body.classList.add('bg-active');
      if (this.source === 'ai') this.startRotation();
      else if (this.source === 'local-image' || this.source === 'url') { /* already set */ }
      else if (this.source === 'local-video') { const v = $('bgLocalVideo'); if (v && v.src) v.play().catch(() => {}); }
      else if (this.source === 'youtube') { const y = $('bgYoutubeUrl'); if (y?.value) this.setYouTube(y.value); }
    },

    clear() {
      this.stopRotation();
      // Revoke object URLs from queue to free memory
      this.queue.forEach(u => { try { URL.revokeObjectURL(u); } catch {} });
      this.queue = [];
      const imgs = [$('bgImg0'), $('bgImg1')];
      imgs.forEach(i => { if (i) { i.src = ''; i.classList.remove('active'); } });
      const vid = $('bgLocalVideo'); if (vid) { vid.pause(); vid.src = ''; vid.style.display = 'none'; }
      const yt = $('bgYoutubeWrap'); if (yt) { yt.style.display = 'none'; }
      const yf = $('bgYoutube'); if (yf) yf.src = '';
      if (this._localObjectUrl) { URL.revokeObjectURL(this._localObjectUrl); this._localObjectUrl = null; }
      const layer = $('bgLayer'); if (layer) layer.style.display = 'none';
      document.body.classList.remove('bg-active');
    }
  };

  // ── Background image cache — per-image Blob storage ──
  // Manifest at 'bg_manifest' holds metadata [{id, prompt, createdAt, seed?, favorited?}].
  // Each image stored separately at 'bg_img_<id>' as a Blob (no base64 overhead).
  //
  // `seed` and `favorited` are optional fields — entries persisted before this
  // feature shipped won't have them, so all readers must use `?? null` /
  // `=== true` patterns rather than expecting them to be present.
  const BG_MANIFEST_KEY = 'bg_manifest';
  // Cache cap. Sized for a comfortable 90-minute session given that users'
  // eyes are closed for most of it: 80 images × default 45s rotation =
  // 60 minutes before a sequential repeat, so a typical session might see
  // one wrap in its final third — and even then, only a handful of
  // moments where eyes are open. Random-order playback feels effectively
  // non-repeating at this size. Storage cost is ~8-15MB total at typical
  // 768×512 JPEG sizes — well under any device's IDB pressure threshold.
  // Favorited entries are protected from eviction (see below).
  const BG_MAX_IMAGES = 80;

  async function bgCacheStore(dataUrl, prompt, seed) {
    const id = 'bg_' + Date.now() + '_' + Math.random().toString(36).slice(2, 6);
    const blob = await fetch(dataUrl).then(r => r.blob());
    const manifest = (await idbGet(BG_MANIFEST_KEY)) || [];

    // Step 1 — save the new blob first. If anything fails after this point
    // the worst case is an orphan blob (small storage leak) rather than a
    // manifest entry that points at a missing blob (broken thumbnail).
    await idbSet('bg_img_' + id, blob);

    // Step 2 — decide evictions in memory before touching IDB further. We
    // evict BEFORE appending the new entry so the new entry can never become
    // its own eviction target (which would happen if every existing slot
    // is favorited and we'd already pushed our fresh non-favorite first).
    // Walk oldest-to-newest preferring non-favorited entries; only fall
    // back to evicting a favorite when literally every slot is favorited.
    const evictedEntries = [];
    while (manifest.length >= BG_MAX_IMAGES) {
      let evictIdx = manifest.findIndex(m => !m.favorited);
      if (evictIdx === -1) {
        // Every entry is favorited — evict the absolute oldest as a last
        // resort. Rare; only happens if the user has explicitly favorited
        // every single slot. The new image will arrive un-favorited and
        // the user can favorite it manually if they want it kept.
        console.warn('[bgCache] all entries are favorited; evicting oldest to make room');
        evictIdx = 0;
      }
      const [removed] = manifest.splice(evictIdx, 1);
      if (removed) evictedEntries.push(removed);
    }

    // Step 3 — append the new entry and persist the manifest. After this
    // write the cache is in a fully consistent state: every entry in the
    // manifest refers to a blob that exists in IDB.
    manifest.push({
      id,
      prompt: prompt || '',
      createdAt: Date.now(),
      seed: (typeof seed === 'number' && Number.isFinite(seed)) ? seed : null,
      favorited: false,
    });
    await idbSet(BG_MANIFEST_KEY, manifest);

    // Step 4 — best-effort cleanup of evicted blobs. If the tab crashes
    // between steps 3 and 4 the orphan blobs simply leak storage; they're
    // no longer referenced by the manifest so they can't cause broken
    // thumbnails. A future self-healing sweep on app start could reclaim
    // them by listing IDB keys with the 'bg_img_' prefix and dropping any
    // not present in the manifest.
    for (const old of evictedEntries) {
      try { await idbDel('bg_img_' + old.id); }
      catch (e) { console.warn('[bgCache] failed to delete evicted blob', old.id, e); }
    }

    // Update count badge + refresh gallery if open (Phase 6 desync fix)
    const countEl = $('bgCacheCount'); if (countEl) countEl.textContent = manifest.length;
    _maybeRefreshGallery();
    return id;
  }
  async function bgCacheGetManifest() { return (await idbGet(BG_MANIFEST_KEY)) || []; }
  async function bgCacheGetUrl(id) {
    const blob = await idbGet('bg_img_' + id);
    return blob ? URL.createObjectURL(blob) : null;
  }
  async function bgCacheGetAllUrls() {
    const manifest = await bgCacheGetManifest();
    const urls = [];
    for (const entry of manifest) {
      const url = await bgCacheGetUrl(entry.id);
      if (url) urls.push(url);
    }
    return urls;
  }
  async function bgCacheDelete(id) {
    await idbDel('bg_img_' + id);
    const manifest = (await idbGet(BG_MANIFEST_KEY)) || [];
    const next = manifest.filter(m => m.id !== id);
    await idbSet(BG_MANIFEST_KEY, next);
    const countEl = $('bgCacheCount'); if (countEl) countEl.textContent = next.length;
    _maybeRefreshGallery();
  }
  async function bgCacheClear() {
    const manifest = (await idbGet(BG_MANIFEST_KEY)) || [];
    for (const entry of manifest) await idbDel('bg_img_' + entry.id);
    await idbSet(BG_MANIFEST_KEY, []);
    const countEl = $('bgCacheCount'); if (countEl) countEl.textContent = '0';
    _maybeRefreshGallery();
  }
  async function bgCacheDownload(id) {
    const blob = await idbGet('bg_img_' + id);
    if (!blob) return;
    const a = document.createElement('a');
    a.href = URL.createObjectURL(blob);
    a.download = id + '.png';
    a.click();
    setTimeout(() => URL.revokeObjectURL(a.href), 5000);
  }
  /** Toggle the `favorited` flag on a manifest entry. No-op for unknown ids.
   *  Returns the new favorited value (or null if the entry wasn't found). */
  async function bgCacheToggleFavorite(id) {
    const manifest = (await idbGet(BG_MANIFEST_KEY)) || [];
    const entry = manifest.find(m => m.id === id);
    if (!entry) return null;
    entry.favorited = !entry.favorited;
    await idbSet(BG_MANIFEST_KEY, manifest);
    return entry.favorited;
  }
  /** Return a sorted+filtered VIEW of the manifest. Doesn't mutate storage.
   *  - mode: 'newest' | 'oldest' | 'favorites' | 'seed'
   *  - favOnly: if true, drop non-favorited entries entirely
   *  Used by renderBgGallery and the lightbox to walk a consistent ordering. */
  function bgCacheSortManifest(manifest, mode, favOnly) {
    let view = (manifest || []).slice();
    if (favOnly) view = view.filter(m => m.favorited === true);
    switch (mode) {
      case 'oldest':
        view.sort((a, b) => (a.createdAt || 0) - (b.createdAt || 0));
        break;
      case 'favorites':
        // Favorites first (descending); within each group, newest first.
        view.sort((a, b) => {
          const fa = a.favorited === true ? 1 : 0;
          const fb = b.favorited === true ? 1 : 0;
          if (fa !== fb) return fb - fa;
          return (b.createdAt || 0) - (a.createdAt || 0);
        });
        break;
      case 'seed':
        // Numerical seed sort; entries without a seed sink to the bottom.
        view.sort((a, b) => {
          const sa = (typeof a.seed === 'number') ? a.seed : Infinity;
          const sb = (typeof b.seed === 'number') ? b.seed : Infinity;
          if (sa !== sb) return sa - sb;
          return (b.createdAt || 0) - (a.createdAt || 0);
        });
        break;
      case 'newest':
      default:
        view.sort((a, b) => (b.createdAt || 0) - (a.createdAt || 0));
        break;
    }
    return view;
  }

  // ====================================================================
  // PERSONA PORTRAITS — generated, cached portraits per persona
  // ====================================================================
  // Each persona can have an optional generated portrait, stored as a blob
  // in IDB under `persona_portrait_<id>`. Generated on demand via the
  // existing textToImagePlugin (same one used for backgrounds), so requires
  // the plugin to be loaded — non-Perchance hosts get a graceful no-op.
  //
  // Generation is MANUAL — triggered by a button in the persona editor or
  // detail panel — to avoid surprise API costs. Once cached, portraits
  // persist forever (until manually regenerated or persona deleted).
  // No automatic background generation.
  //
  // Resolves "Persona portraits" sub-item of #7 textToImagePlugin extensions.
  // Reuses the bgCache pattern: blob stored at predictable IDB key, lookup
  // returns objectURL on demand.
  // ====================================================================
  const PORTRAIT_KEY = id => 'persona_portrait_' + id;

  // Style hints by persona category — feed into the prompt to give each
  // category its own visual language. Empty string = no hint (use base only).
  const PORTRAIT_STYLE_BY_CATEGORY = {
    contemplative: 'soft watercolor wash, muted blue-gray palette, gentle indirect lighting, calm contemplative expression',
    showman:       'theatrical lighting with warm rim glow, confident charismatic expression, slightly dramatic angle',
    somatic:       'soft body-aware silhouette, organic flowing forms, warm earth tones, grounded presence',
    authoritarian: 'high contrast charcoal sketch, formal upright pose, focused steady gaze, monochromatic',
  };
  const PORTRAIT_NEGATIVE = 'text, watermark, logo, signature, nsfw, multiple faces, distorted face, anatomical errors, low quality, blurry, deformed, nudity';

  /** Build a portrait prompt from persona metadata. Combines the name + tagline
   *  + category-specific style hint into a stylized half-body portrait prompt. */
  function _buildPortraitPrompt(persona) {
    const styleHint = PORTRAIT_STYLE_BY_CATEGORY[persona.category] || PORTRAIT_STYLE_BY_CATEGORY.contemplative;
    // Sanitize tagline — strip parens like "(custom)" that aren't visual cues
    const tagline = (persona.tagline || '').replace(/\([^)]*\)/g, '').trim() || 'a hypnotic guide';
    // v2 schema: per-persona image prompt prefix/suffix overrides. Trimmed and
    // joined with commas if present. The category style hint is always included
    // unless the user has provided BOTH custom prefix and suffix (in which case
    // they've taken explicit control and the category default is omitted).
    const prefix = (persona.imagePromptPrefix || '').trim();
    const suffix = (persona.imagePromptSuffix || '').trim();
    const useCategoryHint = !(prefix && suffix); // skip if user has overridden both ends
    const parts = [
      'stylized minimal half-body portrait illustration',
      prefix,
      tagline,
      useCategoryHint ? styleHint : '',
      'soft glowing background, looking ahead with serene focus, ethereal mood, fine art illustration',
      suffix,
      'no background text, no signature',
    ].filter(Boolean);
    return parts.join(', ');
  }

  /** Get cached portrait URL for a persona, or null if not cached.
   *  Returns objectURL — caller is responsible for revoking when done. */
  async function personaPortraitGet(personaId) {
    try {
      const blob = await idbGet(PORTRAIT_KEY(personaId));
      return blob ? URL.createObjectURL(blob) : null;
    } catch { return null; }
  }

  /** Cache a portrait blob for a persona. Overwrites any existing portrait. */
  async function personaPortraitSet(personaId, blob) {
    await idbSet(PORTRAIT_KEY(personaId), blob);
  }

  /** Delete a cached portrait. Called when a persona is deleted. */
  async function personaPortraitDelete(personaId) {
    await idbDel(PORTRAIT_KEY(personaId));
  }

  /** Generate a portrait for a persona. Async — caller awaits the objectURL.
   *  Returns null if the plugin is unavailable, opt-in is off, or generation
   *  fails. Notifies the user on each outcome so they understand what happened.
   *  Per-persona dedup: if a generation is already running for this persona,
   *  returns the existing promise rather than starting a second call. */
  const _portraitInflight = new Map(); // personaId → Promise<URL|null>
  async function generatePersonaPortrait(persona) {
    if (!persona || !persona.id) return null;
    // Dedup: in-flight call for the same persona returns the existing promise
    if (_portraitInflight.has(persona.id)) return _portraitInflight.get(persona.id);

    if (typeof root === 'undefined' || !root.textToImagePlugin) {
      notify.warn('Image plugin unavailable — portraits require Perchance hosting.');
      return null;
    }
    if (!state.remoteProcessingOptIn) {
      notify.warn('Portraits require remote processing opt-in (in Options).');
      return null;
    }

    const promise = (async () => {
      try {
        const prompt = _buildPortraitPrompt(persona);
        // Match the documented plugin contract from perchance-api skill §3.
        // Same rationale as bgEngine.generateOne(): undocumented aliases like
        // `negativePrompt`, `numInferenceSteps`, `steps`, `seed`, `guidanceScale`
        // are routed through Perchance's DSL evaluator by the plugin and break
        // any string containing literal `{` or `}`. Custom personas can have
        // user-typed text in imagePromptPrefix/imagePromptSuffix that flows into
        // this prompt, so the same protection applies.
        const result = await root.textToImagePlugin({
          prompt,
          negative: PORTRAIT_NEGATIVE,
        });
        const dataUrl = result?.dataUrl || String(result || '');
        if (!dataUrl || !dataUrl.startsWith('data:')) {
          notify.warn(`Portrait generation returned no image for ${persona.name}.`);
          return null;
        }
        const blob = await fetch(dataUrl).then(r => r.blob());
        await personaPortraitSet(persona.id, blob);
        notify.success(`Portrait generated for ${persona.name}.`);
        return URL.createObjectURL(blob);
      } catch (e) {
        console.warn('[persona portrait] generation failed:', e);
        notify.error('Portrait generation failed — see console.');
        return null;
      } finally {
        _portraitInflight.delete(persona.id);
      }
    })();
    _portraitInflight.set(persona.id, promise);
    return promise;
  }

  // Gallery render generation — each call increments. Concurrent thumbnail
  // loads check this counter and bail out if a newer render has started.
  // Prevents the desync race where a delete or new-image mid-render leaves
  // the DOM and IDB in disagreement.
  let _galleryRenderGen = 0;
  let _galleryOpen = false;

  async function renderBgGallery() {
    const grid = $('bgGalleryGrid');
    const info = $('bgGalleryInfo');
    const countEl = $('bgCacheCount');
    if (!grid) return;
    // Snapshot a generation id; if another render starts during our async
    // work, our id will be stale and we'll bail before mutating the DOM.
    const myGen = ++_galleryRenderGen;
    const manifestRaw = await bgCacheGetManifest();
    if (myGen !== _galleryRenderGen) return; // a newer render started
    if (countEl) countEl.textContent = manifestRaw.length;

    // Sync the toolbar controls to current state on each render so opening
    // the gallery from anywhere shows the correct active sort/filter.
    const sortSelect = $('bgGallerySortSelect');
    const sortMode = state.bgGallerySort || 'newest';
    if (sortSelect && sortSelect.value !== sortMode) sortSelect.value = sortMode;
    const favBtn = $('bgGalleryFavFilterBtn');
    const favOnly = state.bgGalleryFavOnly === true;
    if (favBtn) {
      favBtn.classList.toggle('active', favOnly);
      favBtn.setAttribute('aria-pressed', favOnly ? 'true' : 'false');
    }

    const view = bgCacheSortManifest(manifestRaw, sortMode, favOnly);
    const favCount = manifestRaw.filter(m => m.favorited === true).length;
    if (info) {
      const totalStr = `${manifestRaw.length} image${manifestRaw.length === 1 ? '' : 's'}`;
      const favStr = favCount > 0 ? ` · ${favCount} favorite${favCount === 1 ? '' : 's'}` : '';
      const filterStr = favOnly && favCount !== manifestRaw.length ? ` · showing ${view.length}` : '';
      info.textContent = totalStr + favStr + filterStr;
    }
    if (!view.length) {
      grid.innerHTML = manifestRaw.length === 0
        ? '<div class="sublabel" style="text-align:center;padding:20px">No cached images yet. Generate some with the preview button or during a session.</div>'
        : '<div class="sublabel" style="text-align:center;padding:20px">No images match the current filter.</div>';
      return;
    }
    // Build grid with placeholder thumbnails, load blobs lazily.
    // Each .bg-gallery-item carries data-id for the action buttons + data-idx
    // (relative to the current sorted view) so clicking the body opens the
    // lightbox at the right position in the same ordering the user sees.
    grid.innerHTML = view.map((c, idx) => {
      const seedTag = (typeof c.seed === 'number') ? `<div class="bg-seed-tag" title="seed">#${c.seed}</div>` : '';
      const favClass = c.favorited === true ? ' is-fav' : '';
      const favActive = c.favorited === true ? ' active' : '';
      const favIcon = c.favorited === true ? '★' : '☆';
      return `
      <div class="bg-gallery-item${favClass}" data-id="${c.id}" data-idx="${idx}" title="${c.prompt ? c.prompt.replace(/"/g, '&quot;').slice(0, 200) : 'click to view'}">
        <img alt="" loading="lazy" style="background:#111">
        <button class="bg-del" data-id="${c.id}" title="delete" aria-label="delete">✕</button>
        <button class="bg-dl"  data-id="${c.id}" title="download" aria-label="download">⬇</button>
        <button class="bg-fav${favActive}" data-id="${c.id}" title="${c.favorited === true ? 'unfavorite' : 'favorite'}" aria-label="favorite" aria-pressed="${c.favorited === true ? 'true' : 'false'}">${favIcon}</button>
        ${seedTag}
      </div>`;
    }).join('');
    // Load thumbnails lazily — bail per-iteration if a newer render started OR
    // if the matching DOM node is gone (item was deleted concurrently).
    for (const entry of view) {
      if (myGen !== _galleryRenderGen) return;
      const url = await bgCacheGetUrl(entry.id);
      if (myGen !== _galleryRenderGen) {
        if (url) URL.revokeObjectURL(url); // we're not going to use this — release
        return;
      }
      const img = grid.querySelector(`.bg-gallery-item[data-id="${entry.id}"] img`);
      if (img && url) {
        img.src = url;
      } else if (url) {
        // Item vanished from the DOM (deleted while we were fetching its
        // thumbnail) — release the object URL we just created.
        URL.revokeObjectURL(url);
      }
    }
    // Per-button handlers — stop propagation so clicking a button doesn't
    // also trigger the body's lightbox-open click.
    grid.querySelectorAll('.bg-del').forEach(btn => {
      btn.addEventListener('click', async (e) => {
        e.stopPropagation();
        await bgCacheDelete(btn.dataset.id);
        // bgCacheDelete already triggers a re-render via _maybeRefreshGallery,
        // but call directly too in case the event hook is bypassed.
        renderBgGallery();
      });
    });
    grid.querySelectorAll('.bg-dl').forEach(btn => {
      btn.addEventListener('click', (e) => {
        e.stopPropagation();
        bgCacheDownload(btn.dataset.id);
      });
    });
    grid.querySelectorAll('.bg-fav').forEach(btn => {
      btn.addEventListener('click', async (e) => {
        e.stopPropagation();
        await bgCacheToggleFavorite(btn.dataset.id);
        renderBgGallery();
      });
    });
    // Click on the item body (anywhere not on a button) opens the lightbox at
    // this position in the sorted view.
    grid.querySelectorAll('.bg-gallery-item').forEach(itemEl => {
      itemEl.addEventListener('click', (e) => {
        // If the click landed on (or inside) one of the action buttons, the
        // button's own handler already handled it via stopPropagation, but
        // belt-and-braces — a defensive closest() check.
        if (e.target.closest('button')) return;
        const idx = Number(itemEl.dataset.idx);
        if (Number.isFinite(idx)) bgLightbox.open(view, idx);
      });
    });
  }

  /** Called by bgCacheStore / bgCacheDelete after they mutate the manifest.
   *  No-op when the inline gallery is collapsed; otherwise triggers a fresh
   *  render which uses the generation-token guard to invalidate any in-flight
   *  load loop. */
  function _maybeRefreshGallery() {
    if (_galleryOpen) renderBgGallery();
  }

  // ====================================================================
  // STYLE PRESET CHIP UI
  // ====================================================================
  // Builds the chip row inside #bgStyleChips from BG_STYLE_PRESETS, restores
  // the active selections from state.bgActiveStylePresetIds, and wires click
  // handlers for toggle behavior. Idempotent — safe to call again on settings
  // import; rebuilds from scratch.
  function buildBgStyleChips() {
    const root = $('bgStyleChips');
    const countEl = $('bgStyleChipCount');
    const clearBtn = $('bgStyleClearBtn');
    if (!root) return;
    const active = new Set(Array.isArray(state.bgActiveStylePresetIds) ? state.bgActiveStylePresetIds : []);
    // Group presets by category to render one row per category.
    const byCat = {};
    for (const cat of BG_STYLE_CATEGORIES) byCat[cat.id] = [];
    for (const preset of BG_STYLE_PRESETS) {
      if (!byCat[preset.cat]) byCat[preset.cat] = [];
      byCat[preset.cat].push(preset);
    }
    const rows = BG_STYLE_CATEGORIES.map(cat => {
      const items = byCat[cat.id] || [];
      if (!items.length) return '';
      const chips = items.map(p => {
        const cls = active.has(p.id) ? 'bg-style-chip active' : 'bg-style-chip';
        // Tooltip shows the actual fragment so users can preview what the
        // chip contributes before tapping it.
        const tip = (p.prompt || '').replace(/"/g, '&quot;');
        return `<button type="button" class="${cls}" data-style-id="${p.id}" title="${tip}" aria-pressed="${active.has(p.id) ? 'true' : 'false'}">${p.label}</button>`;
      }).join('');
      return `<div class="bg-style-cat-row"><span class="bg-style-cat-label">${cat.label}</span>${chips}</div>`;
    }).join('');
    root.innerHTML = rows;
    if (countEl) countEl.textContent = active.size > 0 ? `· ${active.size} active` : '';
    if (clearBtn) clearBtn.disabled = active.size === 0;
    // Wire chip toggles. We rebuild the whole row on each click rather than
    // mutating in place — simpler and the row is tiny.
    root.querySelectorAll('.bg-style-chip').forEach(chip => {
      chip.addEventListener('click', () => {
        const id = chip.dataset.styleId;
        if (!id) return;
        const cur = new Set(Array.isArray(state.bgActiveStylePresetIds) ? state.bgActiveStylePresetIds : []);
        if (cur.has(id)) cur.delete(id); else cur.add(id);
        state.bgActiveStylePresetIds = Array.from(cur);
        buildBgStyleChips();
        try { saveUserSettings(); } catch (e) { /* save is best-effort here */ }
        // Update the diff-bounce ingestion so the recent-changes log reflects
        // style picks alongside typed prompt edits.
        try {
          if (diffBouncers.bgImage) diffBouncers.bgImage.ping?.();
        } catch (e) { /* never break the toggle on telemetry */ }
      });
    });
  }
  // Clear button — wipes all active chip selections.
  document.addEventListener('click', (e) => {
    if (e.target && e.target.id === 'bgStyleClearBtn' && !e.target.disabled) {
      state.bgActiveStylePresetIds = [];
      buildBgStyleChips();
      try { saveUserSettings(); } catch (err) {}
    }
  });

  // ====================================================================
  // GALLERY LIGHTBOX
  // ====================================================================
  // Self-contained module managing the fullscreen image viewer. Opened from
  // renderBgGallery() with a sorted view + index. Handles wheel/pinch zoom,
  // drag-pan, keyboard nav, and toggles favorite/download against the same
  // bgCache* helpers the gallery uses.
  //
  // State is intentionally kept on this object (not in `state`) because it
  // resets every time the lightbox opens and shouldn't be persisted.
  const bgLightbox = {
    view: [],            // sorted manifest snapshot at the moment of opening
    index: 0,            // current position in `view`
    _bound: false,       // event-handler binding guard
    // Fullscreen-mode state — only used when bgFullscreenModal is visible.
    _fsScale: 1,         // current zoom
    _fsPanX: 0, _fsPanY: 0,
    _fsMinScale: 1,
    _fsMaxScale: 8,
    _fsDrag: null,       // mouse/single-finger pan tracking
    _fsPinch: null,      // two-finger pinch tracking
    _fsSwipe: null,      // single-finger swipe tracking (only when _fsScale === 1)

    /** Open the popup with a sorted-manifest view + starting index. */
    open(view, index) {
      if (!Array.isArray(view) || view.length === 0) return;
      this.view = view;
      this.index = Math.max(0, Math.min(index | 0, view.length - 1));
      this._bindOnce();
      const modal = $('bgLightboxModal');
      if (modal) modal.classList.add('visible');
      document.body.style.overflow = 'hidden'; // suppress page scroll behind
      this._loadCurrent();
    },
    close() {
      // Close everything — fullscreen too if it's open.
      this.exitFullscreen({ silent: true });
      const modal = $('bgLightboxModal');
      if (modal) modal.classList.remove('visible');
      document.body.style.overflow = '';
      // Release blob URLs we created for both the preview img and fs img.
      for (const id of ['bgLightboxImg', 'bgFsImg']) {
        const img = $(id);
        if (img && img.src && img.src.startsWith('blob:')) {
          try { URL.revokeObjectURL(img.src); } catch {}
          img.src = '';
        }
      }
    },
    next() {
      if (this.index >= this.view.length - 1) return;
      this.index++;
      this._loadCurrent();
      if (this._isFullscreen()) this._fsLoadCurrent();
    },
    prev() {
      if (this.index <= 0) return;
      this.index--;
      this._loadCurrent();
      if (this._isFullscreen()) this._fsLoadCurrent();
    },

    /** Load the current entry's image + metadata into the preview popup. */
    async _loadCurrent() {
      const entry = this.view[this.index];
      if (!entry) return;
      const img = $('bgLightboxImg');
      const meta = $('bgLightboxMeta');
      const promptEl = $('bgLightboxPrompt');
      const favBtn = $('bgLightboxFavorite');
      const prevBtn = $('bgLightboxPrev');
      const nextBtn = $('bgLightboxNext');
      // Revoke previous blob URL before assigning a new one
      if (img && img.src && img.src.startsWith('blob:')) {
        try { URL.revokeObjectURL(img.src); } catch {}
      }
      const url = await bgCacheGetUrl(entry.id);
      if (img && url) img.src = url;
      if (meta) {
        const parts = [`${this.index + 1} / ${this.view.length}`];
        if (typeof entry.seed === 'number') parts.push(`seed ${entry.seed}`);
        if (entry.createdAt) {
          const d = new Date(entry.createdAt);
          parts.push(d.toLocaleDateString() + ' ' + d.toLocaleTimeString([], { hour: '2-digit', minute: '2-digit' }));
        }
        meta.textContent = parts.join(' · ');
      }
      if (promptEl) {
        // Truncate very long prompts so the popup body doesn't grow.
        const p = entry.prompt || '';
        promptEl.textContent = p.length > 240 ? p.slice(0, 237) + '…' : p;
      }
      if (favBtn) {
        favBtn.classList.toggle('active', entry.favorited === true);
        favBtn.textContent = entry.favorited === true ? '★ favorited' : '☆ favorite';
        favBtn.title = entry.favorited === true ? 'unfavorite (f)' : 'favorite (f)';
      }
      if (prevBtn) prevBtn.disabled = this.index <= 0;
      if (nextBtn) nextBtn.disabled = this.index >= this.view.length - 1;
    },

    /** Enter fullscreen mode — open the dedicated overlay on top of the
     *  preview popup. Preview stays mounted so closing fullscreen returns
     *  the user there at the same view/index. */
    enterFullscreen() {
      const fs = $('bgFullscreenModal');
      if (!fs) return;
      this._bindFullscreenOnce();
      fs.classList.add('visible');
      this._fsResetZoom();
      this._fsLoadCurrent();
    },
    exitFullscreen(opts = {}) {
      const fs = $('bgFullscreenModal');
      if (!fs || !fs.classList.contains('visible')) return;
      fs.classList.remove('visible');
      // Release the fullscreen image's blob URL so we don't leak when the
      // preview re-loads from the same source independently. If we're being
      // called from close() (silent), the close handler will revoke too —
      // double-revoke is a no-op.
      const fsImg = $('bgFsImg');
      if (fsImg && fsImg.src && fsImg.src.startsWith('blob:')) {
        try { URL.revokeObjectURL(fsImg.src); } catch {}
        fsImg.src = '';
      }
      this._fsDrag = null;
      this._fsPinch = null;
      this._fsSwipe = null;
    },
    _isFullscreen() {
      return $('bgFullscreenModal')?.classList.contains('visible') === true;
    },

    /** Load current entry into the fullscreen overlay. */
    async _fsLoadCurrent() {
      const entry = this.view[this.index];
      if (!entry) return;
      const img = $('bgFsImg');
      const meta = $('bgFsMeta');
      const prevBtn = $('bgFsPrev');
      const nextBtn = $('bgFsNext');
      if (img && img.src && img.src.startsWith('blob:')) {
        try { URL.revokeObjectURL(img.src); } catch {}
      }
      const url = await bgCacheGetUrl(entry.id);
      if (img && url) {
        img.src = url;
        img.classList.add('no-transition');
        requestAnimationFrame(() => {
          this._fsResetZoom();
          requestAnimationFrame(() => img.classList.remove('no-transition'));
        });
      }
      if (meta) {
        const parts = [`${this.index + 1} / ${this.view.length}`];
        if (typeof entry.seed === 'number') parts.push(`seed ${entry.seed}`);
        meta.textContent = parts.join(' · ');
      }
      if (prevBtn) prevBtn.disabled = this.index <= 0;
      if (nextBtn) nextBtn.disabled = this.index >= this.view.length - 1;
    },

    _fsApplyTransform() {
      const img = $('bgFsImg');
      if (!img) return;
      img.style.transform = `translate(${this._fsPanX}px, ${this._fsPanY}px) scale(${this._fsScale})`;
      const lbl = $('bgFsZoomLabel');
      if (lbl) lbl.textContent = `${Math.round(this._fsScale * 100)}%`;
    },
    _fsResetZoom() {
      this._fsScale = 1; this._fsPanX = 0; this._fsPanY = 0;
      this._fsApplyTransform();
    },
    /** Zoom around a screen-space focal point — keeps the pixel under the
     *  cursor anchored. */
    _fsZoomAt(factor, focalX, focalY) {
      const newScale = Math.max(this._fsMinScale, Math.min(this._fsMaxScale, this._fsScale * factor));
      if (newScale === this._fsScale) return;
      const stage = $('bgFsStage');
      if (!stage) { this._fsScale = newScale; this._fsApplyTransform(); return; }
      const rect = stage.getBoundingClientRect();
      const sx = focalX - rect.left - rect.width / 2;
      const sy = focalY - rect.top  - rect.height / 2;
      const ratio = newScale / this._fsScale;
      this._fsPanX = sx - (sx - this._fsPanX) * ratio;
      this._fsPanY = sy - (sy - this._fsPanY) * ratio;
      this._fsScale = newScale;
      this._fsApplyTransform();
    },

    /** Bind preview-popup event handlers once. */
    _bindOnce() {
      if (this._bound) return;
      this._bound = true;
      const self = this;
      const modal = $('bgLightboxModal');

      // Buttons
      $('bgLightboxClose')?.addEventListener('click', () => self.close());
      $('bgLightboxPrev') ?.addEventListener('click', () => self.prev());
      $('bgLightboxNext') ?.addEventListener('click', () => self.next());
      $('bgLightboxFullscreen')?.addEventListener('click', () => self.enterFullscreen());
      $('bgLightboxFavorite')?.addEventListener('click', async () => {
        const entry = self.view[self.index];
        if (!entry) return;
        const newVal = await bgCacheToggleFavorite(entry.id);
        if (newVal !== null) {
          entry.favorited = newVal;
          self._loadCurrent();
          renderBgGallery();
        }
      });
      $('bgLightboxDelete')?.addEventListener('click', async () => {
        const entry = self.view[self.index];
        if (!entry) return;
        if (!await confirmAsync('Delete this image from the cache? This can\'t be undone.')) return;
        await bgCacheDelete(entry.id);
        self.view.splice(self.index, 1);
        if (self.view.length === 0) { renderBgGallery(); self.close(); return; }
        if (self.index >= self.view.length) self.index = self.view.length - 1;
        self._loadCurrent();
        if (self._isFullscreen()) self._fsLoadCurrent();
        renderBgGallery();
      });

      // Click on the modal backdrop (but not the card) closes it. Skip when
      // fullscreen is open — its own handlers manage that layer.
      modal?.addEventListener('click', (e) => {
        if (self._isFullscreen()) return;
        if (e.target === modal) self.close();
      });

      // Keyboard navigation — Esc behavior is layered:
      //  - Fullscreen visible → Esc returns to compact preview
      //  - Compact only       → Esc closes
      // This way the preview-card's keyboard handler doesn't fire close()
      // when fullscreen is on top. We attach to document but check both
      // modal states; the fullscreen handler also has its own keydown
      // (registered in _bindFullscreenOnce) which calls preventDefault
      // first if it handles the key.
      document.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => {
        // Fullscreen owns its own keys when visible — see _bindFullscreenOnce.
        if (self._isFullscreen()) return;
        if (!modal?.classList.contains('visible')) return;
        switch (e.key) {
          case 'Escape':     e.preventDefault(); self.close(); break;
          case 'ArrowLeft':  e.preventDefault(); self.prev();  break;
          case 'ArrowRight': e.preventDefault(); self.next();  break;
          case 'Enter':      e.preventDefault(); self.enterFullscreen(); break;
          case 'f': case 'F': e.preventDefault();
            $('bgLightboxFavorite')?.click(); break;
          case 'Delete': case 'Backspace': e.preventDefault();
            $('bgLightboxDelete')?.click(); break;
        }
      });
    },

    /** Bind fullscreen-overlay event handlers once. */
    _bindFullscreenOnce() {
      if (this._fsBound) return;
      this._fsBound = true;
      const self = this;
      const fs = $('bgFullscreenModal');
      const stage = $('bgFsStage');
      const img = $('bgFsImg');

      $('bgFsClose')?.addEventListener('click', () => self.exitFullscreen());
      $('bgFsPrev') ?.addEventListener('click', () => { self.prev(); /* both layers update via prev() */ });
      $('bgFsNext') ?.addEventListener('click', () => { self.next(); });
      $('bgFsZoomIn') ?.addEventListener('click', () => self._fsZoomAt(1.25, window.innerWidth / 2, window.innerHeight / 2));
      $('bgFsZoomOut')?.addEventListener('click', () => self._fsZoomAt(0.8,  window.innerWidth / 2, window.innerHeight / 2));
      $('bgFsZoomFit')?.addEventListener('click', () => self._fsResetZoom());

      // Click backdrop to exit fullscreen (NOT to fully close — that lives
      // on the preview popup). Stage has its own gesture handlers, so a
      // bare click on the stage doesn't bubble here.
      fs?.addEventListener('click', (e) => {
        if (e.target === fs) self.exitFullscreen();
      });

      // Keyboard — only when fullscreen is the topmost layer.
      document.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => {
        if (!self._isFullscreen()) return;
        switch (e.key) {
          case 'Escape':     e.preventDefault(); self.exitFullscreen(); break;
          case 'ArrowLeft':  e.preventDefault(); self.prev();  break;
          case 'ArrowRight': e.preventDefault(); self.next();  break;
          case '+': case '=': e.preventDefault();
            self._fsZoomAt(1.25, window.innerWidth / 2, window.innerHeight / 2); break;
          case '-': case '_': e.preventDefault();
            self._fsZoomAt(0.8,  window.innerWidth / 2, window.innerHeight / 2); break;
          case '0':          e.preventDefault(); self._fsResetZoom(); break;
        }
      });

      // Mouse wheel zoom around cursor.
      stage?.addEventListener('wheel', (e) => {
        e.preventDefault();
        const factor = e.deltaY < 0 ? 1.12 : 0.89;
        self._fsZoomAt(factor, e.clientX, e.clientY);
      }, { passive: false });

      // Drag to pan when zoomed in.
      img?.addEventListener('mousedown', (e) => {
        if (self._fsScale <= 1.0001) return;
        e.preventDefault();
        self._fsDrag = { startX: e.clientX, startY: e.clientY, panX: self._fsPanX, panY: self._fsPanY };
        img.classList.add('dragging');
      });
      document.addEventListener('mousemove', (e) => {
        if (!self._fsDrag) return;
        self._fsPanX = self._fsDrag.panX + (e.clientX - self._fsDrag.startX);
        self._fsPanY = self._fsDrag.panY + (e.clientY - self._fsDrag.startY);
        self._fsApplyTransform();
      });
      document.addEventListener('mouseup', () => {
        if (self._fsDrag) { self._fsDrag = null; img?.classList.remove('dragging'); }
      });

      // Touch — single touch swipes (when at fit) OR pans (when zoomed),
      // two touches pinch-zoom.
      const SWIPE_THRESHOLD = 50;       // px horizontal travel to register a swipe
      const SWIPE_AXIS_RATIO = 1.4;     // |dx| must exceed |dy| × this to count as horizontal
      stage?.addEventListener('touchstart', (e) => {
        if (e.touches.length === 1) {
          const t = e.touches[0];
          if (self._fsScale > 1.0001) {
            self._fsDrag = { startX: t.clientX, startY: t.clientY, panX: self._fsPanX, panY: self._fsPanY };
            self._fsSwipe = null;
          } else {
            // At fit, single touch is a potential swipe-to-navigate.
            self._fsSwipe = { startX: t.clientX, startY: t.clientY };
            self._fsDrag = null;
          }
        } else if (e.touches.length === 2) {
          self._fsDrag = null; self._fsSwipe = null;
          const [a, b] = e.touches;
          const dx = b.clientX - a.clientX, dy = b.clientY - a.clientY;
          self._fsPinch = { startDist: Math.hypot(dx, dy), startScale: self._fsScale };
        }
      }, { passive: true });
      stage?.addEventListener('touchmove', (e) => {
        if (e.touches.length === 1 && self._fsDrag) {
          const t = e.touches[0];
          self._fsPanX = self._fsDrag.panX + (t.clientX - self._fsDrag.startX);
          self._fsPanY = self._fsDrag.panY + (t.clientY - self._fsDrag.startY);
          self._fsApplyTransform();
          e.preventDefault();
        } else if (e.touches.length === 2 && self._fsPinch) {
          const [a, b] = e.touches;
          const dist = Math.hypot(b.clientX - a.clientX, b.clientY - a.clientY);
          const target = self._fsPinch.startScale * (dist / self._fsPinch.startDist);
          const midX = (a.clientX + b.clientX) / 2;
          const midY = (a.clientY + b.clientY) / 2;
          self._fsZoomAt(target / self._fsScale, midX, midY);
          e.preventDefault();
        }
        // No preventDefault on single-touch swipe — let the browser still
        // let users scroll/cancel naturally if they decide it's not a swipe.
      }, { passive: false });
      stage?.addEventListener('touchend', (e) => {
        // Resolve a pending swipe gesture if any (only fires when scale was 1).
        if (self._fsSwipe && e.changedTouches.length > 0) {
          const t = e.changedTouches[0];
          const dx = t.clientX - self._fsSwipe.startX;
          const dy = t.clientY - self._fsSwipe.startY;
          if (Math.abs(dx) >= SWIPE_THRESHOLD && Math.abs(dx) >= Math.abs(dy) * SWIPE_AXIS_RATIO) {
            if (dx < 0) self.next(); else self.prev();
          }
        }
        if (e.touches.length === 0) { self._fsDrag = null; self._fsPinch = null; self._fsSwipe = null; }
      });

      // Double-click toggles between fit and 2× — quick zoom.
      img?.addEventListener('dblclick', (e) => {
        if (self._fsScale > 1.0001) self._fsResetZoom();
        else self._fsZoomAt(2, e.clientX, e.clientY);
      });
    },
  };

  let highwayRAF = null;
  let vizRAF = null;
  let stageResizeObs = null;

  // ── Canvas Visual Renderers (ported from Adaptive Session Studio — MIT) ──
  function hexToRgb(hex) {
    let h = hex.replace('#','');
    if (h.length === 3) h = h[0]+h[0]+h[1]+h[1]+h[2]+h[2];
    const r = parseInt(h.slice(0,2),16), g = parseInt(h.slice(2,4),16), b = parseInt(h.slice(4,6),16);
    return `${Number.isFinite(r)?r:128},${Number.isFinite(g)?g:128},${Number.isFinite(b)?b:128}`;
  }

  function renderTunnel(ctx, W, H, t, color) {
    const cx = W/2, cy = H/2, rgb = hexToRgb(color), maxR = Math.hypot(cx,cy)*1.1, RINGS = 18;
    const mod = getAudioMod();
    // Bass adds forward velocity (rings rush toward viewer faster on bass hits)
    const velMul = 1 + mod.bass * 1.2;
    ctx.fillStyle = '#000'; ctx.fillRect(0,0,W,H);
    for (let i = RINGS; i >= 0; i--) {
      const frac = ((i/RINGS) + (t*0.18*velMul)) % 1, r = frac*maxR, alpha = (1-frac)*0.65+0.05;
      ctx.beginPath(); ctx.arc(cx,cy,r,0,Math.PI*2);
      ctx.strokeStyle = `rgba(${rgb},${alpha})`; ctx.lineWidth = 1.5+(1-frac)*3; ctx.stroke();
    }
    // Voice-pulse light rings — fresh ring spawned on each voicePulse (3Hz cap)
    // Stored on the function itself to persist across frames
    if (!renderTunnel._voiceRings) { renderTunnel._voiceRings = []; renderTunnel._lastSpawn = 0; }
    const now = performance.now();
    if (mod.pulse > 0.6 && now - renderTunnel._lastSpawn > 333) {
      renderTunnel._lastSpawn = now;
      renderTunnel._voiceRings.push({ t: now });
      if (renderTunnel._voiceRings.length > 8) renderTunnel._voiceRings.shift();
    }
    for (const ring of renderTunnel._voiceRings) {
      const age = (now - ring.t) / 1000; // 0..2s lifetime
      if (age > 2) continue;
      const prog = age / 2; // 0..1
      const rr = prog * maxR;
      const ra = (1 - prog) * 0.7 * mod.pulse + (1 - prog) * 0.3;
      ctx.beginPath(); ctx.arc(cx, cy, rr, 0, Math.PI*2);
      ctx.strokeStyle = `rgba(255,215,140,${ra})`;
      ctx.lineWidth = 2 + (1-prog) * 2;
      ctx.stroke();
    }
    const g = ctx.createRadialGradient(cx,cy,0,cx,cy,maxR*0.15);
    g.addColorStop(0,`rgba(${rgb},0.7)`); g.addColorStop(1,`rgba(${rgb},0)`);
    ctx.beginPath(); ctx.arc(cx,cy,maxR*0.15,0,Math.PI*2); ctx.fillStyle = g; ctx.fill();
  }

  function renderPendulumViz(ctx, W, H, t, color) {
    const cx = W/2, cy = H/2, rx = W*0.38, ry = H*0.38, rgb = hexToRgb(color);
    ctx.fillStyle = 'rgba(0,0,0,0.18)'; ctx.fillRect(0,0,W,H);
    ctx.beginPath();
    for (let i = 0; i <= 2400; i++) {
      const frac = i/2400, s = (t - frac*4);
      const x = cx + rx*Math.sin(s*1.23+0.4)*Math.cos(s*0.71);
      const y = cy + ry*Math.sin(s*0.97+1.1)*Math.cos(s*1.03);
      i===0 ? ctx.moveTo(x,y) : ctx.lineTo(x,y);
    }
    ctx.strokeStyle = `rgba(${rgb},0.6)`; ctx.lineWidth = 1.2; ctx.stroke();
  }

  function renderVortex(ctx, W, H, t, color) {
    const cx = W/2, cy = H/2, rgb = hexToRgb(color), maxR = Math.min(W,H)*0.44, ARMS = 6;
    ctx.fillStyle = 'rgba(0,0,0,0.12)'; ctx.fillRect(0,0,W,H);
    for (let arm = 0; arm < ARMS; arm++) {
      const baseAngle = (arm/ARMS)*Math.PI*2 + t*0.6;
      ctx.beginPath();
      for (let i = 0; i <= 300; i++) {
        const frac = i/300, angle = baseAngle + frac*Math.PI*3;
        const r = frac * maxR * (0.8 + 0.2*Math.sin(t*1.5+arm));
        const x = cx + r*Math.cos(angle), y = cy + r*Math.sin(angle);
        i===0 ? ctx.moveTo(x,y) : ctx.lineTo(x,y);
      }
      ctx.strokeStyle = `rgba(${rgb},${0.35+0.25*Math.sin(t+arm)})`; ctx.lineWidth = 1; ctx.stroke();
    }
  }

  function renderMandala(ctx, W, H, t, color) {
    const cx = W/2, cy = H/2, petals = 8, rgb = hexToRgb(color);
    const mod = getAudioMod();
    // Rotation speed × (1 + overall), arm brightness on voice pulse, petal scale on bass
    const speedMul = 1 + mod.overall;
    const armBright = 0.7 + mod.pulse * 0.3;
    const petalScale = 1 + mod.bass * 0.35;
    ctx.fillStyle = 'rgba(0,0,0,0.06)'; ctx.fillRect(0,0,W,H);
    ctx.strokeStyle = `rgba(${rgb},${armBright})`; ctx.shadowColor = color; ctx.shadowBlur = 12 + mod.pulse * 18;
    for (let layer = 0; layer < 4; layer++) {
      const R = (layer+1)*Math.min(W,H)*0.1 * petalScale;
      const spin = t*(layer%2===0 ? 0.2 : -0.15)*speedMul + layer*0.4;
      ctx.globalAlpha = (0.6-layer*0.1); ctx.lineWidth = 1.8-layer*0.3;
      for (let p = 0; p < petals; p++) {
        const base = (p/petals)*Math.PI*2 + spin, tip = base + Math.PI/petals;
        ctx.beginPath(); ctx.moveTo(cx,cy);
        ctx.quadraticCurveTo(cx+R*1.5*Math.cos(base+0.3),cy+R*1.5*Math.sin(base+0.3),cx+R*Math.cos(tip),cy+R*Math.sin(tip));
        ctx.stroke();
      }
      ctx.beginPath(); ctx.arc(cx,cy,R*0.4,0,Math.PI*2); ctx.stroke();
    }
    ctx.globalAlpha = 1; ctx.shadowBlur = 0;
  }

  function renderLissajous(ctx, W, H, t, color) {
    const cx = W/2, cy = H/2, R = Math.min(W,H)*0.42, rgb = hexToRgb(color);
    ctx.strokeStyle = `rgba(${rgb},0.85)`; ctx.lineWidth = 1.8;
    ctx.shadowColor = color; ctx.shadowBlur = 10;
    const a = 3, b = 2, delta = t*0.4;
    ctx.beginPath();
    for (let i = 0; i <= 600; i++) {
      const th = (i/600)*Math.PI*2;
      const x = cx + R*Math.sin(a*th+delta), y = cy + R*Math.sin(b*th);
      i===0 ? ctx.moveTo(x,y) : ctx.lineTo(x,y);
    }
    ctx.stroke();
    ctx.globalAlpha = 0.08; ctx.fillStyle = '#000'; ctx.fillRect(0,0,W,H);
    ctx.globalAlpha = 1; ctx.shadowBlur = 0;
  }

  function renderColorWash(ctx, W, H, t, color) {
    const hue = (t*12 + parseInt(color.replace('#',''),16)%360) % 360, hue2 = (hue+40)%360;
    const grad = ctx.createRadialGradient(W*0.4,H*0.4,0,W/2,H/2,Math.max(W,H)*0.7);
    grad.addColorStop(0,`hsla(${hue},70%,35%,1)`);
    grad.addColorStop(0.5,`hsla(${hue2},60%,20%,1)`);
    grad.addColorStop(1,`hsla(${(hue+180)%360},50%,8%,1)`);
    ctx.fillStyle = grad; ctx.fillRect(0,0,W,H);
    const vig = ctx.createRadialGradient(W/2,H/2,H*0.2,W/2,H/2,H*0.75);
    vig.addColorStop(0,'transparent'); vig.addColorStop(1,'rgba(0,0,0,0.5)');
    ctx.fillStyle = vig; ctx.fillRect(0,0,W,H);
  }

  function renderCanvasPulse(ctx, W, H, t, color) {
    const cx = W/2, cy = H/2, rgb = hexToRgb(color), maxR = Math.min(W,H)*0.46;
    ctx.fillStyle = '#000'; ctx.fillRect(0,0,W,H);
    for (let i = 0; i < 6; i++) {
      const phase = (t*0.45 + i/6) % 1, r = phase*maxR, alpha = (1-phase)*0.7;
      ctx.beginPath(); ctx.arc(cx,cy,r,0,Math.PI*2);
      ctx.strokeStyle = `rgba(${rgb},${alpha})`; ctx.lineWidth = 2+(1-phase)*5; ctx.stroke();
    }
    ctx.beginPath(); ctx.arc(cx,cy,10+4*Math.sin(t*3),0,Math.PI*2);
    ctx.fillStyle = `rgba(${rgb},0.85)`; ctx.fill();
  }

  // Breathing circle — the single most evidence-based hypnotic visual
  function renderBreathingCircle(ctx, W, H, t, color) {
    const cx = W/2, cy = H/2, maxR = Math.min(W,H)*0.38, rgb = hexToRgb(color);
    // 4-7-8 cycle: 4s inhale, 7s hold, 8s exhale = 19s total
    // TEMPO IS LOCKED — audio reactivity ONLY modulates color warmth + halo alpha.
    // Do not introduce any audio-derived multiplier into phase/cycle/frac.
    const cycle = 19, phase = t % cycle;
    // Haptic at cycle start (once per 19s)
    if (phase < 0.05 && t > 0.1) vibrateBreath();
    let frac; // 0-1 breath position
    if (phase < 4) frac = phase / 4; // inhale: expand
    else if (phase < 11) frac = 1; // hold: full
    else frac = 1 - (phase - 11) / 8; // exhale: contract
    frac = frac * frac * (3 - 2 * frac); // smoothstep ease

    // Audio reactivity — color/halo only
    const mod = getAudioMod();
    const warmthShift = mod.envelope * 25; // pulls hue toward warm amber (lower) on voice
    const haloBoost = mod.bass * 0.35;      // outer halo gains alpha on bass

    ctx.fillStyle = 'rgba(0,0,0,0.12)'; ctx.fillRect(0,0,W,H);
    const r = maxR * 0.3 + maxR * 0.7 * frac;

    // Concentric rings at phase offsets
    for (let ring = 4; ring >= 0; ring--) {
      const ringFrac = Math.max(0, Math.min(1, frac - ring * 0.04));
      const rr = maxR * 0.3 + maxR * 0.7 * ringFrac * (3 - 2 * ringFrac);
      const alpha = 0.08 + ring * 0.04;
      // Color shift: cool blue inhale → warm amber exhale (+ warmth from voice envelope)
      const hue = 210 - frac * 30 - warmthShift; // 210 (blue) → 180 (teal) + voice warmth
      ctx.beginPath(); ctx.arc(cx, cy, rr, 0, Math.PI*2);
      ctx.strokeStyle = `hsla(${hue}, 50%, 60%, ${alpha})`; ctx.lineWidth = 1.5; ctx.stroke();
    }

    // Outer halo ring — reacts to bass, sits outside maxR. Does NOT change breath tempo.
    if (haloBoost > 0.02) {
      ctx.beginPath(); ctx.arc(cx, cy, maxR * (1.05 + haloBoost * 0.25), 0, Math.PI * 2);
      ctx.strokeStyle = `hsla(${210 - warmthShift}, 55%, 62%, ${haloBoost * 0.8})`;
      ctx.lineWidth = 3 + haloBoost * 4;
      ctx.stroke();
    }

    // Main circle
    const mainHue = 210 - frac * 40 - warmthShift;
    const grad = ctx.createRadialGradient(cx, cy, r * 0.3, cx, cy, r);
    grad.addColorStop(0, `hsla(${mainHue}, 55%, 55%, 0.35)`);
    grad.addColorStop(1, `hsla(${mainHue}, 45%, 40%, 0.08)`);
    ctx.beginPath(); ctx.arc(cx, cy, r, 0, Math.PI*2);
    ctx.fillStyle = grad; ctx.fill();
    ctx.strokeStyle = `hsla(${mainHue}, 50%, 60%, 0.5)`; ctx.lineWidth = 2; ctx.stroke();

    // Label
    let label = '';
    if (phase < 4) label = 'breathe in';
    else if (phase < 11) label = 'hold';
    else label = 'breathe out';
    ctx.fillStyle = `hsla(${mainHue}, 40%, 70%, 0.6)`;
    ctx.font = '0.8rem -apple-system, sans-serif'; ctx.textAlign = 'center';
    ctx.fillText(label, cx, cy + r + 24);

    // Center dot
    ctx.beginPath(); ctx.arc(cx, cy, 4 + 2 * frac, 0, Math.PI*2);
    ctx.fillStyle = `hsla(${mainHue}, 50%, 70%, 0.8)`; ctx.fill();
  }

  // Moiré — historically the most potent trance device (per ROADMAP)
  // Two concentric-ring patterns with one center slowly orbiting the other
  function renderMoire(ctx, W, H, t, color) {
    const cx = W/2, cy = H/2, maxR = Math.max(W, H) * 0.8;
    ctx.fillStyle = '#000'; ctx.fillRect(0, 0, W, H);
    ctx.strokeStyle = 'rgba(136,136,136,0.45)'; ctx.lineWidth = 1;
    // First grating: centered
    for (let r = 6; r < maxR; r += 12) {
      ctx.beginPath(); ctx.arc(cx, cy, r, 0, Math.PI * 2); ctx.stroke();
    }
    // Second grating: orbiting offset
    const ox = cx + Math.cos(t * 0.15) * 18;
    const oy = cy + Math.sin(t * 0.12) * 14;
    ctx.strokeStyle = 'rgba(136,136,136,0.4)';
    for (let r = 6; r < maxR; r += 12) {
      ctx.beginPath(); ctx.arc(ox, oy, r, 0, Math.PI * 2); ctx.stroke();
    }
    // Soft vignette
    const vig = ctx.createRadialGradient(cx, cy, maxR * 0.2, cx, cy, maxR * 0.6);
    vig.addColorStop(0, 'transparent'); vig.addColorStop(1, 'rgba(0,0,0,0.7)');
    ctx.fillStyle = vig; ctx.fillRect(0, 0, W, H);
  }

  // Starfield warp tunnel — 3D stars projected to 2D with streak lines
  function renderStarfield(ctx, W, H, t, color) {
    const cx = W/2, cy = H/2, STARS = 200, focal = 180, rgb = hexToRgb(color);
    if (!renderStarfield._pool) {
      renderStarfield._pool = [];
      for (let i = 0; i < STARS; i++) renderStarfield._pool.push({
        x: (Math.random() - 0.5) * 600, y: (Math.random() - 0.5) * 600,
        z: Math.random() * 600, psx: 0, psy: 0
      });
    }
    ctx.fillStyle = 'rgba(0,0,0,0.2)'; ctx.fillRect(0, 0, W, H);
    const speed = 1.5 + Math.sin(t * 0.3) * 0.5;
    for (const s of renderStarfield._pool) {
      const psx = s.psx, psy = s.psy;
      s.z -= speed;
      if (s.z < 1) { s.x = (Math.random()-0.5)*600; s.y = (Math.random()-0.5)*600; s.z = 600; s.psx = cx; s.psy = cy; continue; }
      const sx = s.x / s.z * focal + cx;
      const sy = s.y / s.z * focal + cy;
      s.psx = sx; s.psy = sy;
      if (sx < 0 || sx > W || sy < 0 || sy > H) continue;
      const brightness = Math.min(1, (600 - s.z) / 400);
      const size = Math.max(0.5, (1 - s.z / 600) * 3);
