        if (c.craftedSuggestion) state.craftedSuggestion = c.craftedSuggestion;
        if (c.script) {
          const scriptEl = $('scriptText'); if (scriptEl) scriptEl.value = c.script;
          state.fullScript = c.script;
          state.parsedScript = parseScript(c.script);
          showScript();
        }
        renderPersonaGrid(); renderPersonaDetail(); renderMethodGrid(); renderMethodDetail();
        if (typeof updateIntentionHint === 'function') updateIntentionHint();
        if (state.craftedSuggestion) { try { showCrafted(); } catch {} }
        state.stepsCompleted = { 1: true, 2: true, 3: true, 4: true, 5: true, 6: !!c.script };
        goToStep(c.script ? 6 : 5);
        const persona = getPersona(), method = getMethod();
        notify.info(`Shared config loaded: ${persona.name} · ${method.name} · ${c.length || 'medium'}.`, 8000);
        return true;
      }

      notify.warn('Unknown payload kind: ' + payload.kind);
      return false;
    } catch (e) {
      console.error('Share payload load failed:', e);
      notify.error('Could not load shared link: ' + (e.message || e));
      return false;
    }
  }

  async function loadSharedConfig() {
    // new uploadPlugin-backed share links (`?data=<fileId>.gz`)
    try {
      const urlParams = new URLSearchParams(window.location.search);
      const dataId = urlParams.get('data');
      if (dataId) {
        const handled = await loadUploadedSharePayload(dataId);
        if (handled) {
          // Clean up URL so a reload doesn't reinstall
          history.replaceState(null, '', window.location.pathname);
          return;
        }
      }
    } catch (e) { console.warn('data= param handling failed:', e); }

    const hash = window.location.hash;
    if (!hash.startsWith('#cfg=')) return;
    const paramStr = hash.slice(5);
    try {
      const params = new URLSearchParams(paramStr);
      const personaId = params.get('p');
      const methodId = params.get('m');
      const intentType = params.get('i');
      const length = params.get('l');
      const soundscape = params.get('s');
      const rate = params.get('r');
      const viz = params.get('v');
      const intentText = params.get('t');
      const aff = params.get('a');
      const affirmMode = params.get('am') || params.get('affirm');
      const voiceName = params.get('vn') || params.get('voice') || params.get('narrator');

      // Apply settings
      if (personaId && state.personas.find(p => p.id === personaId)) state.selectedPersonaId = personaId;
      if (methodId && METHODS.find(m => m.id === methodId)) state.selectedMethodId = methodId;
      if (intentType && INTENTION_TYPES[intentType]) { state.intentionType = intentType; $('intentionType').value = intentType; }
      if (length && PHASE_DURATIONS[length]) { state.length = length; $('lengthSelect').value = length; }
      if (soundscape) { state.soundscape = soundscape; state.droneEnabled = soundscape !== 'none'; const el = $('soundscapeSelect'); if (el) el.value = soundscape; }
      if (rate) state.speechRate = Math.max(0.5, Math.min(1, parseInt(rate, 10) / 100));
      if (viz) { state.vizOverride = viz; const el = $('vizSelect'); if (el) el.value = viz; }
      if (intentText) { const el = $('intentionInput'); if (el) el.value = decodeURIComponent(intentText).slice(0, 500); }
      if (aff) { const el = $('userAffirmations'); if (el) el.value = decodeURIComponent(aff).slice(0, 500); }
      if (affirmMode) { state.affirmationMode = affirmMode; const el = $('affirmationMode'); if (el) el.value = affirmMode; }
      if (voiceName) { state._savedVoiceName = decodeURIComponent(voiceName).slice(0, 200); applySavedVoiceSelection(); updateVoiceInfo(); }

      renderPersonaGrid(); renderPersonaDetail(); renderMethodGrid(); renderMethodDetail();
      updateIntentionHint(); updateStepUI();

      // Onboarding banner for the recipient
      const persona = getPersona();
      const method = getMethod();
      notify.info(`Shared config loaded: ${persona.name} · ${method.name} · ${length || 'medium'}. Review settings and generate when ready.`, 8000);

      // Mark steps as complete so they can jump to configure/generate in the new 6-step flow
      state.stepsCompleted = { 1: true, 2: true, 3: true, 4: true, 5: false, 6: false };
      goToStep(5); // Start at Configure so they can review

      // Clean URL
      history.replaceState(null, '', window.location.pathname + window.location.search);
    } catch(e) {
      // Fallback: try old gzip+base64 format for backward compat
      try {
        const bytes = Uint8Array.from(atob(paramStr), c => c.charCodeAt(0));
        const stream = new Blob([bytes]).stream().pipeThrough(new DecompressionStream('gzip'));
        const json = await new Response(stream).text();
        const cfg = JSON.parse(json);
        if (cfg.p) state.selectedPersonaId = cfg.p;
        if (cfg.m) state.selectedMethodId = cfg.m;
        if (cfg.i) { state.intentionType = cfg.i; $('intentionType').value = cfg.i; }
        if (cfg.l) { state.length = cfg.l; $('lengthSelect').value = cfg.l; }
        renderPersonaGrid(); renderPersonaDetail(); renderMethodGrid(); renderMethodDetail(); updateStepUI();
        state.stepsCompleted = { 1: true, 2: true, 3: true, 4: true, 5: false, 6: false };
        goToStep(5); // Configure — let user review before generating
        notify.info('Shared config loaded (legacy format). Review and generate.');
        history.replaceState(null, '', window.location.pathname + window.location.search);
      } catch(e2) { console.error('Failed to parse shared config:', e2); }
    }
  }

  /** JSON.stringify replacer that survives corrupt records.
   *  - Skips circular references silently
   *  - Replaces non-finite numbers (NaN, ±Infinity) with null (valid JSON)
   *  - Replaces undefined with null (valid JSON)
   *  - Replaces functions / symbols with null
   *  - Truncates string fields longer than 100KB (defensive against runaway values)
   *
   *  Per the perchance-api skill: a single corrupt entry shouldn't abort an
   *  export of the whole backup — surfaces at least the recoverable parts.
   */
  function _corruptItemReplacer() {
    const seen = new WeakSet();
    let _corruptCount = 0;
    const replacer = function(key, value) {
      // Circular reference detection — JSON.stringify normally throws on these
      if (typeof value === 'object' && value !== null) {
        if (seen.has(value)) { _corruptCount++; return null; }
        seen.add(value);
      }
      if (typeof value === 'number' && !Number.isFinite(value)) { _corruptCount++; return null; }
      if (typeof value === 'undefined')                          { _corruptCount++; return null; }
      if (typeof value === 'function' || typeof value === 'symbol') { _corruptCount++; return null; }
      if (typeof value === 'string' && value.length > 100_000)   { _corruptCount++; return value.slice(0, 100_000) + '…[truncated]'; }
      return value;
    };
    replacer._getCount = () => _corruptCount;
    return replacer;
  }

  async function exportBackup() {
    markFeatureUsed(FEAT.BACKUP);
    // Build the backup object with each source independently try-caught — a
    // failed read of one slice (e.g. corrupt analytics blob) shouldn't abort
    // the whole export. Each fall-back logs a console warning so the user can
    // tell something was skipped if they investigate.
    const safe = async (label, fn, fallback) => {
      try { return await fn(); }
      catch (e) { console.warn(`[backup] ${label} read failed:`, e); return fallback; }
    };
    const backup = {
      version: 5,
      exportedAt: Date.now(),
      personas:  await safe('personas',  () => loadCustomPersonas(), []),
      history:   await safe('history',   async () => (await idbGet('stillness_history_v1')) || [], []),
      program:   await safe('program',   async () => (await idbGet('stillness_program_v1')) || null, null),
      profile:   await safe('profile',   () => getProfile(), {}),
      settings:  await safe('settings',  () => _buildSettingsObj(), {}),
      analytics: await safe('analytics', async () => (await idbGet(ANALYTICS_KEY)) || null, null),
      adaptive:  await safe('adaptive',  async () => (await idbGet(ADAPTIVE_KEY)) || null, null),
      // Hidden-guide unlock state — earned, so users keep it across reinstalls.
      unlocks:   await safe('unlocks',   async () => (await idbGet('stillness_unlocks_v1')) || {}, {}),
    };
    const replacer = _corruptItemReplacer();
    let json;
    try {
      json = JSON.stringify(backup, replacer);
    } catch (e) {
      // Even with the replacer this can fail (very rare — non-stringifiable BigInt etc.).
      // Last-ditch: stringify each top-level slice independently, dropping any that throw.
      console.warn('[backup] full stringify failed even with replacer; falling back to per-slice:', e);
      const partial = { version: backup.version, exportedAt: backup.exportedAt, _partial: true };
      for (const k of Object.keys(backup)) {
        try { JSON.stringify(backup[k], replacer); partial[k] = backup[k]; }
        catch (e2) { console.warn(`[backup] dropped slice "${k}":`, e2); }
      }
      json = JSON.stringify(partial, replacer);
    }
    const corruptCount = replacer._getCount();
    if (corruptCount > 0) {
      console.warn(`[backup] ${corruptCount} corrupt fields replaced during export`);
    }
    let blob;
    try {
      const stream = new Blob([json]).stream().pipeThrough(new CompressionStream('gzip'));
      blob = await new Response(stream).blob();
    } catch(e) { blob = new Blob([json], { type: 'application/json' }); }
    const a = document.createElement('a'); a.href = URL.createObjectURL(blob);
    a.download = `stillness-backup-${new Date().toISOString().slice(0,10)}.gz`;
    a.click();
    // Delay revoke — some browsers cancel the download if URL is revoked before it starts
    setTimeout(() => URL.revokeObjectURL(a.href), 5000);
    if (corruptCount > 0) {
      notify.warn(`Backup exported (.gz). ${corruptCount} corrupt field${corruptCount === 1 ? '' : 's'} skipped — see console.`);
    } else {
      notify.success('Backup exported (.gz).');
    }
  }

  async function importBackup(file) {
    try {
      let text;
      try {
        const stream = file.stream().pipeThrough(new DecompressionStream('gzip'));
        text = await new Response(stream).text();
      } catch(e) {
        text = await file.text();
      }
      const backup = JSON.parse(text);
      if (!backup.version) { notify.error('Invalid backup file.'); return; }
      const ep = loadCustomPersonas(), eIds = new Set(ep.map(p => p.id));
      const np = (backup.personas||[]).filter(p => !eIds.has(p.id));
      if (np.length) { saveCustomPersonas([...ep,...np]); refreshPersonas(); renderPersonaGrid(); }
      const existingHistoryRaw = (await idbGet('stillness_history_v1')) || [];
      const existingHistory = existingHistoryRaw.map(normalizeHistoryEntry);
      const ehIds = new Set(existingHistory.map(h => h.id));
      const incomingHistory = (backup.history || []).map(normalizeHistoryEntry);
      const nh = incomingHistory.filter(h => !ehIds.has(h.id));
      if (nh.length || existingHistoryRaw.length !== existingHistory.length) {
        const mergedHistory = [...nh, ...existingHistory].map(normalizeHistoryEntry).sort((a,b)=>(b.date||0)-(a.date||0));
        mergedHistory.length = Math.min(mergedHistory.length, 50);
        await idbSet('stillness_history_v1', mergedHistory);
        renderSessionHistory();
      }
      if (backup.program && !(await idbGet('stillness_program_v1'))) { await idbSet('stillness_program_v1', backup.program); renderPrograms(); }
      if (backup.analytics) {
        const mergedAnalytics = mergeAnalyticsData(await getAnalytics(), backup.analytics);
        await idbSet(ANALYTICS_KEY, mergedAnalytics);
      }
      if (backup.adaptive && !(await idbGet(ADAPTIVE_KEY))) { await idbSet(ADAPTIVE_KEY, backup.adaptive); }
      // Unlocks — union with existing so an import never *removes* something
      // the user has already earned. Newer unlockedAt wins on collision.
      if (backup.unlocks && typeof backup.unlocks === 'object') {
        const current = (await idbGet('stillness_unlocks_v1')) || {};
        const merged = { ...current };
        for (const [pid, rec] of Object.entries(backup.unlocks)) {
          if (!rec || typeof rec !== 'object') continue;
          if (!merged[pid] || (Number(rec.unlockedAt) || 0) > (Number(merged[pid].unlockedAt) || 0)) {
            merged[pid] = { unlockedAt: Number(rec.unlockedAt) || Date.now() };
          }
        }
        await idbSet('stillness_unlocks_v1', merged);
        state._unlocks = merged;
        try { renderPersonaGrid(); } catch {}
      }
      if (backup.settings) {
        const merged = normalizeSettings({ ..._buildSettingsObj(), ...normalizeSettings(backup.settings) });
        if (_kv) { try { await _kv.settings.set(SETTINGS_KEY, merged); } catch {} }
        try {
          localStorage.setItem(SETTINGS_KEY, JSON.stringify(merged));
          localStorage.removeItem('stillness_settings_v4');
          localStorage.removeItem('stillness_settings_v3');
          localStorage.removeItem('stillness_settings_v2');
        } catch {}
        await loadUserSettings();
      }
      if (backup.profile) {
        const hasExistingProfile = !!($('pfName')?.value?.trim() || $('pfAbout')?.value?.trim() || $('pfGoals')?.value?.trim());
        if (!hasExistingProfile) applyProfileToForm(backup.profile, { save: true });
      }
      await rebuildAnalytics((await idbGet('stillness_history_v1')) || []);
      renderSidebarStats();
      renderHeatmap();
      renderSparkline();
      notify.success(`Imported: ${np.length} personas, ${nh.length} sessions.`);
    } catch(e) { notify.error('Import failed: ' + e.message); }
  }

  // ====================================================================
  // TRANSCRIPT
  // ====================================================================
  function populateTranscript() {
    const panel = $('transcriptPanel');
    if (!panel || !state.parsedScript) return;
    const phases = getPlaybackPhaseOrder();
    let html = '';
    for (const phase of phases) {
      const text = state.parsedScript[phase];
      if (!text || text.trim().length < 10) continue;
      const clean = stripLeadingPhaseHeader(text, phase).replace(/\x5BPAUSE\s+\d+\]/gi, ' ⋯ ').trim();
      // data-phase lets refreshTranscriptActive find headers + bodies for
      // the currently-playing phase without reparsing on every tick.
      html += `<div class="t-phase" data-phase="${escapeHtml(phase)}">${escapeHtml(getPhaseLabel(phase))}</div>`;
      html += `<div class="t-body" data-phase="${escapeHtml(phase)}">${escapeHtml(clean)}</div>`;
    }
    panel.innerHTML = html;
  }

  /** Sync .t-active markers to state._currentPhase. Cheap (~5 querySelectorAll
   *  + class flips) so it's fine to call from the phase-change site as well
   *  as on toggleTranscript open. */
  function refreshTranscriptActive() {
    const panel = $('transcriptPanel');
    if (!panel) return;
    const cur = state._currentPhase;
    panel.querySelectorAll('.t-phase, .t-body').forEach(el => {
      el.classList.toggle('t-active', el.dataset.phase === cur);
    });
  }

  function toggleTranscript() {
    const panel = $('transcriptPanel');
    if (!panel) return;
    panel.classList.toggle('visible');
    const btn = $('transcriptToggle');
    if (btn) btn.textContent = panel.classList.contains('visible') ? '✕ close' : '📜 script';
    // On open: mark current phase active + scroll it into view so the
    // user lands on where playback actually is, not the top of the script.
    if (panel.classList.contains('visible')) {
      refreshTranscriptActive();
      const active = panel.querySelector('.t-phase.t-active');
      if (active) active.scrollIntoView({ block: 'start', behavior: 'auto' });
    }
  }

  // ====================================================================
  // SESSION PLAYER
  // ====================================================================
  /**
   * Validate that the current script has a safe ending structure.
   * Per spec: \{\{Lightener\}\} + \{\{Wake\}\} + \{\{Reintroduction\}\} must exist before session playback.
   * Sleep intention type is exempt — it deliberately drifts into sleep.
   * Also flags unbalanced Deepener/Lightener nesting.
   *
   * @returns \{\{ ok: boolean, missing: string[], unbalanced: object|null, isSleep: boolean \}\}
   */
  function validateSessionSafety() {
    const isSleep = state.intentionType === 'sleep';
    const sections = getScriptSectionsFromState();
    const has = (base) => sections.some(s => phaseBase(s.phaseId) === base);
    const missing = [];
    if (!isSleep) {
      if (!has('lightener'))       missing.push('Lightener');
      if (!has('wake'))            missing.push('Wake');
      if (!has('reintroduction'))  missing.push('Reintroduction');
    }
    const deepenerCount = sections.filter(s => phaseBase(s.phaseId) === 'deepener').length;
    const lightenerCount = sections.filter(s => phaseBase(s.phaseId) === 'lightener').length;
    let unbalanced = null;
    if (!isSleep && deepenerCount !== lightenerCount) {
      unbalanced = { deepenerCount, lightenerCount, diff: deepenerCount - lightenerCount };
    }
    const ok = missing.length === 0 && !unbalanced;
    return { ok, missing, unbalanced, isSleep };
  }

  /**
   * Show the pre-session unsafe-structure warning modal.
   * Returns true if user chooses to proceed anyway, false to go back.
   */
  function showUnsafeSessionModal(safety) {
    return new Promise(resolve => {
      const backdrop = document.createElement('div');
      backdrop.className = 'modal-backdrop visible';
      const issues = [];
      if (safety.missing.length) {
        issues.push(`<li>Missing: <strong>${safety.missing.join(', ')}</strong> — without these, the session ends in trance.</li>`);
      }
      if (safety.unbalanced) {
        const u = safety.unbalanced;
        if (u.diff > 0) issues.push(`<li>${u.deepenerCount} Deepener${u.deepenerCount>1?'s':''} but only ${u.lightenerCount} Lightener${u.lightenerCount===1?'':'s'} — you will go deeper than you return.</li>`);
        else            issues.push(`<li>${u.lightenerCount} Lightener${u.lightenerCount>1?'s':''} without matching Deepeners (${u.deepenerCount}) — structurally inconsistent.</li>`);
      }
      backdrop.innerHTML = `<div class="modal unsafe-modal">
        <div class="unsafe-header">⚠ Incomplete session structure</div>
        <div style="font-size:0.88rem; line-height:1.55; color:var(--text-dim)">
          This session is missing finishing phases required for safe emergence from trance.
        </div>
        <ul class="unsafe-list">${issues.join('')}</ul>
        <div class="unsafe-note">
          <strong>Why this matters.</strong> A proper hypnosis session brings you fully back to normal
          waking awareness. Skipping the Lightener, Wake, or Reintroduction phases may leave you feeling
          groggy, partially dissociated, or — in rare cases — still in a light trance state.
          Only proceed if this is deliberate (e.g. you're running a custom sleep-induction session and intend to drift off).
        </div>
        <div class="modal-actions">
          <button class="btn" id="_unsafeBack">← Go Back</button>
          <button class="btn btn-unsafe" id="_unsafeProceed">Proceed Anyway (Unsafe)</button>
        </div>
      </div>`;
      document.body.appendChild(backdrop);
      const cleanup = (val) => { backdrop.remove(); resolve(val); };
      backdrop.querySelector('#_unsafeBack').addEventListener('click', () => cleanup(false));
      backdrop.querySelector('#_unsafeProceed').addEventListener('click', () => cleanup(true));
      backdrop.addEventListener('click', e => { if (e.target === backdrop) cleanup(false); });
    });
  }

  async function startSession() {
    // Block while AI generation is still streaming — without this guard,
    // clicking "begin" mid-stream would start a session against the
    // partial script that's been written into scriptText so far. The
    // validation gate below MIGHT catch that (missing finishers etc.),
    // but in many partial states the structure looks complete enough to
    // pass and the session would just play a truncated arc.
    if (_aiInProgress) {
      notify.info('Script is still being generated — try again in a moment.');
      return;
    }
    // Emergency cooldown check
    if (state._cooldownUntil && Date.now() < state._cooldownUntil) {
      if (state._debug?.skipCooldown) {
        sdLog('cooldown bypassed — debug mode on');
      } else {
        const remaining = Math.ceil((state._cooldownUntil - Date.now()) / 1000);
        notify.warn(`Please wait ${remaining}s before starting another session.`);
        return;
      }
    }

    let scriptText = $('scriptText').value.trim();
    if (!scriptText) { notify.warn('Generate or paste a script first.'); return; }

    // Structural safety gate — user must explicitly proceed if ending phases are missing
    // or deepener/lightener are unbalanced. Sleep intention is exempt by design.
    const safety = validateSessionSafety();
    if (!safety.ok) {
      if (state._debug?.skipSafetyGate) {
        sdLog('safety gate bypassed — debug mode on', safety);
        state._startedUnsafe = true;
      } else {
        const proceed = await showUnsafeSessionModal(safety);
        if (!proceed) return;
        // Record the unsafe-start in analytics so we can surface it in the post-session review
        state._startedUnsafe = true;
      }
    } else {
      state._startedUnsafe = false;
    }

    // Soft cap on session length — if estimated runtime exceeds safetyConfig.maxSessionMinutes,
    // surface a notify (non-blocking — user already opted into this length via slider).
    const lengthWarn = checkSessionLengthSafety();
    if (lengthWarn?.warn) {
      notify.warn(lengthWarn.message);
    }

    // If script was edited, offer to fix pause timings
    if (_scriptEditedSinceGen) {
      const fix = await confirmAsync('You\'ve edited the script. Would you like to recalculate pause timings to match your changes?\n\nYes = auto-fix pause markers based on sentence length\nNo = keep current timings as-is', { okText: 'recalculate', cancelText: 'keep as-is' });
      if (fix) {
        const fixed = fixScriptTimings(scriptText);
        $('scriptText').value = fixed;
        state.fullScript = fixed;
        state.parsedScript = parseScript(fixed);
        showScript();
        notify.success('Pause timings recalculated.');
        scriptText = fixed; // update local so the session plays the fixed text
      }
      _scriptEditedSinceGen = false;
    }

    // Daily session cap
    if (!(await checkDailyCap())) return;

    // Check TTS availability
    const voiceList = (typeof speechSynthesis !== 'undefined') ? speechSynthesis.getVoices() : [];
    if (voiceList.length === 0) {
      if (!await confirmAsync('No text-to-speech voices are available in this browser. The session will run silently (visuals only). Continue anyway?', { okText: 'continue', cancelText: 'go back' })) return;
    }

    // Stop any configure-tab previews before starting session
    if (typeof _stopAllPreviews === 'function') _stopAllPreviews();

    state.fullScript = scriptText;
    state.parsedScript = parseScript(scriptText);
    state.voice = getSelectedVoice();
    state.abortFlag = false;
    state._finishing = false;
    state._sessionStartTime = Date.now();
    state._pausedTotalMs = 0;      // cumulative time spent paused
    state._pauseStartedAt = null;  // wallclock of current pause start (if paused)
    state._hiddenTotalMs = 0;      // cumulative time spent with tab hidden
    state._hiddenStartedAt = null; // wallclock of current hide-start (if hidden)
    // Unified inactive accumulator — tracks the UNION of pause and hide
    // windows. Single source of truth for "elapsed minus inactive" math
    // since summing _pausedTotalMs + _hiddenTotalMs double-counts the
    // common overlap case (user pauses, screen locks → hidden).
    state._inactiveTotalMs = 0;
    state._inactiveStartedAt = null;
    state._currentPhase = 'settling';
    // Reset {{affirmation}} rotation so a fresh session starts at the first
    // affirmation rather than wherever the previous session happened to end.
    resetAffirmRotation();
    // Estimated total runtime for completedFraction metric (used by rules/triggers).
    // Pulled from script metrics × 60. Null-safe — defaults to 0 if unknown.
    const _estMetrics = getScriptMetrics(state.fullScript || '');
    state._estimatedTotalSec = Math.max(0, (_estMetrics.estimatedMin || 0) * 60);
    // Capture the user's selected speech rate as the BASE for playback dynamics
    // to scale against. Without this, repeated sessions would compound modulation.
    state._baseSpeechRate = state.speechRate;
    resetPlaybackDynamics(); // smoothedScale → 1.0, last apply timestamps cleared

    // Start the audio-reactive RAF loop. It polls the soundscape FFT + ticks
    // the voice envelope. Zero-impact if audioReactiveMode is 'off' — the
    // getAudioMod() read-time helper short-circuits to zeros.
    startAudioReactiveLoop();

    // Webcam awareness — per-session opt-in. If the user checked the box in Step 5,
    // start the camera (consent dialog already shown when they checked the box).
    // Non-blocking — startWebcam handles its own errors and shows status.
    if ($('webcamOptInThisSession')?.checked) {
      startWebcam().catch(e => console.warn('[webcam] start in startSession failed:', e));
    }

    // Hard-commit any pending diffs — session start is a durable anchor point.
    // Bypasses the magnitude gate but still requires hasChange, so identical
    // states won't generate noise entries.
    if (DIFF_BOUNCE_THRESHOLDS.commitOnSessionStart) {
      Object.values(diffBouncers).forEach(b => { try { b.hardCommit('sessionStart'); } catch(e) { console.warn('[DB] hardCommit failed for', b.source, e); } });
    }
    const method = getMethod();

    // Double induction: pick a contrasting second voice
    state._doubleInduction = method.id === 'double-induction';
    state._secondVoice = null;
    if (state._doubleInduction) {
      markFeatureUsed(FEAT.DOUBLE_INDUCT);
      const voices = speechSynthesis.getVoices().filter(v => v.lang.startsWith('en') && v.name !== state.voice?.name);
      // Try to find an opposite-gender voice for maximum contrast
      const primaryName = (state.voice?.name || '').toLowerCase();
      const isMale = /male|daniel|james|david|mark|george/i.test(primaryName) && !/female/i.test(primaryName);
      const opposite = voices.find(v => isMale ? /female|samantha|karen|moira|zira/i.test(v.name) : /male|daniel|james|david|mark/i.test(v.name) && !/female/i.test(v.name));
      state._secondVoice = opposite || voices[0] || null;
      if (state._secondVoice) {
        notify.info(`Double induction: ${state.voice?.name || 'primary'} + ${state._secondVoice.name}`);
      }
    }

    switchScreen('session-screen');
    document.body.classList.add('in-session');
    showSessionControls(); // start auto-hide timer
    // First-session entry — show grouped warning if capabilities are missing.
    // Idempotent (warnMissingCapabilities sets _capsWarnedOnce true on first call).
    warnMissingCapabilities();
    $('personaIndicator').textContent = '· ' + getPersona().name.toLowerCase() + ' ·';
    $('methodIndicator').textContent = '· ' + method.name.toLowerCase() + ' ·';

    // Reset progress UI
    $('sessionProgressFill').style.width = '0%';
    updatePhaseDots(-1);

    // Apply HUD visibility toggles from Configure tab
    const h = state.hud;
    const $s = (id, vis) => { const el = $(id); if (el) el.style.display = vis ? '' : 'none'; };
    $s('fixationDot', h.fixationDot);
    $s('textDisplay', h.textDisplay);
    $s('phaseDots', h.phaseDots);
    $s('phaseIndicator', h.phaseLabel);
    document.querySelector('.session-progress-wrap')?.style.setProperty('display', h.progressBar ? '' : 'none');
    $s('sessionTime', h.sessionTime);
    $s('personaIndicator', h.labels);
    $s('methodIndicator', h.labels);
    $s('audioIndicator', h.labels);

    // Apply visual override (user selection or method default)
    const vizChoice = state.vizOverride === 'auto' ? method.visual : state.vizOverride;
    if (vizChoice === 'none') {
      // Black screen only — skip visual setup
      const stage = $('stage');
      Array.from(stage.children).forEach(c => {
        if (!c.classList.contains('fixation-dot') && !c.classList.contains('audio-indicator')
          && !c.classList.contains('persona-indicator') && !c.classList.contains('method-indicator')
          && !c.classList.contains('session-progress-wrap') && !c.classList.contains('transcript-panel')) c.remove();
      });
    } else {
      setupVisual(vizChoice);
    }
    populateTranscript();
    $('transcriptPanel')?.classList.remove('visible');
    const tBtn = $('transcriptToggle');
    if (tBtn) tBtn.textContent = '📜 script';

    // Start soundscape (replaces old drone toggle)
    if (state.soundscape && state.soundscape !== 'none') {
      try {
        await drone.start(state.droneVolume, state.soundscape);
        $('audioIndicator').textContent = '· ' + state.soundscape + ' ·';
      } catch(e) {
        notify.info('Audio unavailable — tap the screen to enable audio, then try again.');
      }
    }

    // Haptic marker — double-pulse on session start (Android/Chrome)
    vibrateSessionMarker();

    // Warm overlay for sleep sessions (blue-light reduction)
    setSleepOverlay(state.intentionType === 'sleep');

    // Background engine — activate chosen background source
    bgEngine.source = state.bgSource || 'none';
    bgEngine.prompt = state.bgPrompt || '';
    if (bgEngine.source === 'ai') {
      bgEngine.pregenerate(5); // non-blocking — starts generating in background
    } else if (bgEngine.source === 'local-image' && state._bgFile) {
      bgEngine.setLocalFile(state._bgFile);
    } else if (bgEngine.source === 'local-video' && state._bgFile) {
      bgEngine.setLocalFile(state._bgFile);
    } else if (bgEngine.source === 'youtube' && state.bgYoutubeUrl) {
      bgEngine.setYouTube(state.bgYoutubeUrl);
    } else if (bgEngine.source === 'url' && state.bgStaticUrl) {
      bgEngine.setStaticImage(state.bgStaticUrl);
    }
    if (bgEngine.source !== 'none') bgEngine.activate();

    // Media Session API — lock-screen controls for mobile
    setMediaSession(true);

    // Reset post-session controls for fresh state
    const postSlider = $('postStateSlider'); if (postSlider) { postSlider.value = 5; }
    const postVal = $('postStateVal'); if (postVal) postVal.textContent = '5';
    const alertSlider = $('alertnessSlider'); if (alertSlider) { alertSlider.value = 7; }
    const alertVal = $('alertnessVal'); if (alertVal) alertVal.textContent = '7';
    const depthSl = $('depthSlider'); if (depthSl) { depthSl.value = 5; }
    const depthV = $('depthVal'); if (depthV) depthV.textContent = '5';
    const journal = $('journalText'); if (journal) journal.value = '';
    // Conversational check-in state — clear textarea + collapse, reset hint
    const pcText = $('postCheckinText'); if (pcText) pcText.value = '';
    const pcWrap = $('postCheckinTextWrap'); if (pcWrap) pcWrap.style.display = 'none';
    const pcTog  = $('postCheckinTextToggle'); if (pcTog) pcTog.textContent = 'type it instead';
    const pcHint = $('postCheckinTextHint');
    if (pcHint) {
      pcHint.textContent = 'type a few words about how you feel — we\'ll set the sliders for you';
      pcHint.style.color = 'var(--text-faint)';
    }

    // Wake Lock — prevent screen timeout during session
    try {
      if (navigator.wakeLock) {
        state._wakeLock = await navigator.wakeLock.request('screen');
        state._wakeLock.addEventListener('release', () => { state._wakeLock = null; });
      }
    } catch(e) { /* Wake Lock not available or denied */ }

    // Content warning for advanced/intense methods or authoritarian personas
    const needsWarning = method.category === 'advanced' || ['drill-instructor','dominatrix','interrogator','commander','headmistress','surgeon'].includes(getPersona().id);
    if (needsWarning) {
      const warningShown = await confirmAsync(
        'This session uses advanced techniques that may produce intense experiences including dissociation, hallucination, or strong emotional responses.\n\nEnsure you are in a safe, comfortable environment. Do not drive or operate machinery after.\n\nThe "end & re-alert" button is always available.',
        { okText: 'proceed', cancelText: 'go back' }
      );
      if (!warningShown) {
        switchScreen('setup-wizard');
        document.body.classList.remove('in-session'); document.body.classList.remove('controls-hidden'); clearTimeout(_controlsTimer);
        await drone.stop(); stopVisuals();
        bgEngine.clear();
        setSleepOverlay(false);
        // Clear media session metadata set earlier in startSession
        setMediaSession(false);
        // Stop the webcam stream that was started non-blocking earlier in
        // startSession — without this, the camera stays on after returning
        // to the wizard. Reset the opt-in checkbox so the next attempt
        // re-prompts the user (matches the finishSession pattern).
        stopWebcam();
        const _wcCb = $('webcamOptInThisSession'); if (_wcCb) _wcCb.checked = false;
        // Stop the audio-reactive RAF loop started above. Leaving it
        // running would keep polling FFT data while the user browses the
        // setup wizard.
        stopAudioReactiveLoop();
        // Reset session state so visibilitychange doesn't treat this as an active session
        state._sessionStartTime = null;
        state._pausedTotalMs = 0;
        state._pauseStartedAt = null;
        state._hiddenTotalMs = 0;
        state._hiddenStartedAt = null;
        state._inactiveTotalMs = 0;
        state._inactiveStartedAt = null;
        try { state._wakeLock?.release(); } catch(e) {}
        return;
      }
    }

    // Session time tracker
    state._timeInterval = setInterval(() => {
      if (state.paused) return;
      const now = Date.now();
      let inactiveMs = (state._inactiveTotalMs || 0);
      if (state._inactiveStartedAt) inactiveMs += (now - state._inactiveStartedAt);
      const elapsed = Math.round((now - state._sessionStartTime - inactiveMs) / 1000);
      const mins = Math.floor(elapsed / 60);
      const secs = elapsed % 60;
      const timeEl = $('sessionTime');
      if (timeEl) timeEl.textContent = `${mins}:${String(secs).padStart(2,'0')} elapsed`;
    }, 1000);

    // Filter to substantive phases only — drop both missing entries and
    // ones with effectively no content. Without this, a too-short phase
    // (e.g. a 5-char placeholder body) was skipped silently inside the loop,
    // but the dots and progress bar had already counted it; the next phase
    // would then light up the wrong dot. By filtering here, the dot grid,
    // expectedSec budget, and progress percentages all align to the same
    // phase set.
    const phases = getPlaybackPhaseOrder().filter(phase => {
      const t = state.parsedScript?.[phase];
      return t && t.trim().length >= 10;
    });
    renderSessionPhaseDots(phases);
    const phaseLabels = Object.fromEntries(phases.map(phase => [phase, getPhaseLabel(phase).toLowerCase()]));

    const durations = PHASE_DURATIONS[state.length] || PHASE_DURATIONS.medium;
    const expectedSec = phases.reduce((sum, phase) => {
      const base = phase.replace(/-\d+$/, '');
      if (base === 'affirm') return sum + 45;
      return sum + (durations[base] || 60);
    }, 0);
    const safetyCheck = () => {
      if (state._finishing || !state._sessionStartTime) return;
      // If user is paused, reschedule — don't force-end a user-paused session
      if (state.paused) { state._safetyTimeout = setTimeout(safetyCheck, 60000); return; }
      notify.warn('Session exceeded maximum expected duration — finishing with rescue.');
      // Route through emergencyEnd rather than finishSession directly. The
      // user has likely been left in deep trance because the AI overshot
      // their length budget; cold-dumping to the post-screen with no wake
      // sequence is the worst outcome. emergencyEnd handles abortFlag,
      // cancels current speech (system + local), and plays the persona's
      // re-alert before calling finishSession(true). It self-guards on
      // _finishing so we don't preset that flag here.
      emergencyEnd();
    };
    state._safetyTimeout = setTimeout(safetyCheck, (expectedSec * 2 + 300) * 1000);

    for (let pi = 0; pi < phases.length; pi++) {
      const phase = phases[pi];
      if (state.abortFlag) break;
      const phaseText = state.parsedScript[phase];
      // (length check moved into the up-front filter — phases here are all
      // guaranteed substantive)

      // Update phase state — dot, label, and drone frequency updated up
      // front so the HUD reflects the phase you're about to hear.
      state._currentPhase = phase;
      // Phase-aware speech rate. Settling stays at base, induction starts
      // slowing, deepener is slowest, work is moderate, lightener accelerates
      // back, wake briskest, reintroduction normal. Strip dynamic suffix
      // (work-1, deepener-2 etc.) so all sub-phases inherit the base curve.
      const PHASE_RATE_MULT = {
        settling:       1.00,
        induction:      0.92,
        deepener:       0.85,
        level:          0.85,
        work:           0.95,
        affirm:         0.93,
        lightener:      1.00,
        wake:           1.05,
        reintroduction: 1.00,
      };
      const baseKey = phase.replace(/-\d+$/, '');
      state._phaseRateMult = PHASE_RATE_MULT[baseKey] ?? 1.0;
      $('phaseIndicator').textContent = phaseLabels[phase];
      updatePhaseDots(pi);
      // Refresh transcript active marker so an open transcript advances
      // live, and a later-opened transcript lands on the right phase.
      refreshTranscriptActive();
      if (state.droneEnabled && drone.running) drone.setPhaseFreq(phase);

      const cleanText = stripLeadingPhaseHeader(phaseText, phase).trim();
      try { await speakScript(cleanText); }
      catch(e) { console.error(`Phase "${phase}" speak error:`, e); }

      // Progress bar advances AFTER the phase finishes so it reflects
      // "phases completed" rather than "phases attempted". The original
      // (pi / N) calc was undercounting by one — bar stayed at 0% during
      // the entire first phase.
      const pct = Math.round(((pi + 1) / Math.max(phases.length, 1)) * 100);
      $('sessionProgressFill').style.width = pct + '%';
    }

    // Complete progress bar
    $('sessionProgressFill').style.width = '100%';
    updatePhaseDots(phases.length, phases.length);

    // Always finish the session — if abortFlag was set by emergencyEnd,
    // that path calls finishSession(true) itself, so only call if not aborting.
    // Guard against double-finish with a flag.
    if (!state.abortFlag && !state._finishing) {
      state._finishing = true;
      finishSession(false);
    }
  }

  // Interruptible sleep — checks abortFlag & paused state in 200ms chunks so
  // pause/end during a \x5BPAUSE n\x5D marker responds quickly instead of waiting
  // for the whole sleep to finish.
  async function interruptibleSleep(totalMs, bypassAbort = false) {
    const CHUNK = 200;
    let elapsed = 0;
    while (elapsed < totalMs) {
      if (!bypassAbort && state.abortFlag) return;
      while (state.paused && !state.abortFlag) { await sleep(CHUNK); }
      const remaining = totalMs - elapsed;
      await sleep(Math.min(CHUNK, remaining));
      elapsed += CHUNK;
    }
  }

  /** Split an embedded-command-tagged sentence into ordered segments.
   *  Sentinels: \u0001 opens an embedded chunk, \u0002 closes it. The
   *  TTS path applies volume/pitch dip on segments where embedded:true.
   *  Mismatched / orphan sentinels degrade gracefully — they're stripped
   *  and the surrounding text plays normally. */
  function _parseEmbeddedSegments(text) {
    const segments = [];
    let i = 0;
    while (i < text.length) {
      const openIdx = text.indexOf('\u0001', i);
      if (openIdx < 0) {
        segments.push({ text: text.slice(i), embedded: false });
        break;
      }
      if (openIdx > i) segments.push({ text: text.slice(i, openIdx), embedded: false });
      const closeIdx = text.indexOf('\u0002', openIdx + 1);
      if (closeIdx < 0) {
        // Orphan opener — strip it and treat rest as normal.
        segments.push({ text: text.slice(openIdx + 1), embedded: false });
        break;
      }
      segments.push({ text: text.slice(openIdx + 1, closeIdx), embedded: true });
      i = closeIdx + 1;
    }
    return segments.filter(s => s.text);
  }

  async function speakScript(text, bypassAbort = false) {
    // Resolve \{\{user\}\}, \{\{nar\}\}, \{\{intention\}\}, \{\{affirmation\}\} tokens before
    // speech. Runs BEFORE sanitization so any expanded content (e.g. a
    // pasted affirmation with punctuation) is normalized by the passes below.
    let canonical = resolveTokens(text);
    // Sanitize: strip phase markers and malformed pause markers
    canonical = canonical.replace(PHASE_MARKER_STRIP_RX, '');
    canonical = canonical.replace(/\[\s*pause[^\]]*\]/gi, (m) => {
      const num = m.match(/\d+/);
      return num ? `\x5BPAUSE ${num[0]}\x5D` : '';
    });
    // Embedded-command markup [[text]] — Ericksonian technique where the
    // hypnotist drops voice on the command word inside a sentence. Convert
    // to sentinel-wrapped form (\u0001…\u0002) so the generic bracket-strip
    // below doesn't eat them. Per-segment speech opts applied later.
    canonical = canonical.replace(/\[\[([^\[\]]+?)\]\]/g, '\u0001$1\u0002');
    canonical = canonical.replace(/\[[^\]]+\]/g, m =>
      /^\x5BPAUSE\s+\d+\x5D$/i.test(m) ? m : ''
    );
    // Normalize whitespace — strip newlines, collapse spaces
    canonical = canonical.replace(/[\r\n]+/g, ' ').replace(/  +/g, ' ').trim();

    const parts = canonical.split(/\x5BPAUSE\s+(\d+)\]/i);
    const display = $('textDisplay');
    const shouldDuck = drone.running && state.duckingEnabled;
    let isDucked = false;

    for (let i = 0; i < parts.length; i++) {
      if (!bypassAbort && state.abortFlag) break;
      while (state.paused && !state.abortFlag) await sleep(200);
      if (!bypassAbort && state.abortFlag) break;

      if (i % 2 === 0) {
        // ── TEXT SEGMENT — split into sentences for per-sentence TTS ──
        const segment = parts[i].trim();
        if (!segment) continue;
        const sentences = segment.split(/(?<=[.!?])\s+/).filter(s => s.trim());

        // Duck FIRST — let it ramp down during display fade
        if (shouldDuck && !isDucked) { drone.duck(); isDucked = true; }

        for (const sentence of sentences) {
          if (!bypassAbort && state.abortFlag) break;
          while (state.paused && !state.abortFlag) await sleep(200);
          if (!bypassAbort && state.abortFlag) break;

          if (display) {
            display.style.opacity = 0;
            await interruptibleSleep(200, bypassAbort);
            if (!bypassAbort && state.abortFlag) break;
            // Strip embedded-command sentinels from the on-screen text;
            // they're audio cues, not visual.
            display.textContent = sentence.replace(/[\u0001\u0002]/g, '');
            display.style.opacity = 0.5;
          }
          // Segment-aware speak: if the sentence contains embedded-command
          // sentinels, split into normal/embedded/normal chunks and speak
          // each with its own volume/pitch. Otherwise just speak the whole.
          if (sentence.includes('\u0001') && sentence.includes('\u0002')) {
            const segments = _parseEmbeddedSegments(sentence);
            for (const seg of segments) {
              if (!bypassAbort && state.abortFlag) break;
              if (!seg.text.trim()) continue;
              await speak(seg.text, seg.embedded ? { volMult: 0.7, pitchMult: 0.92 } : {});
            }
          } else {
            await speak(sentence);
          }
          // Post-speak buffer — let last audio samples drain. Interruptible
          // so emergencyEnd / pause / abort responds immediately rather than
          // having to wait out the dead-air at every sentence boundary.
          await interruptibleSleep(120, bypassAbort);
        }

      } else {
        // ── PAUSE MARKER ──
        const seconds = parseInt(parts[i], 10) || 4;
        const tempo = state.tempoMultiplier || 1.0;
        const rateScale = 1 / Math.sqrt(Math.max(state.speechRate || 0.65, 0.4));
        const scaled = Math.max(1, Math.round(seconds * tempo * rateScale));

        // Only unduck for pauses ≥ 5s base (long dramatic pauses)
        // Short pauses: stay ducked to avoid pumping
        if (shouldDuck && isDucked && seconds >= 5) {
          drone.unduck(); isDucked = false;
          await interruptibleSleep(scaled * 1000, bypassAbort);
        } else {
          // Stay ducked through short pauses — cleaner audio
          await interruptibleSleep(scaled * 1000, bypassAbort);
        }
      }
    }
    // Always unduck at end of phase
    if (shouldDuck && isDucked) { drone.unduck(); isDucked = false; }
  }

  // ====================================================================
  // LOCAL TTS — Hugging Face Transformers.js SpeechT5 (offline, free)
  // ====================================================================
  // Optional path that uses neural TTS running entirely in the browser.
  // Lazy-loaded only when the user opts in. After first download (~80 MB)
  // the model is cached by the browser's normal HTTP cache + IDB. No
  // network needed at runtime.
  //
  // Trade-offs vs the speechSynthesis path:
  //   + Higher quality on platforms with bad system voices (Linux, older Android).
  //   + Consistent voice across all devices.
  //   + No cloud round-trip.
  //   - First sentence has ~1-3s synth latency on fast hardware, more on slow.
  //   - No native onboundary word events — pulses are time-estimated.
  //   - Big first-load.
  //
  // Falls back to speechSynthesis on any failure (404, OOM, model error).
  const _localTTS = {
    enabled: false,         // user toggle
    ready: false,           // model loaded and synthesizer is callable
    loading: false,
    pipeline: null,         // the Transformers.js pipeline function
    synthesizer: null,
    speakerEmbeddings: null,
    speakerName: 'clb',
    audioCtx: null,
    currentSource: null,
    paused: false,
    pausedAt: 0,
    pausedBuffer: null,
    abortFlag: false,
    _genId: 0,              // bumped on cancel() to invalidate in-flight synth
    // CDN sources — pinned so updates don't break this app silently
    CDN_TRANSFORMERS: 'https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@xenova/transformers@2.17.2',
    SPEAKER_BASE: 'https://huggingface.co/datasets/Matthijs/cmu-arctic-xvectors/resolve/main/',
    SPEAKER_FILES: {
      clb: 'cmu_us_clb_arctic-wav-arctic_a0001.bin',
      slt: 'cmu_us_slt_arctic-wav-arctic_a0001.bin',
      bdl: 'cmu_us_bdl_arctic-wav-arctic_a0001.bin',
      rms: 'cmu_us_rms_arctic-wav-arctic_a0001.bin',
      ksp: 'cmu_us_ksp_arctic-wav-arctic_a0001.bin',
    },
    /** Lazy-load Transformers.js + SpeechT5 model + speaker embedding. Idempotent —
     *  multiple calls during loading wait on the same promise. */
    async init(speakerName = 'clb') {
      if (this.ready && this.speakerName === speakerName) return true;
      if (this.loading) return new Promise(r => {
        const tick = () => this.loading ? setTimeout(tick, 200) : r(this.ready);
        tick();
      });
      this.loading = true;
      this.speakerName = speakerName;
      const setStatus = (msg) => {
        const el = document.getElementById('localVoiceProgressText');
        if (el) el.textContent = msg;
      };
      const setBar = (pct) => {
        const fill = document.getElementById('localVoiceProgressFill');
        const wrap = document.getElementById('localVoiceProgress');
        if (wrap) wrap.style.display = '';
        if (fill) fill.style.width = Math.min(100, Math.max(0, pct)) + '%';
      };
      try {
        if (!this.pipeline) {
          setStatus('Loading the TTS engine…');
          setBar(5);
          // Dynamic import from CDN — works in modern browsers without a build step
          const mod = await import(/* @vite-ignore */ this.CDN_TRANSFORMERS);
          this.pipeline = mod.pipeline;
          // Disable local model loading attempts (we want CDN-hosted weights)
          if (mod.env) {
            mod.env.allowLocalModels = false;
            mod.env.useBrowserCache = true;
          }
          setBar(15);
        }
        if (!this.synthesizer) {
          setStatus('Downloading voice model (~80 MB, one-time)…');
          this.synthesizer = await this.pipeline('text-to-speech', 'Xenova/speecht5_tts', {
            quantized: false,
            progress_callback: (p) => {
              if (p.status === 'progress' && p.progress != null) {
                setBar(15 + p.progress * 0.7);
                setStatus(`Downloading voice model… ${Math.round(p.progress)}%`);
              }
            },
          });
          setBar(85);
        }
        // Load the speaker embedding (a small ~2KB tensor that conditions the voice)
        setStatus('Loading speaker voice…');
        const url = this.SPEAKER_BASE + this.SPEAKER_FILES[speakerName];
        const res = await fetch(url);
        if (!res.ok) throw new Error('speaker fetch ' + res.status);
        const buf = await res.arrayBuffer();
        this.speakerEmbeddings = new Float32Array(buf);
        setBar(100);
        setStatus('Ready. The first sentence may take 1-3 seconds; later ones are faster.');
        this.ready = true;
        return true;
      } catch (e) {
        setStatus('Failed to load: ' + (e?.message || e) + '. Will fall back to system voice.');
        console.warn('[localTTS] init failed:', e);
        this.ready = false;
        return false;
      } finally {
        this.loading = false;
      }
    },
    /** Synthesize and play one chunk. Resolves when playback ends.
     *
     *  Race-safe via generation counter — if cancel() is called while a
     *  synthesizer call is in flight, the in-flight call's resolution is
     *  detected by comparing _genId snapshots, and we drop the buffer
     *  instead of playing it. (Without this, emergencyEnd's cancel + the
     *  subsequent rescue-script speak would be vulnerable to: the cancelled
     *  synth completes after the new speak resets abortFlag, and the
     *  cancelled audio plays anyway.) */
    async speak(text) {
      if (!this.ready || !text || !text.trim()) return;
      this.abortFlag = false;
      const myGen = ++this._genId;
      try {
        // Bound the synth phase. Without this, a hung pipeline call (model
        // OOM, WebGPU device-lost, mid-stream network failure) deadlocks
        // the session — the await never settles, and there's no watchdog
        // around it the way the speechSynthesis path has. 30s sits well
        // above the synth time of any real sentence even on slow hardware.
        // The playback phase is naturally bounded by audio length via
        // source.onended, so we only time-out the synth.
        const SYNTH_TIMEOUT_MS = 30000;
        const out = await Promise.race([
          this.synthesizer(text, { speaker_embeddings: this.speakerEmbeddings }),
          new Promise((_, rej) => setTimeout(() => rej(new Error('localTTS synth timeout')), SYNTH_TIMEOUT_MS)),
        ]);
        if (this.abortFlag || myGen !== this._genId) return;
        const audioData = out.audio;          // Float32Array
        const sr = out.sampling_rate || 16000;
        await this._playBuffer(audioData, sr, myGen);
      } catch (e) {
        console.warn('[localTTS] synth failed for chunk:', e);
        // Re-throw so the speak() wrapper can fall back to system TTS for
        // this sentence rather than silently dropping it.
        throw e;
      }
    },
    async _playBuffer(audioData, sampleRate, myGen, opts = {}) {
      // Prefer the soundscape's audio context if it's running — that way the
      // ambience recorder (which taps drone.master) captures local TTS audio
      // as part of the recording. Fall back to a dedicated context if drone
      // isn't active (e.g. voice-preview before any session has started).
      //
      // Lifecycle reconciliation: drone.stop() nulls drone.ctx but doesn't
      // close the underlying AudioContext, so a stale _sharedCtx ref would
      // otherwise stick to an orphaned (suspended-and-never-resumed)
      // context. Drop stale shared refs, and close any dedicated ctx we're
      // about to abandon for a freshly-available shared one.
      if (this._sharedCtx && (!drone || !drone.ctx || drone.ctx !== this.audioCtx)) {
        this.audioCtx = null;
        this._sharedCtx = false;
      }
      if (drone && drone.ctx && drone.ctx.state !== 'closed') {
        if (!this._sharedCtx && this.audioCtx) {
          // Held a dedicated ctx (e.g. from voice preview); drone is now
          // available, so close the orphan and switch to the shared ctx.
          try { await this.audioCtx.close(); } catch {}
        }
        this.audioCtx = drone.ctx;
        this._sharedCtx = true;
      } else if (!this.audioCtx || this.audioCtx.state === 'closed') {
        this.audioCtx = new (window.AudioContext || window.webkitAudioContext)();
        this._sharedCtx = false;
      }
      // Re-check generation just before scheduling — defense in depth
      if (myGen != null && myGen !== this._genId) return;
      // Fire word-pulse pings on a timer for visualizer compatibility (no
      // real boundary events from neural TTS).
      const dur = audioData.length / sampleRate;
      const wordCount = Math.max(1, Math.round(dur / 0.4)); // ~150 wpm rough
      const pulseInterval = (dur / wordCount) * 1000;
      let pulsesFired = 0;
      const pulseTimer = setInterval(() => {
        if (this.abortFlag || (myGen != null && myGen !== this._genId) || pulsesFired >= wordCount) { clearInterval(pulseTimer); return; }
        // Don't emit visualizer pulses while audio is suspended — wallclock
        // keeps ticking through pause but no audio is actually playing,
        // so we'd be spawning voice-pulses for silence.
        if (this.paused || (this.audioCtx && this.audioCtx.state === 'suspended')) return;
        if (state.audioReactive) state.audioReactive._voicePulseSpawnedAt = performance.now();
        pulsesFired++;
      }, pulseInterval);
      const buffer = this.audioCtx.createBuffer(1, audioData.length, sampleRate);
      buffer.copyToChannel(audioData, 0);
      // Build a single playback chain — extracted because double-induction
      // mode plays the same buffer through two independent panner chains.
      const buildSource = (panValue) => {
        const source = this.audioCtx.createBufferSource();
        source.buffer = buffer;
        const gainNode = this.audioCtx.createGain();
        gainNode.gain.value = state.voiceVolume ?? 1.0;
        // Apply stereo pan when requested. Older browsers without
        // createStereoPanner just skip the pan (still plays mono center).
        let lastNode = gainNode;
        if (Number.isFinite(panValue) && panValue !== 0 && this.audioCtx.createStereoPanner) {
          const panner = this.audioCtx.createStereoPanner();
          panner.pan.value = Math.max(-1, Math.min(1, panValue));
          gainNode.connect(panner);
          lastNode = panner;
        }
        // When sharing the soundscape's context, route through drone.master so
        // ambience recorder picks us up. Otherwise route directly to destination.
        if (this._sharedCtx && drone && drone.master) {
          source.connect(gainNode);
          lastNode.connect(drone.master);
        } else {
          source.connect(gainNode);
          lastNode.connect(this.audioCtx.destination);
        }
        source.playbackRate.value = Math.max(0.5, Math.min(2, state.speechRate || 1));
        return { source, gainNode };
      };

      // Double-induction mode on local TTS: render the same buffer through
      // two stereo-positioned chains simultaneously. Pan ±0.4 keeps each
      // voice clearly lateralized but not so extreme it loses center
      // stability. System TTS path can't do this — its audio is OS-routed,
      // not Web-Audio-routed. opts.allowStereoPan === false bypasses for
      // re-entry from the second call (recursion guard).
      if (state._doubleInduction && opts.allowStereoPan !== false) {
        const left  = buildSource(-0.4);
        const right = buildSource(+0.4);
        // Slight playback rate offset on the second voice (1.5%) creates a
        // subtle phase drift so the two voices don't lock together. This
        // matches the system-TTS double-induction path's u2.rate * 0.92.
        right.source.playbackRate.value = Math.max(0.5, Math.min(2, (state.speechRate || 1) * 0.985));
        this.currentGainNode = left.gainNode;
        this.currentSource = left.source;
        return new Promise((resolve) => {
          let pendingEnds = 2;
          const finalize = () => {
            if (--pendingEnds > 0) return;
            clearInterval(pulseTimer);
            if (this.currentSource === left.source) this.currentSource = null;
            if (this.currentGainNode === left.gainNode) this.currentGainNode = null;
            resolve();
          };
          left.source.onended  = finalize;
          right.source.onended = finalize;
          left.source.start(0);
          right.source.start(0);
        });
      }

      // Single-voice path — unchanged behavior.
      const { source, gainNode } = buildSource(opts.pan);
      this.currentGainNode = gainNode;
      this.currentSource = source;
      return new Promise((resolve) => {
        source.onended = () => {
          clearInterval(pulseTimer);
          if (this.currentSource === source) this.currentSource = null;
          if (this.currentGainNode === gainNode) this.currentGainNode = null;
          resolve();
        };
        source.start(0);
      });
    },
    pause() {
      if (this.audioCtx && this.audioCtx.state === 'running') {
        try { this.audioCtx.suspend(); this.paused = true; } catch {}
      }
    },
    resume() {
      if (this.audioCtx && this.paused) {
        try { this.audioCtx.resume(); this.paused = false; } catch {}
      }
    },
    cancel() {
      this.abortFlag = true;
      // Bumping the generation id invalidates any synthesizer call still
      // in flight — when its `await this.synthesizer(text)` resolves, the
      // myGen !== this._genId check fires and the buffer is dropped instead
      // of being scheduled for playback. Without this bump, a cancel during
      // synth + a quick subsequent speak() would race-play the cancelled
      // audio (because the new speak resets abortFlag back to false).
      this._genId++;
      if (this.currentSource) {
        try { this.currentSource.stop(0); } catch {}
        this.currentSource = null;
      }
      // Clear paused so a teardown via cancel() doesn't leak `paused: true`
      // into the next session — the pulseTimer guard would then suppress
      // visualizer pulses on the first sentence of the new session, and
      // resume() would no-op against any later pause attempt.
      this.paused = false;
    },
  };

  function speak(text, opts = {}) {
    return new Promise(resolve => {
      // Local TTS path — opt-in, must be loaded. On synth-timeout / failure,
      // _localTTS.speak rejects and we fall back to the system TTS path for
      // this sentence so a flaky local model can't deadlock the session.
      // Embedded-command opts (volMult / pitchMult) are honored only on the
      // system path for now; local path uses defaults.
      if (state.useLocalTTS && _localTTS.ready) {
        _localTTS.speak(text)
          .then(resolve)
          .catch((e) => {
            console.warn('[speak] local TTS failed, falling back to system:', e?.message || e);
            _speakSystemPath(text, resolve, opts);
          });
        return;
      }
      _speakSystemPath(text, resolve, opts);
    });
  }
  function _speakSystemPath(text, resolve, opts = {}) {
    if (!('speechSynthesis' in window)) { setTimeout(resolve, text.length * 80); return; }
    // Only cancel if something is unexpectedly still speaking
    if (speechSynthesis.speaking || speechSynthesis.pending) speechSynthesis.cancel();
    setTimeout(() => _doSpeak(text, resolve, opts), speechSynthesis.speaking ? 80 : 20);
  }
  function _doSpeak(text, resolve, opts = {}) {
    const u = new SpeechSynthesisUtterance(text);
    u.voice = state.voice || getSelectedVoice();
    // Apply embedded-command modifiers when present. Multipliers on top of
    // the user's baseline so the relative dip stays consistent across users.
    const volMult   = Number.isFinite(opts.volMult)   ? opts.volMult   : 1.0;
    const pitchMult = Number.isFinite(opts.pitchMult) ? opts.pitchMult : 1.0;
    u.rate = state.speechRate;
    u.pitch = (state.voicePitch || 0.85) * pitchMult;
    u.volume = (state.voiceVolume ?? 1.0) * volMult;
    let settled = false;
    let lastBoundaryAt = Date.now();
    let boundaryEverFired = false;
    // Counter-gate done() so a double-induction's slower secondary utterance
    // (u2 rate = state.speechRate * 0.92, so it always outlasts u) isn't
    // truncated by the next speak() cancelling it. Both u and u2 must end
    // before we resolve.
    let pendingEnds = (state._doubleInduction && state._secondVoice) ? 2 : 1;
    const done = () => {
      if (settled) return;
      if (--pendingEnds > 0) return;
      settled = true;
      clearInterval(silenceWatch);
      resolve();
    };
    u.onend = done;
    u.onerror = done;

    // Word-level highlighting via onboundary (Chrome/Safari desktop)
    const display = $('textDisplay');
    u.onboundary = (e) => {
      lastBoundaryAt = Date.now();
      boundaryEverFired = true;
      // Fire voicePulse for audio-reactive visualizers (spec §2.2)
      if (e.name === 'word') {
        state.audioReactive._voicePulseSpawnedAt = performance.now();
      }
      if (e.name === 'word' && display) {
        const before = text.slice(0, e.charIndex);
        const word = text.slice(e.charIndex, e.charIndex + e.charLength);
        const after = text.slice(e.charIndex + e.charLength);
        display.innerHTML = `<span style="opacity:0.35">${escapeHtml(before)}</span><span style="opacity:1;color:var(--text)">${escapeHtml(word)}</span><span style="opacity:0.35">${escapeHtml(after)}</span>`;
      }
    };

    speechSynthesis.speak(u);

    // Pseudo-envelope fallback for browsers where onboundary never fires
    // (some Android Firefox builds, some TTS engines). Fires a synthetic
    // ~3.5Hz pulse after 500ms if no real boundary has arrived. Stops the
    // moment a real onboundary fires.
    const pseudoStart = setTimeout(() => {
      if (boundaryEverFired || settled) return;
      const baseTime = performance.now();
      const estDurMs = Math.max(500, text.length * 60); // ~60ms per char estimate
      const pulseInterval = 286; // ~3.5Hz
      const pulseTick = () => {
        if (boundaryEverFired || settled) return;
        const elapsed = performance.now() - baseTime;
        if (elapsed >= estDurMs) return;
        state.audioReactive._voicePulseSpawnedAt = performance.now();
        setTimeout(pulseTick, pulseInterval);
      };
      pulseTick();
    }, 500);
    const _origDone = done;
    const _doneWithCleanup = () => { clearTimeout(pseudoStart); _origDone(); };
    u.onend = _doneWithCleanup;
    u.onerror = _doneWithCleanup;

    // Double induction: fire a second utterance simultaneously. Both
    // utterances must end before done() resolves (counter set above).
    if (state._doubleInduction && state._secondVoice) {
      const u2 = new SpeechSynthesisUtterance(text);
      u2.voice = state._secondVoice;
      u2.rate = state.speechRate * 0.92; u2.pitch = 0.75; u2.volume = 0.7;
      u2.onend = _doneWithCleanup;
      u2.onerror = _doneWithCleanup;
      speechSynthesis.speak(u2);
    }

    // ACTIVE SILENCE WATCHDOG — every 2s, check if speech has made progress.
    // Only use boundary-based stuck detection if onboundary actually fires on
    // this platform. Many voices/browsers never fire it — in that case we rely
    // solely on the not-speaking check and hard ceiling.
    //
    // Pause-time accounting: both watchdogs must subtract wallclock spent in
    // paused state, otherwise resuming after a >8s pause makes silentFor
    // exceed the threshold on the first tick after resume and kills the
    // sentence the user just chose to continue. flushPause() maintains the
    // accumulator and is shared by silenceWatch and the ceiling check.
    const speakStartedAt = Date.now();
    let pausedAccumMs = 0;
    let pauseEnteredAt = null;
    const flushPause = () => {
      if (state.paused) {
        if (pauseEnteredAt == null) pauseEnteredAt = Date.now();
        return true;
      }
      if (pauseEnteredAt != null) {
        pausedAccumMs += Date.now() - pauseEnteredAt;
        pauseEnteredAt = null;
        // The boundary clock went stale during pause — give the engine a
        // fresh window to emit its next boundary instead of comparing
        // against a pre-pause timestamp.
        lastBoundaryAt = Date.now();
      }
      return false;
    };
    const silenceWatch = setInterval(() => {
      if (settled) { clearInterval(silenceWatch); return; }
      // CRITICAL: skip all checks while session is paused — speechSynthesis
      // reports speaking=false during pause, which triggers false done()
      if (flushPause()) return;
      const silentFor = Date.now() - lastBoundaryAt;
      if (boundaryEverFired && speechSynthesis.speaking && silentFor > 8000) {
        // Chrome is stuck — boundaries were firing then stopped for 8s
        speechSynthesis.cancel();
        done();
      }
      // Grace period: don't check not-speaking until at least 1s of ACTIVE
      // (non-paused) speech time has elapsed
      if (Date.now() - speakStartedAt - pausedAccumMs > 1000 && !speechSynthesis.speaking && !speechSynthesis.pending) {
        // Speech finished without firing onend (Firefox edge case)
        done();
      }
    }, 2000);

    // Hard ceiling — scaled by speech rate (slower rate = more time needed).
    // The check polls and reschedules so a long pause inside a long sentence
    // doesn't burn the ceiling budget; effectiveElapsed subtracts pausedAccumMs
    // so the ceiling fires on actual speech time, not wallclock.
    const rateMultiplier = 1 / Math.max(state.speechRate || 0.65, 0.4);
    const hardCeiling = Math.max(15000, text.length * 180 * rateMultiplier);
    const ceilingCheck = () => {
      if (settled) return;
      if (flushPause()) { setTimeout(ceilingCheck, 5000); return; }
      const effectiveElapsed = Date.now() - speakStartedAt - pausedAccumMs;
      if (effectiveElapsed < hardCeiling) {
        setTimeout(ceilingCheck, Math.max(2000, hardCeiling - effectiveElapsed));
        return;
      }
      speechSynthesis.cancel(); done();
    };
    setTimeout(ceilingCheck, hardCeiling);
  }
  const sleep = ms => new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, ms));

  // ====================================================================
  // ====================================================================
  // AUDIO RECORDER — captures the soundscape audio graph
  // ====================================================================
  // Taps drone.master (which now also receives local AI voice output when
  // that's enabled). System speechSynthesis still can't be captured — it
  // routes through the OS audio stack, not the page's Web Audio graph.
  let _recorder = null, _recordedChunks = [];

  function startRecording() {
    if (!drone.ctx || !drone.master) { notify.warn('Start a session with a soundscape first.'); return; }
    try {
      const dest = drone.ctx.createMediaStreamDestination();
      drone.master.connect(dest);
      _recorder = new MediaRecorder(dest.stream, { mimeType: 'audio/webm;codecs=opus' });
      _recordedChunks = [];
      _recorder.ondataavailable = e => { if (e.data.size > 0) _recordedChunks.push(e.data); };
      _recorder.onstop = () => {
        if (_recordedChunks.length === 0) return;
        const blob = new Blob(_recordedChunks, { type: 'audio/webm' });
        const a = document.createElement('a');
        a.href = URL.createObjectURL(blob);
        a.download = `stillness-session-${Date.now()}.webm`;
        a.click();
        // Delay revoke so the browser actually initiates the download
        setTimeout(() => URL.revokeObjectURL(a.href), 5000);
        notify.success('Session audio downloaded.');
        _recordedChunks = [];
      };
      _recorder.start();
      notify.info('Recording ambience...');
      const btn = $('recordBtn');
      if (btn) { btn.textContent = '⏹ stop recording'; btn.classList.add('recording'); }
    } catch(e) { notify.error('Recording not supported in this browser.'); }
  }

  function stopRecording() {
    if (_recorder && _recorder.state === 'recording') {
      _recorder.stop(); _recorder = null;
      const btn = $('recordBtn');
      if (btn) { btn.textContent = '⏺ record audio'; btn.classList.remove('recording'); }
    }
  }

  function toggleRecording() {
    if (_recorder && _recorder.state === 'recording') stopRecording();
    else startRecording();
  }

  async function finishSession(wasEmergency = false) {
    clearInterval(state._timeInterval);
    clearTimeout(state._safetyTimeout);
    state.paused = false; // don't leak paused state into the next session
    stopAudioReactiveLoop(); // stop the audio-reactive RAF loop
    resetStateEngine();      // clear engagement EMA, phase tracking, external signals
    clearRuntimeRuleState(); // clear per-rule runtime state (heldSec, lastFiredSec)
    clearTriggerWindowState(); // clear trigger window state (status, openedAt, lastFiredAt)
    state._rulesPreDuckVolume = null;
    // Webcam: stop the stream + clear signals. Per-session opt-in resets — checkbox
    // unticked so the next session starts off; user re-opts in if they want it.
    stopWebcam();
    const _wcCb = $('webcamOptInThisSession'); if (_wcCb) _wcCb.checked = false;
    // Playback dynamics — restore base speech rate so the next session starts at user's choice
    resetPlaybackDynamics();
    state._baseSpeechRate = null;
    closeLivePanel();
    stopRecording(); // Stop any active recording before tearing down audio
    await drone.stop(); stopVisuals(); speechSynthesis.cancel();
    if (state.useLocalTTS && _localTTS.ready) _localTTS.cancel();
    // Clear lock-screen media session so it doesn't linger post-session
    setMediaSession(false);
    document.body.classList.remove('in-session'); document.body.classList.remove('controls-hidden'); clearTimeout(_controlsTimer);
    switchScreen('post-screen');

    // Posthypnotic intrusion-test scheduler. 30 seconds after switching
    // to the post-screen, prompt a fresh test of the trigger. Reaction-time
    // signal — the strongest signal we get for actual trance depth on a
    // demonstration session. Skipped on emergency exit (user needs
    // grounding, not testing) and when there's no real trigger (ongoing-
    // state suggestions don't have a discrete fire to test).
    clearTimeout(state._intrusionTestTimer);
    if (!wasEmergency
        && state.craftedSuggestion?.trigger
        && state.craftedSuggestion.trigger !== 'ongoing state') {
      const triggerText = state.craftedSuggestion.trigger;
      state._intrusionTestTimer = setTimeout(() => {
        // Only fire if we're still on the post-screen — user might have
        // navigated away (back to script, started another session) and
        // we don't want to hijack their flow.
        const stillOnPostScreen = document.getElementById('post-screen')?.classList.contains('active');
        if (!stillOnPostScreen) return;
        notify.info(`If you'd like to test "${triggerText}" — now's a natural moment. The trigger card has a "fired today" button if it works.`, 9000);
      }, 30000);
    }

    // Release wake lock
    try { state._wakeLock?.release(); } catch(e) {}

    // Save session to history — exclude inactive (paused ∪ hidden) time
    // from duration. Single-source via _inactiveTotalMs avoids the
    // pause+hide overlap double-count the per-source sum would produce.
    const _now = Date.now();
    let inactiveMs = (state._inactiveTotalMs || 0);
    if (state._inactiveStartedAt) inactiveMs += (_now - state._inactiveStartedAt); // finishing while inactive
    const elapsed = state._sessionStartTime ? Math.max(0, Math.round((_now - state._sessionStartTime - inactiveMs) / 1000)) : 0;
    const currentProfile = getProfile();
    const scriptMetrics = getScriptMetrics(state.fullScript || '');
    const sessionRecord = {
      id: 'sess_' + Date.now(),
      date: Date.now(),
      guide: getPersona().name,
      method: getMethod().name,
      intention: state.intentionType,
      length: state.length,
      soundscape: state.soundscape,
      durationSec: elapsed,
      completed: !wasEmergency,
      preMood: state._preMood ?? null,
      preTension: state._preTension ?? null,
      preEnergy: state._preEnergy ?? null,
      // Full reproducibility data
      script: state.fullScript || '',
      personaId: state.selectedPersonaId,
      methodId: state.selectedMethodId,
      intentionText: $('intentionInput')?.value || '',
      voiceName: $('voiceSelect')?.value || '',
      userAffirmations: $('userAffirmations')?.value || '',
      affirmationMode: $('affirmationMode')?.value || 'woven',
      speechRate: state.speechRate,
      voicePitch: state.voicePitch,
      voiceVolume: state.voiceVolume,
      droneVolume: state.droneVolume,
      vizOverride: state.vizOverride,
      vizSettings: { ...state.vizSettings },
      duckingEnabled: state.duckingEnabled,
      phaseOrder: getPlaybackPhaseOrder(),
      profileName: currentProfile.name || 'Practitioner',
      profileSnapshot: currentProfile,
      workBlockCount: scriptMetrics.workBlocks,
      metrics: scriptMetrics,
      // Why-this-session tag chips picked on Step 3. Sorted for stable
      // diffs across replays. Empty array if user didn't tap any chips.
      sessionTags: state._sessionTags ? [...state._sessionTags].sort() : [],
      workflowVersion: 11,
      stepsTotal: 6,
    };
    const history = (await idbGet('stillness_history_v1')) || [];
    history.unshift(sessionRecord);
    if (history.length > 50) history.length = 50; // Keep last 50
    await idbSet('stillness_history_v1', history);
    await recordAnalytics(sessionRecord, wasEmergency);
    // Daily metrics aggregate — independent of per-session log, gives Smart Director
    // time-series shape (completion rate, mood trends) over the last 180 days.
    // postMood/depth still null at this point (filled in via post-checkin handler).
    recordSessionInMetricsHistory(sessionRecord, wasEmergency ? 'emergency' : 'completed').catch(e => console.warn('[metrics] record failed:', e));
    updateAdaptiveProfile(sessionRecord, wasEmergency); // non-blocking
    updateSessionMemory(state.fullScript, sessionRecord); // Memory system — non-blocking
    renderHeatmap(); // Refresh activity heatmap
    renderSparkline(); // Refresh rising-score sparkline
    renderSidebarStats(); // Refresh sidebar stats
    // Hidden-guide unlock check — runs after history is committed so the new
    // session participates in the metric calculation. Non-blocking; the modal
    // (if any unlocks fire) appears on top of whatever post-session UI loads.
    // Only run for completed sessions — emergency exits shouldn't earn rewards.
    if (!wasEmergency) {
      try { checkPersonaUnlocks().catch(() => {}); } catch {}
    }
    // Re-apply method gating since the new session may have crossed a threshold
    applyMethodGating().catch(() => {});

    // Haptic marker — double-pulse on session end
    vibrateSessionMarker();

    // Remove warm overlay
    setSleepOverlay(false);

    // Clean up background engine
    bgEngine.clear();

    // Snapshot active program BEFORE advanceProgramDay mutates it — we need
    // {id, name, dayJustCompleted, totalDays, isFinalDay} to populate the post-session
    // program card. advanceProgramDay clears state._activeProgram and (on final day)
    // wipes the IDB record, so reading after-the-fact would lose this data.
    const _programSnapshot = (!wasEmergency && state._activeProgram?.prog && state._activeProgram?.ps)
      ? {
          name: state._activeProgram.prog.name,
          icon: state._activeProgram.prog.icon || '📅',
          dayJustCompleted: (state._activeProgram.ps.currentDay || 0) + 1, // currentDay is 0-indexed, so day 0 finishing = day 1 complete
          totalDays:        state._activeProgram.prog.days,
          isFinalDay:       (state._activeProgram.ps.currentDay || 0) + 1 >= state._activeProgram.prog.days,
        }
      : null;

    // Advance structured program (non-emergency only)
    if (!wasEmergency) advanceProgramDay();

    // Refresh method gating (may unlock new categories)
    applyMethodGating();

    // Build post-session summary
    const mins = Math.floor(elapsed / 60), secs = elapsed % 60;
    const persona = getPersona(), method = getMethod();
    // Pull personalization signals — first name (if user opted in), completed
    // session count (post-this-session), and the just-finished program day.
    const profile = getProfile();
    const firstName = (profile.useFirstName && profile.name) ? profile.name : '';
    const sessionCount = await getSessionCount(); // includes this one (recordSession ran above)
    const ordinalSuffix = (n) => {
      const v = n % 100;
      if (v >= 11 && v <= 13) return 'th';
      return ['th','st','nd','rd','th','th','th','th','th','th'][n % 10];
    };
    const summaryEl = $('postSummary');
    const sessionMarker = !wasEmergency && sessionCount > 0
      ? `<div class="post-row" style="opacity:0.85"><span class="post-label">milestone</span><span class="post-value">${sessionCount}${ordinalSuffix(sessionCount)} completed session</span></div>`
      : '';
    summaryEl.innerHTML = `
      <div class="post-card-title">session summary</div>
      <div class="post-row"><span class="post-label">guide</span><span class="post-value">${escapeHtml(persona.name)}</span></div>
      <div class="post-row"><span class="post-label">method</span><span class="post-value">${escapeHtml(method.name)}</span></div>
      <div class="post-row"><span class="post-label">duration</span><span class="post-value">${mins}:${String(secs).padStart(2,'0')}</span></div>
      <div class="post-row"><span class="post-label">completion</span><span class="post-value">${wasEmergency ? 'ended early' : 'completed'}</span></div>
      ${sessionMarker}
    `;

    // A/B rating prompt — only when a blinded run just finished and the
    // session completed normally. Emergency exits skip rating since the
    // user wasn't in a state to evaluate the experience.
    const abBox = $('postAbRatingBox');
