    { icon: '🔒', title: 'Privacy by default', body: 'Everything stays in your browser. <strong>Remote backup &amp; processing</strong> is OFF by default — when off, no data leaves your device, and intrusive UI (like "page may be stuck" warnings) stays silent too. Turn it on in Options if you want cross-device features. The <strong>debug mode</strong> toggle in Options surfaces developer affordances; a red badge appears whenever it\'s on.' },
    { icon: '✨', title: 'You\'re ready', body: 'Pick a guide and method, or tap a preset. Your first session takes about 2 minutes to set up. Backup your data anytime from the sidebar. The <strong>help &amp; safety resources</strong> button has crisis resources.' },
  ];

  function shouldShowTour() { try { return !localStorage.getItem(TOUR_KEY); } catch { return false; } }
  function markTourSeen() { try { localStorage.setItem(TOUR_KEY, '1'); } catch {} }

  function startTour() {
    let step = 0;
    const overlay = document.createElement('div'); overlay.className = 'tour-overlay';
    const card = document.createElement('div'); card.className = 'tour-card';
    document.body.appendChild(overlay); document.body.appendChild(card);

    function render() {
      const s = TOUR_STEPS[step];
      const isLast = step === TOUR_STEPS.length - 1;
      card.innerHTML = `
        <div style="font-size:1.4rem;margin-bottom:8px">${s.icon}</div>
        <h3>${s.title}</h3>
        <p>${s.body}</p>
        <div class="tour-dots">${TOUR_STEPS.map((_,i) => `<div class="tour-dot${i===step?' active':''}"></div>`).join('')}</div>
        <div class="tour-actions">
          <button class="tour-skip" id="tourSkip">${isLast ? '' : 'skip tour'}</button>
          <div style="display:flex;gap:6px">
            ${step > 0 ? '<button class="tour-next" id="tourBack" style="background:transparent;border:1px solid var(--border);color:var(--text-dim)">← back</button>' : ''}
            <button class="tour-next" id="tourNext">${isLast ? 'done ✓' : 'next →'}</button>
          </div>
        </div>`;
      $('tourSkip')?.addEventListener('click', dismiss);
      $('tourBack')?.addEventListener('click', () => { step--; render(); });
      $('tourNext')?.addEventListener('click', () => { if (isLast) dismiss(); else { step++; render(); } });
    }

    function dismiss() {
      markTourSeen(); overlay.remove(); card.remove();
      document.removeEventListener('keydown', kh);
    }
    const kh = e => { if (e.key === 'Escape') dismiss(); if (e.key === 'ArrowRight' && step < TOUR_STEPS.length-1) { step++; render(); } if (e.key === 'ArrowLeft' && step > 0) { step--; render(); } };
    document.addEventListener('keydown', kh);
    render();
  }

  // ====================================================================
  // VIBRATION HAPTICS (breathing feedback for supported devices)
  // ====================================================================
  function vibrateBreath() {
    // 4-7-8 breathing haptic: inhale pulse, hold, exhale pulse, hold
    if (caps.vibration) navigator.vibrate([400, 3600, 400, 7600]);
  }
  function vibrateSessionMarker() {
    if (caps.vibration) navigator.vibrate([100, 50, 100]);
  }

  // Crisis resources — accessible from footer at any time
  $('crisisLink')?.addEventListener('click', e => {
    e.preventDefault();
    const msg = 'If you are in crisis or experiencing distress:\n\n' +
      '🇺🇸 USA: 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline — call or text 988\n' +
      '🇬🇧 UK/IE: Samaritans — 116 123 (free, 24/7)\n' +
      '🇦🇺 Australia: Lifeline — 13 11 14\n' +
      '🇨🇦 Canada: Talk Suicide — 1-833-456-4566\n' +
      '🌍 International: findahelpline.com\n\n' +
      'This tool is not a substitute for professional care.';
    alertModal('Crisis Resources', msg);
  });

  // Sidebar toggle (mobile)
  $('sidebarToggle')?.addEventListener('click', () => {
    $('sidebar')?.classList.toggle('open');
    $('sidebarOverlay')?.classList.toggle('open');
  });
  $('sidebarOverlay')?.addEventListener('click', () => {
    $('sidebar')?.classList.remove('open');
    $('sidebarOverlay')?.classList.remove('open');
  });
  function openRemoteConsentModal() { $('remoteConsentModal')?.classList.add('visible'); }
  function closeRemoteConsentModal() { $('remoteConsentModal')?.classList.remove('visible'); }

  // Options modal
  $('openOptionsBtn')?.addEventListener('click', () => $('optionsModal')?.classList.add('visible'));
  $('optionsCloseBtn')?.addEventListener('click', () => $('optionsModal')?.classList.remove('visible'));

  // External AI provider selection
  // ── External AI provider + key handling ────────────────────────────────
  // The key field in the UI is shared across providers but the underlying
  // storage is per-provider so users can keep both populated. Each provider
  // switch swaps the visible key without erasing the other.
  // Warning modal fires on the first non-empty key entry per session — once
  // dismissed (with the "I understand" checkbox), it doesn't re-fire.
  let _apiKeyWarningShown = false;

  function _showApiKeyWarning() {
    return new Promise(resolve => {
      const backdrop = document.createElement('div');
      backdrop.className = 'modal-backdrop visible';
      backdrop.style.zIndex = 9999;
      backdrop.innerHTML = `<div class="modal" style="max-width:480px">
        <h3 style="margin:0 0 12px;font-size:1.05rem;font-weight:500;color:var(--neon-amber)">⚠ API key — read this first</h3>
        <div style="font-size:0.84rem;line-height:1.6;color:var(--text-dim);margin-bottom:12px">
          You're about to paste an API key into a browser tab. <strong>Anyone who can run JavaScript in this tab can read it</strong> — including extensions, injected scripts, or shared personas with malicious system prompts.
        </div>
        <ul style="margin:0 0 12px 18px;padding:0;font-size:0.78rem;line-height:1.65;color:var(--text-dim)">
          <li>Use a <strong>scoped, low-spend key</strong> — never your primary account key</li>
          <li>Set a <strong>monthly budget cap</strong> in your provider's dashboard</li>
          <li>Don't import shared personas you don't trust while a key is pasted</li>
          <li>If you suspect compromise, <strong>revoke the key immediately</strong></li>
        </ul>
        <div style="font-size:0.74rem;color:var(--text-faint);line-height:1.5;margin-bottom:14px">
          The key stays in browser memory only (session-only — cleared on reload). The "Perchance AI" provider option is free and requires no key if you'd rather avoid this.
        </div>
        <label style="display:flex;align-items:center;gap:8px;font-size:0.78rem;cursor:pointer;margin-bottom:14px">
          <input type="checkbox" id="apiKeyWarnAck">
          <span>I understand the risk</span>
        </label>
        <div style="display:flex;justify-content:flex-end;gap:8px">
          <button id="apiKeyWarnCancel" class="preview-btn">Cancel — use Perchance AI instead</button>
          <button id="apiKeyWarnProceed" class="preview-btn primary" disabled>Proceed</button>
        </div>
      </div>`;
      document.body.appendChild(backdrop);
      const ack = backdrop.querySelector('#apiKeyWarnAck');
      const proceedBtn = backdrop.querySelector('#apiKeyWarnProceed');
      ack?.addEventListener('change', () => { proceedBtn.disabled = !ack.checked; });
      const cleanup = (val) => { backdrop.remove(); resolve(val); };
      backdrop.querySelector('#apiKeyWarnCancel')?.addEventListener('click', () => cleanup(false));
      proceedBtn?.addEventListener('click', () => cleanup(true));
    });
  }

  $('extAiProvider')?.addEventListener('change', e => {
    state.extAiProvider = e.target.value;
    $('extAiKeyRow').style.display = e.target.value === 'perchance' ? 'none' : '';
    // Migrate from legacy single-slot _extAiKey on first switch — preserves the
    // user's already-pasted key under whatever provider was active before.
    if (state._extAiKey && !state._extAiKeysByProvider[e.target.value]) {
      // Don't auto-attribute the legacy key to the NEW provider; leave it on the OLD
      // one if we can guess. Conservative: drop the legacy slot, user re-pastes.
      state._extAiKey = null;
    }
    // Swap the input value to the cached key for the new provider (or empty)
    const keyInput = $('extAiKey');
    if (keyInput) {
      const cachedKey = state._extAiKeysByProvider[e.target.value] || '';
      keyInput.value = cachedKey;
      state._extAiKey = cachedKey || null;
    }
    // Refresh advanced-bg-controls disabled state — switching to/from the free
    // Perchance plugin changes which extras are safe to send.
    try { _applyAdvancedBgControlGating(); } catch {}
  });

  $('extAiKey')?.addEventListener('input', async e => {
    const newVal = e.target.value || null;
    const prev = state._extAiKey;
    // First non-empty key entry this session — show the security warning.
    // If the user cancels, clear the field and bail back to Perchance.
    if (newVal && !prev && !_apiKeyWarningShown) {
      _apiKeyWarningShown = true; // gate before await so we don't double-fire
      const proceed = await _showApiKeyWarning();
      if (!proceed) {
        e.target.value = '';
        state._extAiKey = null;
        state.extAiProvider = 'perchance';
        const sel = $('extAiProvider'); if (sel) sel.value = 'perchance';
        $('extAiKeyRow').style.display = 'none';
        return;
      }
    }
    state._extAiKey = newVal;
    // Mirror to the per-provider cache so a provider switch + back doesn't lose it.
    const provider = state.extAiProvider;
    if (provider === 'anthropic' || provider === 'openai') {
      state._extAiKeysByProvider[provider] = newVal;
    }
    // Refresh advanced-bg-controls — pasting/clearing the key flips the gate.
    try { _applyAdvancedBgControlGating(); } catch {}
  });

  // ── API key mitigations: per-session call counter + quick clear ─────────
  // The audit log tracks every AI call's provider + source + byte size. This
  // counter is a UI surface over that data: a one-glance signal so the user
  // catches unexpected activity early (a malicious persona hammering the key,
  // or a runaway loop). Updated each time the AI key UI is opened.
  function _refreshExtAiCallCount() {
    const el = $('extAiCallCount');
    if (!el || !_aiAuditLog) return;
    const externalCalls = _aiAuditLog.filter(e => e.provider === 'anthropic' || e.provider === 'openai').length;
    el.textContent = `${externalCalls} call${externalCalls === 1 ? '' : 's'} this session`;
    el.style.color = externalCalls >= 100 ? 'var(--neon-amber)'
                  : externalCalls >= 50  ? 'var(--text-dim)'
                  : 'var(--text-faint)';
    if (externalCalls === 100) {
      // Soft notify on the threshold-crossing call. One-time per session;
      // resets when the page reloads (audit log keeps history but the
      // notify gate is checked against the localStorage flag below).
      if (!sessionStorage.getItem('_extAiHighVolumeWarned')) {
        sessionStorage.setItem('_extAiHighVolumeWarned', '1');
        notify.info('100 external AI calls this session. If unexpected, check the AI activity log under your profile.', 8000);
      }
    }
  }
  // Refresh whenever the options modal opens (covers both manual checks
  // and the on-input handler when the user pastes a fresh key).
  $('openOptionsBtn')?.addEventListener('click', () => setTimeout(_refreshExtAiCallCount, 0));
  // Periodic refresh while the modal is open so the count stays live during
  // background generations. Bound to the visibility of the row.
  setInterval(() => {
    const row = $('extAiKeyRow');
    if (row && row.style.display !== 'none') _refreshExtAiCallCount();
  }, 4000);

  $('extAiClearBtn')?.addEventListener('click', () => {
    // Wipe both the active key, the per-provider cache, and the input.
    // Revert provider to Perchance so the next AI call goes to the free
    // tier. Doesn't clear the audit log — that's read-only history the
    // user might want to inspect.
    state._extAiKey = null;
    state._extAiKeysByProvider = { anthropic: null, openai: null };
    state.extAiProvider = 'perchance';
    const sel = $('extAiProvider'); if (sel) sel.value = 'perchance';
    const inp = $('extAiKey'); if (inp) inp.value = '';
    const row = $('extAiKeyRow'); if (row) row.style.display = 'none';
    try { _applyAdvancedBgControlGating(); } catch {}
    _refreshExtAiCallCount();
    notify.success('API key cleared. Reverted to Perchance AI.');
  });

  // Debug mode — all flags session-only, never persisted. Any change triggers a
  // re-sync of state._debug and re-renders the floating "DEBUG" banner.
  // Debug toggle — single checkbox enables every developer affordance. Mirrors to
  // state._debug, refreshes the corner banner, applies method gating immediately,
  // and shows/hides the memories-inspector button.
  $('debugMode')?.addEventListener('change', () => {
    _syncDebugState();
    const on = !!$('debugMode')?.checked;
    const memBtn = $('viewMemoriesBtn');
    if (memBtn) memBtn.style.display = on ? '' : 'none';
    applyMethodGating(); // re-evaluate the visible tab locks immediately
    // Self persona is gated on debug — re-render the persona grid so it
    // appears/disappears immediately. If the user had Self selected and is
    // turning debug OFF, fall back to the first available persona.
    refreshPersonas();
    if (!on && state.selectedPersonaId === SELF_PERSONA_ID) {
      state.selectedPersonaId = state.personas[0]?.id || null;
      saveUserSettings();
    }
    if (typeof renderPersonaGrid === 'function') renderPersonaGrid();
    if (typeof renderPersonaDetail === 'function') renderPersonaDetail();
  });

  // ── Memories modal (Perchance-chat style: editable cards) ──
  async function renderMemoriesModal() {
    const memory = await getMemory();
    const container = $('memoriesContainer');
    if (!container) return;
    const cards = [];
    // Tier 3 — long-term profile
    cards.push({ tier: 'long-term profile', key: 'longTermProfile', text: memory.longTermProfile || '', placeholder: 'Generated after 15+ sessions. Describes your overall practice patterns.', nodelet: false });
    // Tier 2 — rolling analysis
    cards.push({ tier: 'rolling analysis', key: 'rollingWindow', text: memory.rollingWindow || '', placeholder: 'Generated after 5+ sessions. What works and what to avoid.', nodelet: false });
    // Tier 1 — session digests (individual cards)
    memory.sessionDigests.forEach((d, i) => {
      cards.push({ tier: `session · ${new Date(d.date).toLocaleDateString()}`, key: 'digest', idx: i, text: d.digest || '', placeholder: 'Session summary...', date: d.date, depth: d.depth, moodDelta: d.moodDelta, nodelet: true });
    });
    container.innerHTML = cards.map((c, ci) => `
      <div class="memory-card" data-ci="${ci}" data-key="${c.key}" data-idx="${c.idx ?? ''}">
        <div class="mem-tier">${c.tier}${c.depth != null ? ` · depth:${c.depth}` : ''}${c.moodDelta != null ? ` · Δ${c.moodDelta > 0 ? '+' : ''}${c.moodDelta}` : ''}</div>
        <div class="mem-actions">${c.nodelet ? `<button class="mem-del" data-idx="${c.idx}" title="delete">✕</button>` : ''}</div>
        <textarea rows="${c.text.length > 150 ? 3 : 2}" placeholder="${c.placeholder}">${escapeHtml(c.text)}</textarea>
      </div>
    `).join('') || '<div class="sublabel" style="text-align:center;padding:20px">No memories yet. Complete some sessions to build your memory profile.</div>';
    // Wire delete buttons
    container.querySelectorAll('.mem-del').forEach(btn => {
      btn.addEventListener('click', async () => {
        const idx = parseInt(btn.dataset.idx, 10);
        const mem = await getMemory();
        mem.sessionDigests.splice(idx, 1);
        await saveMemory(mem);
        renderMemoriesModal();
      });
    });
    // Wire auto-save on textarea blur
    container.querySelectorAll('textarea').forEach(ta => {
      ta.addEventListener('blur', async () => {
        const card = ta.closest('.memory-card');
        const key = card.dataset.key;
        const idx = card.dataset.idx;
        const mem = await getMemory();
        if (key === 'longTermProfile') mem.longTermProfile = ta.value;
        else if (key === 'rollingWindow') mem.rollingWindow = ta.value;
        else if (key === 'digest' && idx !== '') mem.sessionDigests[parseInt(idx, 10)].digest = ta.value;
        await saveMemory(mem);
      });
    });
  }

  $('viewMemoriesBtn')?.addEventListener('click', async () => {
    await renderMemoriesModal();
    $('memoriesModal')?.classList.add('visible');
  });
  $('memCloseBtn')?.addEventListener('click', () => $('memoriesModal')?.classList.remove('visible'));
  $('memAddBtn')?.addEventListener('click', async () => {
    const memory = await getMemory();
    memory.sessionDigests.unshift({ sessionId: Date.now(), date: Date.now(), digest: '', depth: null, moodDelta: null });
    await saveMemory(memory);
    renderMemoriesModal();
    notify.info('New memory added. Type to edit, it auto-saves on blur.');
  });
  $('memRefreshBtn')?.addEventListener('click', async () => {
    const memory = await getMemory();
    notify.info('Regenerating summaries...');
    memory.lastRollingAt = 0; memory.lastProfileAt = 0;
    await updateRollingMemory(memory);
    await updateLongTermProfile(memory);
    await saveMemory(memory);
    renderMemoriesModal();
    notify.success('Memory summaries regenerated.');
  });

  $('sidebarSettingsBtn')?.addEventListener('click', () => {
    $('crisisLink')?.click();
  });

  $('remoteProcessingOptIn')?.addEventListener('change', e => {
    if (e.target.checked && !state.remoteProcessingOptIn) {
      e.target.checked = false;
      openRemoteConsentModal();
      return;
    }
    state.remoteProcessingOptIn = !!e.target.checked;
    saveUserSettings();
    notify.info(state.remoteProcessingOptIn ? 'Remote backup + processing enabled.' : 'Remote backup + processing disabled.');
  });
  $('smartDirectorEnabled')?.addEventListener('change', e => {
    state.smartDirectorEnabled = !!e.target.checked;
    saveUserSettings();
    _updateDirectorBiasUI(); // grey out the bias row when director is off
    _refreshAffirmationsBtnState(); // affirmation button enables/disables with SD
  });
  // Affirmation auto-fill button (Step 4) — drafts from profile + intention via Smart Director.
  // Refresh button label as the user types (showing "regenerate" once content exists)
  // and when the page first loads.
  $('generateAffirmationsBtn')?.addEventListener('click', generateAffirmationsFromProfile);
  $('userAffirmations')?.addEventListener('input', _refreshAffirmationsBtnState);
  $('runtimeRulesEnabled')?.addEventListener('change', e => {
    state.runtimeRulesEnabled = !!e.target.checked;
    saveUserSettings();
    if (!state.runtimeRulesEnabled) clearRuntimeRuleState();
  });
  // Webcam opt-in checkbox (Step 5) — first tick triggers the consent modal.
  // If the user declines, immediately untick. Permission is per-session; the
  // checkbox is reset to off at finishSession so the next session re-prompts.
  $('webcamOptInThisSession')?.addEventListener('change', async e => {
    if (e.target.checked) {
      const consent = await requestWebcamConsent();
      if (!consent) {
        e.target.checked = false;
        return;
      }
      // Consent obtained but camera doesn't start until startSession — we just
      // record the intent. The camera itself starts at session begin to avoid
      // burning battery during script editing.
      notify.info('Webcam will activate when the session starts');
    } else {
      // Untick mid-flow — if a session is already running, stop the camera now.
      if (state._webcam.status === 'active') stopWebcam();
    }
  });
  // Live in-session toggle — same modal, immediate start/stop. Lets users turn
  // the camera on or off mid-session without going back to the config screen.
  $('webcamLiveToggle')?.addEventListener('click', async () => {
    const w = state._webcam;
    if (w.status === 'active' || w.status === 'requesting' || w.status === 'loading') {
      stopWebcam();
      notify.info('Webcam off');
      return;
    }
    const consent = await requestWebcamConsent();
    if (!consent) return;
    startWebcam().catch(err => console.warn('[webcam] live toggle start failed:', err));
  });
  // Safety panel — wire the field inputs + reset button. Each change persists
  // immediately so users see settings stick across reloads. Inputs are clamped
  // again on save (defense in depth — load already clamps but a hand-edited
  // value during the session shouldn't slip through).
  function _wireSafetyInput(inputId, field, parse, lo, hi) {
    $(inputId)?.addEventListener('change', e => {
      if (!state.safetyConfig) state.safetyConfig = { ...SAFETY_DEFAULTS };
      const raw = parse(e.target.value);
      if (!Number.isFinite(raw)) return; // ignore garbage input
      const clamped = Math.max(lo, Math.min(hi, raw));
      state.safetyConfig[field] = clamped;
      // Repair min>=max swap on the speech rate fields
      if ((field === 'minSpeechRate' || field === 'maxSpeechRate')
          && state.safetyConfig.minSpeechRate >= state.safetyConfig.maxSpeechRate) {
        notify.warn('Min speech rate must be below max — reset to defaults');
        state.safetyConfig.minSpeechRate = SAFETY_DEFAULTS.minSpeechRate;
        state.safetyConfig.maxSpeechRate = SAFETY_DEFAULTS.maxSpeechRate;
        _renderSafetyPanel();
      }
      // Reflect clamping in the UI if user typed an out-of-range value
      if (Math.abs(clamped - raw) > 0.001) _renderSafetyPanel();
      saveUserSettings();
    });
  }
  _wireSafetyInput('safety_minSpeechRate',        'minSpeechRate',        Number, 0.5, 2.0);
  _wireSafetyInput('safety_maxSpeechRate',        'maxSpeechRate',        Number, 0.5, 2.0);
  _wireSafetyInput('safety_maxSoundscapeVolume',  'maxSoundscapeVolume',  v => Number(v) / 100, 0.0, 1.0);
  _wireSafetyInput('safety_emergencyCooldownSec', 'emergencyCooldownSec', Number, 0, 300);
  _wireSafetyInput('safety_maxSessionMinutes',    'maxSessionMinutes',    Number, 5, 180);
  $('safetyResetBtn')?.addEventListener('click', _resetSafetyConfig);
  _renderSafetyPanel(); // initial render from default state — load() will refresh later
  // Session mode dropdown — apply on change, refresh description + icon.
  $('sessionModeSelect')?.addEventListener('change', e => {
    applySessionMode(e.target.value);
    const mode = SESSION_MODES.find(m => m.id === e.target.value);
    if (mode) {
      const ic = $('sessionModeIcon'); if (ic) ic.textContent = mode.icon;
      const dc = $('sessionModeDescription'); if (dc) dc.textContent = mode.description;
    }
  });
  // Playback dynamics panel — wire the inputs. Each change clamps + persists.
  // The renderer below pushes current state values into the inputs.
  function _renderPlaybackDynamicsPanel() {
    const c = getPlaybackDynamicsConfig();
    const set = (id, v) => { const el = $(id); if (el) el.value = v; };
    const cb = $('pdEnabled'); if (cb) cb.checked = !!c.enabled;
    set('pdMode',           c.mode);
    set('pdCurve',          c.curve);
    set('pdStartScale',     c.startScale);
    set('pdEndScale',       c.endScale);
    set('pdEngagementMin',  c.engagementMin);
    set('pdEngagementMax',  c.engagementMax);
    set('pdSmoothingSec',   c.smoothingSec);
  }
  function _wirePdInput(inputId, field, parse, lo, hi) {
    $(inputId)?.addEventListener('change', e => {
      if (!state.playbackDynamics) state.playbackDynamics = { ...PLAYBACK_DYNAMICS_DEFAULTS };
      const raw = parse ? parse(e.target.value) : e.target.value;
      const val = (lo != null && hi != null && Number.isFinite(raw)) ? Math.max(lo, Math.min(hi, raw)) : raw;
      state.playbackDynamics[field] = val;
      saveUserSettings();
    });
  }
  $('pdEnabled')?.addEventListener('change', e => {
    if (!state.playbackDynamics) state.playbackDynamics = { ...PLAYBACK_DYNAMICS_DEFAULTS };
    state.playbackDynamics.enabled = !!e.target.checked;
    saveUserSettings();
    if (!state.playbackDynamics.enabled) resetPlaybackDynamics();
  });
  _wirePdInput('pdMode',           'mode',           v => v, null, null);
  _wirePdInput('pdCurve',          'curve',          v => v, null, null);
  _wirePdInput('pdStartScale',     'startScale',     Number, 0.5, 1.5);
  _wirePdInput('pdEndScale',       'endScale',       Number, 0.5, 1.5);
  _wirePdInput('pdEngagementMin',  'engagementMin',  Number, 0.5, 1.5);
  _wirePdInput('pdEngagementMax',  'engagementMax',  Number, 0.5, 1.5);
  _wirePdInput('pdSmoothingSec',   'smoothingSec',   Number, 1, 30);
  _renderPlaybackDynamicsPanel();
  // Director calibration slider — updates label live, persists on change (release).
  // Semantic labels are chosen to reinforce "neutral default is 0" rather than
  // implying more-cautious-is-better or vice versa.
  const DIRECTOR_BIAS_LABELS = {
    '-3': 'strongly cautious',
    '-2': 'cautious',
    '-1': 'slightly cautious',
     '0': 'balanced',
     '1': 'slightly forward',
     '2': 'forward',
     '3': 'strongly forward',
  };
  function _updateDirectorBiasUI() {
    const slider = $('directorBiasSlider');
    const label  = $('directorBiasLabel');
    const row    = $('directorBiasRow');
    if (!slider || !label || !row) return;
    const bias = Math.max(-3, Math.min(3, Math.round(Number(slider.value) || 0)));
    label.textContent = DIRECTOR_BIAS_LABELS[String(bias)] || 'balanced';
    // Pace label — separate axis. Updated alongside intensity so the two
    // labels stay in lockstep with their sliders.
    const paceSlider = $('directorPaceSlider');
    const paceLabel  = $('directorPaceLabel');
    const pace = paceSlider ? Math.max(-3, Math.min(3, Math.round(Number(paceSlider.value) || 0))) : 0;
    if (paceLabel) {
      const PACE_LABELS = { '-3':'much slower', '-2':'slower', '-1':'a touch slower', '0':'average', '1':'a touch faster', '2':'faster', '3':'much faster' };
      paceLabel.textContent = PACE_LABELS[String(pace)] || 'average';
    }
    // Render concrete impact for the current notch — same math as
    // computeDirectorBrief so the user sees the actual thresholds in play.
    const impact = $('directorBiasImpact');
    if (impact) {
      const coldStart   = Math.max(0, 3 - bias);
      const compFloorPct = Math.round((0.5 - bias * 0.05) * 100);
      const reentryDays  = 14 + bias * 3;
      const tone =
        bias <= -2 ? 'careful, gently reassuring'
        : bias === -1 ? 'slightly cautious, unhurried'
        : bias === 0 ? 'balanced (no extra tone directive)'
        : bias === 1 ? 'slightly forward, warm momentum'
        : 'forward-momentum, energizing';
      const beginnerNote = bias >= 3 ? 'beginner cap may be skipped at this notch' : 'beginner cap still in effect';
      const paceNote = pace === 0
        ? 'pace at base'
        : `pace ${pace > 0 ? '+' : ''}${pace * 4}% (multiplied into speech rate)`;
      impact.innerHTML = `<strong>at these notches:</strong> cold-start ends after ${coldStart} session${coldStart === 1 ? '' : 's'} · completion floor ${compFloorPct}% · re-entry treated after ${reentryDays} days · ${beginnerNote} · tone: ${tone} · ${paceNote}`;
    }
    // Grey out when Smart Director is off — bias has no effect if nothing runs.
    const enabled = state.smartDirectorEnabled !== false;
    row.style.opacity = enabled ? '' : '0.4';
    slider.disabled = !enabled;
    if (paceSlider) paceSlider.disabled = !enabled;
    $('directorBiasResetBtn') && ($('directorBiasResetBtn').disabled = !enabled);
    // 2D pad sync — dot position reflects current bias/pace. X = pace,
    // Y = bias inverted (positive bias = "forward" = top of pad).
    const pad = $('directorBiasPad');
    const dot = $('directorBiasPadDot');
    if (pad && dot) {
      const xPct = ((pace + 3) / 6) * 100;       // -3 → 0%,  +3 → 100%
      const yPct = ((3 - bias) / 6) * 100;       // +3 → 0%,  -3 → 100%
      dot.style.left = xPct.toFixed(2) + '%';
      dot.style.top  = yPct.toFixed(2) + '%';
      pad.style.opacity = enabled ? '' : '0.4';
      pad.style.pointerEvents = enabled ? '' : 'none';
    }
  }
  $('directorBiasSlider')?.addEventListener('input', e => {
    state.directorBias = Number(e.target.value);
    _updateDirectorBiasUI();
    sdLog('directorBias changed (uncommitted)', state.directorBias);
  });
  $('directorBiasSlider')?.addEventListener('change', () => {
    saveUserSettings();
    sdLog('directorBias saved', state.directorBias);
  });
  // Pace slider — same input/change pattern.
  $('directorPaceSlider')?.addEventListener('input', e => {
    state.directorPace = Number(e.target.value);
    _updateDirectorBiasUI();
  });
  $('directorPaceSlider')?.addEventListener('change', () => saveUserSettings());
  $('directorBiasResetBtn')?.addEventListener('click', () => {
    const slider = $('directorBiasSlider');
    if (slider) slider.value = 0;
    state.directorBias = 0;
    // Reset pace too — the visible button is labeled "reset to balanced"
    // and users would expect both axes to neutralize.
    const paceSlider = $('directorPaceSlider');
    if (paceSlider) paceSlider.value = 0;
    state.directorPace = 0;
    _updateDirectorBiasUI();
    saveUserSettings();
  });

  // 2D Director calibration pad — alternative input to the two sliders.
  // Same -3..+3 integer grid on both axes; pointerdown / pointermove
  // map cursor position to (pace, bias) and update both the sliders and
  // state. Persists once on pointerup so a drag isn't 36 separate saves.
  (function initDirectorPad() {
    const pad = $('directorBiasPad');
    if (!pad) return;
    let dragging = false;
    function applyFromPointer(ev) {
      const rect = pad.getBoundingClientRect();
      // Clamp client coords inside the pad
      const cx = Math.max(rect.left, Math.min(rect.right, ev.clientX));
      const cy = Math.max(rect.top,  Math.min(rect.bottom, ev.clientY));
      const xRatio = (cx - rect.left) / rect.width;   // 0..1
      const yRatio = (cy - rect.top)  / rect.height;  // 0..1
      // Snap to integer steps. -3..+3 = 7 steps; quantize and clamp.
      const pace = Math.round(xRatio * 6) - 3;
      const bias = 3 - Math.round(yRatio * 6);
      const dbSlider = $('directorBiasSlider');
      const dpSlider = $('directorPaceSlider');
      if (dbSlider) dbSlider.value = String(bias);
      if (dpSlider) dpSlider.value = String(pace);
      state.directorBias = bias;
      state.directorPace = pace;
      _updateDirectorBiasUI();
    }
    pad.addEventListener('pointerdown', ev => {
      if (state.smartDirectorEnabled === false) return;
      dragging = true;
      pad.classList.add('dragging');
      pad.setPointerCapture?.(ev.pointerId);
      applyFromPointer(ev);
      ev.preventDefault();
    });
    pad.addEventListener('pointermove', ev => {
      if (!dragging) return;
      applyFromPointer(ev);
    });
    const finishDrag = ev => {
      if (!dragging) return;
      dragging = false;
      pad.classList.remove('dragging');
      try { pad.releasePointerCapture?.(ev.pointerId); } catch {}
      saveUserSettings();
      sdLog('directorBias / directorPace saved via pad', { bias: state.directorBias, pace: state.directorPace });
    };
    pad.addEventListener('pointerup', finishDrag);
    pad.addEventListener('pointercancel', finishDrag);
  })();

  /** Render the per-domain offset rows. Domains read from the intentionType
   *  <select> so they stay in sync with available types. Each row stores
   *  state.directorBiasByDomain[domainId] on change. */
  function renderDirectorDomainOffsets() {
    const wrap = $('directorDomainOffsets');
    if (!wrap) return;
    const sel = $('intentionType');
    if (!sel) { wrap.innerHTML = ''; return; }
    // Pull all option elements regardless of optgroup nesting.
    const opts = Array.from(sel.querySelectorAll('option')).filter(o => o.value);
    if (!state.directorBiasByDomain) state.directorBiasByDomain = {};
    wrap.innerHTML = opts.map(o => {
      const id = o.value;
      const label = o.textContent.trim();
      const cur = Number(state.directorBiasByDomain[id]) || 0;
      return `<label style="display:flex;align-items:center;gap:8px;font-size:0.66rem;color:var(--text-dim)">
        <span style="flex:1;min-width:0;overflow:hidden;text-overflow:ellipsis;white-space:nowrap">${escapeHtml(label)}</span>
        <input type="range" min="-3" max="3" step="1" value="${cur}" data-domain="${escapeHtml(id)}" style="flex:1;max-width:120px">
        <span class="domain-offset-val" data-for="${escapeHtml(id)}" style="font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums;width:20px;text-align:right;color:${cur === 0 ? 'var(--text-faint)' : 'var(--neon-cyan)'}">${cur > 0 ? '+' : ''}${cur}</span>
      </label>`;
    }).join('');
  }
  // Render once on init. Re-render is cheap so just always re-run when the
  // bias UI updates.
  renderDirectorDomainOffsets();
  // Delegate input handler — sliders persist their offset to state on input.
  $('directorDomainOffsets')?.addEventListener('input', e => {
    const target = e.target;
    if (!target?.dataset?.domain) return;
    const domain = target.dataset.domain;
    const val = Math.max(-3, Math.min(3, parseInt(target.value, 10) || 0));
    if (!state.directorBiasByDomain) state.directorBiasByDomain = {};
    if (val === 0) delete state.directorBiasByDomain[domain];
    else state.directorBiasByDomain[domain] = val;
    const valEl = $('directorDomainOffsets').querySelector(`[data-for="${CSS.escape(domain)}"]`);
    if (valEl) {
      valEl.textContent = (val > 0 ? '+' : '') + val;
      valEl.style.color = val === 0 ? 'var(--text-faint)' : 'var(--neon-cyan)';
    }
  });
  $('directorDomainOffsets')?.addEventListener('change', () => saveUserSettings());
  $('remoteConsentCancelBtn')?.addEventListener('click', () => {
    const cb = $('remoteProcessingOptIn'); if (cb) cb.checked = false;
    state.remoteProcessingOptIn = false;
    saveUserSettings();
    closeRemoteConsentModal();
  });
  $('remoteConsentAgreeBtn')?.addEventListener('click', () => {
    const cb = $('remoteProcessingOptIn'); if (cb) cb.checked = true;
    state.remoteProcessingOptIn = true;
    saveUserSettings();
    closeRemoteConsentModal();
    notify.info('Remote backup + processing enabled.');
  });
  $('remoteConsentModal')?.addEventListener('click', e => {
    if (e.target?.id === 'remoteConsentModal') closeRemoteConsentModal();
  });

  // Profile form — save on any input change
  ['pfName', 'pfAbout', 'pfAppearance', 'pfGoals'].forEach(id => {
    $(id)?.addEventListener('input', saveProfile);
  });
  ['pfPronouns', 'pfAge', 'pfExperience', 'pfUseFirstName'].forEach(id => {
    $(id)?.addEventListener('change', saveProfile);
  });
  // Universal "use profile details" toggle — fans out to the three
  // hidden mirrors (pfShareAge / pfShareAppearance / pfShareGoals) so
  // the existing per-field read code in getProfile() stays unchanged.
  $('pfShareAll')?.addEventListener('change', e => {
    const on = !!e.target.checked;
    ['pfShareAge', 'pfShareAppearance', 'pfShareGoals'].forEach(id => {
      const el = $(id); if (el) el.checked = on;
    });
    saveProfile();
  });
  // When the age field changes, surfaces sensitive to the relaxation
  // gate refresh in place so the user sees the effect immediately —
  // softer soundscape hints, no nag prompts, etc. Existing dropdown
  // ordering doesn't reflow (would feel unstable mid-session); changes
  // there require a reload, but that's fine since the order isn't
  // user-actionable.
  $('pfAge')?.addEventListener('change', () => {
    if (typeof _refreshSoundscapeHint === 'function') _refreshSoundscapeHint();
  });

  // AI activity log — read-only modal. Lists recent AI calls' metadata
  // (provider, source, byte size, time). No content. Builds trust by
  // showing exactly what the app sent and where, without re-exposing the
  // user's profile/intention text.
  $('viewAIAuditBtn')?.addEventListener('click', async () => {
    if (!_aiAuditLoaded) await _loadAuditLog();
    const recent = (_aiAuditLog || []).slice(0, 30);
    if (!recent.length) {
      alertModal('AI activity log', 'No AI calls logged yet. The log starts populating once you craft a suggestion or generate a script.');
      return;
    }
    const lines = recent.map(e => {
      const rel = (typeof _relTimeDB === 'function') ? _relTimeDB(e.ts) : new Date(e.ts).toLocaleString();
      const kb = e.bytes >= 1024 ? `${(e.bytes / 1024).toFixed(1)} KB` : `${e.bytes} B`;
      return `${rel}  ·  ${e.provider}  ·  ${e.source}  ·  ${kb}`;
    });
    alertModal('AI activity log', `Recent AI calls (${recent.length} of ${_aiAuditLog.length} on record):\n\n${lines.join('\n')}\n\n— Provider: which AI backend handled the call.\n— Source: where in the app the call originated.\n— Size: byte length of the prompt sent. Content itself is never logged.`);
  });
  // Session daily cap — max 3 sessions per day to prevent compulsive use
  async function checkDailyCap() {
    const history = (await idbGet('stillness_history_v1')) || [];
    const today = new Date(); today.setHours(0,0,0,0);
    const todayCount = history.filter(s => {
      const d = new Date(s.date); d.setHours(0,0,0,0);
      return d.getTime() === today.getTime();
    }).length;
    if (todayCount >= 3) {
      notify.info('You\'ve completed 3 sessions today — that\'s plenty. Rest and let the suggestions integrate. Come back tomorrow.');
      return false;
    }
    return true;
  }

  // Monthly safety re-check — re-display contraindication screen every 30 days

  // Practice-unlock gating — methods unlock with experience
  async function getSessionCount() {
    const history = (await idbGet('stillness_history_v1')) || [];
    return history.filter(s => s.completed).length;
  }

  async function applyMethodGating() {
    const count = await getSessionCount();
    const skip = state._debug?.skipMethodGating;
    if (skip) sdLog('method gating bypassed — debug mode on');
    // Cache the lock state so synchronous renderMethodGrid can apply the same
    // gating per-card without an async re-fetch. Updated in place each call.
    state._methodLockState = {
      sessionCount: count,
      skip: !!skip,
      lockedCats: skip ? new Set() : new Set([
        ...(count < 3 ? ['rapid'] : []),
        ...(count < 7 ? ['advanced'] : []),
      ]),
      reasonFor: (cat) => {
        if (skip) return '';
        if (cat === 'rapid' && count < 3) return `Unlocks after 3 completed sessions (${count} so far)`;
        if (cat === 'advanced' && count < 7) return `Unlocks after 7 completed sessions (${count} so far)`;
        return '';
      },
    };
    // Thresholds: rapid unlocks at 3 sessions, advanced at 7
    document.querySelectorAll('#methodFilterTabs .filter-tab').forEach(tab => {
      const cat = tab.dataset.filter;
      if (!skip && cat === 'rapid' && count < 3) {
        tab.style.opacity = '0.4';
        tab.title = `Unlocks after 3 completed sessions (${count} so far)`;
      } else if (!skip && cat === 'advanced' && count < 7) {
        tab.style.opacity = '0.4';
        tab.title = `Unlocks after 7 completed sessions (${count} so far)`;
      } else {
        tab.style.opacity = '';
        tab.title = skip && (cat === 'rapid' || cat === 'advanced') ? 'Debug: unlocked' : '';
      }
    });
    // Re-render the method grid so per-card lock state stays in sync.
    if (typeof renderMethodGrid === 'function') renderMethodGrid();
  }

  // ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  // HIDDEN PERSONA UNLOCKS — Step 1 reward system
  // ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  // Each entry maps a hidden persona's id (from BUILTIN_PERSONAS where
  // `hidden:true`) to an unlock condition + a hint shown in the locked card.
  // `check(metrics)` receives a `_buildUnlockMetrics()` object and returns
  // boolean. Conditions are checked after every completed session in
  // `checkPersonaUnlocks()`; newly-true ids get a celebratory reveal modal.
  //
  // Design intent: each path rewards a different play pattern — variety,
  // duration, dedication, return-after-absence, etc. — so the surface area
  // is wide enough that most user shapes will hit at least one unlock
  // organically. Hints are *deliberately vague* about exact thresholds so
  // unlocks feel earned, not punched.
  const HIDDEN_PERSONA_UNLOCKS = {
    'cartographer': {
      hint: 'Visits a few different guides — variety reveals new paths',
      check: m => m.completedSessions >= 5 && m.uniquePersonasUsed >= 3,
    },
    'witness': {
      hint: 'Stays the course over many sessions',
      check: m => m.completedSessions >= 10,
    },
    'conductor': {
      hint: 'Listens to several different soundscapes',
      check: m => m.uniqueSoundscapesUsed >= 3,
    },
    'riverman': {
      hint: 'Returns for sleep, again and again',
      check: m => m.sleepIntentionSessions >= 4,
    },
    'dreamer': {
      hint: 'Spends real time in trance',
      check: m => m.longSessions >= 2,
    },
    'echo': {
      hint: 'Steps away — and comes back',
      check: m => m.returnedAfterAbsence === true,
    },
    'cipher': {
      hint: 'Explores different methods within a short window',
      check: m => m.methodsInLastWeek >= 3,
    },
    'lighthouse': {
      hint: 'Long-term steadiness — sessions completed cleanly',
      check: m => m.completedSessions >= 15 && m.emergencyExits === 0,
    },
  };

  /** Compute metrics for unlock checks from history + current state.
   *  Pure — does not mutate. Reads IDB once. Returns a stable shape so
   *  every check sees the same fields even when history is sparse. */
  async function _buildUnlockMetrics() {
    const history = (await idbGet('stillness_history_v1')) || [];
    const completed = history.filter(s => s.completed);
    const personas = new Set();
    const soundscapes = new Set();
    let sleepCount = 0;
    let longSessions = 0;
    let emergencyExits = 0;
    // Track methods used and their timestamps so we can compute the
    // "different methods in a 7-day window" condition.
    const methodEvents = []; // [{methodId, ts}]
    for (const s of history) {
      if (s.personaId) personas.add(s.personaId);
      if (s.soundscape && s.soundscape !== 'none') soundscapes.add(s.soundscape);
      if (s.intentionType === 'sleep' && s.completed) sleepCount++;
      if (s.completed && (s.metrics?.estimatedMin >= 30 || s.actualDurationMin >= 30)) longSessions++;
      if (s.emergencyExit || s.wasEmergency) emergencyExits++;
      const ts = Number(s.date || s.id || 0);
      if (s.methodId && Number.isFinite(ts) && ts > 0) methodEvents.push({ methodId: s.methodId, ts });
    }
    // Sliding 7-day window over methodEvents — the highest count of distinct
    // methods seen in any 7-day stretch, anchored on each event in turn.
    let maxMethodsInWindow = 0;
    methodEvents.sort((a, b) => a.ts - b.ts);
    const WEEK_MS = 7 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000;
    for (let i = 0; i < methodEvents.length; i++) {
      const windowEnd = methodEvents[i].ts;
      const seen = new Set();
      for (let j = i; j >= 0 && (windowEnd - methodEvents[j].ts) <= WEEK_MS; j--) {
        seen.add(methodEvents[j].methodId);
      }
      if (seen.size > maxMethodsInWindow) maxMethodsInWindow = seen.size;
    }
    // "Returned after 7+ idle days" — if the user has at least one completed
    // session AND any two consecutive completed sessions are more than 7 days
    // apart, the more-recent one counts as a return.
    let returnedAfterAbsence = false;
    const completedSorted = completed
      .map(s => Number(s.date || s.id || 0))
      .filter(n => Number.isFinite(n) && n > 0)
      .sort((a, b) => a - b);
    for (let i = 1; i < completedSorted.length; i++) {
      if ((completedSorted[i] - completedSorted[i-1]) >= WEEK_MS) {
        returnedAfterAbsence = true;
        break;
      }
    }
    return {
      completedSessions:    completed.length,
      uniquePersonasUsed:   personas.size,
      uniqueSoundscapesUsed: soundscapes.size,
      sleepIntentionSessions: sleepCount,
      longSessions,
      emergencyExits,
      methodsInLastWeek:    maxMethodsInWindow,
      returnedAfterAbsence,
    };
  }

  /** Check for newly-satisfied unlocks. Persists state.\_unlocks on each new
   *  unlock and shows a celebratory modal. Safe to call any number of times —
   *  idempotent for already-unlocked ids. */
  async function checkPersonaUnlocks() {
    if (!state._unlocks || typeof state._unlocks !== 'object') state._unlocks = {};
    let metrics;
    try { metrics = await _buildUnlockMetrics(); } catch (e) { return; }
    const newlyUnlocked = [];
    for (const [pid, def] of Object.entries(HIDDEN_PERSONA_UNLOCKS)) {
      if (state._unlocks[pid]) continue; // already unlocked
      let pass = false;
      try { pass = !!def.check(metrics); } catch (e) { pass = false; }
      if (!pass) continue;
      state._unlocks[pid] = { unlockedAt: Date.now() };
      newlyUnlocked.push(pid);
    }
    if (newlyUnlocked.length) {
      try { await idbSet('stillness_unlocks_v1', state._unlocks); } catch {}
      // Re-render so the new persona appears in the grid behind the modal
      try { renderPersonaGrid(); } catch {}
      // Show a celebratory reveal modal — these are the only kind of toast
      // we WANT to interrupt with, but we run after the session has already
      // finished, so it's a normal modal flow.
      try { _showPersonaUnlockReveal(newlyUnlocked); } catch (e) { console.warn('unlock reveal:', e); }
    }
  }

  /** Build and show the celebratory unlock modal. Lists each unlocked
   *  persona's name + tagline + one-line "what they're for" pitch, with a
   *  "view all" button that drops to Step 1 with the persona filter set
   *  to show the new arrival. */
  function _showPersonaUnlockReveal(personaIds) {
    const personas = personaIds
      .map(id => BUILTIN_PERSONAS.find(p => p.id === id))
      .filter(Boolean);
    if (!personas.length) return;
    const backdrop = document.createElement('div');
    backdrop.className = 'modal-backdrop visible';
    const isMulti = personas.length > 1;
    const cards = personas.map(p => `
      <div style="padding:14px;border:1px solid var(--border, #2a2f3a);background:var(--bg-deep, #0e1218);margin-bottom:10px">
        <div style="font-size:0.66rem;letter-spacing:0.12em;text-transform:uppercase;color:var(--accent-warm, #d4a772);margin-bottom:6px">✦ new guide unlocked</div>
        <div style="font-size:1.04rem;font-weight:500;color:var(--text, #e8edf2);margin-bottom:4px">${escapeHtml(p.name)}</div>
        <div style="font-size:0.78rem;color:var(--text-dim, #98a2b3);font-style:italic;margin-bottom:8px">${escapeHtml(p.tagline)}</div>
        <div style="font-size:0.74rem;color:var(--text-faint, #6c7689);line-height:1.45">${escapeHtml(p.systemPrompt.split('. ').slice(0, 2).join('. ') + '.')}</div>
      </div>
    `).join('');
    backdrop.innerHTML = `<div class="modal" style="max-width:520px">
      <h2 style="margin:0 0 8px 0;font-size:1.1rem">${isMulti ? `${personas.length} guides revealed` : 'A new guide reveals itself'}</h2>
      <div style="font-size:0.78rem;color:var(--text-dim, #98a2b3);margin-bottom:14px;line-height:1.5">
        Your practice has earned ${isMulti ? 'these voices' : 'this voice'}. ${isMulti ? 'They\'re' : 'It\'s'} now available in Step 1.
      </div>
      ${cards}
      <div class="modal-actions" style="margin-top:14px;display:flex;gap:8px;justify-content:flex-end">
        <button class="btn-ghost" id="_unlockRevealLater">later</button>
        <button class="btn-primary" id="_unlockRevealView">meet ${isMulti ? 'them' : 'them'}</button>
      </div>
    </div>`;
    document.body.appendChild(backdrop);
    const close = () => { try { backdrop.remove(); } catch {} };
    backdrop.querySelector('#_unlockRevealLater')?.addEventListener('click', close);
    backdrop.querySelector('#_unlockRevealView')?.addEventListener('click', () => {
      close();
      // Pre-select the first newly-unlocked persona and jump to Step 1
      try {
        state.selectedPersonaId = personas[0].id;
        if (typeof goToStep === 'function') goToStep(1);
        renderPersonaGrid(); renderPersonaDetail();
      } catch {}
    });
    // Click outside to dismiss
    backdrop.addEventListener('click', e => { if (e.target === backdrop) close(); });
  }

  /** Render a single small "🔒 N hidden" indicator at the bottom of the
   *  persona grid. Each locked persona shows its hint (but not its name) so
   *  the user gets directional guidance without spoilers. */
  function renderHiddenPersonaHint() {
    const grid = $('personaGrid');
    if (!grid) return;
    // Remove any prior hint card so re-renders don't stack them
    grid.querySelectorAll('.hidden-guide-hint').forEach(el => el.remove());
    const lockedIds = Object.keys(HIDDEN_PERSONA_UNLOCKS).filter(id => !state._unlocks?.[id]);
    if (!lockedIds.length) return; // all unlocked → no card
    const card = document.createElement('div');
    card.className = 'card hidden-guide-hint';
    card.style.cssText = 'border:1px dashed var(--border, #2a2f3a);background:transparent;cursor:help;opacity:0.72;';
    const hints = lockedIds.map(id => `<div style="font-size:0.66rem;line-height:1.5;color:var(--text-faint, #6c7689);padding:2px 0">· ${escapeHtml(HIDDEN_PERSONA_UNLOCKS[id].hint)}</div>`).join('');
    card.innerHTML = `<div class="card-category-label" style="color:var(--text-faint, #6c7689)">${lockedIds.length} hidden</div>
      <div class="card-name" style="color:var(--text-dim, #98a2b3)">🔒 not yet revealed</div>
      <div class="card-tagline" style="font-size:0.7rem;margin-top:6px">Each guide unlocks through a different rhythm of practice. Hover for hints.</div>
      <div style="margin-top:8px;display:none" class="hidden-guide-hint-detail">${hints}</div>`;
    // Show hints on hover/tap to reduce visual noise on first load
    let expanded = false;
    card.addEventListener('click', () => {
      expanded = !expanded;
      const det = card.querySelector('.hidden-guide-hint-detail');
      if (det) det.style.display = expanded ? '' : 'none';
    });
    grid.appendChild(card);
  }

  // Persona modal
  $('personaCancelBtn').addEventListener('click', closePersonaEditor);
  $('personaSaveBtn').addEventListener('click', savePersonaFromForm);
  $('personaShareBtn')?.addEventListener('click', shareCurrentPersona);
  $('personaModal').addEventListener('click', e => { if (e.target.id === 'personaModal') closePersonaEditor(); });

  // Hypnotic Induction Profile quiz

  // Init — async to wait for IDB storage migration
  (async function init() {
    detectCaps();
    await _initStorage();
    refreshPersonas();
    const hadSavedSettings = await loadUserSettings();
    await loadProfile();
    // Default-enable adaptive playback dynamics for engaged users (profile
    // age set). Skipped when the user already had explicit saved settings —
    // their persisted choice wins in that case. Anonymous users keep the
    // conservative off-default. The flip is one-time: once enabled and
    // saved, subsequent loads honor whatever the user toggled it to.
    if (!hadSavedSettings && typeof _audioVizSafetiesRelaxed === 'function' && _audioVizSafetiesRelaxed()) {
      if (!state.playbackDynamics) state.playbackDynamics = { ...PLAYBACK_DYNAMICS_DEFAULTS };
      state.playbackDynamics.enabled = true;
    }
    initDiffBouncers(); // wire DiffBouncer coverage to tracked fields
    if (typeof loadVoices === 'function') { loadVoices(); applySavedVoiceSelection(); updateVoiceInfo(); }
    renderPersonaGrid();
    renderPersonaDetail();
    renderMethodGrid();
    renderMethodDetail();
    // Restore filter tab visual state from saved settings
    document.querySelectorAll('#personaFilterTabs .filter-tab').forEach(t => t.classList.toggle('active', t.dataset.filter === state.personaFilter));
    document.querySelectorAll('#methodFilterTabs .filter-tab').forEach(t => t.classList.toggle('active', t.dataset.filter === state.methodFilter));
    renderPresets();
    renderPrograms();
    renderSessionHistory();
    renderHeatmap();
    renderSidebarStats();
    updateIntentionHint();
    // Update bg cache count
    bgCacheGetManifest().then(m => { const el = $('bgCacheCount'); if (el) el.textContent = m.length; });
    // Set soundscape default based on method — but only if no saved settings
    if (!hadSavedSettings) {
      const defaultMethod = getMethod();
      state.soundscape = defaultMethod.drone ? 'drone' : 'none';
      state.droneEnabled = defaultMethod.drone;
      const ssSelect = $('soundscapeSelect');
      if (ssSelect) ssSelect.value = state.soundscape;
    }
    updateStepUI();

    // Load shared config from URL fragment if present
    await loadSharedConfig();

    // AI preload (delayed on mobile to avoid slowing page load)
    if (typeof root !== 'undefined' && root.aiTextPlugin) {
      if (window.innerWidth < 500) setTimeout(() => root.aiTextPlugin({ preload: true }), 5000);
      else root.aiTextPlugin({ preload: true });
    }

    // Onboarding tour for first-time visitors
    if (shouldShowTour()) setTimeout(startTour, 800);

    // Rising-score sparkline
    renderSparkline();

    // Practice-unlock method gating
    applyMethodGating();
    // Advanced bg control gating — disable orientation/strength/steps on the
    // free Perchance plugin (whose documented contract doesn't accept them);
    // re-enable when the user has wired up their own API key.
    try { _applyAdvancedBgControlGating(); } catch {}
    // Persona unlock state — load from IDB so previously-revealed hidden
    // guides stay revealed across reloads. checkPersonaUnlocks() also runs
    // here in case the user just barely crossed a threshold last session
    // (e.g. via direct IDB import); newly satisfied conditions are revealed
    // with the celebratory modal even on init.
    try {
      const stored = await idbGet('stillness_unlocks_v1');
      if (stored && typeof stored === 'object') state._unlocks = stored;
    } catch {}
    try { renderPersonaGrid(); } catch {}
    // Run check after a tick so the rest of init settles first — the modal
    // is jarring during page load if it appears before the grid paints.
    setTimeout(() => { checkPersonaUnlocks().catch(() => {}); }, 1200);

    // Weekly digest — computed quietly on init and stashed on state for
    // passive surface (sidebar stats panel). Previously fired a notify on
    // first-load-of-week which felt aggressive for returning users.
    setTimeout(async () => {
      try {
        const history = (await idbGet('stillness_history_v1')) || [];
        if (history.length < 2) return;
        const now = Date.now();
        const SEVEN_DAYS = 7 * 86400000;
        const cutoff = now - SEVEN_DAYS;
        const recent = history.filter(r => r.date >= cutoff);
        if (recent.length < 2) return;
        const completed = recent.filter(r => r.completed).length;
        const completionPct = Math.round((completed / recent.length) * 100);
        const withMood = recent.filter(r => r.preMood != null && r.postMood != null);
        const avgDelta = withMood.length
          ? (withMood.reduce((s, r) => s + (r.postMood - r.preMood), 0) / withMood.length)
          : null;
        const personaCount = {}, methodCount = {};
        for (const r of recent) {
          if (r.guide) personaCount[r.guide] = (personaCount[r.guide] || 0) + 1;
          if (r.method) methodCount[r.method] = (methodCount[r.method] || 0) + 1;
        }
        const topPersona = Object.entries(personaCount).sort((a, b) => b[1] - a[1])[0]?.[0];
        const topMethod  = Object.entries(methodCount).sort((a, b) => b[1] - a[1])[0]?.[0];
        // Stash for the sidebar to pick up the next time it renders. Passive
        // surface: users discover when they choose to open stats.
        state._weeklyDigest = {
          sessionCount: recent.length,
          completionPct,
          avgMoodDelta: avgDelta,
          topPersona: topPersona || null,
          topMethod:  topMethod || null,
          windowDays: 7,
          generatedAt: now,
        };
        // Trigger a re-render of the sidebar stats so the digest tile
        // appears immediately if the panel happens to be open.
        try { renderSidebarStats(); } catch {}
      } catch (e) { console.warn('[weekly-digest] failed:', e); }
    }, 2500);

    // 24-hour recall probe — same passive treatment. The recall question
    // surfaces inside the post-session trigger card the next time the
    // user gets there organically; no push notification on boot.
    setTimeout(async () => {
      try {
        const history = (await idbGet('stillness_history_v1')) || [];
        if (!history.length) return;
        const now = Date.now();
        const ONE_DAY = 86400000;
        const cutoffOld = now - 3 * ONE_DAY;
        const cutoffYoung = now - 0.75 * ONE_DAY;
        const candidate = history.find(r =>
          r.date >= cutoffOld && r.date <= cutoffYoung &&
          r.completed &&
          r.craftedSuggestion?.trigger &&
          r.craftedSuggestion.trigger !== 'ongoing state'
        );
        if (!candidate) return;
        // Skip if user already logged a fire after that session — they
        // already gave us the signal.
        try {
          const fires = (await idbGet(TRIGGER_FIRES_KEY)) || {};
          const slot = fires[candidate.craftedSuggestion.trigger];
          if (slot?.firedDates?.length) {
            const lastFire = new Date(slot.firedDates[0]).getTime();
            if (lastFire > candidate.date) return;
          }
        } catch {}
        // Stash for the post-session card to show when next reached.
        state._pendingRecallProbe = {
          trigger: candidate.craftedSuggestion.trigger,
          daysAgo: Math.round((now - candidate.date) / ONE_DAY),
          sessionDate: candidate.date,
        };
      } catch (e) { console.warn('[recall-probe] failed:', e); }
    }, 4500);

    // Sync debug state from DOM (checkboxes default unchecked, but this guarantees
    // state._debug matches the visible UI in all cases). Banner re-renders from here.
    _syncDebugState();
    _updateDirectorBiasUI();
    _refreshAffirmationsBtnState(); // initial render so button reflects SD state at load

    // prefers-reduced-motion handling — if user's OS asks for less motion, gentle
    // visualizers and softer audio-reactive defaults. Only applied if the user
    // hasn't explicitly chosen a viz / settings (hadSavedSettings === false).
    // The list below is the "intense" category from the viz dropdown — visuals
    // most likely to be motion-sickness-inducing or seizure-risk-adjacent.
    const INTENSE_VIZUALS = new Set(['flowfield', 'plasma', 'kaleidoscope-flow', 'kaleidoscope-plasma', 'starfield', 'tunnel', 'vortex', 'gamma-flicker']);
    // Skip the whole reduced-motion auto-degrade path when audio/viz
    // safeties are relaxed (profile age set). The OS preference is still
    // available to renderers that want to consult it (gamma-flicker
    // currently does), but we don't reorder dropdowns, retag options
    // with ⚠, or push a "we changed your settings" notify at engaged
    // adults. Casual / unidentified users still get the original
    // protection.
    if (state.caps.reducedMotion && !_audioVizSafetiesRelaxed()) {
      // Tag intense viz options visually so any future selection signals the risk
      const vizSel = $('vizSelect');
      if (vizSel) {
        // Build a flat list of (option, isIntense, parentGroup) so we can
        // reorder while preserving optgroup labels. Intense options sink to
        // the bottom of their respective groups; if a group becomes all-
        // intense, the whole group sinks to the end of the select.
        const groups = Array.from(vizSel.querySelectorAll('optgroup'));
        const looseOptions = Array.from(vizSel.children).filter(c => c.tagName === 'OPTION');
        // Re-tag intense option labels first
        document.querySelectorAll('#vizSelect option').forEach(opt => {
          if (INTENSE_VIZUALS.has(opt.value)) {
            opt.textContent = '⚠ ' + opt.textContent + ' (motion-heavy)';
          }
        });
        // Within each optgroup, move intense options to the end
        groups.forEach(g => {
          const opts = Array.from(g.children);
          opts.forEach(o => { if (INTENSE_VIZUALS.has(o.value)) g.appendChild(o); });
        });
        // Among optgroups themselves, move groups that contain only intense
        // options (or are largely intense) to the end of the select
        groups.forEach(g => {
          const opts = Array.from(g.children);
          const intenseRatio = opts.filter(o => INTENSE_VIZUALS.has(o.value)).length / Math.max(opts.length, 1);
          if (intenseRatio >= 0.5) vizSel.appendChild(g); // moves to end
        });
        // Move any loose intense options to the very end
        looseOptions.forEach(o => { if (INTENSE_VIZUALS.has(o.value)) vizSel.appendChild(o); });
      }
      if (!hadSavedSettings) {
        // Defaults: gentle visual + halved viz dynamics + subtle (not dramatic) audio-reactive
        state.vizOverride = 'candle';
        const vs = $('vizSelect'); if (vs) vs.value = 'candle';
        state.vizSettings.intensity = (state.vizSettings.intensity || 1.0) * 0.5;
        state.vizSettings.speed     = (state.vizSettings.speed     || 1.0) * 0.5;
        if (state.audioReactiveMode === 'dramatic') state.audioReactiveMode = 'subtle';
        notify.info('Reduced-motion preference detected — gentler visualizer defaults applied.');
      }
    }

    // Second persistence attempt — per the perchance-api skill: the first call
    // (inside _initStorage) often gets denied because the browser hasn't seen
    // any user interaction yet. After a user interacts (clicks a step, etc.),
    // browsers are far more likely to grant persistent storage. We re-try once
    // 5s after init in case persistence was denied first time.
    setTimeout(() => {
      if (navigator.storage?.persist) {
        navigator.storage.persist().catch(() => {}); // silent — best-effort
      }
    }, 5000);

    // Emergency-export failsafe — if the page hasn't progressed beyond the first
    // screen after 30 seconds (suggesting a soft-lock somewhere in the boot flow),
    // surface a corner button that lets the user grab their session history before
    // they refresh out of frustration. Self-deletes on first interaction with the
    // app so it doesn't appear in normal usage.
    //
    // Gated on `state.remoteProcessingOptIn` because users who've opted *out* of
    // any extra processing surface presumably want the most minimal UI possible —
    // intrusive "are you stuck?" warnings violate that contract. Power users
    // who've opted into remote backup get the safety net.
    let _emergencyExportShown = false;
    function _showEmergencyExportButton() {
      if (_emergencyExportShown) return;
      _emergencyExportShown = true;
      const btn = document.createElement('button');
      btn.id = 'emergencyExportFailsafe';
      btn.textContent = '⚠ download backup (page may be stuck)';
      btn.title = 'If the app seems frozen, click to download your session history before reloading.';
      btn.style.cssText = 'position:fixed;bottom:60px;right:14px;z-index:99999;background:rgba(80,20,20,0.95);color:#fff;border:1px solid #ff6b6b;padding:8px 14px;border-radius:6px;font-size:0.78rem;cursor:pointer;font-family:var(--font-body);box-shadow:0 4px 14px rgba(0,0,0,0.5);';
      btn.addEventListener('click', () => {
        if (typeof exportBackup === 'function') exportBackup();
        else notify.warn('exportBackup unavailable — try refreshing.');
      });
      document.body.appendChild(btn);
    }
    setTimeout(() => {
      // Only show if user hasn't progressed past step 1 — implies a stuck boot.
      // Operator-precedence note: explicit parens around the OR-chain make the
      // condition "still on step 1 OR (no session AND no script)". Without these,
      // && would bind tighter and we'd fire on any non-step-1 page that hadn't
      // started a session yet (i.e. legitimate use of steps 2-5).
      const onStep1     = document.body.classList.contains('step-1');
      const inSession   = !!document.querySelector('.in-session');
      const hasScript   = !!document.querySelector('#scriptPreview.visible');
      const stillStuck  = onStep1 && !inSession && !hasScript;
      if (stillStuck && !_emergencyExportShown) _showEmergencyExportButton();
    }, 10000);
    // Cancel the failsafe on first user click anywhere — they're clearly not stuck.
    document.addEventListener('click', () => {
      const btn = document.getElementById('emergencyExportFailsafe');
      if (btn) btn.remove();
      _emergencyExportShown = true; // prevent later display
    }, { once: true, capture: true });
  })();
</script>

<script id="workflow-copy-polish-v9">
  (() => {
    const setLabel = (id, label) => { const el = document.getElementById(id); if (el) el.setAttribute('aria-label', label); };
    setLabel('jumpGuideBtn', 'Go back and change the guide');
    setLabel('jumpMethodBtn', 'Go back and change the method');
    setLabel('jumpIntentionBtn', 'Go back and change the intention');
    setLabel('jumpAffirmationsBtn', 'Go back and change your affirmations');
    setLabel('jumpConfigBtn', 'Go back and change the session settings');
    setLabel('btnSkipToScript', 'Skip the guided builder and use your own script');
    setLabel('returnToSetupBtn', 'Go back to the setup screens');
    setLabel('restartBtn', 'Start another session with the current setup');
  })();
</script>
